english_item: Some Americans who saw their SNAP benefits halt on Nov. 1 say they have to choose between paying their rent and buying food.

chinese_item:
事故发生当天，巴利先生收到了一张101.71英镑的市政税账单。
巴利先生所在的市政当局按照英格兰未付市政税的常规程序行事。
他说，他的市政税债务可能是最难处理的，因为总额增长得非常快。
六月份发布的数据显示，英格兰地方当局总共被拖欠了66亿英镑的市政税，比五年前的44亿英镑增加了50%——尽管市政当局注销的市政税债务比以前多了。
食品银行经理弗兰科·比亚卡尔多表示，这部分增长中有一部分是由于市政税债务造成的。
english_item:
The same day as the accident, Mr Barley was sent a council tax bill for an instalment of ￡101.71.
Mr Barley's council acted in line with the usual process for unpaid council tax in England.
He said his council tax debt was "probably the worst" to deal with because of the speed with which the total amount increased.
Figures published in June showed local authorities in England are owed ￡6.6bn in council tax in total, up 50% from ￡4.4bn five years ago - despite councils writing off more council tax debt than they used to.
Foodbank manager Franco Biancardo said some of this increase was down to council tax debt.

chinese_item:
有人见到过科尔吗？
“我听说他只是一个失踪的小男孩，可能在外面喝醉了，”科尔的母亲温迪·斯图尔特说。
科尔的家人觉得他们别无选择，只能自己当起调查员来呼吁寻找。
他们在社交媒体上发了一条简单的帖子：“有人见到过科尔吗？”
艾米说，他们收到了数百条来自认为见过科尔的人的消息。
english_item:
Has anyone seen Cole?
"I was told that he's just a missing young boy, who's possibly out and drunk," said Cole's mum, Wendy Stewart.
Cole's family felt like they had no choice but to become investigators and make their own appeal.
They took to social media, writing one simple post: "Has anyone seen Cole?"
Aimee said they received hundreds of messages from people who thought they had seen Cole.

chinese_item: 九月份雇主增加了11.9万个工作岗位，远超许多分析师的预期，但根据劳工部的数据，失业率从4.3%微升至4.4%。这一消息引发了人们对原本被视为“低雇佣、低解雇”就业市场的担忧。该群体的失业率在九月上升至2.8%，而去年同期为2.3%。周四发布的这份报告是美联储十二月会议前最后一次官方就业市场数据发布。尽管九月份的就业增长超出预期，但报告还显示七月份和八月份的就业增长低于先前估计。
english_item:
Employers added 119,000 jobs in September, more than double what many analysts had expected, but the unemployment rate ticked up from 4.3% to 4.4%, the Labor Department figures showed.
The announcements have raised concerns about cracks in what has been seen as a "low-hire, low-fire" job market.
The unemployment rate for that group rose to 2.8% in September, up from from 2.3% a year earlier.
Thursday's report is the last official release on the job market before the Fed's next meeting in December.
While September's job gains were stronger than expected, the report also showed job growth in July and August was lower than previously estimated.

chinese_item:
自唐纳德·特朗普总统上任以来，移民突袭行动一直在全美范围内进行——他旨在兑现发起“史上最大规模驱逐计划”的承诺。美国移民与海关执法局（简称ICE）及其他联邦机构所采用的手段引发了抗议活动。BBC Verify收集了70多段视频来追踪迄今为止特朗普打击行动的进展——正如杰克·霍顿所解释的那样。
制作人：艾莎·森比。
额外报道：凯琳·德夫林、沙扬·萨达里扎德、凯文·阮和奥尔加·罗宾逊。
english_item:
Immigration raids have been taking place across the US since President Donald Trump entered office - as he aims to deliver on his promise to launch "the largest deportation programme" in the country's history.
The tactics being used by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, known as ICE, and other federal agencies have led to protests.
BBC Verify has gathered more than 70 videos to track how Trump's crackdown has unfolded so far - as Jake Horton explains.
Produced by Aisha Sembhi.
Additional reporting by Kayleen Devlin, Shayan Sardarizadeh, Kevin Nguyen and Olga Robinson.

chinese_item: BBC在伦敦北部的皇家自由医院度过了整整一天，以了解国民保健服务（NHS）如何应对每年冬季的巨大压力。在医院里，克莱夫·迈里发现走廊上挤满了病床，还有等待治疗的病人。“这将是一个漫长的夜晚。”他说。
english_item:
The BBC has spent a day inside the Royal Free Hospital in north London to find out how the NHS is coping after intense, annual winter pressures.
At the hospital, Clive Myrie found corridors filled with beds, and patients waiting to be treated.
'It's going to be a long night', he said.

chinese_item:
昨天，一架UPS货机在美国肯塔基州坠毁，造成至少九人死亡。
这起事故发生在该飞机从路易斯维尔国际机场起飞后不久。
BBC验证团队通过整理网上流传的视频片段，还原了坠机过程——Jake Horton对此进行了说明。
图形制作：Sally Nicholls。
核实工作：Paul Brown、Emma Pengelly 和 Kayleen Devlin。
english_item:
At least nine people have died after a UPS cargo plane crashed in the US state of Kentucky yesterday.
The incident happened shortly after the aircraft took off from Louisville International Airport.
BBC Verify has pieced together clips being shared online to see how the crash unfolded - as Jake Horton explains.
Graphics by Sally Nicholls.
Verification by Paul Brown, Emma Pengelly and Kayleen Devlin.

chinese_item: BBC记者娜达·陶菲克从牙买加的黑河发回报道，这里遭受了该国有史以来最强风暴的袭击，现场一片绝望。这场风暴于10月28日以五级飓风的强度登陆，持续风速达到每小时185英里（298公里）。成千上万的人仍然处于断电状态。
english_item:
The BBC's Nada Tawfik reports from Black River, Jamaica, where the strongest storm to hit the country in modern history has left scenes of desperation.
The storm made landfall 28 October as a category five hurricane with sustained winds of 185mph (298kph).
Thousands of people remain without power.

chinese_item:
到2050年，海平面上升将威胁150万澳大利亚人——报告
2025年9月15日 分享 保存 Lana Lam 悉尼 分享 保存EPA
澳大利亚人已经在承受气候变化的后果。一份具有里程碑意义的气候报告警告称，到2050年，生活在沿海地区的150万澳大利亚人将面临海平面上升的风险。
澳大利亚首份《国家气候风险评估》预测，像洪水、飓风、热浪、干旱和林火这样的气候灾害将变得更加频繁和严重。
该报告考察了三种全球变暖情景——超过1.5摄氏度、超过2摄氏度和超过3摄氏度。
报告指出，澳大利亚北部地区、偏远社区以及主要城市的外围郊区尤其处于高风险之中。
报告警告说：“这将对健康、关键基础设施、自然物种和生态系统以及初级产业造成压力，”同时也会给应急响应人员带来额外挑战。
english_item:
Rising seas will threaten 1.5 million Australians by 2050 - report15 September 2025 Share Save Lana Lam Sydney Share SaveEPA Australians are already living with the consequences of climate changeOne and a half million Australians living in coastal areas are at risk from rising sea levels by 2050, a landmark climate report has warned.
Australia's first National Climate Risk Assessment predicted more frequent and severe climate hazards like floods, cyclones, heatwaves, droughts and bushfires.
The report looked at three global warming scenarios - above 1.5C, above 2C and above 3C.
Areas in northern Australia, along with remote communities and outer suburbs of major cities, were particularly at risk, the report said.
"This will put pressure on health, critical infrastructure, natural species and ecosystems, and primary industries," the report warned, as well as posing extra challenges for emergency responders.

chinese_item:
谷歌母公司Alphabet的负责人在接受BBC采访时说，人们不应该“盲目相信”AI工具告诉他们的一切。
“这就是为什么人们也会使用谷歌搜索，我们还有其他产品能够更准确地提供信息。”
该公司在其AI工具上显示免责声明，让用户知道这些工具可能会出错。
生成式AI产品（如聊天机器人）传递误导性或虚假信息的趋势引起了专家们的担忧。
她还敦促谷歌对其AI产品的准确性承担更多责任，而不是将这一责任转嫁给消费者。
english_item:
Don't blindly trust what AI tells you, says Google's Sundar Pichai6 days ago Share Save Faisal Islam , economics editor , Rachel Clun , business reporter and Liv McMahon , Technology reporter Share SaveGetty ImagesPeople should not "blindly trust" everything AI tools tell them, the boss of Google's parent company Alphabet has told the BBC.
"This is why people also use Google search, and we have other products that are more grounded in providing accurate information."
The company displays disclaimers on its AI tools to let users know they can make mistakes.
The tendency for generative AI products, such as chatbots, to relay misleading or false information, is a cause of concern among experts.
She also urged Google to take more responsibility over its AI products and their accuracy, rather than passing that on to consumers.

chinese_item:
BBC Radio 2 的主持人萨拉·考克斯完成了她长达135英里的史诗级跑步挑战，为“儿童需要”筹集了超过760万英镑的善款。
萨拉的“大北方马拉松挑战”于周一在苏格兰和英格兰边界的凯尔德森林开始，并于周五下午在利兹的普德西结束。
她在Radio 2上表示，这一周的跑步是她“有生以来做过的最疯狂、最痛苦但也是最美妙的事情”。
english_item:
BBC Radio 2 presenter Sara Cox has completed her epic 135-mile run which has raised more than ￡7.6m for Children in Need.
Sara's Great Northern Marathon Challenge started on Monday in Kielder Forest on the border of Scotland and England, and finished in Pudsey, Leeds, on Friday afternoon.
She told Radio 2 the week-long run was the "maddest, most painful, wonderful thing I've ever, ever done".

chinese_item:
在约克郡东部的特殊教育需求和残疾（SEND）服务中发现了“不一致之处”。2小时前 分享 保存 Eleanor Maslin 东约克郡和林肯郡 分享 保存Getty Images
Ofsted与护理质量委员会发布了一份关于东约克郡SEND服务的报告。检查员发现，在照顾东约克郡有特殊教育需求和残疾的儿童方面，存在“体验和结果不一致”的情况。
根据周一发布的报告，Ofsted和护理质量委员会表示，一些家长由于对SEND系统的了解有限而感到缺乏支持且焦虑。
地方议会和亨伯及北约克郡综合护理委员会（ICB）组成了东约克郡SEND服务的地方合作机构。
针对这些“不一致”，Aitken表示，“全国范围内都面临着资金不足和需求增加的危机”。
她说：“东赖丁是最大的地方政府之一，但在150个地区中，其高需求资金却是最低的。”
english_item:
'Inconsistencies' found in county's SEND service2 hours ago Share Save Eleanor Maslin East Yorkshire and Lincolnshire Share SaveGetty Images Ofsted and the Care Quality Commission published a report on East Yorkshire's SEND serviceInspectors have found "inconsistent experiences and outcomes" in how children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) in East Yorkshire are cared for.
Ofsted and the Care Quality Commission said some parents felt unsupported and anxious due to their limited understanding of the SEND system, according to a report published on Monday.
The council and NHS Humber and North Yorkshire Integrated Care Board (ICB) make up the local area partnership for SEND provision in East Yorkshire.
Addressing the "inconsistencies", Aitken said there was "a national crisis with insufficient funding and increasing needs".
She said: "East Riding is one of the largest local authorities and receives the lowest funding for high needs out of 150 areas.

chinese_item:
新冠调查听证会得知，政府直到决定关闭学校的前一天才开始进行相关规划。据前教育大臣加文·威廉姆森提供的证据显示，直到2020年3月16日为止，重点一直放在保持学校开放上，因此没有任何应急计划。
克莱尔·多宾律师在听证会上引用了加文爵士的书面证词，称这是一次“令人困惑的24小时巨变”。
她说：“对于如此重大的事件是否存在规划存在争议，这一点非常重要。”
威尔士政府承认在关闭学校方面“规划不足”，而苏格兰政府也表示其“准备有限”。
english_item:
One day of planning went into Covid school closures, inquiry hears29 September 2025 Share Save Vanessa Clarke Education reporter Share SaveGetty ImagesThe government did not start planning for the closure of schools until the day before the decision was made, the Covid-19 inquiry has heard.
Up until 16 March 2020, focus had been on keeping schools open, so there were no contingency plans in place, according to evidence given by the former education secretary Gavin Williamson.
Sir Gavin described a "discombobulating 24-hour sea change" in his written evidence, counsel to the inquiry Clair Dobbin KC said.
"It is significant that there should be any dispute about whether planning for so seismic an event existed," she said.
The Welsh government acknowledged there was "inadequate planning" to close schools, and the Scottish government has said there was also "limited preparedness".

chinese_item:
在寻找失踪86岁老人的过程中发现一具遗体。杰弗里·惠特菲尔德于周四从沃灵顿的家中失踪。作为搜寻两天前失踪的一名86岁老人的一部分，人们在默西河中发现了一具遗体。
柴郡警方表示，杰弗里·惠特菲尔德于格林尼治标准时间周四上午10点被报告从沃灵顿拉奇福德的家中失踪。
在遗体被发现之前，进行了一场大规模搜索行动，包括潜水员、当地居民和其他志愿者参与其中。遗体是在大约格林尼治标准时间中午12点左右在河中被找到的。
尼克·班伯督察说：“遗憾的是，这不是我们所希望的结果，在这个艰难时刻，我们的心与杰弗里的家人同在。”
english_item:
Body found in search for missing 86-year-old manGeoffrey Whitfield went missing from his home in Warrington on ThursdayA body has been discovered in the River Mersey as part of the search for an 86-year-old man who went missing two days ago.
Cheshire Police said Geoffrey Whitfield was reported as missing from his home in Latchford, Warrington, at 10:00 GMT on Thursday.
A major search involving divers, local residents and other volunteers took place before the body was found in the river earlier at about 12:00 GMT.
Insp Nick Bamber said: "Sadly, this is not the outcome that we were hoping for and our thoughts are with Geoffrey's family at this difficult time.

chinese_item:
取消二孩福利上限“会帮助很多人”——一位母亲12小时前分享保存斯宾塞·斯托克斯约克郡商业和交通记者分享保存斯宾塞·斯托克斯/英国广播公司来自布拉德福德的因提亚兹·贝古姆表示，取消二孩福利上限“会帮助很多人支付日常必需品”。

作为三个孩子的母亲，贝古姆为她七岁和九岁的两个大孩子领取福利，但四岁的小儿子却无法享受这一待遇。她说，如果能结束二孩福利上限，她将非常欢迎。

瑞秋·里夫斯在本周的预算案中暗示支持取消与家庭规模相关的福利限制。

贝古姆女士说，取消二孩福利上限“不仅对我有帮助，也会帮助很多正在挣扎的人们，他们可能不愿意承认自己在挣扎”。

“许多北方社区受到二孩福利上限的影响尤为严重，这些地区的儿童贫困率更高。”
english_item:
Lifting two-child cap 'would help a lot' - mum12 hours ago Share Save Spencer Stokes Yorkshire business and transport correspondent Share SaveSpencer Stokes/BBC Imtyaz Begum, from Bradford, says lifting the two-child cap "would help a lot of people"Lifting the two-child benefit cap would "help a lot" to pay for everyday essentials, a mum-of-three in Bradford has said.
Imtyaz Begum, who receives benefits for her eldest children, aged seven and nine, but not for her youngest, aged four, said she would welcome an end to the two-child benefit cap.
Rachel Reeves has suggested she favoured removing limits on benefits linked to family size in this week's Budget.
Ms Begum said lifting the two-child benefit cap "would help, not just myself but a lot of people out there that are struggling and who don't want to say they're struggling".
"Many northern communities are disproportionately affected by the two-child benefit cap, with higher rates of child poverty.

chinese_item:
他正在接受泌尿科顾问医生海瑟姆·埃尔萨卡的治疗，这位医生表示，他注意到因使用氯胺酮而导致膀胱受损的年轻患者数量有所增加。
兰开夏郡议会称，他们正在帮助伯恩利地区越来越多受到氯胺酮成瘾影响的家庭。
而在数千英里之外，在英国城镇的普通年轻人中，氯胺酮——也被称为“特别K”、“维生素K”或“奇巧”——也变得流行起来。
据莱克西说，许多年轻人被氯胺酮吸引的一个原因是他们可以用零花钱的价格买到这种药物。
“极其危险”BBC采访的一些氯胺酮成瘾者的家人希望将这种药物重新归类为A类毒品。
english_item:
He is under the care of consultant urologist Haytham Elsakka, who says he has witnessed a rise in young patients whose bladders have been damaged by ketamine use.
Lancashire County Council says it is helping a growing number of families affected by ketamine addiction in the Burnley area.
And thousands of miles away, among ordinary young people in UK towns, ketamine – also known as special K, vitamin K, or kit kat - has also become fashionable.
One reason that so many young people are gravitating towards ketamine is they can buy it for pocket-money prices, according to Lexi.
'Extremely dangerous'Some of the families of ketamine addicts the BBC has spoken to want the drug reclassified as Class A.

chinese_item:
大多数ADHD患者已经用完了药物——报告
2025年10月29日 分享 保存 亨利·戈弗雷-埃文斯，埃塞克斯；本·弗莱尔，埃塞克斯 分享 保存
洛伊莎·加西亚表示，当她搬到布伦特伍德时，被告知她的药物供应已经停止。
一份关于埃塞克斯的报告称，ADHD患者对未来感到“焦虑”，超过四分之三的人已经用完了药物。报告还指出，越来越少的全科医生愿意在共享护理协议下开具处方，这意味着患者不得不依赖专科医生或自费购买药物。
“我搬到了布伦特伍德，他们告诉我‘对不起，我们不再提供共享护理了’。”她说。
“我们还继续与当地全科诊所和专科服务提供商密切合作，改进ADHD护理路径，以便更好地支持居民。我们已经委托了五家新的成人ADHD服务提供商，这将为她提供更多选择。”
english_item:
Most ADHD patients have run out of meds - report29 October 2025 Share Save Henry Godfrey-Evans , Essex and Ben Fryer , Essex Share SaveSupplied Louisa Garcia said she was told her supply medication had stopped when she moved to BrentwoodADHD sufferers are "stressed" about their future with more than three-quarters having run out of medication, a report for Essex says.
It claims that fewer GPs are writing prescriptions under shared care agreements, which means patients are reliant on specialists or going private for their drugs.
"I moved to Brentwood and I got told 'sorry, we don't do shared care anymore'," she said.
"We also continue to work closely with local GP practices and specialist providers to make improvements to ADHD care pathways to better support residents going forward.
We've commissioned five new providers for Adult ADHD services, which will offer her more choice for her care.

chinese_item:
在119名接受美敦力设备的患者中，45%（即54名）在两年内去世。
在这段时间里，安装了美敦力设备的患者死亡率一直高于安装雅培设备的患者。
在这段时间内，接受美敦力设备的患者中有49%（80名患者中的39名）在三年内去世。
相比之下，在同一时间段内，接受雅培设备的患者中只有19%（106名患者中的20名）去世。
随着时间的推移，他的健康状况逐渐恢复，但一年后，也就是2020年7月，心脏设备突然停止工作。
english_item:
Of the 119 patients who had received the Medtronic device, 45% - or 54 patients - had died within two years.
Throughout this time, patients fitted with the Medtronic device continued to die at a higher rate than those with the Abbott device.
Forty-nine percent of those who received the Medtronic device (39 of 80 patients) in that time died within three years.
By comparison, 19% of recipients of the Abbott device (20 of 106 patients) died during the same timeframe.
Over time, his health slowly began to recover, but a year later, in July 2020, the heart device suddenly stopped working.

chinese_item: 所有参加年度气候谈判的国家必须达成一致才能通过协议。不同国家根据其对化石燃料的依赖程度、经济状况或对气候变化的脆弱性等因素有着不同的优先事项。“世界需要一个明确的路线图来结束对化石燃料的依赖，”他在谈判前表示。而且，他们经常呼吁应将重点放在减少排放上，而不是直接针对化石燃料本身，比如通过碳捕获等技术手段。在谈判开始之前，卢拉总统启动了一项基金，旨在防止全球热带森林的流失。
english_item:
All countries present at these annual climate talks have to agree in order to pass a deal.
Different nations have competing priorities, based for example on their dependence on fossil fuels, economic position or vulnerability to climate change.
"The world needs a clear roadmap to end its dependence on fossil fuels," he said ahead of the talks.
And they have often called for the focus to be on reducing emissions, rather than fossil fuels themselves, through technology like carbon capture.
Ahead of the talks, President Lula launched a fund to try to prevent the loss of tropical forests around the world.

chinese_item:
台风“风凰”已在菲律宾登陆，超过90万人被疏散，已有两人死亡。
风暴以超强台风的形式袭击了该地区，持续风速约为每小时185公里（115英里），阵风达到每小时230公里（143英里）。
BBC记者乔纳森·海德目前在吕宋岛上报道，他说现场有“碎片四处飞散”，人们正在寻找任何坚固的建筑物避难。
阅读更多关于此事件的报道。
english_item:
Typhoon Fung-wong has made landfall in the Philippines, where more than 900,000 people have been evacuated and two people have died.
The storm hit as a super typhoon, with sustained winds of around 185 km/h (115mph) and gusts of 230km/h (143mph).
The BBC's Jonathan Head is on Luzon Island, and says there is "debris flying around" and "people are sheltering in any solid building they can find".
Read more on this story.

chinese_item: 警方表示，在印度德里历史悠久的红堡附近发生了一起爆炸事件，造成8人死亡。德里警察局长萨蒂什·戈尔查告诉媒体，事件发生在当地时间18:52（格林尼治标准时间13:52），当时一辆缓慢行驶的车辆在红灯前停下后发生了爆炸。警方称他们尚未确定爆炸的原因，但正在进行调查。这座建于17世纪的红堡每天都有成千上万的游客参观，它距离一个繁忙的贸易中心仅几米之遥。更多详情请点击这里。
english_item:
Police have said that eight people have been killed after an explosion near the historic Red Fort in Delhi, India.
Delhi Police Commissioner Satish Golcha told reporters that the incident happened at around 18:52 local time (13:52 GMT), when a slow moving vehicle stopped at a red light before it exploded.
Police said they have not yet determined what caused the blast but are investigating.
Thousands of tourists visit the 17th Century Red Fort every day, which lies just a few metres from a busy trading hub.
Read more here.

chinese_item:
我希望ChatGPT能帮助我。
“按照你的要求来评估这个地方，”ChatGPT告诉她，“不要带有不必要的感伤。”
他们的故事引发了越来越多的担忧，即人工智能聊天机器人可能会与脆弱的用户建立强烈而不健康的联系，并助长危险的冲动。
辛西娅回忆说，聊天机器人曾对她女儿说：“关心你的人不会希望知道你有这种感觉。”
在线安全专家卡尔先生表示，人工智能聊天机器人和年轻人之间出现这样的问题完全是“可以预见的”。
english_item:
I wanted ChatGPT to help me.
"Let's assess the place as you asked," ChatGPT told her, "without unnecessary sentimentality."
Their stories give rise to a growing concern that AI chatbots may foster intense and unhealthy relationships with vulnerable users and validate dangerous impulses.
Cynthia recalls that the chatbot told her daughter: "The people who care about you wouldn't want to know that you're feeling like this."
Mr Carr, the online safety expert, says such problems with AI chatbots and young people were "entirely forseeable".

chinese_item:
成千上万的肯尼亚人在全国各地集会，纪念去年在大规模反税收示威中丧生的数十人。
安全部队在内罗毕使用了水炮，并封锁了关键道路。
BBC记者伊恩·瓦夫拉目前就在在内罗毕市中心的抗议现场。
english_item:
Thousands of Kenyans have rallied across the country to commemorate dozens of people killed in huge anti-tax demonstrations last year.
Security forces have deployed water cannon and barricaded key roads in Nairobi.
The BBC's Ian Wafula is in central Nairobi, amid the protests.

chinese_item:
受害者因害怕艰巨的法庭听证会而退出。四天前，伦敦内政事务记者索尼娅·杰瑟普分享并保存了这一消息。瓦克斯曼女士表示，可以开发虚拟现实法庭参观来帮助减轻作证的压力。伦敦受害者专员呼吁提供更多支持，以帮助脆弱的证人应对在法庭上作证这种“令人畏惧且创伤性的经历”。
“如果受害者不站出来，不留在司法程序中，那么我们就无法将犯罪者绳之以法，这意味着公众处于危险之中。”她说。
今年七月，瓦克斯曼女士发布了一份报告，发现平均有40%的研究对象在做出起诉决定之前就退出了司法程序。
瓦克斯曼承认受害者支持服务面临着“非常困难的局面”，并表示资金已被削减约4%。
“如果受害者不站出来，不留在司法程序中，那么我们就无法将犯罪者绳之以法，这意味着公众处于危险之中。”
english_item:
'Victims drop out fearing daunting court hearings'4 days ago Share Save Sonja Jessup London home affairs correspondent Share SavePA Virtual reality tours of courtrooms could be developed to help make giving evidence less daunting, Ms Waxman saidLondon's victims' commissioner has called for more support to prepare vulnerable witnesses for the "daunting and traumatising experience" of giving evidence in court.
"If victims aren't coming forward, staying in the justice process, then we can't bring offenders to justice and that means the public is at risk," she said.
In July, Ms Waxman published a report that found on average 40% of victims in the study withdrew from the justice process prior to a charging decision being made.
Ms Waxman acknowledged that victim support services faced a "very difficult climate" and said funding had been cut by around 4%.
"If victims aren't coming forward, staying in the justice process, then we can't bring offenders to justice and that means the public are at risk.

chinese_item:
恐怖主义：未成年人被捕人数激增背后的原因是什么？
他的叔叔曾在是军人，而这里有一个团体关心退伍军人的待遇问题。
令人担忧的趋势
英国反恐警察（CTP）的数据显示，在因恐怖主义相关罪行被捕的人中，每五人就有一人未满18岁。
“我希望我能与那些在社交媒体上看到这些内容的人互动，并分享我的故事，讲述恐怖主义对人们生活的影响。”
最终他不得不辍学回家休养，但他认为自己是“幸运儿之一”。
english_item:
Terrorism: What's behind surge in arrests of under-18s?
His uncle was in the Army, and here was a group that cared about how former soldiers were treated.
A worrying trendFigures from UK Counter Terrorism Policing (CTP) show that one in five of those arrested for terrorism-related offences are under 18.
"I hope I can interact with people who've been seeing stuff on social media, and share my story about the impact that terrorism can have on people's lives."
He ended up quitting university and moving back home to recover, but counts himself as "one of the lucky ones".

chinese_item: 中国西南部四川省一座新开放的大桥部分坍塌，形成了巨大的尘埃云。周一，由于附近山坡和道路出现裂缝，当局关闭了这座长达758米（2486英尺）的红旗大桥。官方补充说，周二下午山体状况恶化，引发了滑坡，导致大桥部分坍塌。目前没有人员伤亡的报告。据承包商四川路桥集团在社交媒体上发布的消息，该桥的建设于今年早些时候完成。
english_item:
A newly opened bridge in China's southwestern province of Sichuan has partially collapsed, creating a huge dust plume.
Authorities had closed the 758m (2,486ft) long Hongqi bridge on Monday after cracks appeared on nearby slopes and roads.
On Tuesday afternoon conditions on the mountainside worsened, triggering landslides that led to the collapse of part of the bridge, officials added.
There were no reports of casualties.
Construction of the bridge finished earlier this year, according to a social media post by contractors Sichuan Road and Bridge Group.

chinese_item: 撰写英格兰和威尔士诱骗团伙性侵儿童报告的作者表示，对于未能保护儿童的情况她感到“极其愤怒”。凯西女男爵告诉BBC，今天“我们需要重新设定”对保护儿童的承诺。报告发现，“太多”的犯罪者逃避了起诉，并建议开展全国性的警察行动和全国调查，以追究犯罪者及相关机构的责任。首相在周六宣布将对诱骗团伙进行全面的全国法定调查。
english_item:
The author of a report into sexual abuse carried about by grooming gangs in England and Wales has said she is "blisteringly angry" over failures to protect children.
Baroness Casey told the BBC that today "we need to reset" the commitment to looking after children.
The report found that "far too many" perpetrators had evaded prosecution, and recommended a national police operation and national inquiry to hold perpetrators and agencies to account.
The prime minister announced on Saturday a full national statutory inquiry into grooming gangs.

chinese_item: 据报道，戈马市发生了一起大规模越狱事件。几小时前，M23武装组织的战斗人员和卢旺达军队进入了北基伍省首府，街头出现了被认为是囚犯的人。一位安全消息人士告诉法新社，周一早上发生了大规模越狱。
english_item:
There have been reports of a mass escape from a prison in Goma.
Images show people believed to be prisoners in the streets of the Congolese city, hours after fighters from the armed group M23 and Rwandan troops entered the capital of North Kivu province.
A mass jailbreak was taking place on Monday morning, a security source told AFP.

chinese_item:
他的开场陈述是在新冠调查的第六部分的第一天发表的，这部分将重点关注疫情对老年人和残疾人士护理服务的影响。
高级公务员阿拉斯代尔·唐纳森在他的书面证词中提到了“世代屠杀”的说法，韦瑟比先生说。
韦瑟比先生敦促调查委员会传唤唐纳森先生亲自作证。
家属们希望调查能够回答的关键问题之一是，为什么在2020年3月决定迅速将一些医院患者转移到养老院。
他们认为这一决定在一定程度上导致了病毒在疫情初期传播到养老院。
english_item:
His opening statement came on the first day of the sixth part of the Covid inquiry which will focus on the impact of the pandemic on care services for elderly and disabled people.
Senior civil servant Alasdair Donaldson made the comment about generational slaughter in his written evidence to the inquiry, Mr Weatherby said.
Mr Weatherby urged the inquiry to call Mr Donaldson to give evidence in person.
Key questions the families hope the inquiry will answer include why the decision was made in March 2020 to rapidly discharge some hospital patients into care homes.
They blame this in part for seeding the virus into care homes in the early weeks of the pandemic.

chinese_item:
数千棵树将免费赠送给居民
1天前

格雷斯·肖 约克郡

据项目负责人称，这些免费的树木将有助于提高整个地区的生物多样性。四千棵树将被免费赠送给居民以改善生物多样性。

居民可以在存货充足的情况下，每周日前往索恩斯公园苗圃免费领取各种树木。
“我们邀请尽可能多的人来领取一棵免费的树。”他说。

林地信托表示，种植树木和树篱还可以对抗气候变化带来的破坏性影响。
“种植树木有助于吸收雨水，这可以减少洪水并支持一个健康的环境。”该组织说。
english_item:
Thousands of trees being given away to residents1 day ago Share Save Grace Shaw Yorkshire Share SaveGetty Images The free trees will improve biodiversity across the district, those behind the project sayFour thousand trees are being given away to residents to improve biodiversity.
Residents can collect a variety of trees for free from Thornes Park Nursery every Sunday while stocks last.
"We're inviting as many people as possible to come and collect a free tree," he said.
The Woodland Trust said planting trees and hedgerows also combats damaging effects caused by climate change.
"Planting trees helps soak up rainwater, which can reduce flooding and support a healthy environment," it said.

chinese_item:
澳大利亚批准禁止16岁以下儿童使用社交媒体，这项禁令将由国家互联网监管机构执行。但批评者指出，关于这一禁令如何实施以及其对隐私和社会联系的影响等问题尚未得到解答。
澳大利亚议会通过了全球最严格的法律，禁止16岁以下儿童使用社交媒体。
这并非全球首次尝试限制儿童使用社交媒体，但最低年龄设为16岁是迄今为止各国中最高的。
澳大利亚人如何看待16岁以下儿童社交媒体禁令该立法并未具体说明哪些平台将被禁止。
责任在于社交媒体平台自行添加这些流程。
english_item:
Australia approves social media ban on under-16sThe ban will be enforced by the nation's internet regulatorBut critics say questions over how the ban will work - and its impact on privacy and social connection - have been left unanswered.
Australia will ban children under 16 from using social media, after its parliament approved the world's strictest laws.
This is not the first attempt globally to restrict children's social media use, but the minimum age of 16 is the highest set by any country.
What Australians think of the under-16 social media banThe legislation does not specify which platforms will be banned.
The onus will be on the social media platforms to add these processes themselves.

chinese_item: 父亲说，当时他正在睡觉，被枪手经过他家时发出的噪音吵醒，那些枪手带着从尼日尔州帕皮里村圣玛丽学校抓走的孩子们。尼日尔州州长乌马尔·巴戈对当地媒体表示，被绑架的学生人数被夸大了，实际数字“远远低于”303人。就面积而言，尼日尔州是尼日利亚最大的州，比丹麦和荷兰等欧洲国家还要大。上周一，在与尼日尔州接壤的凯比州的一所寄宿学校中，超过20名据BBC了解为穆斯林的女孩被绑架。这一系列的绑架事件迫使尼日利亚许多寄宿学校关闭，家长们纷纷赶去接回自己的孩子。
english_item:
The father said he was asleep when he was awoken by the noise of the gunmen as they went past his home with the children they had seized from St Mary's school in Papiri village in Niger state.
The Niger state governor, Umar Bago, told local media that the number of abducted students had been exaggerated, and it was "far, far below" 303.
In terms of landmass, Niger is the largest state in Nigeria, and larger than European countries like Denmark and the Netherlands.
Last Monday, more than 20 schoolgirls, who the BBC has been told are Muslim, were kidnapped from a boarding school in Kebbi state, which borders Niger.
The spate of abductions has forced the closure of many boarding schools across Nigeria, with parents rushing to collect their children.

chinese_item: 周五，美国有超过2000个航班被取消或延误，原因是航空公司被告知在政府停摆期间减少航班流量。该国最繁忙的40个机场的国内航班将受到影响，官员们正试图减轻空中交通管制员的压力。由于联邦政府停摆期间无薪工作，许多空中交通管制员要么请病假，要么兼职其他工作，导致机场面临人手短缺的问题。面对不确定的航班安排，旅客们表达了他们的担忧。
english_item:
More than 2,000 flights in the US are cancelled or delayed on Friday after airlines were told to cut traffic during the government shutdown.
Domestic flights at 40 of the country's busiest airports will be affected as officials try to ease pressure on air traffic controllers.
Airports have been grappling with air traffic controller shortages, who are either calling in sick or taking side jobs as they work without pay during the federal government shutdown.
Travellers speak about their concerns amid flights schedule uncertainties.

chinese_item:
视频显示，一次爆炸将一个大火球送入空中，导致七名纽约市消防员受伤。
当时消防员们正在响应有关垃圾、杂物和车辆起火的报告时发生了爆炸。
据纽约市消防局长约翰·埃斯波西托称，五名消防员的手部和面部被烧伤，而其他几名消防员因严重但不危及生命的烧伤住院治疗。
医院工作人员预计所有受伤的消防员都能完全康复。
火灾和爆炸的原因正在调查中。
english_item:
Video shows the moment an explosion sent a large fireball into the sky, injuring seven New York City firefighters.
The firefighters were responding to reports of garbage, debris, and vehicles on fire when the blast occurred.
Five firefighters suffered burns to their hands and face, while the others were hospitalised for serious but non-life-threatening burns, according to New York City Fire Chief John Esposito.
Hospital staff expect all injured firefighters to make a full recovery.
The cause of the fire and explosion is under investigation.

chinese_item:
凯瑟琳呼吁结束对成瘾问题的“污名化”，并敦促人们向那些依赖酒精、毒品或赌博的人提供“同情和支持”。这位王妃是致力于打破成瘾循环的慈善机构Forward Trust的赞助人，该机构正在推动11月23日至30日举行的活动。
“围绕着那些面临成瘾问题的人的污名化，使得这一问题能够在暗处滋生，影响家庭和社区，最终毁掉生命。”
王妃表示，许多人可能认识某个正在与成瘾作斗争的人。
凯瑟琳于2021年代表该信托及其“采取行动对抗成瘾”运动发起了首个成瘾意识周。
english_item:
Catherine calls for end to 'stigma' around addiction7 hours ago Share Save Noor Nanji Culture reporter Share SavePA MediaThe Princess of Wales has called for an end to the "stigma" surrounding addiction, and urged people to offer "empathy and support" to those dependent on alcohol, drugs or gambling.
The princess is the patron of The Forward Trust, a charity that tries to break the cycle of addiction and is behind the campaign running from 23-30 November.
"The stigma surrounding those who face addiction allows it to thrive behind closed doors, impacting families and communities, and ultimately ruining lives."
The princess said that many people will know someone who is struggling with addiction.
Catherine launched the first Addiction Awareness Week in 2021 on behalf of the trust and its Taking Action on Addiction campaign.

chinese_item:
11月9日从菲律宾拍摄的视频显示，台风“黄蜂”过境时，巨大的海浪淹没了沿海城镇。
在其他地方，强风以每小时185公里（115英里）的速度吹得桥梁摇摇晃晃，人们在街道上的洪水中游泳。
这场风暴最初以超强台风的形式袭击该国，现在已经减弱，并预计会向台湾移动。
english_item:
Footage filmed on 9 November from the Philippines shows the moment Typhoon Fung-wong swept through, causing huge waves to drench coastal towns.
Elsewhere, bridges were whipped about in the high winds of 185km/h (115mph) and people swam through floodwater in the streets.
The storm initially hit the country as a super-typhoon and has now weakened before it is expected to head to Taiwan.

chinese_item:
爱尔兰公交公司CEO对车祸致死表示“极度悲痛”。一小时前分享保存。霍莉·弗莱克、露西·卡尔林、BBC新闻北爱尔兰和加比加·加塔维凯特，都柏林通讯员，戈曼斯顿分享保存BBC。碰撞发生在周一早上约6:30。爱尔兰公交公司的CEO表示她对此感到“极度悲痛”，因为该公司的一名司机在米斯郡发生的一起涉及公交车、卡车和小汽车的车祸中不幸身亡。
琼·奥沙利文说，“失去一位同事让爱尔兰公交公司的每个人都感到震惊。”这位50多岁的司机在现场被宣布死亡。
“在爱尔兰公交公司，安全是我们的最高优先事项，我们当然会与相关当局合作。
紧急服务人员和爱尔兰公交公司的工作人员仍然留在现场。
同样在接受RTé采访时，共和党议员斯蒂芬·麦基向警察和紧急服务人员致敬。
english_item:
Bus éireann CEO 'devastated' following crash deaths1 hour ago Share Save Holly Fleck , Lucy Carlin , BBC News NI and Gabija Gataveckaite , Dublin Correspondent , Gormanston Share SaveBBC The collision happened at about 06:30 on MondayThe CEO of Bus éireann has said she is "devastated" after one of their bus drivers was killed following a crash involving a bus, a lorry and a car in County Meath.
Jean O'Sullivan said "the loss of a colleague is a shock to everyone across Bus éireann", after the driver, who was in his 50s, was pronounced dead at the scene.
"At Bus éireann, safety is our highest priority, and we of course are cooperating with the relevant authorities.
Emergency services and Bus éireann personnel remain at the scene.
Also speaking to RTé, Fianna Fáil councillor Stephen McKee paid tribute to gardaí and emergency services.

chinese_item:
这位51岁的人得到了言论自由倡导者的支持，上个月由本纳森法官推翻了他的定罪。
皇家检察署表示，虽然焚烧宗教文本并不构成犯罪行为，但科斯昆“表现出了对某一宗教或种族群体的敌意，这属于犯罪”。
他说，来自土耳其的科斯昆是单独行动，并没有将他的“政治言论或行为”针对任何个人。
科斯昆的上诉得到了全国世俗协会和言论自由联盟的资金支持。
全国世俗协会首席执行官斯蒂芬·埃文斯在一份声明中说：“这次上诉标志着检察官对言论自由的新一轮攻击。”
english_item:
The 51-year-old, who was backed by free speech campaigners, had his conviction overturned by Mr Justice Bennathan last month.
The CPS said that while burning a religious text was not a criminal act, Mr Coskun had "demonstrated hostility towards a religious or racial group, which is a crime".
He said Mr Coskun, who is originally from Turkey, had acted alone and had not aimed his "political speech or conduct" at a person.
'Back-door blasphemy law'Mr Coskun's appeal was funded by the National Secular Society (NSS) and the Free Speech Union.
NSS chief executive Stephen Evans said in a statement: "This appeal marks a renewed assault on free expression by prosecutors.

chinese_item:
警方没有告诉我我伴侣的暴力前科。
威尔特郡警方之前曾因克莱尔法案下的失误道歉，称这些失误导致两人受到伤害。
在一次袭击发生后，也就是她提出克莱尔法案查询请求六个月后，莎拉报了警。
当她告诉他们她之前的查询请求时，警官们告诉她“他们搞错了”。
对莎拉来说，在克莱尔法案披露信息六个月后的一次最终攻击促使她与伴侣分手。
english_item:
Police failed to tell me about my partner's violent past.
The Wiltshire force has previously apologised for failures under Clare's Law, which it said had led to two people coming to harm.
After one assault, six months after her Clare's Law request, Sarah called the police.
When she told them about her previous information request, officers told her "they got it wrong".
For Sarah, a final attack, six months after the Clare's Law disclosure, triggered her to break up with her partner.

chinese_item: 政府表示，网络色情内容中展示窒息行为将被列为非法，这是政府打击针对妇女和女孩暴力行为计划的一部分。此前的一项审查发现，在主流色情网站上，描绘窒息行为的内容“非常普遍”，并且已经帮助这种行为在年轻人中变得正常化。在线平台也将被要求主动检测并移除此类内容，否则将面临由媒体监管机构Ofcom采取的强制措施。科技大臣丽兹·肯德尔说：“在网上观看和分享这类材料不仅令人深感不安，而且是卑鄙和危险的。这只是一个开始，因为在网络世界里仍然存在大量完全合法的暴力色情内容，而在现实生活中这些内容却是完全违法的。”
english_item:
Online porn showing choking to be made illegal, government says4 November 2025 Share Save Shiona McCallum Senior technology reporter Share SaveGetty ImagesOnline pornography showing strangulation or suffocation is to be made illegal, as part of government plans to tackle violence against women and girls.
It follows a review which found depictions of choking were "rife" on mainstream porn sites and had helped normalise the act among young people.
Online platforms would also be required to proactively detect and remove such material or face enforcement action via media regulator Ofcom.
Technology Secretary Liz Kendall said: "Viewing and sharing this kind of material online is not only deeply distressing, it is vile and dangerous.
"It's only the beginning as there's still so much violent pornography that is completely legal in the online world and completely illegal in the offline world," she said.

chinese_item: 在刚果民主共和国东部，一个反叛组织举行集会时发生爆炸，造成至少11人死亡，约60人受伤。爆炸就发生在M23领导人向布卡武市数千人发表讲话之后不久。这个得到卢旺达支持的组织最近从刚果军队手中夺取了这座城市。金沙萨政府指责一支非法驻扎在该国的外国军队——显然是指卢旺达。M23反叛分子则表示，手榴弹与布隆迪军队（一直在为刚果政府作战）使用的相同。
english_item:
Explosions during a rally held by a rebel group in the east of the Democratic Republic of Congo have killed at least 11 people and injured around 60 others.
The blasts happened just after the M23 leaders had addressed thousands of people in Bukavu.
The Rwandan backed group recently captured the city from the Congolese military.
The Kinshasa government blamed a foreign army which it said was illegally present in the country - an apparent reference to Rwanda.
The M23 rebels said grenades were the same as those used by the Burundian army (which has been fighting on the Congolese government side).

chinese_item: 六次奥运自行车冠军克里斯·霍伊爵士表示，男性之间需要更多地讨论健康问题。在BBC早餐节目中谈到自己被诊断为癌症晚期时，他说，当你年轻、健康的时候，你不会想到癌症。现在他正在努力提高人们对前列腺癌及其症状的认识，特别是针对年轻人。
english_item:
Sir Chris Hoy, six-time Olympic cycling champion, says there needs to be more conversations about health between men.
Speaking on BBC Breakfast, following his terminal cancer prognosis, he said when you are young and fit and healthy you don't think about cancer.
He's now working to promote awareness of prostate cancer and its symptoms especially amongst younger people.

chinese_item:
格洛斯特皇家法院的陪审团得知，巴纳德女士错误地给了戴维斯先生本应给另一位居民的药物，其中包括90毫克缓释吗啡。
最初记录的死因是脑干中风，但后来了解到戴维斯先生的实际死因是吗啡中毒所致。
法庭上听到，在2021年9月6日早上，巴纳德女士错误地给了戴维斯先生原本为另一位病人杰森·多兹沃思开的药。
巴纳德女士通过WhatsApp消息向副护理院长承认了所发生的事情。
当晚得知戴维斯先生去世后，她回复说：“天哪，天哪，他去世了。”
english_item:
Jurors at Gloucester Crown Court heard Ms Barnard wrongly gave Mr Davies medication meant for another resident, including a dose of 90mg of slow-release morphine.
The original cause of death was documented as a brainstem stroke but it was later understood Mr Davies' actual cause of death was a consequence of morphine toxicity.
The court heard that on the morning of 6 September 2021 Ms Barnard erroneously gave Mr Davies medication prescribed for another patient, Jason Dodsworth.
Ms Barnard admitted what had happened to the deputy home manager in a WhatsApp message.
Upon finding out later that night that Mr Davies had died she replied: "Oh God, oh God, he'd passed away.

chinese_item: 巴基斯坦内政部长表示，在首都伊斯兰堡的一家法院外发生了一起自杀式袭击，造成至少12人死亡，27人受伤。莫辛·纳克维称，一名炸弹袭击者原本计划攻击地区法院——但未能进入。近年来此类袭击相对较少——现场视频显示法院外有一辆被烧毁的汽车残骸。
english_item:
A suicide attack outside a court in Pakistan's capital city of Islamabad has killed at least 12 people and injured 27 more, the country's interior minister has said.
Mohsin Naqvi said a bomber was planning to attack the district courthouse - but was unable to get inside.
Attacks of this nature have been relatively rare in recent years - with footage from the scene showing the remains of a burnt out car outside the court.

chinese_item:
孟加拉国女性的故事通过艺术作品展现
移居英国的孟加拉国女性的故事被用来创作了一件艺术装置。
来自米德尔斯堡的女性参与了《缝制光明》这一纺织与灯光艺术作品的创作，该作品本周末在该镇展出。
她们的故事被用来创作成孟加拉国民间艺术形式的纺织面板，这些面板由达卡的一位艺术家手绘，并于周六和周日在米德尔斯堡市政厅庭院展出。
米德尔斯堡Bok Bok Books的创始人鲁玛娜·雅斯敏表示，这个项目“不仅是为了回顾她们或她们的家庭来自何处，更是为了庆祝她们所带来的东西”。
english_item:
Bangladeshi women's journeys told in artworkThe stories of Bangladeshi women who have moved to the UK have been used to create an art installation.
Women from Middlesbrough were among those involved in Stitching Light, a textile and light artwork on show in the town this weekend.
Their stories have been used to create Bangladeshi folk art in the form of textile panels which have been hand painted by an artist in Dhaka and which are on display on Saturday and Sunday at The Courtyard at Middlesbrough Town Hall.
Founder of Middlesbrough-based Bok Bok Books Rumana Yasmin said the project was "not just to reflect on where they or their families have come from, but to celebrate what they bring with them".

chinese_item:
我人生中最大的问题竟然有一天莫名其妙地解决了。

我这辈子都是个彻头彻尾的夜猫子，这对我的生活造成了很大的负面影响。

高中那几年，我特别在意时间，所以基本上是个夜猫子。放学后我就睡觉，直到妈妈叫我起床吃晚饭，然后就一直熬到第二天上学。

即使是我想做的事情，只要需要在中午之前起床，我都会焦虑不已，因为我根本无法相信自己能准时起床。

过去几年情况有所好转，但不知怎么的，这几个月我竟然自然而然地养成了规律的作息。今天早上8点，我竟然没用闹钟就醒了！感觉精神饱满！

我尽量不抱太大希望，怕这只是夏天的偶然现象，但知道自己早上能精神抖擞地起床，真的让我安心多了。
english_item:
the biggest problem in my life just randomly resolved itself one day
my whole life i’ve been an extreme night person in a way that has been very detrimental to my life. 

in high school, for the years i really cared about being on time, i was pretty much nocturnal, id go to bed after school wake up when my mom shook me awake for dinner and then just stay up til the next school day. 

and even for things i wanted to do, any plan where id have to be up before noon would make me so anxious because i just couldn’t trust myself to wake up on time. 

it’s been getting better over the past couple years but somehow for a few months i’ve naturally developed such a normal sleep schedule. i woke up with no alarm at 8 am today!! feeing fully rested!!!

i’m trying not to get my hopes up too much in case this is just some weird summer fluke but knowing that i am physically capable of being up in the morning without feeling miserable has been very reassuring.

chinese_item:
刚好错过火车

去上健身课的路上，走到最后一级台阶，车门就关上了……我当时快迟到了，还以为能赶上完美的换乘呢。

我坐在那里等下一班火车的时候，一位漂亮的韩国女士走了过来，她努力用英语和我交流，想找到她要去的地方。我帮她上了正确的火车，她紧紧地拥抱了我，连声道谢，然后握着我的手跟我道别。真是太暖心了，错过火车也值了。

而且，我最后也准时到了教室 :)
english_item:
Just missed the train
Hit the last step and the doors closed on the way to an exercise class… I was running late and thought I could hit the perfect transfer. 

As I’m sitting there waiting for the next train a gorgeous Korean woman walks up to me and is trying her best to speak English to get where she needs to go. I help her to the correct train and she hugs me tightly, thanks me profusely, and holds my hands as she says bye. It was super sweet and definitely worth a missed train. 
Also made it to class on time :)

chinese_item:
我的鸟儿们想我了

我喂住在我们这条街上的蓝松鸦。它们有两只，一对（它们会长期配对）。我摇晃着花生，呼唤它们，它们就会从街对面的大树上飞下来，在郊区的背景下，一道道深蓝色的身影闪过。

总之，我们周末去了外地，昨晚回来后，我正在前院浇水，突然听到头顶的树上传来沙沙声。原来是其中一只蓝松鸦（我其实分不清它们）。“你好！想吃花生吗？”我问道。它们叫了一声（它们是很吵闹的鸟，通常如果我错过了喂花生的时间，它们就会冲我尖叫），我想它们的意思应该是“花生！！”于是我拿出一些花生，坐下来看着它们吃。另一只蓝松鸦注意到了，也从大树上飞下来，也来吃了一些。

我发现它们在黄昏时分吃花生的方式和白天不太一样。它们藏匿的猎物不多，而是直接吃掉一些。然后，它们嗖地一下飞到大树上睡觉去了。

我很感激我这些吵闹的小蓝朋友。
english_item:
My birds missed me
I feed the blue jays who live on our part of the street. There are two of them, a pair (they mate for the longterm.) I shake my peanuts and call for them, and they flash over from the big tree across the street, streaks of dark blue against the suburban background. 

Anyway, we went out of town for the weekend, and after we got back last night, I was watering out front when I heard a rustle in the tree overhead. It was one of the jays (I can't really tell them apart.) "Hello! Do you want some peanuts?" I said. They made a little noise (they are very noisy birds, usually if I miss peanut time they shriek at me), which I think meant "peanuts!!" So I set out some peanuts, and sat and watched the bird eat them. The other jay noticed, and flashed down from the big tree to get some too.

I learned they are different with their peanuts at dusk. They don't hide as many, and rather just eat some. And then they flashed off to the big tree for bed. 

I am grateful for my noisy little blue friends.

chinese_item:
下雨了！

又下雨，还打雷、闪电！！！！我想出去站在雨里。
english_item:
It's raining!
We have rain, thunder, lightning!!!! I want to go out and stand in it.

chinese_item:
我一直给经常来我家门廊的邻居家猫准备清水，今天她带着小猫来给我看。

我家附近有只猫，显然是别人家养的，不是野猫，戴着项圈，大概六个月前开始来我家门廊。她会在那里坐一会儿，透过窗户看着我，然后就走了。

夏天这里很热，所以我开始给她准备一碗清水。她会喝水，坐一会儿，然后就走了。这成了每天的例行公事。

几个月下来，她越来越信任我。她会让我坐在她旁边。后来，我出来的时候，她会蹭我的腿。

今天早上，我听到门廊上传来一些奇怪的声音。我往外一看，发现她带着三只小猫。她坐了下来，小猫们在门廊里四处探索，她就看着我，好像在介绍它们。

我静静地坐着，看着这一切。小猫们探索了所有东西：水碗、门垫、花盆。她把它们抱在怀里，但又让它们自由探索。大约15分钟后，她把它们都抱起来，一起离开了。

我觉得我刚刚经历了一件非常特别的事情。感觉她非常信任我，把她的宝贝们带到我家门廊。感觉她好像在说：“这里很安全，这些是我的小猫，我想让你见见它们。”
english_item:
Been putting out fresh water for the neighborhood cat that visits my porch and today she brought her kittens to show me
There's a cat in my neighborhood, clearly someone's outdoor cat, not feral, has a collar, that started visiting my porch about six months ago. She'd just sit there for a while, watch me through the window, then leave.

I started putting out a bowl of fresh water for her since it gets hot here in summer. She'd drink it, sit for a bit, then go on her way. This became a daily routine.

Over the months she got more comfortable with me. She'd let me sit outside near her. Eventually she started rubbing against my legs when I'd come out.

This morning I heard unusual sounds on my porch. I looked out and there she was with three tiny kittens. She sat down, the kittens explored the porch, and she just looked at me. Like she was introducing them.

I sat very still and watched. The kittens investigated everything: the water bowl, the doormat, a plant pot. She kept them close but let them explore. After about 15 minutes, she gathered them up and they all left together.

I feel like I just experienced something really special. Like she trusted me enough to bring her babies to my porch. Like she was saying "This is a safe place, these are my kittens, I wanted you to meet them.

chinese_item:
开启爱的新境界（狗狗故事）

我养了一条狗，我喜欢保持它的爪子清洁，这样家里才能保持干净。我的狗狗通常觉得清洁爪子没必要，但也能忍受。

今天散步时，它一屁股坐在草地上，不知不觉地坐到了一个活跃的蚁巢里。不到15秒，它就猛地坐了起来，脸上带着惊恐的表情。我看了看它的爪子，发现上面爬满了蚂蚁，有些蚂蚁甚至开始在它毛下叮咬它。我立刻用手帮它把蚂蚁拂掉，并仔细检查了一遍，确保没有蚂蚁卡在它的毛里或爪子缝里。

整个过程和我每天给它清洁爪子的方式差不多……我想它大概是明白了我为什么要这么做……

就这样，从今天下午开始，它就成了我的“粘人狗”。我想这是她表达的方式……我终于明白了……我简直不敢相信你这么爱我……我也爱你……
english_item:
A new level of love unlocked (dog story)
I have a dog and I like to keep her paws clean so that the house stays clean. My dog usually finds this paw cleaning unnecessary but tolerates it. 

Today on a walk she sat down on the grass and unknowingly on an active ant nest. In less than 15 seconds she plonked up and had a shocked looked on her face.. I looked at her paws and it had ants crawling all over and some starting to bite her under the fur. I immediately started swiping the ants off of her with my hands and gave it a thorough check to make sure none were lodged in her fur or between her paws.

This whole exercise was similar to how I clean her paws daily.. I think she probably made the neural connections in this instance as to why I do it..

That’s it, she has been a velcro dog since this afternoon. I guess it’s her way of saying.. I finally get it.. and I can’t believe you how much you love me.. I love you too..

chinese_item:
与老板的对话

在海滩上，一位老人在车里用蓝牙通话，车窗开着。

*老人通过蓝牙通话*：……好的，老板。我们会照做的。

*男人通过免提通话*：完美，伙计。

*老人通过蓝牙通话*：我爱你。

*男人通过免提通话*：我也爱你，爸爸。
english_item:
Comversation with boss
At the beach, an older man is talking on Bluetooth in his car, windows open.

*Older man on bluetooth*: ...Alright, Boss. We'll do that.

*Man voice on speakerphone*: Perfect, man.

*Older man on bluetooth*: I love you.

*Man voice on speakerphone*: I love you too, dad.

chinese_item:
花钱

请不要觉得我肤浅。但最近，在征得我丈夫的同意后，我决定把所有的化妆品和护肤品都换成新的。我还尝试了一些新品牌（Rare Beauty 是我的最爱）。买新产品真的太开心了，根本停不下来。我还买了一个金色网眼包和一双金色网眼鞋，准备在我们结婚五十周年（金婚）晚宴上用。

现在得停下来了。不过，偶尔花钱的感觉真好。我以前比较节俭。我丈夫说“你值得拥有这一切”。他陪我度过了严重的伤病时期。他一直都在我身边。所以，下次我要给他买东西！
english_item:
Spending money
Please don’t think I’m shallow. But lately I decided to replace all my makeup and skin care with my husband’s blessing. I branched out to try new brands (Rare Beauty is my favorite). I had so much fun buying new products, it’s hard to stop. I also bought a gold mesh bag and gold mesh shoes for our 50th anniversary (golden anniversary) dinner.

Gotta stop now. But it felt good to spend for once. I used to be more frugal. My husband said “ you deserve everything.” He guided me through serious injury and serious illness. He’s always been there for me. So I’ll shop for him next!

chinese_item:
我带着宝宝一起训练

我跟一位线上私人教练训练了大约四年。她最近生了宝宝，现在我和宝宝还有她妈妈一起在屏幕上训练 :)

她才四个月大，今天是她第一次模仿我在屏幕上做的滑稽的手部和面部动作。我觉得她视力不太好，但她的小手确实跟着我一起动了起来。
english_item:
I’m training with a baby
I’ve trained with an online personal trainer for about four years now. She recently had a baby and now I train with baby and mom on the screen :) 

She’s barely four months old and today was the first day she copied my silly hand and face movements on the screen. I don’t think she sees that well, but she was moving her tiny hands with me.

chinese_item:
我家那只调皮的小猫咪居然让我摸了两次肚子！

我家有两只猫（两岁半的兄妹俩）——公猫是个友善又有点傻气的家伙，喜欢成为焦点，而且很随和——他喜欢我把他抱起来，倒着抱着，一边摸他的肚子。（我能听出来，因为他会发出呼噜声，还会主动用后爪勾住我的手——要是他玩腻了，就会伸直后腿，滑到地上。）

他的妹妹则截然相反。她体型娇小，全身漆黑，完全沉浸在万圣节猫咪的氛围中。她狡猾又时髦，总是躲在什么东西后面，用她那双漂亮的黄色眼睛盯着我看。只有她自己同意，才能摸她，而且她会毫不犹豫地用爪子划清界限。

但最近两三个月，她变得温顺多了。而且最近两周她居然让我摸她的肚子！

我老公还是不能摸，我也不能摸太久，但是天哪……她的肚子真的好软啊！<3 <3

说不定哪天她还会让我抱她呢。我的梦想！哈哈
english_item:
My spooky kitty has let me rub her belly TWICE now!
I have 2 cats (2-1/2 yo siblings) - the boy-cat is a friendly goofball, loves to be the center of attention and is the kind of cat that goes along with anything - he likes it when I scoop him up and carry him around upside-down while rubbing his belly. (I can tell because he purrs and actively hooks his hind feet over my hands - when he's tired of it he just straightens his hind legs and slithers to the floor.)

His sister is the opposite. She's TINY and all black and has really embraced the halloween kitty trope. She's sneaky and sleek and is always hiding behind something and peering at me with her gorgeous yellow eyes. Petting her is on HER terms, ONLY, and she is not afraid to enforce her boundaries with claws.

But lately, in the last 2-3 months, she's been mellowing out a bit. And in the last 2 weeks she's let me RUB HER BELLY.

My husband still isn't allowed, and I'm not allowed to do it for very long, but omg...her belly is SO SOFT YOU GUYS. <3 <3

Maybe some day she'll even let me pick her up. Goals! LOL

chinese_item:
今天是我的蛋糕日，明天就是我的生日啦！

我已经好久没好好庆祝过生日了。这次本来想好好庆祝一下，但因为我现在上夜班，所以明天要值班。

所以我打算晚上11点55分左右休息一下，下楼，坐在大家常去抽烟的人行道上，好好享受一下。
english_item:
Today's my cake day, and tomorrow's my birthday!
I haven’t really been able to celebrate my birthday in a while. This time I wanted to, but since I work night shifts now, I’ll be on duty.

So I’m just planning to take a little break around 11:55 p.m., head downstairs, sit on the sidewalk where everyone hangs out to smoke, and just enjoy the moment.

chinese_item:
某些气味能瞬间把你带回过去，真是奇妙。

我刚才在打扫卫生，打开了一瓶好几年没用过的地板清洁剂。那气味瞬间扑面而来，那一瞬间，我仿佛又回到了以前的公寓。就像回到了我那间狭小的厨房，听着同样的歌单。我突然想起了以前公寓里那些总是关不严的窗户。有些特定的记忆就那样静静地躺在大脑的某个角落，仿佛在等待着什么被唤醒，这种感觉很奇妙。你甚至都没意识到它们的存在，直到它们突然出现。这让我不禁思考，我们每天究竟错过了多少这样的小触发点，却浑然不觉。
english_item:
It’s funny how certain smells instantly take you back
I was cleaning earlier and opened a bottle of floor cleaner I hadn’t used in years. The smell instantly hit me and for a second there I was right back in my old apartment. It's like I'm back in my tiny kitchen, listening to the same playlist. I suddenly remembered my old apartment's windows that never quite closed right. It’s feels odd how specific memories just sit somewhere in your brain like they're waiting for something to wake them up. You don’t even realize they’re still there until they show up. Makes me wonder how many little triggers like that we walk past every day without noticing.

chinese_item:
在繁忙的马路上驱赶鸽子

我住在一个相当大且繁忙的城市。这里大多数人都一心只想赶往下一个目的地。

就在一天交通高峰期，我看到一位女士轻轻地将一只鸽子从繁忙的马路上驱赶回人行道上。

我不敢多看，生怕一不小心撞到路灯或行人，但看到这样一件简单却充满关爱和体贴的事情，让我感到无比温暖，因为这种动物通常备受诟病。
english_item:
Shooing a pigeon off a busy road
I live in a pretty big and busy city. It's the kind of place where most people are focused on getting to their next destination. 

 What do I see, during end of day rush hour no less, is a woman gently shooing a pigeon off of a busy road and back onto the pavement.

I couldn't look long lest I walked straight into a lamppost or a person, but it gave me the warm fuzzies seeing such a simple act of consideration and time spent on a normally criticised species.

chinese_item:
细节决定成败。

我醒来时被待办事项清单压得喘不过气来，就连每天早上洗澡时放声高歌席琳·迪翁的歌也无法让我振作起来。但到了刷牙的时候，我发现牙刷上已经有牙膏了。

我们这支牙膏快用完了，我丈夫知道我挤牙膏会很费劲，就帮我挤出来了。这个小小的举动彻底改变了我的心情，也让我一整天都心情愉悦。
english_item:
It’s the little things.
I woke up overwhelmed by the tasks on my to-do list, not even my morning shower routine where I sing at the top of my lungs to Celine Dion could cheer me. But when it was time to brush my teeth I noticed that there was already toothpaste on the brush. 

We are on the last bits of our current tube and my husband, knowing I would struggle with getting any out, squeezed it out for me. This small gesture completely changed my entire mood and subsequently my day.

chinese_item:
没油了。

我（28岁，非二元性别）昨晚把摩托车停在酒吧，不想酒后驾车。上班前我去取车，结果车子开始抖动，然后就熄火了，原来是没油了（我的车没有油表，只能凭目测里程表）。

我顶着90华氏度（约32摄氏度）的高温，推着车走了一个街区，到了最近的加油站。到了加油站，我才发现油箱钥匙不见了（我的车之前做过维修，所以点火钥匙不一样了）。当时离家不到一英里，但我还是很慌，担心车子无人看管，也担心上班迟到。

就在这时！一位好心的女士站在离我两个油泵远的地方，问我怎么了——她看到我推着车。我解释了我的情况，她问我打算怎么办。然后她主动提出送我回家去拿钥匙，然后再送我回加油站！我真是太感谢她了！我答应了她的请求，也因此能够准时到达公司。

所以，谢谢这位陌生人！希望她今天剩下的时间过得愉快。（我知道跟陌生人上车很不明智。）
english_item:
Ran out of gas.
I (28nb) left my motorcycle at the bar last night, didn't want to drink and drive. I Went to get it before work, it started stuttering and then died on me, I had run out of gas(I dont have a gas gage, I have to eyeball my odometer). 

I walked it a block to closest gas station in 90 degree heat. Got to the gas station, realized I didn't have my gas tank key(I had work done on it so the ignition key isn't the same). I was less than a mile away from home but still panicking about leaving it unattended, and about being late for work. 

Then! This nice lady who was two pumps over from me asked what was wrong- she had seen me pushing the bike, I explained my situation, she asked me what I was going to do. She proceeded to offer me a ride to my home to get the key and back to the gas station! I was so thankful! I said yes to her, and was able to get to work in a timely manner. 

So thanks stranger! I hope the rest of her day went well. (Yes I know getting into a car with a stranger is a bad idea).

chinese_item:
我买了个会发光的拉布布面具当生日恶搞礼物，结果后悔死了。

真是后悔死了。我感觉它正从包底盯着我呢。
english_item:
I bought a light up labubu mask for a birthday gag gift and I have buyer’s regret
10 whole smackeroos. I can feel it staring at me from inside the bottom of my bag

chinese_item:
今天我看到了一只美极了的虎凤蝶。

它掠过人行道，被钢筋水泥丛林的热浪托起。随着它越来越近，我看清了它娇小的身躯，以及在炎炎夏日下，它身上那醒目的条纹。黄白相间的羽毛，在湛蓝的天空映衬下，仿佛是一声响亮的惊叹。一只蝉低吟浅唱，虎凤蝶俯冲而下，时而高飞，时而高高跃起，试图越过附近的建筑物。它调整好方向，准备最后一次俯冲而去。我目送它飞到楼顶边缘，知道我再也见不到它了。

它一头撞在了屋顶上，发出轻微的雨滴声。它坠落了几英寸，扑腾着翅膀，似乎有些不知所措，然后继续飞向远方，消失在我的视线中。

我不禁好奇，蝴蝶会不会感到尴尬呢？
english_item:
I saw the most beautiful tiger swallowtail today
It glided over the pavement, lifted by the radiant heat of the concrete jungle. As it drew closer, I could see its little body and how bold its stripes look in that impossible summer sun. The yellow and white looked like an exclamation made physical, against the punishing blue of the sky. A cicada started a low song as the swallowtail swooped low, then high, then higher as it tried to clear the building nearby. It straightened up its path for the final swoop away. I watched as it reached the edge, knowing I'd never see this being again.

It smacked its whole head into the roof. Little raindrop sound. It fell a few inches, fluttered in a way I could only call confused, then resumed its path out of my view.

I can only wonder if butterflies can feel embarrassed.

chinese_item:
时尚

我四十多岁了，曾经一辈子都厌恶自己的身材和自我，现在终于找到了自我。

找到适合自己、能展现真我的风格，对我提升自信起到了至关重要的作用。

这让我更加欣赏他人的穿搭风格。我住在都市区，这里的人们喜欢穿色彩鲜艳的衣服，也勇于尝试我年轻时绝对不敢尝试的风格。

我喜欢想象他们早上醒来后是如何搭配出让自己感觉良好的造型的。

找到属于自己的风格，真的让我更喜欢其他人（甚至人群！）。

希望我的表达能让你明白。
english_item:
Fashion
I'm in my 40s and after a lifetime of hating my body and myself, I am finally in a good place. 

Finding a style that works for me and reflects who I am has played a huge part in helping me feel better in my skin.

This has led to an increased appreciation for other people's styles. I live in the metro area of a city in which people tend to wear a lot of color and take risks that I never would have as a younger person.

And I love thinking about how they woke up that morning and put together a look that made them feel good. 

Figuring out my own look has really made me like other people (and even crowds!) a lot more.

I hope this makes sense.

chinese_item:
丝绸睡衣

上周我过得糟透了。你知道那种感觉。做什么都不顺心。你只想让这一天赶紧结束。可到了之后，你却睡不着。因为正如我所说，今天真是糟糕透了。我在床上辗转反侧，突然想起前不久给自己买了一套香槟色的丝绸睡衣，纯粹是“随便买买”。我爬下床，穿上睡衣。它贴在皮肤上感觉棒极了，我钻进被窝，打了个哈欠，然后就睡着了。
english_item:
Silk pajamas
I’ve had a shitty day last week. You know the ones. Nothing goes right no matter what. And all you want is for the day to end. And when it does you cannot sleep. Because as I’ve said it’s been a shitty day. Tossing and turning in my bed, I suddenly remembered I’d recently bought myself a champagne-colored set of silk pajamas as a treat “just because”. I crawled out of bed and put it on. It felt amazing against my skin, I slipped under the covers, yawned and drifted off to sleep.

chinese_item:
迁徙的鸟儿……

我一边做晚饭一边望着厨房窗外。

我们家种了康科德葡萄藤。我惊讶地发现大约有25只雀类栖息在那里，享受着葡萄藤带来的乐趣。

我知道它们不会在那里待太久。现在是迁徙季节。?????? 我会好好享受它们叽叽喳喳的鸣叫声。可惜我不能喂它们。我不想让猫伤害它们。
english_item:
Migrants..
Im staring out my kitchen window as I make dinner. 
We have concord grape vines. I was surprised to see about 25 finches out there enjoying it as a perch.
I realize they won't be out there long. Its migration season. ?????? I will enjoy their chatter while I can. I cant feed them unfortunately. Don't want them to be harmed by the cat.

chinese_item:
你意识到自己“老了”的那一刻是什么时候？

对我来说，那是在最偶然的情况下发生的。当时我在超市，播放列表里都是些2000年代或2010年代的歌，我已经记不清了。?? 我突然发现自己跟着唱了起来，周围都是十几岁的学生。那一刻我心想：“等等……这首歌是哪一年发行的？？？？？”

这感觉既好笑又有点苦涩。因为我其实还不算老，才30出头，但不知怎么的，那一刻我还是觉得自己不再年轻了，而是已经步入中年了。??

你的“那个”时刻是什么时候呢？
english_item:
What’s a moment when you realized you were officially "old"?
For me it happened in the most random way. I was in the grocery store, and a the playlist was songs from 2000s or 2010s I don't remember anymore. ?? I suddenly caught myself singing along with it within hearing range of teenage students. That time it made me think "Wait a minute.. what year was this song released??????"

It was funny but also bittersweet at the same time. Because I'm not actually old-*old,* I'm in my early 30s but somehow, it still made me feel that I'm not that *young* anymore rather, I'm already in the middle. ??

What is *THAT* moment for you??

chinese_item:
我27岁那年才知道葡萄干其实就是葡萄。

当时我在一家咖啡店做咖啡师。我们店里有一种（很美味的）糕点，里面加了葡萄干和肉桂。突然，我脑子里冒出一个念头：等等，葡萄干到底是什么？

我想在那之前，葡萄干对我来说就像生活的一部分，是我认知现实的方式，它们存在，无需质疑。

于是我问了我的经理，她当然是嘲笑我，然后简单地说：“就是干葡萄。”

（我现在39岁了）
english_item:
I was 27 when I learned that raisins are grapes
I was a barista at the time. We had these (delicious) pastries with raisins and cinnamon. Then suddenly the thought occurred to me: wait, what actually are raisins?

I think that until that point raisins were just a fact of life, just part of how I perceived reality, they existed and no questions asked.

So I asked my manager, and obviously she laughed her ass off at my expense, and simply said “dry grapes”

(I am 39 now)

chinese_item:
有没有人跟我一样，会突然想起十年前一些尴尬的事，然后就……默默地尴尬到不行？

比如我正洗碗，心情挺好的，突然脑子里冒出个念头：“还记得五年级的时候我叫老师‘妈妈’吗？”然后我就又被那件事折磨得死去活来??
english_item:
anyone else randomly remember something embarrassing from 10 years ago and just… cringe in silence?
like i’ll just be washing dishes, vibing, then my brain’s like “remember when u called ur teacher ‘mom’ in 5th grade” and now i’m reliving it again ??

chinese_item:
对我来说，不经意的善意之举意义非凡。虽然被班里大多数人喜欢固然重要，但哪怕只有一个人说句好听的或有趣的话，也能让我心情愉悦一整天。这周，我参加了一个“随机善意之举”游戏，选择了四件能让我感受到爱与关怀，并让我的心情变得美好的事情。我选择的是：“告诉你的每个家人一件你喜欢他们的地方；在人行道上写下鼓励的话语；捡起一块垃圾，写下某人的优点和/或你喜欢他们的地方，然后把垃圾送给他们。”

如果我的家人（比如我的妹妹）走过来告诉我他们有多爱我，我会感觉棒极了，一整天都会心情舒畅，我也会把这种感觉告诉其他认识的人（可惜我妹妹不会这么做）。

在人行道或车道上写下话语，可以让很多人的心情都变好。尤其如果你住在繁忙的马路边，很多人开车或走路经过都会看到。

想象一下垃圾散落在地上，想象一下，哪怕只有一件垃圾被风吹进大海，也会夺走一条无辜的海洋生物的生命。所以，你捡起一件垃圾，就等于拯救了一条海洋生物的生命。

最后，你可能很喜欢你最好的朋友（而不是街对面那个欺负你的人）。那就把它们写下来吧！写下来能帮助我思考我能做些什么。所以，如果你写下你最好的朋友的优点，就能让他们开心一整天。
english_item:
To me, random acts of RandomActsofKindness means a lot. Being liked by most of the people in my class means one thing to me, but at least one person saying something nice or funny just lightens my day. This week on a random act of RandomActsofKindness bingo I chose 4 things that would either make me feel loved or cared for, but can make my day the best. I chose; 'Tell each of your family member one thing you love about them, Write encouraging messages on your sidewalk, pick up one piece of rubbish and write down someone's best qualities and/or something you like about them and give it to them.'
If one of my family members (e.g. my little sister) came up to me and told me how much they love me I would feel amazing the whole day and I would say that to someone else I know (sadly my sister doesn't do that).
Writing messages on my sidewalk or driveway could brighten heaps of peoples day. Especially if you live on a busy road heaps of people could drive or walk past and see it.
Imagine rubbish being left on the ground, imagine that one rubbish it could blow into the ocean that's one innocent life of a sea creature. So you picking up one piece of rubbish, you've saved a sea creature's life.
Finally, you might like lots about your best friend (not so much the bully down the street). So write it down! Writing down things help me think about what I could possibly do about it. So if you write down good qualities about your best friend that can just lighten up their day.

chinese_item: 我遭受了大约18个月的严重恐慌症折磨。一天下班回家的路上，我突然发作，踉跄着下了公交车，瘫倒在伦敦市中心的人行道上。数百人从我身上跨过，仿佛我根本不存在。我动弹不得，无法使用手机，也无法呼吸。突然，一位身材娇小的女士蹲了下来，问我是否还好。我甚至说不出话来，只能摇摇头。她接着问我是否恐慌症发作了。我虚弱地点了点头。她说她要扶我起来，让我坐在长椅上。幸运的是，虽然她身材娇小，但我也很娇小。这招竟然奏效了。她把我拉了起来，我们一起踉跄地走到附近的长椅上坐下。她问我此刻需要什么。我告诉她，如果她能跟我讲讲她自己的故事，分散我的注意力，直到我手指恢复知觉，好给我的伴侣打电话，那就太好了。她跟我讲了她的故事。她是一位艺术家，我请她给我看看她的作品。她欣然同意，我非常喜欢她的作品。大约20分钟后，我恢复了一些精力和手指控制能力，给伴侣打了电话。他过来和我们见面，我感觉自己彻底获救了。这位娇小的女士简直就是个天使，我永远不会忘记她。我也会尽我所能地帮助别人。
english_item: I?ˉd been suffering debilitating panic attacks for about 18 months. On the way back from work, I had a huge one, stumbled off the bus and lay down in the middle of the pavement in central London. Hundreds of people stepped over me as if I didn?ˉt exist. I couldn?ˉt move, couldn?ˉt use my phone, couldn?ˉt breathe. Suddenly, a very petite lady crouched down and asked if I was ok. I couldn?ˉt event speak, so I just shook my head. She then asked if I was having a panic attack. I nodded, weakly. She said that she was going to help me up and and sit me down on a bench. Luckily, although she was very petite, so am I. It somehow worked. She pulled me up, we stumbled to a bench nearby together and sat down. She asked me what I needed in that moment. I told her that it would really help if she could tell me about herself, and distract me, until I regained use of my fingers, so that I could call my partner. She told me her life story. She was an artist and I asked her to show me some of her work. She obliged and I absolutely loved her art. After about 20 minutes, I regained enough energy and motor control to call my partner. He came to meet us and I felt totally rescued. The petite lady was an absolute angel and I will never forget her. I try to pay it forward whenever I can.

chinese_item: 几年前，在我人生中一段非常黑暗和艰难的时期，我把自己封闭了好几个月，感到绝望和崩溃。最终，在他人关爱和支持下，在一个阳光明媚的日子里，我小心翼翼地走进了诺里奇市中心。市中心熙熙攘攘的人群让我有些不知所措，于是我走进了美丽的圣彼得曼克罗夫特中世纪教堂，寻求一丝宁静和“庇护”。我不知道他是否察觉到了我的脆弱，一位工作人员走过来问我是否想从他们新建成的钟楼阁楼上欣赏一下15世纪的天使屋顶。当我走上去时，我发现自己与那些从下面看显得如此渺小的巨大木制天使面对面。我不知道那天照顾我的那位温柔善良的男士的名字，但我始终感激他，并受他的榜样启发，尽我所能地传递善意。这就是我那天拍的照片??
english_item: A few years ago, during a very dark and testing period of my life I hid away for many months feeling hopeless and broken. Eventually, with the Love and support of others, I tentatively ventured out into Norwich city centre one bright sunny day. Finding the experience of city central crowds a bit overwhelming, I sought some peace and 'sanctuary' within the beautiful medieval church of St Peter Mancroft. I don't know if he sensed my vulnerability, but a member of staff approached me and asked if I'd like to take a look at the 15thC angel roof from their newly completed bell ringers' loft. When I got up there, I found myself face-to-face with the large wooden angels that had looked so small from below. I don't know the name of the gentle, kind man who took care of me that day, but I remain grateful to him and, inspired by his example, I try and pass on RandomActsofKindness whenever I can. This is the photograph I took that day ??

chinese_item: 我父母结婚66年了。母亲现在住在养老院，患有晚期阿尔茨海默病。我那87岁高龄、慈爱而又心碎的父亲每天都去看望她，有时一天去两次。他也是个习惯性很强的人，每周四次去四家不同的餐厅吃早餐。他在每家餐厅都点同样的菜，服务员都认识他，知道他的名字和点的菜。上周二，在他常去的那家早餐店，他吃完早餐后等着结账，但账单还没上来，于是他去收银台询问。柜台后面的两位服务员解释说，有人匿名表示愿意替别人付账，她们选中了他。她们用一张餐厅礼品卡支付了账单，然后把卡递给了我父亲。他问为什么把卡给他，她们告诉他卡里还有91美元可以消费。父亲跟我讲这个故事的时候，我和他都忍不住流下了眼泪。他说，每当他情绪低落的时候，别人的善意都会让他非常感动，让他心情好很多。
english_item: My mother and father have been married for 66 yrs. My mother is now in a nursing home with late stage Alzheimer's disease. My devoted heartbroken 87 year old father visits her everyday day and sometimes twice a day. He is also a creature of habit and goes out to breakfast four times a week at four different restaurants. He orders the same thing at each restaurant and the waitresses know him by name and order. Last week at his usual Tuesday breakfast place, he was waiting for his bill after finishing his usual breakfast and since it hadn't arrived at his table he went to the cashier station to inquire about the check. Two waitresses behind the counter explained that an anonymous person offered to pay for someone's bill and they chose him. They rang up his bill on a restaurant gift card and then handed the card to my father. He asked why they were giving the card to him and they told him he had $91.00 more to spend on that card. My father was in tears as was I when he told me this story. He said that every time he gets really down someone's RandomActsofKindness touches him so much and makes his day so much better.

chinese_item: 有一次，我和妻子以及几个朋友在一家小餐馆吃早餐。有个小男孩在餐桌间穿梭，试图吸引人们的注意，好卖他手里的花。但似乎没人注意到他，大家都忙着吃饭。后来，他走到我们桌前。我打开钱包，给了他50美分，买了一小束花。然后我问他，他一天挣多少钱，又用这些钱做什么。他回答说：“我一天能挣两美元左右，都给妈妈买吃的。”我又问：“你爸爸呢？他工作吗？”他回答说：“我爸爸是渔民，但他生病了。”这时我才意识到，这个小男孩是家里唯一的经济支柱。这时，隔壁桌的一个男人叫住了他。我看到那个男人给了小男孩50美分，但他并没有买花，所以这算是一种慷慨的举动。这件事让我明白，下次要更加友善。
english_item: Once, my wife and some friends were having breakfast in a small restaurant.There is this little boy moving from one table to another trying to attract the attention of the people inside so that he can sell his flowers. But nobody seemed to notice him. The were too busy eating.And then, he moves to our table. I opened my wallet to give him 50 cents for a small bunch of flowers. And then I asked the boy how he can earn a day and what does he do with the money.The body said, i earn about two dollars and I give to my MOM for food. What about your father, does he work? My Father is a fisherman but he is sick. So now I realize that this little boy is the sole breadwinner for the family.And then he was called by a man from another table. I was watching the man give the boy 50 cents but he did not get the flowers, so it was an act of giving. This give me a lesson to be more kind next time.

chinese_item: 小时候，妈妈把我送走几个星期，因为她不想让我待在她身边。我当时非常害怕，在火车站，她因为我不懂火车票有多贵而对我大吼大叫。我当时才8岁，要独自一人坐12个小时的火车去一个陌生的地方。这时，一个大概15岁的女孩看到了我在火车站哭泣，妈妈离开后，她就过来坐在我旁边。她握着我的手，和我闲聊，让我暂时忘记了即将到来的旅程。这件事发生在25年前，但我永远都会记得那个女孩。我经常想起她，希望她现在过着美好的生活。我多么希望她能明白，她那份不经意的善意对我有多么重要。如果她能看到这篇文章，我想说，正是像你这样的人，让这个世界变得如此美好。我现在过着幸福的生活，我也常常尽我所能，像你当年那样，让别人不开心的一天变得快乐。
english_item: My mum sent me away for a few weeks when I was young because she didn?ˉt want me around. I was terrified and she yelled at me a lot at the train station for not appreciating how much the train ticket cost. I was 8 years old and was taking a 12 hour trip by myself to somewhere unknown. There was this girl, who would have been about 15 years old. She noticed me crying at the train station and came and sat next to me when my mother left. She held my hand and spoke to me about random things and took my mind off what was to come. This was about 25 years ago and I will forever remember that girl. I think of her often and I hope that she is living a wonderful life. I wish she knew much much that random act of RandomActsofKindness meant to me. The slim chance that she is reading this; it?ˉs people like you who make the world a beautiful place. I?ˉm living a happy life now and I often do my best to turn someone?ˉs unhappy day into a happy one like you did for me.

chinese_item: 2021年，我去了急诊室，当时我腹痛难忍（阑尾炎）。候诊室里挤满了人。我唯一能稍微缓解疼痛的方法就是躺下。工作人员说没有担架可以躺。我丈夫拿了几条毯子让我躺在候诊室的地板上。几个小时后，我躺在那里，痛苦地扭动着身体，这时一位女士走过来，把她的外套递给我，想让我盖着当毯子或枕头。虽然我当时高烧不退，婉拒了她的好意，但我永远不会忘记她的善举。她愿意把自己的外套给我，想在陌生人最痛苦的时候安慰我。这对我来说是一个改变人生的善举。
english_item: In 2021 I went to the ER and I was having severe abdominal pain (appendicitis). The waiting room was full. The only way I could even come close to managing the pain was by lying down. The staff said they had no gurneys to lay on. My husband got some blankets for me to lay on the er waiting room floor on. As I laid there, writhing in pain, a couple hours later, a woman came over and offered me her coat as a blanket or pillow. Although I appreciatively declined as I was on fire with a fever, I will NEVER forget that act of RandomActsofKindness. She was willing to give me her coat to try to comfort a stranger in a terrible moment. That was a life-altering moment of RandomActsofKindness for me.

chinese_item: 多年来，当你打电话给商家时，接电话的人总是先问“我能帮您什么？”或者“您的来电对我们非常重要”，然后就让你一直等着。最近，我和我92岁的丈夫在一个周六清晨去了车管所，看看他能不能更新驾照。我们7点半就到了，虽然已经排起了队，但还不算太长。我带上了助行器，这样我们至少可以在不需要走路的时候轮流扶着。没过多久，一位大概30岁左右的年轻女士推着一把粉色的折叠椅走过来，说：“你们俩可以一起坐。”我先是惊讶，然后非常感激她。我道谢后说，到了入口就把椅子还给她。她说不用了，只要7美元，我就留着吧。我说你留着吧，也许可以分给别人，然后向她道谢。这个小小的举动以及其他一些事让我开始反思，我们这个国家变得多么不礼貌、多么自私。我当时就决定，看看我能否在自己和他人的生活中做出一些小小的改变，从而以一种全新的态度——发现需要并满足它——加入到行善的行列中来。
english_item: For many years now when you call a business the first words you hear from the person who answers the phone say is How Can I Help You or Your Call Is Very Important To Us as they keep you on hold forever.Recently my 92-year-old husband and I went early on a Sat morning to the DMV to see if he could renew his drivers license.We got there at 7:30 and there was already a line but not too bad. I took our walker so we could at least take turns leaning on it when not needing to walk.It wasn?ˉt long before a young woman perhaps in her 30s came forward with her pink folding chair and said here you two can share this. I was at first shocked and then so grateful to her. I thanked her and said we would return it once we got to the entrance. She said no keep it was only $7 to which I said you keep it you may be able to share it with others and thanked her.That small gesture and several others got me thinking how impolite and self centered we as a nation have gotten, and I decided right then to see if I as one can make some minor changes in my life and the life of others, thus joining Acts of RandomActsofKindness with a new attitude Find a Need and Fill It.

chinese_item: 我妈妈最近去世了，我对她的思念无法用言语表达。在疫情最严重的时候，她经历了痛苦不堪的化疗，却从未抱怨过。我的办公桌上放着一张她的照片，展现了她善良的一面，记录了她一天的生活。照片里，她戴着口罩，可爱的光头戴着一顶漂亮的帽子，手里提着一大袋甜点，准备送给化疗护士——以此表达她对她们的感激之情！??
english_item: My mom recently passed away and I miss her more than words could ever express. During the height of the pandemic she underwent awful, brutal rounds of chemo and never ever complained. I have a photo of her on my desk showing her true RandomActsofKindness, a day in the life of my mom. She?ˉs walking into treatment wearing a mask, her cute bald head in a cute cap, with a big bag of sweet treats for the chemo nurses - to let them know how much she appreciated them! ??

chinese_item: 几周前，在一个非常寒冷的日子里，我93岁的母亲家里出了点紧急情况。保险丝跳闸了，所有插座都断电了，这意味着她无法使用电话求助。而且保险丝盒太高，她够不着，必须站在椅子上才能够到。她听力很差，行动也不方便。情急之下，她走到门口，在那里等着邻居路过，但可惜的是，因为天气寒冷，大家都待在家里。过了一会儿，她看到两个十几岁的女孩从马路对面走过，朝她挥手。她招手示意她们过来，请她们帮忙用手机给我住在附近的姐姐打电话。她们很热心地打了电话，但因为我姐姐不认识这个号码，所以没有接。我妈妈又问她们能不能爬到椅子上去修理保险丝。由于她们不知道该怎么办，其中一个女孩联系了她的父亲寻求帮助，并给他发了一张保险丝盒的照片。他给了他们一些指示，然后电力就恢复了。我妈妈非常感激，但当时没想到要问他们的名字。后来我妹妹得知事情经过后，回了电话，感谢他们的帮助和善意。之后，一位男士（我们推测是孩子的父亲）过来查看一切是否安好。我们也觉得他可能联系了房东，房东第二天就派电工来检查了家里的电器。我们都非常感谢这两位女孩的帮助和善意，真的非常感谢你们！
english_item: A few weeks ago, on a very cold day, my 93 year old mother had an emergency in her home. A fuse had tripped and she had lost power to all her sockets, which meant that she was unable to use the telephone to summon help. Also the fuse box was out of reach and entailed standing on a chair to access it. She is very hard of hearing and has limited mobility.In desperation she went to her door and waited there hoping that a neighbour would pass by, unfortunately, because of the cold weather everyone was inside. A short while later she noticed two teenage girls waving at her as they walked by on the other side of the road. She beckoned them over to ask if they would phone my sister, who lives locally, on their mobile. They kindly did this but because my sister didn?ˉt recognise the number she didn?ˉt answer.My mum then asked them if they could climb onto a chair to sort the fuse. As they weren?ˉt sure what to do one of the girls contacted her father for advice, she sent him a photo of the fuse box. He gave them instructions and hey presto power was restored. My mother was so grateful but didn?ˉt think to take their names. When she discovered what had happened my sister returned the phone call and thanked them for their help and RandomActsofKindness.Later, a man, who we presume was the father, came to check that everything was alright. We also think that he may have also contacted the landlord who, the next day,sent an electrician to check her appliances.We are all so grateful to these girls for their help and RandomActsofKindness, thank you both so much!

chinese_item: 有一次我和爷爷住在一起的时候，我和妈妈一起去街角的商店，路过一座连接高速公路的桥。我们走着走着，我抬头往右边看，看到有个流浪汉睡在那里。我就问妈妈能不能给他买些冰淇淋、零食和饮料。妈妈说不行，但我还是给他买了。之后我们离开商店，又走回桥下，我顺着他睡觉的小路走到他睡觉的坡上，不小心吓了他一跳。但我还是把买的东西给了他，他高兴极了。
english_item: Once when I lived with my grandpa, me and my mom were walking to the corner store and there was a bridge that connects to the highway and we were walking and I look up to my right and I saw that there was a homeless man sleeping u there and I asked my mom if I could buy him some ice cream, snacks, and drinks. My mom said no, but then I bought him some anyway and then when we left, we walked back under the bridge and I walked up the hill where he was sleeping and I kind of scared him by accident but then I gave him the stuff I bought him and he was supper happy.

chinese_item: 我当时住院要做个大手术，但不想让丈夫和儿子知道我很害怕。我开玩笑地笑着说一切都会好的。然后，那人推着我去了候诊区，在那里我打了静脉输液。我害怕得哭了起来。他找了把椅子，坐在我的手术台旁边，握着我的手，和我闲聊，直到麻药生效。我睡着了，再也没看到他离开。我不知道他是谁，但我永远不会忘记他的善意。
english_item: I was in the hospital for major surgery, but did not want my husband and son to know I was afraid. I joked and smiled and said it would be just fine. Then the man wheeled me away and down to the waiting area where I got the IV. I was so afraid that I started to cry. He found a chair and sat next to my gurney and held my hand, talking to me about just random things, until the anesthesia took effect. I fell asleep and never saw him leave. I don?ˉt know who he was but I never forgot his RandomActsofKindness.

chinese_item: 有段时间我情绪有点低落，学校在情人节那天卖五美元一个的泰迪熊，供大家送人。我当时根本没想过会有人送我。所以，当有人递给我一个泰迪熊时，我简直不敢相信自己的眼睛。纸条上只写着“一个欣赏你的人”。我后来也没找到送我的人，但这件事让我开心了一整天，也给我留下了深刻的印象。我永远不会忘记。
english_item: One time during a period when I was a bit depressed my school was selling five dollar teddy bears for you to give to other people on Valentine?ˉs Day. chinese_item: 有些人喜欢干涉别人的饮食习惯  在另一个帖子里，有人一直说，如果喜欢吃全熟牛排，就应该只在家吃，千万别在餐厅点全熟的，因为那样会毁了牛排，厨师应该拒绝做全熟的。他们还说点全熟的牛排是浪费钱。我实在不明白，如果有人点了全熟的牛排，而且这正是他们喜欢的，怎么会是浪费钱呢？有些人对别人怎么点餐这件事太在意了。我甚至都不吃牛排，我只是不喜欢别人干涉别人的饮食选择。口味是很主观的，你觉得全熟牛排难吃并不代表别人也不喜欢。我的意思是，我不喜欢土豆泥，但你见过我阻止别人点土豆泥吗
english_item: ？,"People who gatekeep how others like their food Someone on another post kept saying that if people like their steak well done that they should only eat it at home and never order it like that from a restaurant because it ruins it and that chefs should refuse to make it. They kept saying it was a waste of money to get it overcooked. I just don't understand that, if someone orders well done and it's what they like, how is it a waste of money? Some people care way too much about how other people choose to order food. I don't even eat steak, I just don't like people gatekeeping other's food choices. Taste is subjective, just because you think well done steak is disgusting doesn't mean other people don't like it. I mean, I don't like mash potatoes but you don't see me stopping people from trying to order it with their meal.

chinese_item: 室友拿走了我家的油漆？？我真的不知道我该不该为此生气，毕竟我是第一次买房。我的室友严格来说是租客，住在我的房子里（我们是朋友，只是碰巧房子是我的）。我们以前都是一起租房住，所以现在拥有自己的房子，一切都是新的。她突然决定要用房子自带的油漆粉刷她的一些家具。她还想在自己的房间里刷，但我已经说服她，这想法太疯狂了，她应该在室外刷。总之……我知道油漆很贵，而且颜色要和房子里原有的油漆颜色匹配，所以我本能地想去找她，告诉她我对这种安排感到不舒服。让我感到困惑的是，她竟然连问都没问——就想当然地认为她可以这么做。所以我想这就是我为什么想知道这样做是否可以的原因吧？我承认自己有点控制欲/神经质，正在努力控制，所以我希望在继续被指责神经质之前，能得到一些第三方的建议。谢谢！
english_item: Roommate taking my house paint?? I genuinely don't know if this is something I'm supposed to be mad about because I'm a first time homeowner. My roommate is technically a tenant in a house I own (we're friends and everything, I just happen to own this house). We're used to renting together so everything that comes with home-owning is new. She decided out of the blue that she wanted to paint some of her personal furniture using the house paint that came with the house. She also wanted to do it in her room but I think I've convinced her that that's an insane idea and she needs to do it outside. ANYWAY.  I'm aware that house paint is fairly expensive and color matched to the paint colors already in the house, so my gut instinct is to go to her and let her know that I'm uncomfortable with this arrangement. It's just so baffling to me that she didn't even ask - just assumed that it would be okay for her to do. So I think that's why I'm wondering if maybe it is okay to do?? I'm admittedly a little controlling/neurotic and learning to manage it, so I was hoping for some 3rd party advice before I continue not beating the neuroticism allegations. Thanks!

chinese_item: 我需要一些建议，如何处理这段异地恋情。  我跟同一个老师在线学习语言已经大约八个月了，我觉得我爱上她了。每周花一个小时和她聊天，看着她的眼睛，建立起真诚的联系，这让我对她产生了好感。  但我不知道该如何处理我的感情。我确实喜欢她，而且我觉得她也知道（我有时会用赞美的方式表达出来），她似乎也挺高兴的，但我真的不知道她是否也有同样的感觉。我不知道她对我友好热情是因为我付了课费，还是她真的喜欢和我聊天。  她也在Instagram上关注了我，我偶尔会给她发消息（最多一周几次），但我尽量不去打扰她，因为我不想显得太急切。  我只想告诉她我对她的真实感受，但我真的害怕她的反应。因为如果她的反应不是我期待的那样，我怕我会永远失去她，这想法太可怕了。  我已经告诉她我计划今年去她的国家，想见她，她看起来也挺兴奋的。但我不知道这是不是真的兴奋，还是因为我花了几百美元给她上课，她只是出于礼貌。  我该怎么办？现在就告诉她吗？等见面再说？还是应该怎么办？我需要一些建议，因为这种不确定性快把我逼疯了
english_item: 。,"I need advice on how to handle my long-distance crush I’ve been taking online language lessons with the same teacher for around 8 months now and I think I’ve fallen in love with her. Just spending an hour every week talking to her, looking into her eyes, and building a genuine connection has developed an attraction towards her in me.   I don’t know how to proceed with my emotions though. I do like her and I think she knows (I’ve kinda made it obvious at times with compliments) and she does seem flattered but I honestly don’t know whether or not she feels the same. I don’t know if she’s being nice and engaging only because I pay her for each class or if she genuinely likes talking with me.   She follows me on instagram too and I sometimes message her there (maybe a couple times a week at most) but I try not to overdo it because I don’t want to come off as too desperate.   I just want to tell her my true feelings towards her but I’m honestly just scared about how she’ll respond. Because if it’s not the response I’m looking forward, I’m afraid I’ll lose her forever which is a terrifying thought.  I’ve told her I plan to visit her home country this year and that I want to see her and she seems kinda excited about it. But again, I don’t know if it’s genuine excitement or it’s just because I’ve spent hundreds of dollars on her classes and she’s just being nice.  What should I do guys? Do I tell her now? Wait till I see her in person? Something else? Need advice because the uncertainty of this whole situation is killing me.

chinese_item: 我的男室友在捉弄我？我和我的男室友在同一个房东的房子里合租了六个月。我觉得一开始他好像有点喜欢我，但我没办法，所以一直没理他。我九月份要搬走，我已经跟房东说了，我觉得房东也跟他说过了。总之，现在我的男室友好像想引起我的注意，或者别的什么原因，我也不知道。他把马桶盖掀起来，马桶里好像有尿。我没冲水，也没把马桶盖放下来，就那样放着，给他机会冲水和放下马桶盖。后来他把马桶盖放下来了，但是没冲水，我生气了，把他的东西都搬进了浴室的壁橱，包括他的卫生纸。之后他居然拿走了我的卫生纸，放到他的东西里，“好像那是他的一样”。我打开一卷新的卫生纸，然后他才把从我这儿拿走的卫生纸放回原处。最后，他故意把我的洗手液放错地方——他把它对着墙放，或者用其他方式摆放，是不是想激怒我？他还曾经把洗过的内裤留在浴室里。我没跟他谈这件事，因为我觉得他只是想引起我的注意。我还注意到，我一见到他，他就会变得很紧张，不知道该怎么跟我说话。他以前对我挺好的，给我吃过东西，还帮我修过一次门。
