The cherry on top was my next table also used the blanket even though they were hesitant at the offering at first. 

People are cute and I love them.

chinese_item:
我和老公有个关于早已倒闭的音乐流媒体服务的玩笑。

我们刚开始约会的时候，我好像滔滔不绝地跟他讲了一大堆Launchcast流媒体服务以前有多棒，还对它的各种精彩功能赞不绝口。

十年过去了，他还是会找个合适的时机提起这件事，而我总是带着一丝伤感地回应：“太早了，老兄，太早了。”

我仍然很怀念Launchcast，每次他提起我曾经花了将近15分钟滔滔不绝地赞美一个早已消失的流媒体服务时，我都会忍不住笑出声来。
english_item:
Husband and I have a running joke about a long-dead music streaming service
When we started dating apparently I gave him a long, impassioned speech about how amazing the Launchcast streaming service used to be and waxed poetic about the many awesome features it had.

Ten years later he still waits for the perfect moment to bring it up in conversation, and I always respond mournfully, “too soon, man, too soon.”

I still miss Launchcast, and he makes me laugh every time he reminds me that I spent nearly 15 minutes raving about a long-defunct streaming service.

chinese_item:
我所在办公楼的保安开始在我桌上放巧克力。

我在卡迪夫的一栋办公楼工作。前台有个保安，年纪比较大，大概五十多岁，每次我来上班他都会跟我打招呼。

大约两个月前，我那天心情很糟糕。我来上班的时候情绪很低落，眼睛因为在路上哭而通红。他问我是否还好，我说还好，只是早上不太顺利。

当我走到办公桌前时，发现桌上放着一块Twirl巧克力。我以为是同事留下的。

下周五，又一块Twirl巧克力。还是放在我桌上的同一个位置。

这种情况每周五都会发生。我最后问了同事们是不是有人故意放巧克力给我，他们都说没有。

上周我提前到公司，看到那个保安正从我的办公桌附近走开。我问他是不是他放的巧克力。

他有点不好意思，说是的。他说他注意到我周五总是看起来很疲惫，觉得这或许能帮到我。

我道谢并表示非常感谢。他只是点点头，然后回到了前台。

今天早上我又拿到了我的巧克力棒。还是那种卷曲的。总是周五才有。

这虽然是件小事，但知道有人关心我，想让我的生活更美好，真的让我感觉很温暖，也让我的这一周都变得更好了。
english_item:
the security guard at my building has started leaving a chocolate bar on my desk
i work in an office building in cardiff. theres a security guard who works the front desk, older guy probably late 50s, always says good morning when i come in.

about two months ago i was having a really bad day. came in visibly upset, eyes red from crying on the commute. he asked if i was alright and i said yeah just a rough morning.

when i got to my desk there was a twirl bar sitting there. i figured someone from my team left it.

next friday another twirl bar. same spot on my desk.

this kept happening every friday. i finally asked my coworkers if anyone was leaving me chocolate and they all said no.

last week i came in early and saw the security guard walking away from my desk area. i asked him if he was the one leaving the chocolate.

he got a bit embarrassed and said yeah. said he noticed i always look tired on fridays and thought it might help.

i thanked him and said i really appreciate it. he just nodded and went back to the front desk.

got my chocolate bar again this morning. still a twirl. always on fridays.

its such a small thing but it genuinely makes my week better knowing someones paying attention and trying to make my day a bit nicer.

chinese_item:
周三在咖啡馆和顾客的精彩对话

我是一名咖啡师，有很多有趣的常客，偶尔也会遇到一些非常搞笑的陌生人。周三对我们来说真是疯狂的一天，我遇到了有史以来最生气的顾客，当时正值客流高峰期。她怒气冲冲地离开两分钟后，当天第1000位商务人士走了进来。他身材高挑，三十多岁，黑发，穿着浅蓝色衬衫，一口极其悦耳、略带贵族腔的英式口音。以后我们就叫他C，代表顾客。

**我，打电话给他：**嗨！

**C：**你好，你好吗？

**我，以为他察觉到我可能快要哭了，只是出于礼貌：**我很好，你呢？

**C：**我好暖和！我好暖和！我感觉自己从未如此温暖过！我需要你们店里最冰的饮料。

**我：**好的，比如冰水？

**C，真心实意地笑着：**哈哈哈哈！我喜欢！不，我其实想的是你们的草莓奶油……

**我：**草莓奶油星冰乐？中杯？

**C：**好的！！

**我：**好的，请问顾客叫什么名字？

**C（当然）：**布莱尔。

布莱尔给了小费，然后走开了，成了第二个点草莓奶油星冰乐的顾客，也是我见过的第一个三十多岁的男士。他和另一个商务人士坐下来享用。

再见了，布莱尔。我会永远怀念你。
english_item:
wednesday's best conversation with a customer at my cafe
I'm a barista and have lots of funny regulars, but occasionally, a very funny rando. Wednesday was a wild day for us, involving the most pissed-off customer I have ever dealt with, right in the middle of a crazy rush, and two minutes after she stormed out, the 1000th businessman of the day came in. Tall thirty-something with dark hair, a pale blue shirt, and an immensely pleasant, kind of posh British voice. He will henceforth be referred to as C for customer.

**me, calling him up:** hi! 

**C:** hello, how are you?

**me, assuming he's picked up on how I may be on the verge of tears and is just being polite:** doing good, how are you?

**C:** I am WARM. I am so warm. I am the warmest I have ever been! And I need the *coldest* drink you have. 

**me:** okay, like maybe some ice water?

**C, genuinely laughing:** ahahaha! I love that! No, I was actually thinking of your strawberries and cream.... 

**me:** strawberry creme frappuccino? medium size?

**C:** yes!!

**me:** okay, can I get a name for the order?

**C (of course):** Blair. 

Blair tipped and wandered off to become the second customer, and first 30-sthg dude, I have ever seen order a strawberry creme frappuccino, and sat down with another businessman to enjoy it. 

Adieu, Blair. I will think of you fondly forever.

chinese_item:
一个让我感到无比温暖的早晨

我的儿子（20岁）是个夜猫子。那天早上我得赶很早的航班，要离开四天，所以前一天晚上很早就睡了。

早上我拿起行李的时候，发现儿子留了一张纸条，上面列着一些我没忘记带的东西：

充电器

牙刷
钱包等等。

纸条底部他写道，他知道我会饿，所以给我做了个三明治。——放在冰箱里的三明治袋里。

而且三明治上还抹了黄油。

（哈哈，我总是抱怨他做三明治不抹黄油会很干！）

我想那天飞机上可能只有我不饿。

这件事已经过去几周了，但每次想起它，我都会忍不住微笑。那种温暖的感觉依然让我觉得自己把他教育得很好。
english_item:
A morning that gave me the warm and fuzzies
My son (20) is a night owl. I had to catch a very early morning flight, to be away for 4 days and went to bed early the night before.

When I grabbed my bag in the morning I found my son had left a note with a list of items to check I had not forgotten.
Charger
Toothbrush 
Wallet etc.

At the bottom of this note he wrote that he knows I'll get hungry and had made me a sandwich. -Left in the fridge, in a sandwich bag.
And it had butter on it.
( Hahaha I always complain that his sandwichs will be dry when he makes them without it!)

I think I was the only one on the flight that wasn't hungry.

It was a couple of weeks ago and I can't help but smile when I think of it. I still get the warm and fuzzies and makes me feel like I've raised him well.

chinese_item:
蜘蛛爬上了我的胳膊

我躺在沙发上，一边吃着芝士脆饼，一边侧躺着，脸朝下，从肾脏/肋骨区域往下趴着。就在几分钟前，我抓了一大把芝士脆饼——或者说，是几块烤焦的——然后感觉胳膊痒痒的。这倒也不奇怪，我的衣服和沙发上到处都是饼干屑。我心想，那肯定也是饼干屑。你猜是什么？一只棕色隐士蜘蛛。
english_item:
Spider Crawled Up My Arm
Im laying on the couch smacking on Cheeze Itz and laying sideways more or less sideways on my back and face frontal from around kidneys/rib cage area down. And just a few minutes ago I was smacking on a decent handful of Cheeze Itz or say a damn few of the burnt things and then I feel a tickle on my arm well that's no surprise, I have crums all over my shirt and on the couch. I just say to myself well, that must be a crum to. Guess what it was. A brown recluse.

chinese_item: 我现在能游两英里，大概一个小时十分钟，不管多大年纪都算很快了。不过，我身上散发着老奶奶的气息。大多数时候，不管地铁多拥挤，总会神奇地冒出一个座位。那天我要去布朗克斯参加一个选民登记活动的志愿者工作，有点迟到了，不太确定要去哪儿，但感觉自己精神抖擞地跳上了车，站在一位拄着拐杖、带着一只非常可爱的小狗的老先生后面。几秒钟之内，就有人在我面前打了个响指，示意我坐下休息，就在老先生前面。我示意他坐下，因为他看起来正忙着拄拐杖、遛狗。他那惊恐的表情真是让我哭笑不得！他可是个绅士啊！他怎么可能让一位老太太给他让座呢？
english_item:
Granny Vibes
Now I can swim 2 miles in 1 hour & 10 minuteish which is pretty darn quick no matter how old you are however do I throw out the granny vibes. Most of the time whenever I get on the subway, no matter how crowded, a seat magically appears. I try to look gracious. Was going up to the Bronx to volunteer at a voter registration drive running a little late unsure exactly where I was going but felt like I had perkily jumped on the train behind an older man with a cane & a very cute lap dog. Within seconds somebody was snapping their fingers in my face indicting a place for me to rest my apparently weary bones, right in front of that man. I gestured that he should sit down because it looked like he was juggling a lot with the dog & cane. The look of horror he gave me! He was a gentleman! He would never take a seat from a little old lady.

chinese_item:
机场里一对可爱的老夫妇

我（28岁，女）在机场登机口旁边坐着一对可爱的老夫妇。他们正在伸展身体，为长途飞行做准备。老公请妻子帮他“掰掰背”（就是双臂交叉，另一个人向后靠，利用重力放松背部）。她试了试，但几秒钟后就喊道：“我太矮了！”

于是我站起来，不好意思地说我身高1米8，如果他不介意的话，我可以试试。他笑着答应了！他双臂交叉抱在胸前，我轻轻地把他抱了起来。他说这招管用，他感觉好多了！

我不知道我有没有真的帮他“掰”好背，但他们俩都笑得很开心，这让我感到无比幸福。这是我最近一段时间以来和陌生人之间最奇妙也最温暖的一次交流。
english_item:
Cute old couple in airport
I (28f) was sitting next to a cute little old couple at the airport gate. They were stretching in preparation for the long flight. The husband asked his wife to help him crack his back (where you cross your arms and the other person picks you up while leaning back to let gravity decompress your back) She tried, but after a few seconds exclaimed "I'm just too short!"

So I stand up and sheepishly say that I'm 6' and can give it a try if he's comfortable. Laughing, he accepts! He crosses his arms over his chest and I gently picked him up. He said it worked and he felt much better!

I dunno if I actually succeeded in cracking his back, but they were both laughing and smiling and it just made me so happy. It was the most bizarre yet warm connection I've had with a stranger in a while.

chinese_item:
终于搞清楚怎么把两副耳机连到一部手机上了……

……现在我和老公睡前可以一起听有声书了，就算他睡在宝宝房里也一样（这样我终于可以睡个好觉了）。即使在忙碌的年轻家庭生活中，我们也能找到相处的时间！
english_item:
Worked out how to connect two sets of headphones to one phone...
... and now my husband and I can listen to our audiobook together before bed even when he's sleeping in the baby's room (so I can get a solid night of sleep for once). Finding ways to be together, even when the busyness of young family life makes it tricky!

chinese_item:
每次我们一起分享零食，都会等着对方先吃最后一块。

我最近搬回和妈妈一起住，我发现我们俩都不愿意吃任何分享食物的最后一块。比如从附近小店买了好吃的冰淇淋三明治？我们一人拿一块，但最后一块会一直放在冰箱里，直到冻成冰块。找到一块漂亮的蛋糕？最后一块蛋糕也会一直放在那里，直到变质。

我觉得我们都在等着对方先吃，但谁也不肯吃。我不知道这是不是从她那里学来的，但这确实挺有意思的。
english_item:
Whenever we share a snack, we both wait for the other to take the last piece
I’ve recently moved back in with my mom and I noticed that neither of us will allow ourselves to take the last piece of any shared food. Got some nice ice cream sandwiches from the local place? We each take one, but the last one stays in the freezer so long it solidifies. Find a cute cake to enjoy? The last piece of cake sits there until it goes stale. 
I think we’re both waiting for the other one to take it but neither of us will. I don’t know if I learned that from her or not but it’s just something kind of interesting.

chinese_item:
居家电影之夜

这周我和妻子手头比较紧，没多少钱出去玩，所以我们去了便利店，买了些零食和爆米花，然后回家后花了大约五分钟（我们关系中我最喜欢的部分之一）一边拌嘴一边选电影。说来也巧，她错过了最后二十分钟，睡着了。不过考虑到这周我们相处的时间不多，这样的家庭电影之夜已经很不错了。
english_item:
Inside movie night
My wife and I didn't have a ton of money to go out this week, so we went to a convenience store, bought some snacks and popcorn, then took about 5 minutes playfully bickering (one of my genuine favorite parts about our relationship) choosing a movie when we got back to the house. She missed the last 20 minutes falling asleep funnily enough but it was really nice considering we didn't have a lot of time together this week.

chinese_item:
想起教我系鞋带的那个孩子

场景：我系鞋带已经有一段时间了，算得上是个老手了。五年级第一天，我爸教我系鞋带。我跟他说普通的鞋带结扎手，他就给我演示了他爸爸教他的那种大结。他的手特别大，我的手特别笨。那个结对我们俩来说都完美。

现在，六年级开始了。我看着曾经站过的队伍。一个孩子走了出来，径直走到我面前。他喊道：“我就是这么系鞋带的！”然后，他用他那双从二手店淘来的耐克鞋完美地系上了我们学的那个结。他咧嘴一笑，仿佛变魔术成功了。我也跟着高兴起来——哇，太棒了！我也是！

我们成了朋友，聊了很久。我突然意识到，他爸爸或者其他什么人肯定教过他系鞋带——不然他怎么会觉得这么新鲜，还跟我分享呢？两个孩子，相隔一年，却在同一天学会了系鞋带，真是挺有意思的。
english_item:
Thinking about the kid who taught me to tie my shoes
Set the scene: I'd been tying my shoes for awhile; I was a seasoned shoe tier. My dad taught me the first day of 5th grade. I told him the regular knot hurt my hands, so he showed me the big knot his daddy taught him. His hands were unusually large, mine were unusually clumsy. The knot was perfect for both of us.

Now, 6th grade begins. I watch over the lines I once stood in. A kid wanders out, right up to me. He shouted "this is how I tie my shoes!" Then did our knot perfectly on his goodwill nikees. He beamed up, having performed his magic trick perfectly. I light up- wow yeah! Me too!

We had a friendship that lasted a conversation long. Just hit me his dad or whomever must have just taught him to tie his shoes- why else would he find it novel enough to share? Kinda cute it was the same day for two different kids, a year apart.

chinese_item:
晚安，各位

大多数晚上，当我决定该睡觉的时候，我都会说“晚安，各位”。周围没人会听到，而且这句话也从来不是说给谁听的，只是我习惯性的一件事。但今晚我发现自己又说了这句话，不禁好奇它是说给谁听的。

不过，我觉得它其实并不是说给谁听的。把它理解为向宇宙道晚安固然不错，但我更喜欢把它当作我自己的私人仪式。不过，今晚，我要把它说给你们听。晚安，各位 :)
english_item:
Goodnight everyone
Most nights, when I decide it’s time to actually sleep, I’ll say “goodnight everyone”. Nobody’s around to hear it, and it was never directed towards anyone, it was just a thing I did. But tonight I caught myself saying it again and wondered who it was for. 
I dont think it’s really ‘for’ anyone, though. It’s nice to think of it as a general goodnight to the universe, but I think I like it as my own private ritual. Tonight, though, I’ll directed it towards y’all. Goodnight everyone :)

chinese_item:
一个孩子和他的妈妈在商店里

孩子：我在外面玩了一整天，感觉好累啊！

妈妈：（听不清）

孩子：如果我的膝盖没力气呢？那会很糟糕吗？

妈妈：有点儿。
english_item:
A kid and his mom at the store
Kid: Ive been outside playing all day, I feel whoa!

Mom: (inaudible)

Kid: if I have weak knees? Is that bad?

Mom: kinda

chinese_item:
花园里的树掉了一片红叶。

屋前的枫树几乎枝繁叶茂。今天早上，一片星形的红叶脱落，落在了露台的桌子上。

一整天，其他的叶子都纹丝不动。十一月的微风吹拂着枝条，但叶子依然挺立，仿佛在等待某种征兆。

那片红叶仍然留在金属桌上。正午时分，一缕阳光照射在它的中心，叶脉清晰可见，宛如一幅微型地图。

昨晚我梦见那棵树一下子落光了所有的叶子，在草地上铺成了一张猩红色的毯子。醒来后，一切都和梦里一样，只是桌子上少了那片孤零零的红叶。
english_item:
The tree in the garden lost a single red leaf.
The maple in front of the house retains almost all its leaves. This morning, a single red star-shaped leaf broke off and fell onto the patio table.

No other leaves have moved all day. The November breeze moves the branches, but the leaves remain firm, as if waiting for some sign.

The red sheet is still on the metal table. At noon, a ray of sunlight illuminated its center, making the pattern of veins visible like a tiny map.

Last night I dreamed that the tree released all its leaves at once, forming a scarlet blanket on the grass. When he woke up, everything was the same, except for that single solitary sheet on the table.

chinese_item:
剪头发了

我一直想去商场里那家理发店剪头发，但他们总是爽约。就算开了，通常也只有一个理发师，而且总是很忙。

我跟之前“首选”理发店闹翻了，现在也去不了了。事情是这样的：那个女理发师之前在家兼职剪头发，后来涨价了，价格反而更贵了。而且她总是临时取消预约，感觉我永远是她最不优先考虑的，这让我很恼火。所以我干脆不再回复她的消息，也不再预约了。另外，因为我比较容易忘记她的名字和长相，几个月后我就把她忘了。

我几个月前几乎没再理发，后来又去了我之前常去的那家理发店，直到她走过来跟我说“哇，你胆子真大啊”，我才反应过来“哦，我去，是她”。她依然很专业，手艺也不错，但是，唉。

我讨厌理发，感觉自己像只野兽，每隔六个月左右就要被抓来剪一次毛。事实上，居然没人注意到我剪了头发（我也没指望他们会注意到），这对我来说，就证明了理发根本不值30美元。
english_item:
got a hair cut
i've been trying to get my hair cut at this place in the mall for a while now but they're never open when they say they will be. when they are open, there's usually just one stylist working there anyway and they're often busy. 

i had a falling out a 'first choice' and can't really go back there. it was this whole thing where this lady was cutting hair on the side at home but then she raised her prices so that it wasn't any cheaper to see her. also, she kept cancelling at the last minute all the time and it was frustrating how i was obviously always her last priority. i just stopped replying to her or booking anything. also, because i'm a real people person, i forgot her name and what she looks like after a couple of months. 

i went back to first choice months after basically ghosting her and didn't even realize it was her cutting my hair until she was like 'wow, you have some real balls coming around here' and i was like 'oh, fuck, it's her'. she was still professional and did a good job but, ya. 

i hate getting hair cuts and feel like some wild animal that's got to be wrangled in every six months or so to be shorn. the fact that nobody even noticed i got a hair cut (didn't expect them to) is, to me, proof that haircuts aren't worth $30.

chinese_item:
我比想象中更像我爸。

我被爸爸拉去帮我们社区剧院做灯光和特效。我对这行一点兴趣都没有，但为了他，我还是会做的。

今天晚上我一直在尝试给一些填充鸟安装装置，让它们能按照指令从天而降（这居然是个很常见的舞台指示），结果餐桌上渐渐堆满了羽毛、电磁铁和电线。

一开始还算整齐，但渐渐地就蔓延开来，现在桌子和周围的地板上都乱糟糟的，连备用的灯光控制台和台灯都挤在厨房的椅子上。

当我跑到自己的桌子前找缝纫针（鸟儿们需要缝上磁铁）时，我才意识到，我的桌子也同样乱糟糟的，边缘都垂到了地板上。我这才意识到，爸爸肯定影响了我做创意项目的方式。尽管如此，我还是拨开蕾丝线轴和碎布，找到了我知道就在那里的针线盒，结果爸爸却叫我从一个乱七八糟、没有标记的盒子里拿出一把特定的螺丝刀。

相比之下，妈妈的工作空间却小得可怜，而且极其简陋，她对我们俩的习惯都既爱又恨。
english_item:
I'm more like my dad than I thought
I've been roped into helping my Dad with lighting and FX for our community theatre. I have no passion for it, but I'll do it for him.

This evening has been spent trying to rig stuffed birds to fall from the sky on cue (a surprisingly common stage direction) and so the dining room table has been gradually taken over by feathers, electromagnets, and cables.

It started out tidy enough, but it gradually spread, and now the table and surrounding floor are covered in an untidy electronic sprawl, with a spare lighting board and a lamp taking station on kitchen chairs.

The realization that he must have influenced the way I do creative projects came when I ran to my own desk to find a sewing needle (the birds need to have magnets sewn on) and realized that it's a similarily untidy mess spilling into the floor at the edges. Still, I swept aside lace bobbins and scrap fabric to find the tin of needles that I knew was there only for Dad to call after me to bring over a specific screwdriver from an unmarked, unorganized box. 

Mum in contrast, somehow has the tiniest, most minimal workspace and is lovingly annoyed by both our habits.

chinese_item:
自发的拥抱

我成长在一个家庭文化中，孩子们必须时刻用拥抱或亲吻来迎接大人。早上、晚上睡觉前、在外面见到大人时，都要这样做。

强迫式的亲昵。非常尴尬。所以我不要求我的孩子这样做。

但这并不意味着我们不爱孩子。作为父母，我们会在他们没有要求的情况下给予他们身体上的关爱。

所以今天，我三岁的儿子突然毫无征兆地让我停下脚步，弯下腰。然后，他给了我一个自发的拥抱和亲吻。啊……多么平凡生活中的幸福啊。
english_item:
Spontaneous hugs
I grew up in a family culture that required kids to always greet adults with a hug or kiss. In the morning, before going to bed, when meeting adults outside of the home. 

Coerced affection. Very cringe. Which is why I don’t require my kids to do the same.

Which is not to say that we don’t shower our kids with affection. We as their parents give them physical affection without them needing to ask for it.

And so today it made me smile hugely when out of nowhere and apropos of nothing my 3 year old just makes me stop mid stride and asks me to bend down. Then he gives me a spontaneous hug and kiss. Ahhhh. What ordinary life bliss.

chinese_item:
化繁为简，化繁为简……

我的伴侣知道我出了名的不擅长管理日程。我经常把日程记错日期，或者忘记接受邀请。他不会像有些人那样直接指出我的收件箱里还有待处理的事项——虽然这确实很烦人——而是总是很体贴。今天早上，他提醒我说：“你今天的日程安排里有一些小小的好消息”，以此提醒我接受邀请。当然，他说的没错，但我最喜欢他的地方在于，他总是如此体贴地包容我的小缺点。我希望每个人都能拥有这样的体贴。??
english_item:
Turning the mundane into the cheerful…
My partner knows that I am notoriously bad at my calendar. I am frequently putting entries on the incorrect day or forgetting to accept invitations. Instead of pointing out that there are calendar items in my inbox —which can certainly be annoying — he is always kind about it. This morning, he said “there is some minor good news in your calendar additions today,” as a way of reminding me to accept invites. Of course he was correct, but mostly I love him for being so sweet about my shortcomings. I wish thoughtfulness like that for everyone. ??

chinese_item: 作为战俘，一些女性在忍受饥饿配给和疾病折磨的同时，仍坚持劳作，直至三年后重获自由。
english_item: Held as prisoners of war, some women endured starvation rations and disease but continued to work until their liberation three years later

chinese_item: 诉讼称，该德州夏令营场地的所有者明知该地区易发洪水，却未制定应急预案，导致超过20名露营者和辅导员遇难。
english_item: The lawsuit claims that the Texas camp’s owners knew the area was prone to flooding but failed to plan for such an event, resulting in the deaths of more than 20 campers and counselors

chinese_item: 只有15%的家庭有资格获得法律援助，这意味着受害者往往被迫自己代表自己。
english_item: Only 15 per cent of families are eligible for legal aid, meaning victims are often forced to represent themselves

chinese_item: 美国联邦政府停摆已创下历史最长纪录。在此期间，民众每天临时抱佛脚，想方设法帮助自己的社区渡过难关。
english_item: Everyday people are improvising stopgap efforts to support their communities through the longest government shutdown in U.S. history

chinese_item: 以色列国防军士兵讲述如何在加沙将巴勒斯坦青少年用作人体盾牌，并称该地区已成为“僵尸启示录”。
english_item: IDF soldiers tell how they use teenage Palestinians as human shields in Gaza and have left the enclave a ‘zombie apocalypse’

chinese_item: 互联网观察基金会表示，描绘婴幼儿的AI生成虐待内容激增。
english_item: The Internet Watch Foundation said there has been a surge in AI-generated abuse content depicting infants

chinese_item: 上周《独立报》披露，英国政府承诺向全球抗击艾滋病、结核病和疟疾基金提供的资金从10亿英镑削减至8.5亿英镑，这一决定可能导致25万人面临生命危险。
english_item: The cut from ￡1bn to ￡850m pledged to the Global Fund to Fight Aids, TB and Malaria – risking 250,000 lives – was revealed by The Independent last week

chinese_item: 有一次在家办公，我给部门里一位不太熟悉的同事打了个电话。通话的目的是让他确认一些项目细节，结果我们聊了将近一个小时，主要内容是生活和工作的种种挑战。像大多数对话一样，人们会互相问候“你好吗？”，但很少有人真正倾听对方的回答。对方决定坦诚相待，分享自己的想法，而我也决定用同理心倾听，全身心地投入到当下。这种善意的举动让我们俩都感觉比通话前更有活力。
english_item: One time during a regular day at the (home)office, I called a colleague in my department which I do not know quiet well. The purpose of the call was for him to confirm any project details, we ended up talking about an hour mainly on the struggles of life and work. Like most conversations, people ask each other how they are doing, but only rarely people actual listen to the response of that question. The other person decided to be authentic and share what's on it's mind AND I decided to listen compassionately and BE present in the moment. A valuable act of RandomActsofKindness that helped us both leaving the call more energized than we entered it.

chinese_item: [1] 超市收银员清点我买的东西时，发现我身上的钱比实际多出了12美元。我开始从袋子里拿出东西，这时另一位顾客递给我一张20美元的钞票。“别给自己找麻烦了，”我对他说。“我给你讲个故事，”他说。“我妈妈得了癌症，正在医院里。我每天都去看她，给她带花。今天早上我去看她，她因为我又把钱花在花上而生气了。她要求我用这笔钱做点别的事。所以，给你，收下这些花吧。这是我妈妈的花。”——莱斯利·瓦格纳。顺便说一句，这个故事是从网上看到的，所以别封我的号，谢谢。
english_item: [1] When the supermarket clerk tallied up my groceries, I was $12 over what I had on me. I began to remove items from the bags, when another shopper handed me a $20 bill. ?°Please don?ˉt put yourself out,?± I told him. ?°Let me tell you a story,?± he said. ?°My mother is in the hospital with cancer. I visit her every day and bring her flowers. I went this morning, and she got mad at me for spending my money on more flowers. She demanded that I do something else with that money. So, here, please accept this. It is my mother?ˉs flowers.?± ¨C Leslie Wagner. By the way this is the story from the internet so dont ban me thank u

chinese_item:
在一个雨天的早晨，我给租来的车加满油后，沿着加拿大布谢维尔的莫尔塔涅大道飞驰，想尽快驶入通往美国缅因州的高速公路。

突然，我听到左侧车道上一辆车鸣笛，吓了一跳。我心想，可能是警察。他用右手指了指我的车。我感觉有些不对劲，但又说不上来。那天早上八点，路上车水马龙，还下着雨。我的车在红绿灯前已经和他隔了一个车道。雨势很大，我下车很困难，但出乎意料的是，我看到他从车里出来，走到我的车旁，帮我盖上了油箱盖——我加完油后忘了盖上。几秒钟后，绿灯亮了，我看到他浑身湿透地跑了回去。我都没来得及向他道谢。
english_item:
after i put up gas on my rented car one rainy morning I speed up along Boulevard de Mortagne, Boucherville, Canada to connect me sooner to the freeway for Maine USA.
I was stunned when I heard a honk from a car in the other left lane. In my mind, I thought it could be a cop. He motioned his right hand pointing at my car. I sensed something was wrong but i couldn"t figure what was it. At 8 o'clock in that morning, the road was filled up with traffic plus the rain. My car was already 1 lane apart from him at the stop light. With the downpour it was difficult for me to step out but surprisingly I saw him getting out from his car came to my car and put in the gas cover which i forgot to put back after my gas filling. After seconds, just timely, the green light was on, I saw him running back so drenched. I never had time to thank him.

chinese_item: 体育课上，我们在屋顶上玩捉迷藏，玩得非常激烈。前段时间下了雨，橡胶地面湿漉漉的。我沿着湿橡胶地面的角落跑，因为太专注于奔跑，没注意到自己一头扎进了一大滩水里。我滑倒在水里，重重地摔在了膝盖上。膝盖流血了，我不知道该怎么办。眼泪涌上眼眶，火辣辣地疼。这时，我感觉有人轻轻拍了拍我的背。她说她要带我去医务室。虽然要爬六层楼梯，而且我的膝盖还在流血，走起来很费劲，但她还是一路扶着我，逗我笑，直到把我送到医务室。就这样，我们成了最好的朋友。因为当有人像这样救了你的命，你怎能不成为最好的朋友呢？
english_item: In P.E., we were playing a very intense game of tag on the rooftop. It rained recently so the rubbery ground was damp. I ran on the corner of the rubbery damp ground and I had been so focused on running, I didn't realize I just ran into a big puddle of water. I slipped on the water and fell badly on my knee. It was bleeding I didn't know what to do. My eyes burned with tears. Then I felt a gentle tap on my back. She said she would take me to the nurse. Even though it was six flights of stairs and with my bleeding knee I couldn't walk very well, she still supported me and made me laugh all the way to the nurse's office. And just like that, we became best friends. Because when somebody saves your life like that you can't help but become best friends.

chinese_item: 我总是会回复朋友发给我的私信，他们在私信里坦诚地告诉我他们的真实感受，以及他们觉得生活有多么艰难。对他们来说，见面往往很困难，他们的焦虑程度非常高，他们的需求也很复杂，目前正在接受心理治疗。给朋友发信息是他们保持定期联系、感受到彼此陪伴的一种方式。我觉得对他们来说，收发信息就像一条生命线，让他们能够真正坦诚地表达自己，不用试图掩饰自己的感受。我总是会回复，并且尽我所能地支持他们，但不会试图“解决”他们的问题或“改变”他们面临的困境。在我人生最艰难的时期，这个人给予了我极大的支持——从不评判，只是倾听，陪伴。这是我力所能及的，因为我想让他们知道，无论他们面临怎样的挑战，无论他们感觉多么低落，他们都对我的生活产生了巨大的影响。
english_item: I always respond to my friend's private messages to me in which they candidly tell me how they are really feeling and how hard they find life. Meeting up in person is often hard for them, their anxiety levels are extremely high, they have really complex needs and are having therapeutic support. Messaging their friends is one way they can have a sense of regular contact and feel connected. I sense it is a lifeline for them to send and receive messages, and to be able to be really direct and honest and not try to hide how they are feeling. I always respond and I try to be as supportive as I can without attempting to 'fix' hem or 'solve' their significant challenges. This person was extremely supportive of me at a very difficult time in my life - never judging, just listening, being there. This is the least I can do as I want them to know that, whatever challenges they are facing, however low they feel, they have made a huge difference to me in my life.

chinese_item: 很多很多年前，我还在上高中的时候，曾在麦当劳打工。有一天，一个大概四五岁的小女孩和她爸爸一起进来。他们在柜台点了餐，然后到桌子旁坐下吃饭。但小女孩一直回到柜台前看着我。最后，她说：“你真漂亮。” 我这辈子从来没听人这么说过。这句话改变了我对自己的看法。我经常想起那个小女孩，也很感激她爸爸那天给了她和我说话的机会。我很漂亮。我知道这一点，因为一个学龄前儿童告诉我的，而学龄前儿童是不会说谎的。
english_item: I worked at Mcdonald's when I was in high school many, many years ago. One day a very young girl, maybe 4-5 years old, came in with her father. They placed their order with me at the counter then went to a table to eat. But the little girl kept coming back to the counter and looking at me. Finally, she said, "You're so pretty." I had never in my life been told that. It changed the way I thought of myself. I often reflect on that little girl, and am so thankful that her father gave her the opportunity to talk to me that day. I'm pretty. I know it because a preschooler told me, and preschoolers don't lie.

chinese_item:
午休时分，艾拉坐在公园的长椅上，看着熙熙攘攘的人群。她打开三明治时，注意到旁边长椅上坐着一个男孩，正盯着她的食物。男孩衣衫褴褛，双颊凹陷。

艾拉一言不发，把三明治掰成两半递给他。男孩先是露出惊讶的表情，然后露出了灿烂的笑容，那笑容仿佛能照亮整个人。“谢谢。”

这半个三明治对艾拉来说并不贵，却温暖了一颗饥饿的心，让她感到无比幸福，这份温暖持续了好几天。
english_item:
Ella sat on a park bench during her lunch break watching people rush by. As she opened her sandwich, she noticed a boy sitting on the next bench staring at her food. His clothes were worn, and his cheeks were sunken.
Without a word, Ella split her sandwich in half and handed it to him. The boy looked surprised, then smiled the kind of smile that lights up more than a face.Thank you
That half sandwich didn't cost Ella much, but it fed a hungry soul and warmed her heart for days.

chinese_item:
疫情初期，我希望能做些力所能及的事来帮助他人。从十五岁起，我就一直以各种方式参与志愿活动，那时我曾为盲人读书。我们镇成立了新冠疫情应对小组，我看到了机会，于是便行动起来。

我的第一份工作是去别人那里取布料，然后送到附近的一个小镇。那里有一群妇女正在为养老院的工作人员制作布口罩，因为当时口罩很难买到。我在一家购物中心的停车场遇到了其中一位妇女，她虽然要照顾年幼的孩子，却仍然愿意与他人分享自己的技能，这让我非常感动。我带着希望离开了。

我的第二份工作是去一位农妇那里取饲料袋。她捐赠了这些饲料袋，以便我们装满食品杂货，分发给有需要的人。路边设有取货点，还有一些小隔间，里面放着罐头食品、保健品和美容用品，供需要的人匿名领取。我又一次带着希望离开了。

在充满不确定性的时期，总会有伸出援手的人。罗杰斯先生告诉我们要寻找他们。我轻易地找到了他们，并被他们无私的行为和不经意的善举所感动。我永远不会忘记他们。
english_item:
In the beginning of the pandemic I wanted to do something to help others. I had volunteered in some capacity since I was fifteen years old when I read to people who were blind. I saw an opportunity when our town formed a Covid Response Team. I was ready.
My first job was to pick up fabric from someone and take it to a nearby town where a group of ladies made cloth masks for nursing home personnel since any masks were hard to find. I met one of the ladies in a mall parking lot and was touched that she was busy with small children but wanted to share her skills with others. I drove away with a feeling of hope.
My second job was to pick up feed bags from a farmer?ˉs wife. She donated them so that we could fill them with groceries for whoever needed them. There were pickup spots along roads and small enclosures with canned goods and health and beauty aids for anyone to anonymously take when needed. I drove away again with a feeling of hope.
In the midst of uncertainty, there are always the helpers. Mr. Roger?ˉs told us to look for them. I easily found them and was inspired by their selfless acts, their random acts of RandomActsofKindnesses. I?ˉll never forget them.

chinese_item: 有一天，我发现我的朋友饿了，而我有很多吃的。于是我给了他一些，结果他一直缠着我不放，还偷了我的饭盒和水。
english_item: one day I realized my friend was hungry and I had a lot of food. so I gave him some and know he wont leave me alone and he steals my lunch box and my water

chinese_item:
午夜刚过，萨拉·亚希亚（Sara Yahia）疲惫不堪、焦虑不安地待在急诊室里，等待家人就诊。这时，她注意到一位父亲带着三个年幼的孩子，他们脸色苍白，神情痛苦。父亲显然心烦意乱，竭尽全力地寻求帮助，但他有限的英语却成了沟通的障碍。

萨拉远远地看着这一切，听到他用阿拉伯语说话。

她走上前去，主动提出帮忙。父亲的眼中顿时充满了如释重负和希望。她做了自我介绍，并在接下来的几个小时里，成为了连接父亲恐惧和孩子们获得救治的桥梁。

她并非医院的医护人员。她只是一个会说两种语言，并且真心实意地陪伴他们，直到一切明朗起来的人。

到了晚上，孩子们都得到了治疗，正在休息。父亲满怀感激地看着她，把手放在胸口。

那个夜晚深深地印在了萨拉的脑海里，让她更加深刻地体会到自己为何如此坚信善意；不是那种虚伪的善，而是那种悄悄走上前去，轻声问道：“我能帮上什么忙吗？”你不需要头衔，也不需要计划。只需要同理心和行动的意愿。有时候，这就足以改变一切。
english_item:
It was just past midnight when Sara Yahia found herself in the emergency room, exhausted and anxious, waiting for a family member to be seen when she noticed a man with three small children who looked pale and visibly uncomfortable. The father, clearly distraught, was doing his best to get help, but his limited English stood in the way.
Then Sara, watching the scene from afar, heard him speak Arabic.
She walked over and offered her help. His eyes widened with relief and hope. She introduced herself and became a bridge between his fear and their care for the next few hours.
She wasn?ˉt part of the hospital staff. She was simply someone who spoke both languages and cared enough to stay with them until everything made sense.
By the end of the night, the children had been treated and were resting. The father looked at her, eyes full of gratitude, and placed his hand over his heart.
That night stayed with Sara as a quiet reminder of why she believes so deeply in RandomActsofKindness; not the performative kind, but the one that steps in softly and asks, ?°How can I help??±
You don?ˉt need a title or a plan. Just empathy and the willingness to act. Sometimes, that?ˉs enough to change everything.

chinese_item: 有一次我在学校，我和朋友看到一个女孩想玩，但其他人都不愿意和她一起玩，因为她的玩法和他们不一样。我们就过去和她一起玩，我和朋友觉得那些人对她很没礼貌，仅仅因为她和他们不一样。如果那些孩子继续这样下去，又没有人出声制止，她甚至可能会受到心理创伤，甚至产生焦虑。
english_item: One time I was at school and me and my friend saw someone that wanted to play but they did not want to play with her because she was a different style as them so we went to play with her and me and my friend thought that they were very rude to her just because she was different. If those kids kept going and nobody said nothing, she can go up to even being traumatized or even anxiety.

chinese_item: 我在一个码头停车场爆胎了。一个轮胎完全瘪了，另一个胎压不足，而我当时没有手机。我看到一群人在修另一辆车的引擎，就问他们能不能帮忙打电话求助，通话费我来付。他们做得更好，拿来一个充气泵到我的车旁，把两个轮胎都充上了气，还说如果我直接回家，第二天就能有足够的气去修理。他们分文未取。这已经是第二次有人帮我修轮胎了。第一次是在一座桥上，那里禁止停车。我停了下来，打开了引擎盖。一位好心人发现我没有备胎，就卸下我的轮胎，立刻去修理好，然后送还给我。与此同时，高速公路巡警过来查看情况，但他们没有给我开罚单，只是提醒我待在人行道上，那里更安全。我非常感激在我需要帮助的时候遇到这些善良又耐心的人，因为我本应该为出行做好更充分的准备。
english_item: My tires gave out in a marina parking lot. One went flat, another low, and I had no cell phone. I saw a group of men working on the engine of another car and asked if one could call for help, I would pay for the call. They did one better, brought a compressor over to my car, filled both tires and said, if I went straight home, I would have enough air to get in for repairs the next day.They charged me nothing. This is the second time someone helped me with a tire. The first time was on a bridge where It wasn't legal to stop. I did and put my hood up. A kind man found I had no spare, took off my tire, got it repaired right away and brought it back to me. In the meantime, highway patrol came to see me and chose not to ticket me, only cautioning me to wait outside the car, on the sidewalk, a safer place to be.. I was so grateful to meet these kind and patient helpers in my times of need when I should have been better prepared for travel.

chinese_item: 每隔一个星期天，我都会去教堂做义工，教二年级的孩子。我们都知道，孩子们说话没遮没掩饰，想到什么就说什么。上周日，也就是2022年2月13日，我接待了两个继妹和她们上幼儿园的弟弟，弟弟患有自闭症——我喜欢有特殊需要的孩子，他们太可爱了——其中一个孩子，蒂莫西，嘲笑SJ——那个有特殊需要的男孩——其中一个继妹走到蒂莫西面前，站出来和她弟弟说话，直截了当地对蒂莫西说：“你不应该以貌取人，如果你患有自闭症，我嘲笑你，你肯定也不高兴！” 我当时简直震惊了。
english_item: Every other Sunday, I volunteer at my church, I teach 2nd graders. And we all know kids, they have NO filter, so they just say whatever comes to mind. And this past Sunday, 02132022, I had two step-sister's and their kindergarten brother who had autism-I love kids who need special needs, they're so cute-and this one kid, Timothy mad fun of and laughed at SJ-thoe boy with special needs-and one of the sisters walked up to Timothy and stood up to her little brother, and told Timothy like it was, she said, "You shouldn't judge kids by how they look, you wouldn't like it if YOU had autism and I made fun of you!" And my mind was just BLOWN.

chinese_item: 大约三年前，我和男友喝得有点醉，正要离开酒吧等出租车回家。这时，我们看到马路对面一个看起来很沮丧的十几岁女孩（大概十五六岁）正在打电话哭泣。当时是周六晚上，快到午夜了，人很多，所以等她挂断电话后，我穿过马路过去看看她是否安好。她说她被寄养家庭赶了出来，正想去十英里外的奶奶家，但找不到路，身上也没有现金坐公交车或出租车（我们住在一个小镇，非接触式支付并不普及）。出租车来了之后，我让她上了车，给了司机15英镑的车费。女孩虽然抱怨，还问我要了联系方式，说她奶奶之后可以把钱转给我，但我告诉她只要她能安全到家就行，没关系。这让我男友很不高兴，他觉得我被“骗”了，我们只能走回家了。时间快进到几周前，一个陌生女孩在同一家酒吧里走过来问我，我是不是那个付钱让她安全回家的红发女士，她还告诉我她至今都非常感激我。直到她提起这件事，我才想起来。她请我喝了一杯，然后我们就各自离开了。
english_item: Around 3 years ago, I was drunkenly leaving the pub with my boyfriend to wait for our taxi home when we saw a visibly distressed teenage (15/16 year old) girl on the other side of the road crying down the phone. As this was around midnight on a busy Saturday night, I crossed to make sure she was okay once she?ˉd hung up. She said she?ˉd been kicked out of her foster home and was trying to get to her Grandma?ˉs house 10 miles away but couldn?ˉt find a way there and didn?ˉt have any cash for a bus/taxi (we live in a small town, contactless payments weren?ˉt as common). When our taxi arrived, I put her in it and gave the driver ?ê15 for her fare as much as the girl protested and asked for my details so her grandma could transfer me the money later. I told her it didn?ˉt matter as long as she got home safe, much to my boyfriends dismay as he was convinced I?ˉd been ?°conned?± and we now had to walk home. Cut forward to a few weeks ago, when a random girl came up to me in the same pub and asked if I was the ginger woman who paid for her to get home safely and told me how much she appreciates it to this day. I?ˉd forgotten all about it until she mentioned it. She bought me a drink and went our separate ways.

chinese_item: 今天我去马里兰炸鸡店点餐，结果发现钱忘在车里了……我点完餐才想起来钱忘在车里，就让后面等的女士先走了……等我回来的时候，我们正闲聊着……那位女士坐下准备吃饭的时候，收银员把我的食物递给我，我说：“我还没付钱呢。” 她说那位女士已经付过了……我当时就懵了……为什么？她以为我没钱吗？我想不出别的理由，也不知道该怎么想……但我还是走过去跟她说：“这是我很久以来遇到的最贴心的人了”，或者类似的话，然后我说了声“上帝保佑你”。她站起来，我们拥抱了一下，我说了一些类似“我要把这个传承给其他人”之类的话；……我仍然不知道她为什么这么做，我也不太在意，我只是优雅谦逊地接受了这份礼物，当我需要唤醒内心善良的一面时，我就会想起她……她的名字叫莉莲。
english_item: Today I was at Maryland Fried Chicken and placed my order but had left my money in the car....I placed my order but realized I'd left my money in the car and let the lady waiting behind me go ahead...when I got back we were just making small talk...when the lady went to sit down to eat the cashier handed me my food and I said "I have not paid you yet." She said the lady had paid for it...I was stunned...like why? Did she think I did not have the money? I could not imagine any other reason and didn't quite know what to think....but I just walked over to her and said "that is the sweetest thing that somebody has done for me in a long time" or something like that and I said "God Bless You." She stood up and we hugged and I said something like I'm going to pass this on to someone else or something like that;....I still don't know why she did it and I don't really care I just accept the gift with grace and humility and I think about it when I need to summon my better angels...her name was Lillian.

chinese_item: 他居然又回来了，尽管我已经跟他断绝了联系。  各位救命啊?? 我之前和一个住在我几个小时车程外的男生聊天，他简直是个混蛋，明明我已经跟他断绝了联系，他还是把我从所有社交平台上删除了。结果几天前他又给我发了短信……他装得好像什么都没发生过一样（我也是，因为我真的很想念他和他的幽默），虽然我们永远不可能在一起，但这种感觉真的好真实……感觉我们之间火花四射，但我该不该再跟他断绝联系呢
english_item: he came running back even though i cut him off guys help?? i was speaking to a boy who lives a few hours from me and he was an absolute prick and he removed me on everything even though i cut him off and he texted me again a few nights ago…he’s acting like nothing happened (and so am i because i genuinely missed him and his humour) and even though we’re never gonna date it just feels so real…like our chemistry is unmatched but do i cut him off again…?

chinese_item: 当有人问我问题，我回答了，但他们却一直追问，直到我给出他们想要的答案，因为他们觉得我在撒谎。  我记得我第一次和朋友打电话的时候，我才12岁，那时候大部分都是视频通话。我爸不介意我露脸，只要我的头发和房间不乱就行。  那天晚上很晚了，我的朋友们都在视频通话，只有我一个人关着摄像头。我爸听到了，以为我开了摄像头，就把我叫过去问我：“你开摄像头了吗？是还是不是？”我说没有。我刚说完，他又问了一遍：“是还是不是？”我还是说没有，因为这是事实。  没有就是没有
english_item: 。,"When someone asks me a question and I answer but they keep asking me until I give them the answer they want because they think I’m lying i remember when i first starting calling my friends, i was 12 and they were mostly video calls and my dad is fine with me showing my face as long as my hair or my room isn't messy  it was late night and my friends were on call and it was a video one and i was the only one with my camera off and my dad heard and thought i had my video on so he called me over and asked me did you have your camera on yes or no? i said no and right after i said no he asked again YES OR NO? i said no again because it was true  no means no

chinese_item: “哦，别担心，它很乖，不会咬人！”  你的狗对你很乖，它也不会咬你。我不了解你的狗，我不知道它到底有什么习性，而狗的本能是保护主人免受潜在威胁。我当了八年披萨外卖员，现在是邮递员，就算你养的是世界上最温顺的狗，除非你能管住它，否则我还是不想靠近它。  上次我被狗咬的时候，我正在送披萨。猜猜看，在那之前我听到狗主人说的最后一句话是什么？各位，管好你们的狗
english_item: ！,"“Oh don’t worry she’s nice she doesn’t bite!” Your dog is nice to YOU. Your dog doesn’t bite YOU. I don’t know your dog, I don’t know what tendencies your dog actually has, and your dog’s natural instinct is to protect its owner from perceived threats. I was a pizza delivery guy for eight years and I’m a mailman today, you can have the sweetest dog on the planet and I still don’t want to be anywhere near it unless you have control over it.   Last time I got bit by a dog I was delivering pizza, try taking a guess at what the last thing I heard come out of the owner’s mouth was before that happened. Control your dogs, people.

chinese_item: 小组面试  我刚刚经历了一场噩梦般的经历。小组面试的主题是政治辩论。（我一点也不擅长辩论，所以辩论对我来说简直是噩梦。）我尽力了，但说到一半的时候，我突然大脑一片空白，一个字也说不出来，胸口像被狠狠地刺痛了一下。我幸运地忍住了眼泪，但一离开面试室，所有的情绪都涌了出来。我不明白为什么其他人能如此轻松地表达自己，而且在发言时不会忘记他们所学到的一切，包括对世界的认知和对自己的了解。在这种场合，我感觉自己就像个机器人，我甚至都记不起自己在面试中说了些什么。请问，下次参加小组面试，你们是怎么做到的
english_item: Group interviews Just went through my biggest nightmare. A group interview where a topic of discussion was political debate. (I am not argumentative at all so debating is not something that comes easy to me) I tried my best but half way through speaking I froze and could not utter a word and felt those same sharp pains in my chest. I luckily held my tears in but as soon as i left it all came out. I don’t understand how others can so easily articulate themselves and not forget everything they’ve ever learned about the world and even themselves as soon as they are speaking. I feel like I am on autopilot in these settings, I couldn’t even tell you what i said in the interview because I can’t remember. Please if you have advice for next time… How do you conquer a group interview!

chinese_item: 我最讨厌那些“情绪诱导”别人却拒绝真正提问或直接讨论问题的人。  我快40岁了，已经是个成年人了，我对这种人（尤其是女性）实在忍无可忍。  举个例子：艾莉和凯是好朋友，认识几十年了。艾莉说了句很中性的话，凯却觉得是在说她坏话。凯没有像艾莉那样，把这当成人身攻击（她知道艾莉不是那种阴阳怪气的人），而是选择一笑置之，甚至都没问问艾莉到底是什么意思。相反，她开始辩解，然后好一阵子不理艾莉。几天后，在艾莉和凯的互动中，凯总是试图通过表现得……格外热情、格外警惕、拐弯抹角的方式来了解/安抚艾莉的情绪。艾莉很了解凯，知道她情绪不稳定，但也知道如果她直接问，凯肯定会再次辩解，否认自己有什么问题。艾莉也知道，凯会跟任何想确认凯是否真的应该把这件事放在心上的人谈论它。而且因为他们习惯于确认凯的感受永远是对的，所以他们也会肯定艾莉绝对是在搞被动攻击。即使艾莉根本没有。  这感觉就像回到了中学时代，我受够了成年人还这样。能不能像个成年人一样好好谈谈？我都这么大了，受不了这种幼稚的行为
english_item: 。,"I can't stand people who emotionally prompt but refuse to actually ask questions or talk directly about a thing. I'm an adult, pushing 40, and I'm out of patience for people (mostly women) who do this.  Example: Elle and Kay are close friends and have known each other for a few decades. Elle said something neutral and Kay took it as a negative comment about her. Instead of Kay refusing to take it as a personal attack (knowing her friend isn't passive aggressive like that) and letting it roll off, or even asking Elle exactly what she meant, she gets defensive and then stops responding for a while. Days later, in interactions between Elle and Kay, Kay keeps trying to assess/fix the emotional state of Elle by being...extra. Extra nice, extra on-guard, dancing around subjects. Elle knows Kay enough to see that Kay is emotionally disregulated, but also knows that if Elle tries to ask directly then Kay will get defensive all over again and deny being anything other than normal. Elle also knows that Kay will talk about the situation with literally anyone else looking to confirm that Kay should have taken it personally, and because they are used to confirming that Kay's feelings are always right they will confirm that Elle was definitely being passive-aggressive. Even if Elle was not.   This feels like middle school, and I'm tired of grown-ass people who do this. Just talk about it like an adult. I'm too old for this.

chinese_item: 为了学校组织的旅行，我不得不进行模拟面试。  我的面试一直都很顺利，直到第五次模拟面试，我的表现和与房产经纪人的互动都让我非常失望，得分很低。  我发现自己很害怕问她问题，害怕告诉她关于我自己的事情。我只是走到她的桌子前，握了握手，然后坐下。  她一直在说，我不知道什么时候该插话，我不想打断她，所以当她说话的时候我该怎么办？  面试结束后，她要给我打分，而我却还要紧张地和她对视，这简直是我经历过的最煎熬的事情。  我其实相信她真的想招人，或者她真的想帮我，但她给我的分数太低了。  于是我把这件事告诉了我的朋友，他的一番话让我改变了对整件事的看法。  “那位房产经纪人对你更严格。”  现在我觉得那位房产经纪人其实是在根据我的表现打分。  现在我开始觉得，之前四次面试他们对我太宽容了，不想让我因为担心被评得不好而感到难过和焦虑。  我讨厌面试时感到焦虑，也讨厌和年龄差距较大的人交谈，因为和家人以外的人交流会让我感到不安和恐惧。我以前工作过，但是尝试一份新的工作，要处理各种不同的事情，会让我感到不知所措，压力太大了
english_item: So I had to do practice job interviews for a school trip My interviews were going great until I practice on my 5th interview and I was graded horribly for both how I acted and my lack of interaction with the real-estate agent.   I noticed I was terrified to ask her questions, tell her things about myself, all I did was walk up to her table, shook her hand, sat down.   She was doing the talking and I don't know when to jump in and talk, I didn't want to interrupt her so what was I supposed to do when she was talking.  Eye contact while being nervous about what she thought of me while she had to grade me after the interview, was the most nerve-wracking thing I had to endure.   I'm actually convinced that she was actually trying to hire someone or she actually was serious about her trying to help me but she graded me harshly.  So I told my friend about this then he said something that made me change my perspective of the whole situation.   real-estate agent was more strict on you.   Now I think that the real-estate agent was actually grading me on my performance.  Now I'm starting to think that my last 4 interviews were to soft on me and didn't want to make me feel bad and anxious about being graded poorly.  I hate feeling anxious during interviews or talking out of my age range because when I have to interact with someone outside of home it make me unnerved and petrified. I have worked before but trying to work at a new job where they do different stuff freaks me out it's too much.

chinese_item: 他们居然用纸巾擦屁股！我现在在上大学，基本上所有杂物都是我买的。冰箱、微波炉、马桶刷，都是我的。我不介意，但我还买了宿舍浴室里其他一些必需品（我们宿舍是公用的）。最近厕纸用完了，因为最后三包都是我买的，我以为他们会明白我的意思，自己去买新的，结果他们居然开始用纸巾擦屁股了。我跟他们解释过这样会堵塞管道，最后还得我们自己掏钱，但他们还是觉得用纸巾更好，即使我们学校商店里明明就有厕纸卖。我已经让他们买了好几遍了，毕竟最后三包都是我买的。
english_item: They're wiping with paper towels I'm in college rn, and I(m) have been responsible for paying basically all miscellaneous goods. Fridge, mine, Microwave, mine, toilet brush mine. I don't mind it but I've also bought several other goods needed for our bathroom in our dorm (We have a shared bathroom for our room). Recently our toilet papers run out seeing as I have bought the last 3 packs, I figured they'd get the idea and buy some new ones, nope they've started wiping the paper towels. I've tried explaining how it'll clog the plumbing and we'll end up paying for it but that hasn't helped, they've still decided that the alternative of paper towels is better even when our campus store literally sells tp. I've asked them multiple times to buy it seeing as how I've already bought the last 3 packs of it.

chinese_item: 电影/电视剧里的婚外情  我从未被背叛过，也没背叛过任何人。我的父母也不是因为婚外情之类的原因离婚的，所以我也不知道为什么自己会这样，但我就是非常非常讨厌在电影和电视剧里看到或听到出轨的情节。这让我非常不舒服，感觉它严重影响了我看影视作品，因为我会尽量避免任何包含这类内容的东西。  我也不知道自己是不是很奇怪。有人跟我一样吗？我真的不明白为什么这件事对我的影响这么大，明明它并没有在我的生活中发生过。也许是某种根深蒂固的恐惧吧

chinese_item: 她为什么要伤我的心？  抱歉，我喝得有点醉了。根据以往的经验，我知道以后可能会后悔。  在我很小的时候，从0岁到大约21岁，我经常做这样的梦。梦境神秘而充满冒险，我会置身于奇幻的场景中，结识一些人，并和??他们成为朋友。  与这些人相处的感觉与我现实生活中的任何互动都截然不同。就好像我在照镜子，看到自己的倒影，虽然外貌和性格都不同，但他们就是我。  我们会一起玩耍，一起经历这些冒险。  19岁那年，我遇到了一个女孩。她让我感觉就像进入了梦境一般。仿佛那些梦境是某种“预告片”，预示着我将在现实生活中遇到的东西。她告诉我她有感觉统合障碍。那是我第一次了解到这个概念。我从未想过自己也可能患有这种障碍。我一直以为自己正好相反。现在我明白了，我当时只是受到了创伤，而她提供了一种镜像神经元式的参照，让我体验到“正常”的功能。  八年前她突然消失了。从那以后，我一直心碎不已，深受创伤。  为了获得那种体验，我曾多次主动要求与“高敏感人群”见面。我是芬兰人，我觉得我的文化氛围特别封闭，这更让我感到困扰。  我该怎么办
english_item: Why did she have to break my heart Sorry I'm pretty drunk. From experience I know that I'll likely regret this later on.  When I was young, age 0 up until about 21, I used to have these dreams. They were mysterious and adventurous, I'd hang out in mystical scenarios and meet people whom I'd befriend.   Dealing with these people was distinctly different from any real-life interactions I had had. It was as if I was looking at a mirror image of myself, whoever had different looks and a different personality and yet they were distinctly ME.  We'd hang out and go on these adventures.  When I was 19 I met this girl. She made me feel exactly as if I had entered one of those dreams. As if they had served as a trailer for something I was supposed to find in real life. She told me she has sensory processing sensitivity. That's when I first learned about this concept. It never occurred to me that I might have it too. I thought I was the opposite. Now I understand that I was just traumatized and she provided some kind of mirror neuron reference for me to experience normal functionality   She ghosted me 8 years ago. I have been heartbroken and traumatized ever since.  I've specifically inquired to meet highly sensitive people multiple times in order to get that experience. I'm Finnish and I feel like my local culture is particularly evasive which doesn't help.  What can I do?

chinese_item: 有人对别人表现出的不知感恩感到困惑吗？  我不确定该如何恰当地回应那些随意拿取我的东西，却对一些小事既不道歉也不感激的人。我知道我做错了，但我真的很难理解什么情况下我才应该生气。  如果有人送我东西或者特意为我做事，我会非常注意地多次感谢他们，或者为他们帮我的忙造成的不便道歉。但当别人没有做出同样的回应时，我几乎会感到恼火或者觉得自己被占了便宜。  我在某些方面也很“英式”。在英国，我们出于礼貌会说很多话，但其实并非真心话。例如，如果我想要最后一点东西（比如别人分给我的食物），我还是会主动给对方，但似乎存在一条不成文的规定：对方会拒绝，等等。如果有人说他们不介意为你做些不方便的事，你其实也明白这只是客套话，并非真心实意，所以你不会真的让他们去做。  举个例子：我的伴侣来自另一个国家，性格比较直接。问题是我经常主动提出很多事，而他总是欣然接受。事后我会感到内疚，觉得自己被占了便宜。我觉得他很没礼貌，这让我很困扰
english_item: Anyone confused by perceived ungratefulness? I'm not sure how to appropriately react to people being comfortable taking from me and not overly apologetic and grateful for minor things. I know I'm in the wrong but I find it really difficult to understand what is appropriate to feel annoyed at.   If someone gives me something or goes out of their way for me, I'm very conscious of thanking them more than once or apologising for inconveniences caused if they do me a favour. When other people don't reciprocate this, I almost feel annoyed or taken advantage of.  I'm also very 'British' about certain things. In the UK we say a lot of things for politeness that we don't really mean. For instance, if I want the last of something (like shared food), I would offer the other person anyway, but there's an unspoken rule that the other person refuses and so on. Also if someone says they don't mind doing something inconvenient for you, you kind of understand that it's not a real offer and they are just being polite, so you wouldn't really let them do it.   Example: my partner is from another country and is more direct. The problem is that I over-offer things, and he simply accepts. Then afterwards I feel guilty, almost taken advantage of. I feel like he's rude and it bothers me.

chinese_item: 音乐是一种天赋。你不必擅长它，因为你也不必唱得不好。除非你是专业人士。即便如此，有些人也能写出很棒的歌词，但唱起来却像吞了个卡祖笛似的。有些人会用烟火、激光、烟雾机或奇装异服来弥补。如果你不是专业人士，那就唱歌、打鼓、在钢琴/合成器上随意弹奏，或者尝试其他能引起你共鸣的乐器。试试钢鼓或排箫，它们上面有音符，大概有八个左右。这些乐器能发出非常优美柔和的声音。去唱卡拉OK吧。轻声唱，大声唱。轻柔地唱，用力地唱。像鸟儿一样歌唱。它们就是会歌唱。唱对音符，唱错音符。唱错歌词。时不时地打断自己。遇到这种情况，试着对着麦克风笑一笑。虽然只有一部分人觉得好笑，但音乐始终陪伴着我们，无论我们做得“对”还是“错”。这就像在蹦床上跳跃，有些人只是随意蹦跳，有些人则能翻跟头。两者都值得享受其中的乐趣。去他妈的西蒙·考威尔。我讨厌评判或评价那些拥有如此美妙和谐嗓音的人。我讨厌我的朋友们有时会对自己过于苛刻，但他们在音乐方面却天赋异禀，天生就懂得如何运用音乐。我不怪他们，我怪的是《美国偶像》、《达人秀》或《X音素》之类的节目，它们误导人们，让他们以为只有“天选之子”才配成为音乐家。
english_item: Music is a gift. You don't have to be good at it because you don't have to be bad at it. Unless you're a professional. Even then, some people still write great lyrics, but sing like they just swallowed a kazoo. Some people use pyrotechnics or lasers or fog machines or wacky costumes to compensate. If you're not a professional, just sing or drum or doodle around on a piano/synth or maybe some other instrument that's reaching out to you. Try a steelpan or a pan drum, they have notes across them, about 8 or so. Really nice and pillowy sounds from that instrument. Do karaoke. Sing quietly, sing loudly. Sing lightly, sing heavily. Sing because of why the birds sing. They just do. Sing the right notes, sing the wrong notes. Sing the wrong lyrics. Interrupt yourself. Try to laugh directly into the mic when that happens, it's only funny to some people because music is still there for us, no matter what we did right or wrong. It's like bouncing on a trampoline, some people just jump around, some do backflips. Both sides deserve fun. F*** Simon Cowell. I hate judging or reviewing others for what is a blatantly beautiful and harmonious voice. I hate knowing my friends will sometimes be harsh on themselves, yet are outright gifted when it comes to naturally understanding how music works. I don't blame them, I blame Idol or Got Talent or X-Factor for tricking people into thinking only the chosen ones deserve to be musicians.

chinese_item: 极度社交隔离后感到绝望  （26岁，女）我觉得我的大脑因为缺乏社交而严重错乱。我依赖父母，却找不到工作，因为我害怕所有人。我简直不敢相信这就是我的生活。我甚至无法沉浸在电视节目中，因为只要看到正常人，我就会焦虑不安，甚至希望自己能变成他们。我受不了了。这和普通人的痛苦完全不同。我感觉自己快要疯了，没有人能理解我。我逃避一切。我的大脑让我觉得我不准出门，因为我不想被任何人注意到。这和普通人的经历完全不符，我再也坚持不下去了。我感觉自己像个外星人，总是假装自己是个正常人。我整天都躺着。妈妈是我唯一真正的朋友。我在这里发帖只是因为Reddit上的人都很友善，而我无法与现实生活中的人建立联系。我没有任何爱好。我找不到工作，因为如果我尝试，脸上就会露出那种滑稽的恐慌表情，让别人感到不自在。我的大脑会因为过度关注别人如何轻松自如地运转而无法处理任何事情。我真不希望任何人经历这种痛苦。  我的处境似乎无法挽回，因为我的行为模式已经根深蒂固。我不知道该如何改变我的大脑。我知道我经常说“我做不到”，但我仍然想相信自己还有希望
english_item: hopeless after extreme social isolation (26f) i think my brain is wired so incorrectly from not socializing. i’m dependent on my parents and cannot get a job because i’m terrified of all people. i can’t believe this is my life. i can’t even get lost in a show because i start getting anxiety when i see normal people and wish i was born them instead. i can’t take this. this isn’t like normal suffering. i’m insane in my own head and no one can understand me. i avoid everything. my brain has convinced me that i’m not allowed to leave the house because i don’t want to be perceived my anyone. this is so abnormal compared to the average human experience i can’t take this another day. i feel like an alien, always pretending to be a normal person. i just lay down all day. my mom is my only true friend. i’m only posting here because people are kind on Reddit and i can’t connect with real life people. i have no hobbies. i can’t get a job because if i tried i’d get that ridiculous panicked look on my face and make people uncomfortable, it stops my brain from doing tasks because i’m so hyperfocused on wondering how other people’s brains function so easily. i would not wish this on ANYONE.  my situation feels irreversible because my behaviors feel so ingrained in me. i don’t know how to fix my brain. i know i say a lot of “i can’ts” but i still want to believe there could be hope for me.

chinese_item: 自我厌恶  我一直都很讨厌自己，现在随着青春期的深入，这种感觉似乎愈演愈烈（16岁，男）。我刚从学校毕业，成绩一塌糊涂，因为三年内旷课四次被开除，就算去上课也会惹是生非。我以前从来没有朋友，唯一的朋友也去世了。我知道我181厘米的身高高于平均水平，但每次看别人，他们看起来都比我更高更漂亮。我讨厌我的痘痘，也不敢跟父母说话，因为他们怨恨我被开除，觉得我“败坏了他们的名声”。他们甚至说，他们觉得我长大后会虐待自己的孩子。我曾多次流落街头，现在我甚至不敢主动接近别人，总觉得自己配不上别人的关注。  我紧张得要命，肋骨骨折后竟然20多天都没去看医生。前几天我跟人社交，却连一句回应都说不出来。我的手总是出汗，只要有人靠近我，我就会非常紧张。我甚至都没从父母那里学到基本的卫生习惯，直到我走投无路才在YouTube上自学。我真的不知道该怎么办了，父母恨我，兄弟姐妹也恨我，因为被开除，我的成绩一落千丈，我甚至都无法与人正常交流了

chinese_item: 嘲笑别人不知道某些事情这完全说不通。你不可能生来就什么都知道。在某个时候，你也不知道别人不知道的事情。你和他们一样，都是在某个时候学会的，即使你学得更早。很难找到一个完美的例子，因为我见过太多这样的例子了。比如有人评论说“哇，我以前不知道还能这样”，然后评论区里所有人都开始嘲笑他，好像他很蠢似的。简单地笑一笑，说“哈哈，是啊，兄弟，真不敢相信你居然不知道”，完全没问题，但为了博取网络关注而侮辱别人的智商就很奇怪了。还有一种老套的情况：有人问了一个真诚的问题，却遭到铺天盖地的攻击，因为他的问题被解读为攻击，或者更糟糕的是，他不知道某些事情本身就是一种冒犯。或者当人们滥用“故意无知”这个词，并想当然地认为那些相信可怕事情的人一定是故意的？还装出一副正义凛然、仿佛仇恨那些被误导的人就能带来进步的样子。唉，真不知道该说什么，更多的人需要意识到，不是每个人都能同时理解所有事情，也不是每个人都能掌握所有事实……而嘲笑他们绝对不是教育他们的正确方式。
english_item: Ridiculing people for simply not knowing things Just makes zero sense. You weren't born knowing everything. At some point, you didn't know about the thing the other person didn't know about. You learned it at some point just like they did, even if it was way earlier in life.  It's hard to think of the perfect example of this because there are so many ways that I see it happen.  Like someone comments "woah I didn't realize that worked that way" and everyone in the comments starts making fun of them like they must be stupid. A simple laugh and "lol yeah dude I can't believe you didn't know that" is totally fine, but it's weird when you actually start insulting someone's intelligence for internet points. Or the age-old situation where someone asks an honest question and gets hate bombed because their question is somehow interpreted as an attack, or worse still, that it's somehow offensive that they didn't know something.  Or when people throw around the term "willful ignorance" way too lightly and assume people who believe awful things must be somehow believing those things… on purpose? Acting like it's righteous and makes progress to hate on the misinformed. Idk man, more people need to realize not everyone figures stuff out at the same time or has their facts in order… and ridiculing them is NOT how you teach them.

chinese_item: 情绪崩溃  毕业后我在西班牙生活了三年，现在准备搬回美国。我不得不辞职，因为我工作很痛苦，精神状态也很糟糕，而且很不幸，因为辞职我的签证也失效了。我知道这是最好的决定，也是我唯一的选择，但我真的非常害怕。我一直都不擅长适应变化——我有严重的注意力缺陷多动障碍（ADHD），而且是个高敏感人群（HSP）。离开我一手建立起来的家，再加上现在的政治局势，要回到美国，这让我每天都在哭泣。  我想我只是想寻求一些安慰的话语，一些应对过渡期的建议，以及在这样黑暗和不确定的时期如何保持希望。在这个世界上做一个敏感的人真的太难了。我内心充满感受，也为我们——我的同胞们——感到担忧。我不想让自己陷入绝望。这个子版块给了我很多安慰。感谢阅读，祝大家一切顺利
english_item: Emotional af I’m getting ready to move back to the States from Spain after living here for 3 years post grad. I had to quit my job because I was miserable and was severely struggling with my mental health, and sadly lost my visa because of quitting. I know it’s the best decision/ the only one I have but I’m terrified. Never been good with transition - I have intense ADHD and am a HSP. The combination of moving away from the home I’m built and facing moving back to the United States with the current political situation has me crying daily.  I guess I’m just looking for some words of comfort, tips for dealing with transition, and maybe how to remain hopeful during such dark and uncertain times. Being a sensitive creature in the world is so hard. I feel so deeply and am so worried about us- my fellow humans. Don’t want to give it to the hopelessness. This subReddit has brought me so much comfort. Thanks for reading, sending love to all <3

chinese_item: 我表妹暗恋我  我今年19岁，表妹（她现在18岁）的行为让我很不舒服，这种情况已经持续好几年了。  大概在她10岁的时候，她和家人来我的国家旅行。那时候，她总是抱我，亲我的脸颊，甚至当着我们父母的面叫我“男朋友”。这可不是孩子间的玩闹，而是那种近乎痴迷又怪异的行为。我记得当时我把自己锁在房间里，就为了躲开她，然后把这件事告诉父母。  现在，我搬到了她所在的国家，我们仍然会在家庭聚会上见面。有时候我会去她家过夜。问题是，她在我面前的行为仍然很奇怪。比如，我们坐在一起的时候，她会坐得特别近，几乎贴着我，还会用一种让我很不舒服的眼神看着我。我真的不知道该怎么办。  我想让她明白，我并不那样看待她，她的行为让我很困扰。但我不想伤害她，也不想让家里其他人感到尴尬。  我该如何划清界限，又不伤害她的感情呢
english_item: My cousin has a crush on me I’m a 19-year-old guy, and my cousin (she’s 18 now) has been acting in a way that’s made me really uncomfortable for years.  She and her family had a trip to my country when she was about 10 I think. Back then, she would constantly hug me, kiss me on the cheek, and call me her “boyfriend” right in front of our parents. It wasn’t just playful kid stuff either; it felt obsessive and weird . I remember locking myself in my room just to get away from her and tell my parents about it.  Now, I’ve moved to the country where she currently lives, and we still see each other during family events. Sometimes I stay over at her house. The thing is, she still acts weird around me. For example, when we’re sitting together, she’ll sit extremely close, like right up against me, and just glance at me in a way that makes me uncomfortable. I honestly don’t know how to handle this.  I want to make it clear to her that I don’t see her that way, and that her behavior bothers me. But I don’t want to hurt her or make things awkward with the rest of the family.  How do I set boundaries without hurting her feelings?

chinese_item: 他嘲笑我。  我以前喜欢过一个男生，就叫他乔什吧。去年我喜欢了他好几个月，后来慢慢地就放下了。但那段时间，他也对我有点意思，他夸我漂亮，跟我调情，帮我做作业，书包重的时候帮我提，还给我写了生日信（虽然我从来没告诉他我的生日），他还帮我挡过欺负我的小霸王。但慢慢地，这一切都渐渐淡了，我不再跟他说话，他也不再跟我说话了。暑假期间，我胖了10公斤，家里出了点事，加上我之前得过暴食症，所以体重反弹了。  暑假结束了，我不再喜欢他了，回到学校后，我不知道当初喜欢他的时候是不是忽略了他那些糟糕的品质，但我开始看清他的真面目了。他是个粗鲁无礼、令人作呕的家伙。我们俩的关系慢慢变得很尴尬，彼此都开始讨厌对方。今天，我旁边坐着一个女生，她是乔什的朋友，要知道他们俩都很受欢迎。她跟乔什开玩笑说“那是你女朋友”，我当然知道她是在开玩笑，所以就没理会，他们俩继续笑个不停。他们那种做法也很恶毒，我又没对他们俩有什么不礼貌的，真不知道他们为什么要这么做。总之，这件事成了压垮骆驼的最后一根稻草，我永远都不会喜欢他
english_item: ！,"He made fun of me I used to like those guy, I’ll call him Josh.I liked him for MONTHS last year, and I slowly got over him. But during the time I had a crush on him, he was kind of reciprocating it, he called me beautiful, he would flirt with me,he did all of my work for me,carried my bag when it was heavy and made me a birthday letter although I never told him when my birthday was, he defended me against a bully, but slowly it all just kind of fizzled out, I stopped talking to him and he stopped talking to me. During the summer, I gained 10kg, my family had issues and I was recovering from bulimia which led me to gain weight which I had previously lost.  Summer break finished, I didn’t like him anymore and I came back to school, idk if I had put aside all his trash qualities when I liked him but I started to see him for his true self. He was a rude and overall ew kind of guy. Slowly we both developed a mutual awkward relationship, we both started to dislike each other.Today, I was sitting next to this girl who is friends with Josh, mind you they’re both popular, and she told Josh as a joke “that’s your girl” obviously I understand she means it as a joke so I just ignored it and they kept going and laughing.The way they were doing it was so malicious as well, like I’m not rude to either of them so idk why they felt the need to do that, anyway that was the final straw and I’ll NEVER EVER even slightly like him!

chinese_item: 对抗式对话风格  我有个朋友（认识30年后，我实在无法忍受了），她有对抗式对话风格。她说的每句话她都反驳，非要对任何事情提出不同的事实、观点或假设。这种情况不只针对我，而是针对所有人。这让我很沮丧，我干脆不说话，只好听着。这让人精疲力竭，也让人很沮丧。问题是：她说自己是高敏感人群（HSP），我很好奇，因为这种对话风格完全无视对方的感受，否定他人的感受。这完全不是我印象中高敏感人群的特征。大家怎么看
english_item: Oppositional conversational style I had a friend (after 30 years I just couldn't any more), who had Oppositional Conversational Style. She contradicted everything I said. Just had to provide alternative facts or points of view or suppositions to EVERYTHING. This was not just with me, but everyone. It would shut me down and I quit talking, just listened. It was exhausting and depressing. Question: she says she's an HSP, and I'm curious about that because that conversational style completely ignores the other person's feelings, it invalidates others. Which is not a trait I associate with HSP at all. Thoughts?

chinese_item: 有些人善良得不适合这个世界。  有些人天真无邪，心地纯洁，在这个并不总是把善良视为珍宝的世界里，他们难以生存。当我观看《声之形》时，硝子深深地打动了我——她的纯真、她那份静谧的温暖，以及即使面对残酷也毫不动摇的善良。她从不反抗，从不发火。她只是默默地存在着——然而，这个世界却因此伤害了她。  我意识到……像她这样的人真实存在。他们或许不常被人注意到。他们或许会在被嘲笑、被利用或被忽视后，隐藏起自己的善良。但他们确实存在。有些是孩子，他们不明白为什么这个世界对他们如此残酷。有些是成年人，他们学会了沉默，学会了自我封闭，以免再次受到伤害。还有一些人……已经迷失了方向，因为没有人保护他们。  我深深地感受到保护像硝子这样的人的使命，就像我看到硝子的遭遇时一样。我知道还有很多人和我一样，渴望保护那些最纯真、最脆弱、最善良的人。如果你也有同感，请联系我。让我们聊聊。让我们一起寻找支持和保护那些最需要帮助的人的方法。  你是否遇到过善良到不属于这个世界的人？你相信现实生活中真的存在这样的人吗？我的私信是开放的。如果你对此有所感触，请分享出去
english_item: Some people are just too kind for this world There are people who are so innocent, so pure-hearted, that they struggle to exist in a world that doesn’t always treat RandomActsofKindness as something to be cherished. When I watched A Silent Voice, I was deeply moved by Shoko—her innocence, her quiet warmth, and her unwavering RandomActsofKindness even in the face of cruelty. She never fought back, never lashed out. She just was—and yet, the world hurt her for it.  And I realized… people like her exist in real life. They may not always be noticed. They might hide their RandomActsofKindness after being mocked, taken advantage of, or ignored. But they are here. Some are children who don’t understand why the world is unkind to them. Some are adults who have learned to stay silent, to shrink themselves so they won’t be hurt again. And some… have already been lost, because no one was there to protect them.  I feel deeply about protecting people like this, just as I felt when I saw Shoko’s struggles. I know there are others out there who share this feeling—the urge to protect the most innocent, the most vulnerable, the most kind-hearted among us. If you feel the same, let’s connect. Let’s talk. Let’s find ways to support and protect those who need it most.  Have you ever met someone who was too kind for this world? Do you believe people like this exist in real life? my DMs are open tho, And if this speaks to you, share it pls

chinese_item: 未经允许就故意拍摄他人照片或视频……  我经常在大学的 Yikyak 页面和 TikTok 上看到有人拍摄完全陌生的人。然后，当他们偶尔被指出时，他们会用一些完全不靠谱的借口为自己辩解，比如“这是公共场所，合法”……但即便如此，这仍然完全不合理。合法并不总是等同于道德。而且，抛开法律层面不谈，这种行为本身就很*没礼貌*。我也见过一些比较有分寸的例子，比如有人录制了一对陌生情侣的求婚过程，然后通过隔空投送发送给他们（没有发布到任何地方），之后立即从手机里删除。但可惜的是，这样的例子非常少见
english_item: 。,"People purposefully taking photos or videos of others without their permission... chinese_item: 那些因为思想龌龊而破坏谈话的人。  我大多数时候说话都不经过深思熟虑。我经常会说一些如果被不合时宜的人听到可能会产生误解的话。我们都是普通人，都会犯这种错误。让我恼火的是，当我跟朋友认真地想聊一些事情的时候，他们却满脑子龌龊的想法，比如“哇哦”或者说“哥们儿，别说了”、“你刚才说什么？”之类的，然后就喋喋不休地谈论我说了些“下流话”，明明他们只是想找个借口开个玩笑。这真的让我很生气，因为我这样说话意味着我真的想表达我的想法。如果别人说的话脱离语境会显得很荒谬或不恰当，我倒不介意用轻笑来表示一下，但如果他们故意转移话题，拿我快速说话时说错的话开玩笑，那就真的让人很生气也很恼火了。我只想谈谈我想谈的话题，但他们总是打断我，不让我回到正题，根本不让我说。（我跟他们说过一些具体的例子，比如他们只是思想龌龊，但他们不肯接受，非要说我说了些不恰当的话，明明我并没有那个意思。）就让我好好说说话，别因为每个人都会做的事就对我冷嘲热讽
english_item: 。,"People Who Ruin Conversations by Being Dirty Minded. I am someone who doesn't plan out exactly how im going to say something most of the time. What annoys me is when I'm telling my friends something I really want to talk about and they are dirty minded and go WOAH or make comments like keep that to yourself buddy and WHAT DID YOU JUST SAY? and then proceed to not shut up about how im saying dirty things when they are clearly avoiding the context to try and joke. Just let me speak without making fun of me profusely for something everyone does.

chinese_item: 我受够了孤独，我想摆脱这种状态……可是该怎么做呢？！  一开始我以为大家都讨厌我，但其实是因为我把自己封闭起来了。我妈也说我封闭，看起来很凶。我的数学老师也说我看起来“像个闭着眼睛的人”。  我和数学老师长谈了一次，因为我真的需要倾诉，毕竟我以前被霸凌过。那段经历给我留下了创伤和创伤后应激障碍，所以在新学校我一个朋友都没有。我试着和别人说话，但大多数人都不感兴趣……也许是因为我缺乏自信，或者说话方式不对？我只是想要一些朋友。  我一直在考虑转学，因为我的高中很小，所有女生都来自同一所学校，或者通过朋友的朋友认识的。所以没人认识我。我觉得自己和这里的氛围格格不入。我感觉自己不属于这里。但我会努力变得更开放一些，这样在接下来的两个月里，我就可以决定是否要转学了。  怎么办？我该怎么摆脱这种状态？我觉得自己被评判了，出于本能，我干脆不说话，只顾着玩手机。我不是不感兴趣，只是害怕说错话被欺负。其实我已经有点招人讨厌了。  请帮帮我，我想要朋友，一个人太孤独了。但我又没有勇气

chinese_item: 我该如何应对因无辜生命遭遇不幸而产生的极度悲伤和焦虑？  内容预警：死亡、动物死亡、战争、虐待  我感到有些不知所措，不知道该如何应对这种情况，想知道是否有人也有过类似的经历，或者有什么建议可以帮助我：  每次看到可爱或无辜的东西，我都会不由自主地想到，比如，它身处战火之中，或者遭遇了什么可怕的事情。我从小就是这样。  比如，如果我看到一只可爱的小动物，我就会不由自主地想“如果它惨死会怎样？”然后我的大脑就会不受控制地胡思乱想，各种极其血腥恐怖的画面会涌入我的脑海。或者，我会想到可爱的儿童用品（玩具、毛绒玩具等等）可能遭受虐待，或者身处战区。  我的大脑和身体似乎无法承受无辜生命与恐怖交织带来的悲伤。而它可能发生在任何东西上，这更让人感到恐惧。  我会避免购买那些被认为是可爱的物品，因为如果这些物品附近发生了什么不好的事情，我会感到无比的悲伤，甚至几乎恐慌。  也许我对美好的事物“被毁”或变成令人难过的东西有某种恐惧或创伤，我不确定。但如果能知道如何应对这些强烈的情绪波动，比如如何重新看待它们，那就太好了——它会瞬间扰乱我的情绪，彻底改变我的心情。  我不知道是什么原因造成的。有人有什么想法、经历或应对技巧吗
english_item: How do I handle feelings of immense grief and anxiety over bad things happening to something innocent? TW : Death, animal death, war, abuse   Feeling a bit lost with how to deal with this and wondering if anyone else experiences these feelings and or has any advice on how to deal with it the following:   Every time I see something cute or innocent, I have an intrusive thought of like, that thing in the middle of a war or like something terrible happening to the thing. I’ve been like this since I was a kid.   Like, if I see a cute baby animal, I automatically think “What if it dies a horrible death?” And then my brain spirals from there with super graphic, gruesome images popping into my head. Or - the idea of cute children’s items (toys, stuffed animals, etc.) being in the presence of abuse, or like, in a war zone.   It’s like my brain and body cannot handle the grief of innocence juxtaposed with horror. And the fact that it has the potential to happen to anything makes it even scarier.  I will avoid buying items that are considered cute because if something bad happens near said item, I feel this incredibly heavy grief and almost panic over it.   Maybe I have some kind of fear or trauma related to good things being “ruined” and turned into something upsetting, I’m not sure. But knowing how to handle these intense waves of emotion & like reframe them would be helpful - it can throw me off in an instant and change my whole mood.   I don’t know what causes this. Does anyone have any thoughts, experiences, or coping skills?

chinese_item: 如何才能真正让潜在的室友明白，我百分百不介意吃坚果？我对坚果严重过敏，只要碰到坚果，或者坚果以任何方式接触到我的皮肤，就会起荨麻疹。谢天谢地，目前还没有出现过敏性休克，但即使只吃一点点坚果，也会导致过敏性休克。我又要搬进宿舍了，我真的想让室友明白，我绝对不能容忍任何坚果。我知道宿舍是共享空间，需要做出一些妥协，但这对我来说是生死攸关的大事，所以我希望我的室友能认真对待。我之前的室友非常不配合，几乎在所有烹饪中都使用花生油，这迫使我甚至不敢把任何东西放进冰箱，如果想在厨房用东西，就必须戴手套。我的新宿舍有个类似约会软件的匹配系统，你需要自我介绍，然后和最合拍的人匹配。我应该怎么在个人简介里写我的过敏情况才能不显得无礼呢？我不想显得太强势，但毕竟这是生死攸关的大事。谢谢！
english_item: How to REALLY get the point across that I'm 100% but free to potential roommates? I have an extremely severe allergy to nuts, if I touch them or they make contact with my skin in any way I'll break out in hives, thankfully no anaphylaxis yet, but I do go into anaphylactic shock when I eat even the smallest amount of nuts. I'm moving into dorms again, and I REALLY want to get the point across that I won't tolerate any nuts whatsoever. I know dorming is a shared space and compromises have to be made, but this is a matter of life and death for me so I want to make sure my roomie takes it seriously. My last roommates were very uncooperative and used peanut oil to cook with virtually every mean, forcing me to not even put anything in the fridge and wear gloves if I wanted to use anything in the kitchen. My new dorm situation has a matching system kinda like a dating app, you sell yourself and match with who best aligns with yourself, what should I say about my allergies on this bio without being rude? I don't want to come across as a hard ass but again.. life and death. Thanks!!

THANKS

We can have friendly chats, but we're not friends, not enemies either. Just kinda do our own things. I bought something online and left my credit card out on my desk, in my room when I was done because I was in a rush to go to class. Didn't think much of it. The next day, I got a notification that the card was used to purchase a Hulu subscription, and I remembered that my roommate had been complaining a show she liked had been moved to Hulu, and she didn't have Hulu. chinese_item: “演讲前先喝杯水试试。”真是服了。  我问学校能不能单独跟老师汇报，而不是在全班同学面前汇报，结果被要求出示一份心理报告，里面包含了我的季节性情感障碍（SAD）诊断（我已经提供了）。学校却说这份报告无效，因为是两年前的，而且SAD“不是慢性病”，所以我“现在应该已经好了”。  我说我担心自己因为无法正常演讲而丢分，结果，说真的，他们居然让我“演讲前深呼吸”和“喝杯水让思绪平静下来”。他们还拿我正在接受心理治疗这件事来攻击我，说“哦，那你已经采取措施控制病情了，我们也无能为力了”。  学校大肆宣扬“学术诚信”，说如果我单独跟老师汇报，对其他同学不公平。  我能拿到心理医生写给学校的信，但这仍然让人非常沮丧，而且很麻烦。真讨厌他们不但没帮我，反而处处与我作对
english_item: Just try drinking a cup of water before your presentation’. Fml. Asked my school if I could present one on one to the teacher instead of in front of the class, had to show a psychology report including my SAD diagnosis to the school (which i provided them with) . Was told it was invalid because it was from 2 years ago and I could've 'gotten over it by now' since SAD is 'not a chronic condition'  Said I was concerned I would lose marks because of my inability to present, and, no joke, was told to try 'breathing before my presentation' and 'drinking a cup of water to slow down my thoughts'. They used the fact i was seeing a psychologist against me because 'oh, you're already taking measures to manage it then so there's nothing more we can do'.  The school made a huge fuss about 'academic integrity' and said it's not fair to the rest of the class if I present one-on-one to the teacher.   I am able to get the school a letter from my psychologist about it, but it's still frustrating as f and a bit of a hassle. Hate that they were working against me instead of trying to help.

chinese_item: 那些因为意见不合就进行身材羞辱的人我最近发了一篇有点争议的帖子。有些人查看了我的发帖记录，并留言对我进行身材羞辱。他们居然会这么做，这已经够让人恼火的了，尤其是我在帖子中已经明确说明自己刚生完孩子。但当我查看这些人的发帖和评论记录时，我惊讶地发现他们当中很多人也是女性，而且都是母亲。说实话，我原本以为他们大多是男性。看到女性和母亲们对其他女性和母亲进行身材羞辱，真是令人愤怒。她们最应该感同身受，也最应该想要制止这种行为，而不是助长它。

chinese_item: 面对不断传来的不公消息，你们是如何控制自己的情绪的？  每次听到有人因为限制性的生育法律而丧命，而这种死亡本可避免，我都会像失去亲人一样悲痛欲绝。每次听到这样的消息，我都会忍不住流泪。虽然我住在保守州，但我有足够的资源获得所需的帮助。想到如果我出生在不同的环境下，我也可能面临同样的命运——尤其是作为一名黑人女性——这让我心碎。  我正在尽我所能，从我力所能及的范围内做些事情——向生育组织捐款，参与社会活动。但我想要看到改变*立刻*发生。我无法忍受再听到任何一起本可避免的死亡。你们是如何应对的？你们是如何控制自己的情绪的？  我知道我们只能专注于自己能够掌控的事情，但或许是因为我不相信来世，所以我无法承受那些弱势群体宝贵生命的逝去
english_item: How do you regulate your emotions with constant news of injustice? Everytime I hear news of someone dying a preventable death due to restrictive reproductive laws I mourn as if I'm mourning a sibling. It genuinely brings me to tears each and everytime. Although I live in a red state I have the resources to get the help I need, so to know that if I was born under different circumstances that could easily be me - especially as a Black woman - breaks my heart.   I am doing what I can from my corner of the world - donating to reproductive orgs, participating in social organizations. But I want to see change *now*. I cannot bear to hear even one more preventable death. How do y'all deal? How do y'all regulate your nervous systems?  I know we can only focus on what we have control over but perhaps because I don't believe in an afterlife, I cannot handle precious lives of vulnerable people being lost.

chinese_item: 葬礼焦虑  大家好！  我患有广泛性焦虑症，大概有15年了。当然，过去几年情况有所好转，但我仍然会焦虑（谁不是呢，呵呵）。  我很快就要去参加一个葬礼了，我非常害怕，感觉胸口剧痛、胃酸过多、背痛、恶心（当然，每个人的症状都不一样，但这些是我焦虑严重时的症状~~）。  逝者是我几年前的朋友；她晚年不太喜欢我（找不到更合适的词来形容），但我仍然对她有美好的回忆。我非常想去参加她的葬礼，支持她的家人和爱人，但有一个很大的问题。  我觉得那个曾经性骚扰我的男人也会去。而且我觉得他那个曾经折磨我的朋友也会去。我觉得以前欺负过我的人（恐同、辱骂、霸凌等等）也会去。  我好害怕，我再也不想见到他们，但我又很想去，想给那些需要支持的人一些安慰。  我会和我的伴侣以及他们的父母，还有我的另一个朋友和她的伴侣一起去，但我对异国他乡的焦虑感瞬间飙升。  有人能给我一些建议吗？我会非常感激，也许这篇帖子也能帮到其他有类似经历的人。  TLDR：我必须去参加一个葬礼，我觉得我的伴侣和其他以前欺负过我的人都会去，我焦虑得不行
english_item: Funeral-Related Anxiety Hi all!   I suffer with generalised anxiety disorder and have for the past 15 years or so. It’s of course gotten better over the past few years but I still get anxious (don’t we all, heheh)..  So I’ll be attending a funeral soon and I’m freaking out, like full chest pain, stomach acid, back pain, nausea anxiety (of course everyone’s symptoms are different, but these are mine when things get bad~~).  The person was a friend from a couple of years back; she didn’t particularly like me towards the end (lack of a better phrase) but I still have good memories of her. I want so badly to be there for her and to support her family / loved ones, but there’s one big issue.  I think the man who SA’d me is going to be there. And I think his friend who tormented me is going to be there. And I think other people who have tormented me in the past (homophobia, name calling, bullying, etc.) are going to be there.   I’m terrified, I don’t ever want to face them again, but I want so badly to be there as a support for those who need it.   I’ll be attending with my partner and their parents, and my other friend and her partner, but my anxiety about foreign places has just hiked up a million decibels.  Would anyone have any advice? I would really appreciate it and maybe this post can help others in a similar situation <3  TLDR: I have to attend a funeral and I think my SA’er and other people who have tormented me in the past are going to be there, major anxiety is kicking in!

chinese_item: 新室友保释出狱，而且有精神分裂症的迹象？新室友搬进了一栋四居室的房子，房东把房间单独出租。他性格有点古怪，但还算友好。极其邋遢。第二天晚上，他就在卫生间里抽烟喝酒，到处乱扔烟头和易拉罐。我跟他说了，他道了歉，但还是继续。最近几个早上，我都是早上六点左右被他扯着嗓子大喊大叫吵醒。我以为他在打电话，但他会冲出房子，我从窗户看到他自言自语，但其实根本没人理会。查看邮件时，我看到了当地警方保释部门的信，楼下的邻居还给我发了一篇文章，详细介绍了他去年被捕的情况（涉嫌袭击）。我把尖叫的视频发给了房东，但他好像没什么反应。一个女人五天前刚搬进来，现在就已经开始找新房子了。我还有几周就搬走了。尽量不去理会室友，但可惜的是，这里只有我会说出我的顾虑。我猜我就是来发泄一下的，哈哈。过去一个月压力很大，因为要突然搬家，不过好消息是下一个住处是一室一厅。虽然更贵了，但安宁是无价的。
english_item: New roommate is out on bail + showing signs of schizophrenia? New guy moved our a 4 bedroom house, landlord rents rooms individually. Awkward but friendly guy. INCREDIBLY messy. Second night he was smoking cigarettes and drinking alcohol in the bathroom, leaves butts and cans all over, i brought it up to him, he apologized and continued. The past few mornings, im talking 6am, ive woken up to him screaming at the top of his lungs. I assumed he was on the phone, however he'd storm out of the house and id see from the windows hes talking to absolutely no one. Upon checking mail, i saw a letter from the local police bail services, and a lower unit neighbor sent me an article detailing his arrest last year (assault charges). Ive sent videos of the screaming to the landlord, but dude doesnt do much. A woman just moved in 5 days ago and is already looking for a new place. I have a few weeks left here until im out. Trying not to engage, but unfortunately im the only roommate here that will vocalize my concerns.  Guess im just here to vent lol. Been a stressful past month having to suddnely move, but the bright side is this next place will be a one-bedroom. More expensive, yes, but cant put a price on peace.

chinese_item: 有人会说我聪明，但事实并非如此，实际上我学习起来很吃力吗？我不知道在这里絮絮叨叨这些是否合适。很多人觉得我很聪明，因为我能提出一些关于直觉的深刻问题，而且我能从不同的角度解读事物，有些解读看似巧妙，但其实是错的。我知道这是智力的一种表现，但问题是，真正聪明的人通常能够分辨出哪种解读是正确的，或者最终能够自己解答大部分的深刻问题。正是这种能力，让人们无法判断我是否想得太多。然而，我无法解答自己的问题，尤其是在数学、经济学等逻辑性很强的学科上。我经常会遇到这样的问题：一个问题会引出另一个问题，最终导致我花很长时间才能理解背后的直觉，这影响了我的成绩和学习。但一旦我真正理解了某些东西，我的理解深度就远超大多数人。百分比和比率就是一个简单的例子。很多人都知道怎么算，也知道它们在纸面上的含义，但如果我问他们背后的数学原理是如何体现纸面上的解释的，很多人却会出乎意料地感到困惑。平均值之类的概念也是如此，人们会说“哦，这好像有点道理”，但他们其实并不真正理解它的含义，只是觉得“感觉对了”。这就是我的问题所在……如果我无法理解数学是如何反映直觉/解释的，那么我的大脑就根本无法理解这个概念。我的讲师们觉得我问的问题很聪明，但他们看到我的成绩后却很惊讶。有人和我一样吗？
english_item: DAE get called smart, when this is far from the truth and you actually struggle to learn ? Idk if this is the right place to ramble abt this. But many people think im smart because I ask insightful questions regarding intuition and I can interpret things in different ways, some of those interpretations might seem clever but are still wrong. Now I know this is a trait of intelligence, but the problem is but people who are truly intelligent can usually filter out which interpretation is right or can end up answering most of their own insightful questions. And this ability is what distinguishes people from saying whether im overthinking something or not. I however cannot answer my own questions especially with logical subjects like maths econ etc, I have this problem where one question leads to another and I end up taking forever to understand the intuition behind something which hinders my grades and learning. But when I do learn something I understand it to a deep level better than most people. A simple example of this is percentages and ratios, a lot of people know how to do them and know what they mean on paper but if I asked them how the math behind it actually represents what the interpretation is on paper, many people surprisingly struggle. Same with concepts like average, like people say oh yeah that kinda makes sense but they actually have no idea what its truly saying, it just feels right. Thats the problem for me...... if I cant understand say how the maths reflects the intuition/interpretation then my brain wont understand the concept at all. My lecturers think im smart with the questions I ask them, but then they see my grades and are surprised, is anyone else like this?

chinese_item: 我本想做好事，结果却适得其反，请帮帮我。  自从疫情以来，我现实生活中几乎没有朋友，只有几个网友，但我非常珍惜朋友。我在Instagram上有一个朋友，感觉和他很亲近，所以想为他做点什么。我决定发帖提到他，内容是“感谢这位好朋友，感觉真好！”。我以前从没这么做过，我们认识也有一段时间了，感觉一直不错。我发帖的时候很紧张，但又觉得这会是个惊喜。  结果，他的伴侣看到了，还问了他。然后他问我为什么，说这让他很尴尬，很奇怪，我们只是朋友而已。接着他又问我，为什么我感觉好不好就应该归他管——当时我直接就哭了。我解释说我只是想表达一下好意，我知道我们只是朋友，但我真的很在乎我的朋友。  所以，我做了件超出自己舒适区的事，本来以为对别人会很好，结果却完全搞砸了，感觉自己搞砸了一段友谊，而且显得很奇怪。但我想，他可能不像我那么在乎我，或者说我投入的感情比他多得多。我真的不明白，为什么会觉得奇怪而不是很好。希望事情能好转，但和SA做这件事已经够难了，现在又出了这档子事
english_item: Tried to do something nice and it backfired, please help Since the pandemic I haven't had any friends IRL, only a few online and I care about friends deeply. I have a friend in IG that I felt close to and felt like doing something nice for. I decided to post and mention them with something reading Feeling good thanks to a great guy! I'd never done it before and we've known each other for a while and things felt good. I was so nervous to post it but I thought it would be a nice surprise.  Well turns out their partner saw it and apparently questioned him about it. He then asked me why and told me it put him in an odd position, that it was weird and that we are just friends. Then asked why is he supposed to be the source of me feeling good or not - at that point I was just in tears. I've explained that I just did it to be nice and I know we're just friends but that I'm someone who really cares about the friends I do have.  So something I did out of my comfort zone that I thought would be really nice for someone else has gone completely wrong and I feel like I've fucked a friendship and come across as weird. But I guess he doesn't care about me as much as I do about him, or I'm much more invested than him I don't really get how it was weird rather than nice. Hopefully things work out but doing it with SA was hard enough and now this.

chinese_item: 工作真的太让人焦虑了  28岁，女。我最近工作压力很大，感觉精疲力竭。更糟糕的是，同事们还逼我当着大家的面发言，我觉得他们觉得我这么害怕很好笑。说实话，我都不好意思承认。我真的受不了这种“玩笑”。我跟一位资深同事说了这件事，他跟那个嘲笑我的同事谈过了，结果他现在拒绝跟我说话……我现在感觉压力好大，也觉得很别扭。真希望大家都能更理解我
english_item: Work is so triggering 28F I've been so burnt out from work. It doesn't help that my coworkers pressure me to talk in front of the group, I think they think it's funny that I'm so scared of it. It's embarrassing for me to admit it. I can't cope with the banter. I told a senior and he spoke with the main coworker who was making fun of me, but now he refuses to talk to me..
