Residents were forced to seek shelter from the high water on rooftops.
Ahead of Typhoon Kalmaegi, the Office of Civil Defence said "close to 150,000" people had been evacuated.

chinese_item:
持械团伙在年轻家庭面前袭击一名男子
这起袭击事件发生在布罗克本的卡尔德罗斯大道上。四名持械男子将一名37岁的男子从家中拖出，在其伴侣和孩子面前实施了警方称之为“令人发指”的攻击。
受害者在西洛锡安布罗克本卡尔德罗斯大道外的住所附近遭到袭击，导致严重受伤。事件发生在周一晚上约19:30。
受害者的伴侣和孩子没有受伤，但警方表示他们被这一事件吓得“非常害怕”。
袭击者乘坐一辆白色或银色面包车逃离现场，并朝卡尔德罗斯路方向驶去。
目前尚未公布这些男子的外貌描述。
english_item:
Armed gang attack man in front of young familyThe attack took place in Cardross Avenue in BroxburnFour armed men carried out what police described as an "abhorrent" attack on a 37-year-old man in front of his partner and child after dragging him from his home.
The victim suffered serious injuries in the assault outside his house in Cardross Avenue in Broxburn, West Lothian, at about 19:30 on Monday.
His partner and child were not injured but police said they were left "very frightened" by what happened.
The attackers fled in a white or silver van and drove off towards Cardross Road.
No description of the men has so far been released.

chinese_item: 巴西的亚马逊雨林面临新的砍伐威胁，因为有人试图推翻一项长期保护该地区的禁令。这个团体支持这项正式名称为“亚马逊大豆禁令”的规定，因为他们认为这有助于确保英国的大豆供应链不涉及森林砍伐。“我们的州有很大的发展空间，而大豆禁令阻碍了这一发展，”万德莱·阿泰德斯告诉BBC。“我不能种植大豆，但我可以用同一片土地种植玉米、水稻、棉花或其他作物。”在禁令实施之前，为了扩大大豆种植和养牛业而进行的森林清理是导致亚马逊地区森林砍伐的主要原因。
english_item:
Brazil's Amazon rainforest at risk as key protection under threat8 days ago Share Save Justin Rowlatt , Climate editor and Jessica Cruz , South America producer Share SaveBBC / Tony JolliffeThe Amazon rainforest could face a renewed surge of deforestation as efforts grow to overturn a long-standing ban that has protected it.
The group supports the ban, which is known officially as the Amazon Soy Moratorium, because they argue it helps ensure UK soy supply chains remain free from deforestation.
"Our state has lots of room to grow and the soy moratorium is working against this development," Vanderlei Ataídes told the BBC.
"I can't plant soya beans, but I can use the same land to plant corn, rice, cotton or other crops.
Before the moratorium, forest clearance for soya expansion and the growth of cattle ranching were the main drivers of Amazonian deforestation.

chinese_item:
“阴霾散去”：亨廷顿病治疗取得突破2025年10月22日 分享 保存 克里斯·克拉多克 来自泽西岛的社区记者 分享 保存BBC 劳拉·道格拉斯在听到关于新治疗方法的消息时，情绪激动得难以自抑。32岁的劳拉·道格拉斯来自泽西岛，她的母亲、姨妈和祖母都死于亨廷顿病。她形容这种遗传病治疗上的突破是“完全令人震撼的”。此前，由于没有治疗方法，劳拉决定不进行该疾病的基因检测，她说现在感觉“未来头顶上的一大块乌云已经消散了”。道格拉斯女士表示，这一突破性消息给了她希望。

阿什利·德尔加多因为亨廷顿病失去了独立生活的能力。德尔加多女士从她的父亲安东尼奥·德尔加多那里遗传了亨廷顿病，后者后来也因该疾病去世。阿什利·德尔加多小时候就目睹了亨廷顿病对她父亲的影响。根据英国国家医疗服务体系（NHS）的数据，如果父母中有一方携带亨廷顿病基因，子女有50%的概率会遗传该病。
english_item:
'Cloud lifted' after Huntington's breakthrough22 October 2025 Share Save Chris Craddock Jersey communities reporter Share SaveBBC Laura Douglas was overcome with emotion when she heard the news about the new treatmentA woman whose mother, aunt and grandmother died from Huntington's disease has described a breakthrough in treating the genetic condition as "totally overwhelming".
Laura Douglas, 32, from Jersey, said a "big cloud over my future" had lifted after previously deciding not to be tested for the disease as there was no treatment.
Ms Douglas said the news of the breakthrough had given her hope.
Ashley Delgado has lost her independence because of Huntington's diseaseMs Delgado inherited Huntington's from her father, Antonio Delgado, who later died from the disease.
Ashley Delgado Ms Delgado saw how Huntington's disease affected her dad when she was a childPeople have a 50/50 chance of inheriting Huntington's from a carrying parent, according to the NHS.

chinese_item:
连续几个周末都举行了抗议活动，预计难民将抵达克劳伯勒一事引起了公众极大关注。
政府有法定责任为那些否则会流离失所的寻求庇护者提供住宿。
寻求庇护者指的是因迫害、战争或暴力而离开自己国家的人。
国民保健署也已确认，所有安置在军营中的寻求庇护者将在该县注册全科医生。
拉特先生表示，在训练营安置寻求庇护者的成本与将他们安置在酒店大致相同。
english_item:
With protests held on successive weekends, the anticipated arrival of asylum seekers in Crowborough is of huge public interest.
The government has a statutory obligation to provide accommodation for asylum seekers who would otherwise be destitute.
Asylum seekers refer to those who leave their country due to persecution, war, or violence.
The NHS has also confirmed all asylum seekers placed in the army camp will be registered with GPs in the county.
Mr Larter has said housing asylum seekers at the training camp would cost roughly the same as accommodating them in hotels.

chinese_item: 由于教室发霉，特殊需求学校的学生被迫在家学习。三天前分享保存罗比·梅里迪斯 BBC新闻北爱尔兰教育与艺术记者 分享保存温迪·加拉格尔表示，即使在教室被宣布安全时，她也一直担心安德鲁待在里面的问题。位于伦敦德里郡的一所特殊学校有四间移动教室再次因漏水、真菌生长和霉变等问题而关闭。因此，科尔雷恩的桑德尔福德特殊学校的30多名学生不得不留在家中远程学习。教育局（EA）承认这对家庭和学校来说是一段非常不安定的时期，“对此我们深感遗憾”。此次沟通与之前告知学校教室可以安全使用的消息相矛盾。“脆弱的孩子们及其心烦意乱的家长受到了严重的辜负。”伯罗斯告诉BBC新闻北爱尔兰分部。
english_item:
Special needs school pupils at home due to mouldy classrooms3 days ago Share Save Robbie Meredith Education and arts correspondent, BBC News NI Share SaveWendy Gallagher Wendy Gallagher said she had worried about Andrew being in the classrooms even when they were declared safeFour mobile classrooms at a County Londonderry special school have again been closed due to problems including leaks, fungal growth and mould.
As a result, more than 30 pupils at Sandelford Special School in Coleraine face having to stay at home and learn remotely.
The Education Authority (EA) admitted it had been "a very unsettling time for families and the school, which we deeply regret".
"This communication is contrary to previous communication received during which the school was informed the classrooms were safe for use.
"Vulnerable children and their upset parents have been badly let down," Burrows told BBC News NI.

chinese_item:
地球观察欧洲是一个帮助居民在其社区内研究自然的慈善机构，该机构与志愿者合作，在50个地点测试是否存在金属。
北北安普敦郡议会最近同意成立一个工作组，以调查镇上的公共卫生和污染问题。
凯特·布拉德布鲁克/英国广播公司报道，志愿者们在周六聚集在一起，对这50个地点之一进行了测试。马洪女士的儿子出生时患有马蹄足，她说她参与进来是因为想排除水道中的污染物。
“这是为了真正检测到底发生了什么，以及是否有什么我们应该担心的事情。”
“我们认为这些物质仍然存在于地下，并且被告知它们可能会渗入科尔比居民的水道中。”
english_item:
Earthwatch Europe, a charity that helps residents investigate nature in their communities, worked with volunteers to test if metal was present across 50 sites.
North Northamptonshire Council had recently agreed to set up a working group to examine public health and contamination issues in the town.
Kate Bradbrook/BBC Volunteers gathered on Saturday to test one of the 50 sitesMs Mahon, whose son was born with clubfoot, said she got involved as she wanted to rule out contaminants in the waterways.
"It is to really test what is going on and if there is anything we should be concerned about."
"We feel it is still in the ground and have been told it can leach into the waterways for the people of Corby.

chinese_item:
英国寻求通过更严格的测试来遏制AI生成的儿童性虐待图像

2025年11月12日 分享 保存 Liv McMahon 科技记者 分享 保存Getty Images

英国政府将允许科技公司和儿童安全慈善机构主动测试人工智能工具，以确保这些工具无法生成儿童性虐待图像。周三宣布的一项对《犯罪与警务法案》的修正案将授权“授权测试者”在模型发布前评估其生成非法儿童性虐待材料（CSAM）的能力。

NSPCC在线儿童安全政策经理Rani Govender对这些措施表示欢迎，认为这将鼓励企业对其模型和儿童安全承担更多责任并接受审查。“政府必须确保AI开发者有强制性的义务使用这一条款，使防止儿童性虐待成为产品设计中不可或缺的一部分。”

“确保儿童安全”
english_item:
UK seeking to curb AI child sex abuse imagery with tougher testing12 November 2025 Share Save Liv McMahon Technology reporter Share SaveGetty ImagesThe UK government will allow tech firms and child safety charities to proactively test artificial intelligence tools to make sure they cannot create child sexual abuse imagery.
An amendment to the Crime and Policing Bill announced on Wednesday would enable "authorised testers" to assess models for their ability to generate illegal child sexual abuse material (CSAM) prior to their release.
Rani Govender, policy manager for child safety online at the NSPCC, welcomed the measures for encouraging firms to have more accountability and scrutiny over their models and child safety.
"Government must ensure that there is a mandatory duty for AI developers to use this provision so that safeguarding against child sexual abuse is an essential part of product design."
'Ensuring child safety'

chinese_item:
男子在学生公寓被刺身亡，嫌犯面临谋杀指控
本杰明·卡塔巴纳将于周一出庭诺里奇地方法院
一名男子在一处为学生和专业人士提供的住宿楼内死亡后，另一名男子被控谋杀。
周六凌晨5点20分左右，紧急服务部门接到报告称，在诺里奇索默利顿街的索默利顿大厦有一名男子被刺伤。
尸检结果显示，死因是胸部刺伤。
警方表示，来自索默利顿街的刚果籍男子本杰明·卡塔巴纳已被控谋杀。
他已被还押候审，并将于周一出庭诺里奇地方法院。
english_item:
Murder charge after man stabbed at student flatsBenjamin Katabana will appear at Norwich Magistrates' Court on MondayA man has been charged with murder after a person died at an accommodation block for students and professionals.
Emergency services were called to reports of a man with a stab wound at Somerleyton House in Somerleyton Street, Norwich, at about 05:20 GMT on Saturday.
A post-mortem examination indicated the cause of death was a stab wound to the chest.
Benjamin Katabana, of Somerleyton Street, who police said was a Congolese national, has been charged with murder.
He has been remanded in custody to appear before Norwich Magistrates' Court on Monday.

chinese_item:
专家警告称，NHS需要制定更好的减肥注射计划。他们提醒说，这些注射只是应该提供的一系列非污名化护理的一部分。
顶级专家警告说，迫切需要进行审查，以确保英格兰的人们能够在NHS获得如Wegovy和Mounjaro这样的减肥注射治疗。
他们表示，政府必须解决NHS肥胖服务中的一些根本问题——长期资金不足、人员配置挑战以及医疗资源获取不平等。
肥胖健康联盟希望看到肥胖治疗（包括减肥注射）能够得到公平的获取机会。
肥胖健康联盟主任凯瑟琳·詹纳表示，减肥注射是有效的，但这并不是全部。
english_item:
NHS needs better plan around weight loss jabs, warn expertsThey warn the injections are only part of what should be a wider package of non-stigmatising care.
An urgent review is needed to make sure people in England can get weight loss jabs such as Wegovy and Mounjaro on the NHS, top experts warn.
They say the government must fix some fundamentals issues in NHS obesity services - chronic underfunding, workforce challenges and unequal access to care.
The OHA wants to see equitable access for obesity treatments, including weight loss injections.
Katharine Jenner, director of the OHA, said the weight loss jabs were effective, but that was not the whole picture.

chinese_item:
这一举措正值威斯敏斯特的议员们正在审议一项法案，该法案将在英格兰和威尔士合法化协助死亡。
这项法案与马恩岛的立法有一些相似之处——人们必须是患有绝症、年满18岁并且在全科医生处注册。
米莉·布伦金索普-弗伦奇多年来一直在为协助死亡立法而奔走呼吁，但她自己的儿子詹姆斯却因癌症去世。然而，也有一些充满激情的支持者多年来一直在为此立法而奋斗。
她儿子的离世非常艰难且痛苦，这坚定了她对为绝症患者提供协助死亡的支持。“任何一个理智的人都不会反对协助死亡，如果他们像我一样不得不坐在那里看着自己的儿子死去的话。”
“我真心希望当时有协助死亡的选择，我真的这么想，因为我的儿子会选择这种方式。”
english_item:
The move comes as MPs in Westminster are scrutinising a bill that would legalise assisted dying in England and Wales.
It has some similarities to the Isle of Man legislation - people must be terminally ill, over 18 and registered with a GP.
Millie Blenkinsop-French has campaigned for assisted dying legislation for years, but saw her own son James die from cancerHowever, there have been some passionate campaigners who have spent years fighting for this legislation.
It was a very difficult, painful death that cemented her belief in assisted dying for the terminally ill. "Nobody in their right mind would be against assisted dying if they had to sit, like I did, and watch my son die.
"I wish with all my heart and soul that assisted dying had been in then, I really do, because he would have opted for it.

chinese_item:
但是在音乐节开始前两天，经过数月的追查后，塞布说他联系了格拉斯顿伯里音乐节主办方，确认了招待通行证的分配情况。
2023年，迈尔斯故技重施，向大约50人出售格拉斯顿伯里音乐节门票，但最终未能交付。
他们两人都是被迈尔斯·哈特承诺会提供门票的。
实际上，萨默塞特议员并没有特别途径可以获得格拉斯顿伯里音乐节的门票，苏珊娜·哈特也并未参与迈尔斯的票务计划。
格拉斯顿伯里音乐节的门票需求巨大，通常在几分钟内就会售罄。总计，凯和星光公司的客户为了从迈尔斯·哈特那里获得格拉斯顿伯里音乐节门票，共花费了近100万英镑。
english_item:
But two days before the festival, after chasing Miles for months, Seb says he contacted Glastonbury Festival to confirm the allocation of hospitality passes.
In 2023, Miles was at it again, selling Glastonbury tickets to about 50 people and failing to deliver.
Both of them had, in turn, been promised the tickets by Miles Hart.
In fact, Somerset councillors have no special access to Glastonbury Festival and Susannah Hart had no involvement in Miles's ticket schemes.
Getty Images Glastonbury tickets are in huge demand and usually sell out within minutesIn total, customers of Kai and Star Gaze spent almost ￡1m on Glastonbury tickets to be supplied by Miles Hart.

chinese_item:
BBC调查揭露非法牙齿美白行业 2025年10月29日 分享 保存 丹尼尔·奥多诺霍 & 劳拉·奥尼尔 BBC西北部调查 分享 保存Getty
BBC的一项调查发现，一些可能灼伤牙龈、损坏牙齿的非法牙齿美白治疗正在停车场和居民家门口进行。
有些凝胶含有超过非处方产品法定限值500倍以上的漂白剂，在社交媒体上公然销售。
在英国，使用含氢过氧化物浓度超过0.1%的牙齿美白产品进行的治疗只能由注册于英国牙科总会（GDC）的牙医和其他专业人士执行。
“等你一切准备好了，我可以把你介绍给我的牙科制造商，让你自己品牌化牙齿美白产品。”
一位政府发言人敦促公众，如果对所在地区销售的牙齿美白产品有疑虑，应“联系公民咨询局或当地贸易标准部门”。
english_item:
Illegal teeth-whitening industry exposed by BBC29 October 2025 Share Save Daniel O'Donoghue & Laura O'Neill BBC North West Investigations Share SaveGettyIllegal teeth-whitening treatments that can burn gums and destroy teeth are being handed over in car parks and on doorsteps, a BBC investigation has found.
Some gels, containing more than 500 times the legal limit of bleaching agent for over-the-counter products, are sold blatantly on social media.
In the UK, treatments using teeth-whitening products containing more than 0.1% hydrogen peroxide can only be carried out by dentists and other professionals registered with the General Dental Council (GDC).
"Once you're all settled, I can then pass you over to my dental manufacturer to brand your own teeth-whitening products.
A government spokesman urged the public to "contact Citizens Advice or their local authority Trading Standards department" if they had concerns over teeth-whitening products for sale in their area.

chinese_item:
一家所谓的“裸体化”网站因未实施年龄验证措施而被罚款5.5万英镑。根据网络安全部门的规定，这类措施是必须的。
英国通信管理局Ofcom对提供AI工具的Itai Tech Ltd进行了调查，这些工具允许用户编辑图片，似乎可以去除某人的衣物。
周四，Ofcom表示已因该公司未能进行年龄验证对其处以5万英镑的罚款，并因其未回应信息请求额外加罚5千英镑。
BBC新闻已联系Itai Tech Ltd寻求评论。
Ofcom称，在其调查开始后不久，该公司决定让其网站对英国用户不可用，这一因素已在考虑在内。
english_item:
Deepfake 'nudify' site fined ￡55,000 over lack of age checks4 days ago Share Save Liv McMahon Technology reporter Share SaveGetty ImagesThe operator of a so-called "nudification" site has been fined for failing to put in age verification measures, which are required under online safety laws.
The regulator Ofcom investigated Itai Tech Ltd, which provides AI tools allowing users to edit images to seemingly remove someone's clothing.
On Thursday, Ofcom said it had fined the company ￡50,000 for its age check failings, plus an additional ￡5,000 for not responding to its information requests.
BBC News has contacted Itai Tech Ltd for comment.
Ofcom said its fine accounted for the company's decision to make its site unavailable to UK users, which it said occurred shortly after the investigation started in May.

chinese_item:
海滩污染导致宠物狗死亡，主人发出警告

6天前 分享 保存 分享 保存
艾米丽·布拉德利-肖的狗狗珀西在食用了海灵岛海滩上一种有柴油味的蜡状物质后一天内就被安乐死了。
一位女士敦促养狗人士提高警惕，因为她的宠物在食用了海滩上的一种“有柴油味的物质”后一天内就被实施了安乐死。
11月11日，艾米丽·布拉德利-肖带着珀西在海灵岛海滩散步时，它吃下了一种淡色的蜡状物质。
经过检测发现该物质已经进入它的肺部，并且第二天无法恢复，“我们在家人陪伴下为它进行了安乐死”。
哈文特区议会表示，他们通过环境署得知费里角地区存在这种物质。
“我总是开玩笑说我是他的情感支持人类，但实际上他是我的情感支持动物。”
english_item:
Beach pollution blamed for dog's death by owner6 days ago Share Save Share SaveEmily Bradley-Shaw Percy had to be put to sleep a day after eating the waxy, diesel-smelling substance on Hayling Island beach near the Ferry BoatA woman has urged dog owners to be on their guard after her pet had to be put to sleep a day after eating a "diesel-smelling substance" on a beach.
Emily Bradley-Shaw was walking Percy on Hayling Island beach on 11 November when he ate a pale, waxy substance.
After tests revealed the substance was in his lungs and he would not recover the following day "he was euthanised with our family present".
Havant Borough Council said it was alerted to the substance at Ferry Point by the Environment Agency.
I always joked I was his emotional support human but really he was my emotional support animal.

chinese_item:
两名男子因18岁青年死亡被控谋杀。亚当·内文在西洛锡安布罗克斯本的一起骚乱后去世，两名男子因此被控谋杀并出庭受审。
警方于周五凌晨约1点15分接到报警，前往麦肯大道的一处房产。亚当·内文随后在周日于医院去世。
来自西洛锡安的25岁的康纳·詹金森和来自阿姆代尔的28岁的谢恩·詹金森周一在爱丁堡治安法院出庭。
两人都被控谋杀，并被还押候审。
english_item:
Two charged with murder after death of 18-year-oldTwo men have been charged following Adam Nevin's deathTwo men have appeared in court charged with murder after an 18-year-old man died following a disturbance in Broxburn, West Lothian.
Adam Nevin died in hospital on Sunday after police were called to a property on McCann Avenue at about 01:15 on Friday.
Connor Jenkinson, 25, from West Lothian and Shane Jenkinson, 28, from Armadale, appeared at Edinburgh Sheriff Court on Monday.
They were both charged with murder and were remanded in custody.

chinese_item:
男子在虐待案撤销数周后杀害妻子
2025年5月6日 分享 保存 分享 保存
苏格兰警方称，维罗尼卡·钦扬加在布克斯本的自家后花园中向丈夫求饶时被杀害。
这名47岁的国民医疗服务体系工作人员在之前的家庭虐待案件撤销后仅几周就谋杀了妻子，并被判至少18年监禁。
去年6月9日，卡西凯·钦扬加在西洛锡安布克斯本的家中后花园杀害了42岁的维罗尼卡·钦扬加。当时她正在向他求饶。
这位42岁的母亲曾预测钦扬加可能不会原谅她之前向警方举报他的行为。
维罗尼卡（原名奇茨德佐）参与了一个帮助国际医护人员适应新国家的项目。
他还补充说：“尽管有些事件被报告，但由于维罗尼卡·钦扬加显然不愿意出庭，刑事诉讼并未完成。”
english_item:
Man killed wife weeks after abuse case collapsed6 May 2025 Share Save Share SavePolice Scotland Veronica Chinyanga begged for her life as her husband turned on her in their back garden in BroxburnAn NHS worker who murdered his wife just weeks after an earlier domestic abuse case collapsed has been jailed for a minimum of 18 years.
Kasikai Chinyanga, 47, killed Veronica Chinyanga as she begged for her life in the back garden of their home in Broxburn, West Lothian, on 9 June last year.
The 42 year-old mother had predicted Chinyanga may not forgive her for previously reporting him to police.
His wife - born Chitsidzo, but known as Veronica - was involved in a project to help international health workers adapt to a new country.
He added: "Despite some being reported, criminal proceedings were not concluded due to an apparent reluctance on the part of Veronica Chinyanga to attend court.

chinese_item:
谁杀害了逃离俄罗斯寻求自由生活的年轻车臣女子？
有人看到两个人离开了她被发现的那栋楼，其中包括艾莎特在去世前不久结识的一名女子。
艾莎特的朋友说她是一个非常开朗的人，渴望与人建立联系。多位知名批评车臣当局的人士在国外遇害。
但艾莎特·拜穆拉多娃是已知的第一位在逃离俄罗斯后不久就死于可疑情况的车臣女性。
瓦莱里·哈什/法新社通过盖蒂图片社报道，那些在欧洲寻求庇护的车臣女性通常避免与流亡社群交流。
english_item:
Who killed young Chechen woman who fled Russia for a life of freedom?
Two people were seen leaving the building where she was found, including a woman Aishat had befriended not long before her death.
Aishat Baimuradova/Instagram Her friends say Aishat was a very open person seeking connection with peopleSeveral high-profile critics of Chechen authorities have been killed abroad.
But Aishat Baimuradova is the first known Chechen woman to have died in suspicious circumstances, not long after fleeing Russia.
VALERY HACHE/AFP via Getty Images Chechen women who have sought refuge in Europe often avoid communicating with the diaspora

chinese_item:
试管婴儿服务的地区差异让一名女性感到“被剥夺”了机会。13小时前 分享 保存 Holly Nichols 高威科姆 分享 保存Holly Nichols/BBC Kelly-Marie Madden-Giles 在2024年开始了她在NHS的唯一一轮试管婴儿治疗。

由于所在地区的NHS试管婴儿服务政策，这名女性感到自己“被剥夺”了机会，并且觉得时间紧迫。然而，在Madden-Giles女士居住的地方，女性在35岁之前只能获得一次部分周期的试管婴儿服务。

Holly Nichols/BBC Madden-Giles女士通过自费的一轮试管婴儿治疗生下了女儿Tulley。

在英格兰，NHS资助的试管婴儿服务因地区而异，每个综合保健委员会（ICB）都设定了自己的资格标准和周期数。现年35岁的Madden-Giles女士在2024年3月开始接受NHS资助的试管婴儿治疗，当时她33岁。
english_item:
IVF postcode lottery left woman feeling 'robbed'13 hours ago Share Save Holly Nichols High Wycombe Share SaveHolly Nichols/BBC Kelly-Marie Madden-Giles started her one and only round of IVF on the NHS in 2024A woman felt "robbed" and like she was "running out of time" due to NHS IVF access in her county.
However, where Mrs Madden-Giles lives, women were offered one partial cycle up to the age of 35.
Holly Nichols/BBC Mrs Madden-Giles gave birth to her daughter Tulley after a private round of IVF.
Access to NHS-funded IVF in England varies by postcode as each ICB sets its own eligibility criteria and number of cycles.
Mrs Madden-Giles, 35, started NHS-funded IVF in March 2024 when she was 33 years old.

chinese_item:
印度庆祝女子盲人T20世界杯夺冠。本次比赛采用单循环赛制，共有六支队伍参赛，分别是印度、尼泊尔、巴基斯坦、斯里兰卡、澳大利亚和美国。
这次胜利发生在印度赢得首个女子单日国际板球世界杯几周之后，预计将为该国残疾运动员带来极大的鼓舞。
印度女子板球队赢得了首届盲人T20世界杯冠军后，全国上下欢庆这一胜利。
“有些胜利提醒我们，当才华不受限制时，任何挑战都不算太大。”前印度女子板球队队长米塔利·拉杰在X平台上写道。
世界杯于11月11日在德里拉开帷幕。
english_item:
India celebrates as women lift T20 World Cup for the blindThe tournament featured six teams - India, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Australia and the US - in a single round-robin.
The victory, which came weeks after India won its first Women's One-Day International World Cup , is expected to provide a fillip to sportspersons with disabilities in the country.
Indians are celebrating after the country's women cricketers won the inaugural Twenty20 World Cup for the blind.
"Some victories remind us that no challenge is ever too big when talent refuses to be limited," Mithali Raj, former captain of the India women's cricket team, wrote in a post on X.
The World Cup began on 11 November in Delhi.

chinese_item:
从鸡蛋盒到旧丝袜——这些学生把垃圾变成了时尚
来自北爱尔兰各地的学生们周四在中央大车站展示了他们的作品
多米尼克和艾菲用报纸、火车票和垃圾袋制作了他们的服装
鸡蛋盒、垃圾袋、绳子和报纸剪报通常会被扔进垃圾桶然后被遗忘，但一些学生却利用这些材料创作出了真正的时尚。
“垃圾时装”活动已经来到了贝尔法斯特，北爱尔兰各地的学生有机会将废弃物变成时尚。
展示由旧丝袜、鸡蛋盒、塑料袋等制成的服装的舞台占据了市中心的中央大车站。
“垃圾时装”起源于爱尔兰，是世界上最大的青少年可持续时尚竞赛，挑战12至19岁的年轻人使用100%可回收材料创作可穿戴的艺术品。
该活动与Translink合作，重点关注可持续时尚和出行。
english_item:
From egg cartons to old tights - these students turned junk into fashionStudents from across Northern Ireland showcased their creations in Grand Central Station on ThursdayDominic and Aoife made their outfits from newspapers, train tickets and bin bagsEgg boxes, bin bags, rope, and newspaper clippings are usually thrown in the bin and forgotten about, but some students have taken those materials and created some serious fashion.
Junk Kouture has arrived in Belfast and students from across Northern Ireland have had the opportunity to take waste materials and turn it into fashion.
The stage showcasing outfits made from old tights, egg cartons, plastic bags and many others took over Grand Central Station in the city centre.
Junk Kouture, created in Ireland, is the world's largest youth sustainable fashion competition that challenges young people between the ages of 12 to 19 to create wearable art out of 100% recyclable materials.
It teamed up with Translink for the initiative focusing on sustainable fashion and travel.

chinese_item:
骗子冒充警察进行诈骗，多塞特郡警方正在调查该地区发生的八起快递诈骗及未遂案件。据多塞特警方称，在11月7日至14日之间，伯恩茅斯、布里德波特、普尔贝克、普尔和克里斯彻奇的老年人成为了目标。
警方发言人表示，这些骗子冒充警察，并声称已经逮捕了一名能够访问受害者银行账户的人。
骗子建议受害者前往银行提取现金，随后会有一名便衣警察来收取这笔钱。
警方透露，在三次事件中，受害者将数千英镑现金交给了骗子。
english_item:
Fraudsters impersonate police to scam victimsEight reports of courier fraud and attempted courier fraud across Dorset are being investigated by police.
Elderly victims were targeted in Bournemouth, Bridport, Purbeck, Poole and Christchurch between 7 and 14 November, Dorset Police said.
A police spokesperson said the fraudsters impersonated police officers and claimed to have a person in custody who had gained access to the victims' bank accounts.
The victims were advised to go to their banks and withdraw cash, which would then be collected by a plain-clothed officer.
On three occasions, the victims handed over cash to the fraudsters, totalling several thousand pounds, police said.

chinese_item:
一名对儿童实施性侵犯的罪犯因“令人发指”的虐待行为被判入狱20年。2025年10月3日 分享 保存 分享 保存
格洛斯特郡警察局报告，马修·亚历山大在被认定对两名儿童犯下16项罪行后，被判入狱20年。
现年48岁的马修·亚历山大来自林迪，周四在一陪审团一致裁定其有罪后被判刑。此前，他在7月份于格洛斯特刑事法院接受了审判。
宣判后，亚历山大的一名受害者表示：“我永远无法原谅他对我所做的一切。”
另一名受害者说：“他的虐待行为极大地影响了我的成长。我觉得自己不得不更快地长大，不能成为我想成为的那个孩子。”
english_item:
Child rapist jailed for 'horrendous' abuse3 October 2025 Share Save Share SaveGloucestershire Constabulary Matthew Alexander was jailed for 20 years after being found guilty of 16 offences against two childrenA man has been jailed for 20 years after he was found guilty of multiple historic counts of raping and sexually assaulting two girls.
Matthew Alexander, 48, from Lydney, was jailed on Thursday after a jury unanimously found him guilty of 16 offences following a trial at Gloucester Crown Court in July.
Following the sentencing, one of Alexander's victims said: "I will never be able to forgive him for what he has done to me."
One of Alexander's victims said: "His abusive behaviour has had a massive impact on my growing up.
"I felt that I had to grow up faster and I couldn't be the child that I wanted to be.

chinese_item:
研究表明，每在社区护理上花费100英镑，否则就需要在医院护理上花费131英镑。
这有助于解释为什么卫生大臣韦斯·斯特里廷承诺要把NHS转变为邻里健康服务。
在这里，医院医生、全科医生、护士、职业治疗师、地方议会的社会护理团队、心理健康专家以及慈善机构工作人员将在同一屋檐下工作。
她在上世纪90年代中期开始，在卫生部和NHS执行部门工作了十多年。
那么，政府真的能够将NHS重塑为邻里健康服务吗？
english_item:
Research suggests that every ￡100 spent on community care would otherwise cost ￡131 for hospital care.
This helps explain why Health Secretary Wes Streeting has promised to turn the NHS into a neighbourhood health service.
Here, hospital doctors, GPs, nurses, occupational therapists, council social care teams, mental health professionals and charity staff work under one roof.
She worked for the Department of Health and NHS executive for more than a decade from the mid-1990s.
So can the government really reshape the NHS into a neighbourhood health service?

chinese_item:
直到五年前，一位医院的职业治疗师给了她一个不同寻常的处方：种一株向日葵。
她帮助塑造了皇家鸟类保护协会（RSPB）今年早些时候在西米德兰兹推出的旗舰“自然处方”计划。
野生动物信托基金会的多姆·希金斯表示，支持绿色社会处方的证据是明确的。但绿色社会处方的未来仍不确定。
野生动物信托基金会健康与教育负责人多姆·希金斯说：“毫无疑问，大自然对心理健康有积极影响。”
当BBC就此询问政府时，政府没有就绿色社会处方的未来发表评论，但表示试验计划后两年的评估结果将在“适时”公布。
english_item:
That was until five years ago, when a hospital occupational therapist gave her an unusual prescription: Grow a sunflower.
She helped shape the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds (RSPB)'s flagship "nature prescription" scheme for the West Midlands, launched earlier this year.
Andy Alcroft/BBC Dom Higgins, from the Wildlife Trusts, saids the evidence in favour of green social prescribing was clearBut the future of green social prescribing is uncertain.
Dom Higgins, head of health and education for The Wildlife Trusts, said: "It is unequivocal that nature improves mental health.
When approached by the BBC, the government did not comment on the future of green social prescribing but said the evaluation of the second two years of the trial scheme would be published "in due course".

chinese_item:
谢丽尔·梅赫卡尔于10月22日在曼哈顿的纽约大学朗格尼健康中心接受了世界上首例全机器人双肺移植手术。
梅赫卡尔是一名医护人员，她患有慢性阻塞性肺病已有十年之久，在感染新冠后病情变得更加严重。
在符合这项手术条件之前，她花了多年时间寻找解决方案。
该技术基于微创手术，旨在缩短住院时间和加快恢复期。
手术近一个月后，梅赫卡尔即将出院。
english_item:
Cheryl Mehrkar was the recipient of the world's first fully robotic double lung transplant on 22 October at NYU Langone Health Center in Manhattan.
Mehrkar, who is a medic, had suffered with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease for a decade that became more severe when she experienced a bout of Covid.
She had spent years researching for a solution before becoming eligible for this procedure.
The technique builds on minimally invasive surgeries and aims to reduce hospital stays as well as speed up recovery periods.
Almost a month after the surgery, Mehrkar is now days from being released from hospital.

chinese_item:
英国央行行长会在利率问题上扮演圣诞老人还是吝啬鬼？
英国央行行长安德鲁·贝利预计，通胀将在明年年初降至接近3%的水平。
英格兰银行外议论纷纷。
贝利表示，在降息之前，他希望看到即将发生的变化是否能证实这一观点；劳动力市场的疲软也可能起到一定作用。
央行还指出，去年的预算措施——比如提高雇主国民保险费和最低工资——在过去一年中加剧了物价压力。
但央行报告明确表示，去年的预算措施通过增加雇主成本导致了物价上涨压力，并使企业在招聘时更加犹豫。
english_item:
Will Bank of England governor play Santa or Scrooge on interest rates?
The Bank of England governor, Andrew Bailey, expects inflation to fall to close to 3% early next yearThere is a buzz outside the Bank of England.
Governor Andrew Bailey said he wanted to see if forthcoming developments confirmed this view before cutting rates; weakness in the labour market could also play a part.
The Bank also noted last year's Budget measures – such as an increase in employer National Insurance Contributions and minimum wages – contributed to price pressures over the last year.
But the Bank's report makes clear that last year's Budget measures have contributed to price pressures, and hiring hesitancy by adding to employer's costs.

chinese_item:
福岛灾难：核电站发生了什么？
在福岛核电站，巨大的海浪冲破了沿海防御工事，淹没了反应堆，引发了一场重大灾难。
福岛灾难被国际原子能机构评定为七级事件，这是最高等级的事件，也是继1986年切尔诺贝利灾难之后第二个达到这一级别的事件。
福岛第一核电站位于福岛县的双叶町。
核电站的系统检测到地震后自动关闭了反应堆。
english_item:
Fukushima disaster: What happened at the nuclear plant?
At the Fukushima nuclear power plant, the gigantic wave surged over coastal defences and flooded the reactors, sparking a major disaster.
The Fukushima Disaster is classified as a level seven event by the International Atomic Energy Agency, the highest such event and only the second event to meet this classification after the 1986 Chernobyl disaster.
The Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant is in the town of Okuma, in Fukushima Prefecture.
Systems at the nuclear plant detected the earthquake and automatically shut down the reactors.

chinese_item:
移民政策的变化将对苏格兰的护理人员产生“深远影响”。有人声称，让合法移民等待更长时间才能在英国永久定居，将对苏格兰的护理行业产生“极其负面的影响”。
代表独立社会护理服务的苏格兰护理组织表示，这些变化会阻碍“急需”的国际工人，而这些工人在护理行业中发挥着“至关重要的作用”。
苏格兰护理组织去年的一份报告发现，超过66%的成员正在积极从海外招聘员工。
报告还发现，在某些地区，国际招聘的护理人员几乎占了全部劳动力。
“这些政策还传递了一个有害的信息，即移民护理人员所做出的宝贵贡献并未得到充分认可或重视。”
english_item:
Immigration changes will 'profoundly impact' Scottish care workers3 days ago Share Save Share SaveGetty ImagesMaking legal migrants wait longer before settling permanently in the UK would have a "profoundly negative impact" on Scotland's care sector, it has been claimed.
Scottish Care, which represents independent social care services, says the changes would deter "much-needed" international workers who have a "vital role".
A report last year by Scottish Care found just over 66% of its members were actively recruiting from overseas.
It also found internationally recruited care workers made up nearly 100% of the workforce in some areas.
"They also send a damaging message that the invaluable contributions of migrant care workers are not fully recognised or valued.

chinese_item:
一位议员呼吁政府介入，以阻止兰开夏郡10家养老院和日间中心可能面临的关闭问题。
兰卡斯特和韦尔地区的议员凯特·史密斯已经就这些地点向议会提交了请愿书，其中包括兰卡斯特的维尔景观日间中心。
“我正在寻找一切可能的方法来保护兰卡斯特的成人日间中心，”史密斯说。
她表示，关闭日间中心可能会“使我们社区中最脆弱的一些居民被孤立”。
目前，拯救维尔景观日间中心的请愿书已经收集到了超过1000个签名。
english_item:
'I want government to intervene to save care homes'4 days ago Share Save Gina Millson , Political reporter BBC Lancashire and Paul Faulkner , Local Democracy Reporting Service Share SaveCat Smith MP Cat Smith MP says Vale View Day Centre in Lancaster is a valuable facility and wants to do all she can to save itAn MP is calling on the government to intervene over the potential closure of 10 care homes and day centres in Lancashire.
Cat Smith, Lancaster and Wyre MP, has petitioned Parliament over the sites, including Vale View Day Centre in Lancaster.
"I'm looking to pull any lever possible to be able to defend the adult day centre in Lancaster," Smith said.
She said closing the day centre risked "isolating some of the most vulnerable residents in our community".
The petition to save Vale View currently has more than 1,000 signatures.

chinese_item: BBC Verify的一项调查揭示，与俄罗斯有关的虚假信息活动利用人工智能克隆了英国公职人员的身份。BBC记者奥尔加·罗宾逊追踪并采访了一位来自英格兰普雷斯顿的紧急医疗顾问，他震惊地发现自己声音被伪造，并用于在今年早些时候波兰总统选举前传播恐慌情绪的视频活动中。
english_item:
A BBC Verify investigation has revealed that the identities of British public sector workers have been cloned using AI by a Russian-linked disinformation campaign.
The BBC's Olga Robinson has tracked down and spoken to an emergency medical adviser from Preston in England, who was shocked to learn his voice had been faked in a video campaign spreading fear ahead of Poland's presidential election earlier this year.

chinese_item:
我们中有多少人最终会被诊断出患有注意力缺陷多动障碍（ADHD）？
是不是因为我们越来越擅长识别这种病症了呢？
所有我交谈过的专家都坚定地同意一点：对于ADHD患者来说，尽早诊断并接受治疗是非常重要的。
他说：“我见过很多患者情况好转，重新回到工作岗位或继续他们的学业。”
有关ADHD的支持信息，请参阅BBC行动热线；获取NHS提供的ADHD建议。顶部图片：Getty Images
english_item:
How many of us will end up being diagnosed with ADHD?
Are we just getting better at recognising it?
All the experts I speak to firmly agree on one point: it is much better for someone with ADHD to be diagnosed and treated as early as possible.
He says: “I have seen so many patients getting better, getting back into work or back into education.
See BBC Action Line for support on issues around ADHD Read ADHD advice from the NHS Top picture: Getty Images

chinese_item:
巴基斯坦内政部长称，伊斯兰堡发生自杀式炸弹袭击致12人死亡
2025年11月11日

据巴基斯坦内政部长表示，在首都伊斯兰堡的一家法院外发生的自杀式袭击已造成12人死亡，至少27人受伤。

巴基斯坦总理谢赫巴兹·谢里夫指控称，有“印度积极支持”的极端组织参与了此次袭击。

近年来，伊斯兰堡的自杀式爆炸事件较为罕见。

一位律师表示，当时他正在法院外停车，听到了一声巨响。

爆炸发生后，警方封锁了伊斯兰堡地区法院附近的区域。
english_item:
Suicide bombing in Islamabad kills 12, says Pakistan's interior minister11 November 2025 Share Save Caroline Davies , Pakistan correspondent, Islamabad and Dearbail Jordan Share SavePakistan: Suicide attack outside Islamabad court kills 12A suicide attack outside a court in Pakistan's capital Islamabad has killed 12 people and injured at least 27 others, the country's interior minister said.
Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has alleged that extremist groups "actively backed by India" were involved.
Suicide blasts in Islamabad have been rare in recent years.
A lawyer who said he was parking his car outside the court at the time described hearing a "loud bang".
Police cordoned off an area close to a district court in Islamabad after the blast

chinese_item:
美国近东难民援助组织（Anera）在停火开始六周后，在加沙中部的扎瓦伊达开设了这个社区厨房。
当时，以色列封锁已经持续两个月，禁止所有食品和其他物资进入，库存正在减少。
现在，随着更多食品被允许进入，情况有所改善。
“那些必需品不被允许进入加沙进行人道主义援助分发。”
来自加沙北部贾巴利亚、有七个孩子的父亲阿卜杜勒·卡里姆·阿卜杜勒·哈迪说：“我完全筋疲力尽了。”
english_item:
American Near East Refugee Aid (Anera) opened this community kitchen in al-Zawayda in central Gaza after the ceasefire began six weeks ago.
Back then, two months into an Israeli blockade, preventing the entry of all food and other goods, stocks were dwindling.
Now, with more food allowed to enter, the situation has improved.
"Those essentials are not allowed to enter Gaza for humanitarian aid distribution."
I'm totally wiped out," says Abdul Karim Abdul Hadi, a father-of-seven from Jabalia in northern Gaza who receives food from Anera.

chinese_item: 南非的外包行业，包括会计支持和呼叫中心，正在强劲增长。Esethu Dywili表示，他从事的高薪会计工作给了他改变家庭生活的机会。“南非有着非常棒的工作态度，南非人也充满活力，”德班的一位特许会计师Simon Wheeler说，他也在这个行业工作。在新冠疫情期间，许多公司转向南非的外包财务公司Makosi和PKF Octagon来填补数百个职位空缺。“如果我开始谈论南非，他们就会立刻离开。”他还担心南非外包行业的机会并没有在全国范围内平均分配。
english_item:
How the outsourcing sector became South Africa's newest goldmine22 October 2025 Share Save MaryLou Costa Business reporter Share SaveVentrica South Africa's outsourcing sector, including accountancy support and call centres, is growing stronglyEsethu Dywili says his lucrative accountancy work has given him the chance to change his family's life.
"There's just such a great work ethic in South Africa, and an energy about South Africans," says Simon Wheeler, a Durban-based chartered accountant who also works in the sector.
It turned to South African outsourcing finance firms Makosi and PKF Octagon to fill hundreds of roles during the Covid-19 pandemic.
"And if I were to embark on a conversation about South Africa, they would march out the door straight away."
He's also concerned that not all opportunities in the South African outsourcing industry are being spread equally across the country.

chinese_item:
弗兰克·伯恩原本来自利物浦，但现在住在基斯利的斯特顿法院。他曾是男子合唱团的一员，直到去年该团体解散。
斯特顿法院的经理卡罗琳·考兰德表示，这是一项“重要”的工作。
她说：“我们从新冠疫情中了解到，养老院里的人们非常孤立，因此我们尝试将社区中的不同人群聚集在一起。”
除了应对养老院内的社交隔离问题外，这些跨代际活动还旨在提高孩子的沟通技巧、社交行为，并增强他们的自信心和自尊心。
养老院居民和年轻人还可以在面对面访问时加入书信交流和绘画等活动。
english_item:
Originally from Liverpool but now living in Steeton Court in Keighley, Frank Byrne was part of a men's choir until it disbanded last year.
Steeton Court manager Caroline Cowland says it is an "important" piece of work.
"We learned from Covid that people were very isolated in care homes [so] we have tried to bring different people from the community together," she said.
Alongside tackling social isolation in care homes, the intergenerational activities also aim to benefit a child's communication skills, social behaviour and increase confidence and self-esteem.
Care home residents and young people can also add letter sharing and drawing to their in-person visits.

chinese_item:
阴沉的天空，渐弱的光线：空气污染如何夺走印度的阳光

2025年10月13日 分享 保存 苏提克·比斯瓦斯 印度通讯员 分享 保存 印度时报通过Getty Images

印度正面临严重的空气污染危机，在全球污染最严重的国家中排名前十。印度正在失去阳光。

科学家发现，孟买的日照时间正在逐渐减少。冬季时，由于雾霾、逆温现象以及印度-恒河平原上农作物焚烧产生的高浓度空气污染，会形成散射光线的气溶胶，从而减少了日照时间。

冬季朦胧的阳光可能会散射或扩散，降低光照强度，但不会完全阻挡阳光，因此仪器仍然记录为日照时间。

这些模式对农业、日常生活以及印度的太阳能雄心提出了担忧，同时也指出了太阳能板可能最有效的地方。

根据特里帕蒂教授的说法，空气污染加剧了这一问题。
english_item:
Foul skies, fading light: How air pollution is stealing India's sunshine13 October 2025 Share Save Soutik Biswas India correspondent Share SaveHindustan Times via Getty Images India faces a severe air pollution crisis, ranking among the world's top 10 most polluted countriesIndia is losing sunlight.
Getty Images Mumbai's sunshine hours are gradually declining, the scientists foundIn winter, high air pollution from smog, temperature inversions and crop burning across the Indo-Gangetic plains produces light-scattering aerosols, which reduce sunshine hours.
Hazy winter sunlight may scatter or diffuse, lowering intensity without entirely blocking sunshine, which instruments still record as sunshine hours.
These patterns raise concerns for agriculture, daily life and India's solar energy ambitions, while highlighting where solar panels could be most effective.
According to Prof Tripathi, air pollution compounds the problem.

chinese_item: “自从感染了新冠，我的生活彻底改变了。”2025年10月2日 杰米·莫里斯 英格兰南部 分享 保存 Getty Images 本周，来自多塞特郡的儿童和年轻人在新冠调查中发言。一位来自多塞特郡的少女告诉新冠调查委员会，她的持续症状让她常常感到被排除在外。此次调查希望了解疫情的长期影响，并预计将持续四周。“现在我有持续的症状，这让我无法像大多数青少年那样去做很多事情，”她说。莫莉在医院度过了很长一段时间，她说她被迫错过了大部分的中学教育。来自韦茅斯的萨米·麦克法兰是Long Covid Kids的首席执行官兼联合创始人，该组织代表英国超过11,000个家庭。
english_item:
'Since having Covid my life has completely changed'2 October 2025 Share Save Jamie Morris South of England Share SaveGetty Images Children and young people from Dorset have spoken at the Covid Inquiry this weekA teenager from Dorset has told the Covid-19 inquiry her ongoing symptoms often leave her feeling "left out".
The inquiry hopes to understand the ongoing impact of the pandemic and is expected to last four weeks.
"Now I live with on-going symptoms that stop me from doing the things that most teenagers can," she said.
Molly spent long periods in hospital and said she was forced to miss out on most of her secondary education.
Sammie McFarland, from Weymouth, is the CEO and co-founder of Long Covid Kids, which represents more than 11,000 families in the UK.

chinese_item:
警员否认因疏忽驾驶导致女子死亡
2022年平安夜，默西塞德郡警察局的一辆巡逻车在利物浦肯辛顿的希勒路撞上了一名女子。这名22岁的女子蕾切尔·摩尔在下班回家的路上不幸身亡，时间是格林尼治标准时间20:09。
来自沃特里的32岁警员斯科特·汤普森当时正在响应紧急呼叫。经过独立警察行为办公室为期三年的调查后，他于本月早些时候被起诉。
在曼彻斯特治安法院出庭时，他选择在曼彻斯特明舒尔街刑事法院进行陪审团审判，而不是由治安法院处理。
他将于1月8日出庭，并获得了无条件保释。
english_item:
PC denies causing women's death by careless drivingA Merseyside Police officer whose patrol car struck a woman on Christmas Eve in 2022 has denied causing her death by careless driving.
Rachael Moore, 22, died on Sheil Road, Kensington, Liverpool, at 20:09 GMT, as she walked home from her job as a carer.
PC Scott Thompson, 32, of Wavertree, who had been responding to an emergency call, was charged earlier this month after a three-year investigation by the Independent Office for Police Conduct.
At Manchester Magistrates' Court, he elected to have a jury trial at Manchester Minshull Street Crown Court rather than be dealt with by the magistrates' court.
He is set to appear in court on 8 January and was granted unconditional bail.

chinese_item: 一位母亲在未经同意的情况下接受了“痛苦”的健康检查。8天前 分享 保存 Tom Burgess 东北和坎布里亚 分享 保存 BBC 哈特尔普尔大学医院表示，在Dawn Lord投诉后已经进行了改进。Dawn Lord女士，现年51岁，在没有给予知情同意的情况下接受了一次宫腔镜检查，并形容这一过程“极其痛苦”。她原本以为自己只是去哈特尔普尔大学医院讨论未来的治疗方案，结果医生告诉她需要取一个活检样本。“我不知道发生了什么，只听到医生一直在说‘拿不到，拿不到’。”Lord夫人说道。她在预约后的五个月，即十月时向医院提出了投诉，并收到了道歉。医院方面表示：“我们未能达到为患者提供高标准护理的目标，并已致信向Lord夫人道歉。”
english_item:
Mother had 'agonising' health test without consent8 days ago Share Save Tom Burgess North East and Cumbria Share SaveBBC The University Hospital of Hartlepool said improvements had been made after Dawn Lord's complaintA woman who did not give informed consent for a hysteroscopy has described the procedure as "agonising".
Dawn Lord, 51, believed she was attending an appointment at the University Hospital of Hartlepool to discuss future procedures when her doctor told her she needed to take a biopsy.
"I don't know what's happening and I just keep hearing the doctor say 'can't get it, can't get it'," Mrs Lord said.
Mrs Lord complained to the hospital and received an apology in October, five months after her appointment.
They said: "We have not met the high standard of care we strive to provide to our patients and have written to Mrs Lord to apologise.

chinese_item: 一名男子在学生公寓遇刺身亡后，警方逮捕了一名涉嫌谋杀的嫌疑人。紧急服务部门接到报告称，在诺里奇索默利顿街的一处房产内发现一名男子被刺伤，时间大约为格林尼治标准时间5点20分。诺福克警方表示，这名30多岁的男子在现场被宣布死亡，而一名20多岁的男子已被拘留。警方还补充说，调查期间有数人暂时从家中撤离。
english_item:
Murder arrest after man dies at student flatsEmergency services were called to reports of a man with a stab wound at a property in Somerleyton Street in Norwich at about 05:20 GMTA man has been arrested on suspicion of murder after a death at an accommodation block for students and professionals.
Emergency services were called to reports of a man with a stab wound at Somerleyton House in Somerleyton Street, Norwich at about 05:20 GMT.
Norfolk Police said the man, in his 30s, was pronounced dead at the scene and a man in his 20s had been detained.
The force added a number of people were being temporarily moved from their homes while inquiries continued.

chinese_item:
《在线安全法》的相关措施于7月生效，这意味着网络和社交媒体公司现在在法律上有责任保护儿童和年轻人的在线安全。
在线安全专家大卫·赖特CBE表示，这种情况“非常令人担忧”，但他补充说，“很大程度上改变需要时间”。
《在线安全法》生效后，我们再次进行了这项测试，这次是与BBC的《晨间直播》节目合作，并将我们的发现分享给了主持人扎拉·麦克德莫特。
男孩们在TikTok上没有看到任何特别令人担忧的内容，三个YouTube账号中的两个也是如此。
“请不要仅仅依赖我们现在有了这些神奇的规范（《在线安全法》行为准则），就认为一切都会好起来。”
english_item:
Measures came into force in July as part of the Online Safety Act, meaning online and social media companies are now legally responsible for keeping children and young people safe online.
Online safety expert David Wright CBE said it was "really concerning" but added "in large part it's going to take time" for change to happen.
After the Online Safety Act came into effect, the exercise was repeated, this time in collaboration with BBC's Morning Live programme, sharing our findings with presenter Zara McDermott.
The boys were not shown any content that was particularly concerning on TikTok, and two of the three YouTube profiles were similarly clear.
"Please don't just rely that we've now got these magical codes [Online Safety Act codes of practice] that it's all going to be okay.

chinese_item:
我追着一个飘荡的塑料袋走了半个街区

今天我走在回家的路上，突然注意到一个塑料袋被风吹起，开始沿着人行道飘落。我以为它只会翻滚几英尺就停下来，但它却一直飘着。它不停地滑行、旋转，仿佛在尝试不同的飞行姿势。我不假思索地放慢了脚步，跟在它后面。我们突然变成了两个朝着同一个方向走的陌生人。

有时它能跳得很高，越过人行道上的裂缝。有时它又贴着地面飘，几乎擦着地面。风向变换，它突然向左或向右转弯，摇摇晃晃，好像不知道该落在哪里。一辆车经过，一阵风把它吹起，懒洋洋地旋转起来，看起来竟有些别扭的优雅。

我追着它走了大约半个街区，最后它被树枝勾住，停了下来。塑料袋微微晃动，仿佛在喘息。我站在那里看了它一会儿，看着它渐渐平静下来，然后继续往前走。我知道，这一切都不重要。但那几分钟里，我感觉自己好像在参与一场微不足道的冒险。只有我、风，还有一块塑料，它正努力地模仿着生命的模样。
english_item:
Followed a drifting plastic bag for half a block
I was walking home today when I noticed a plastic bag catch the wind and start floating down the sidewalk ahead of me. I thought it would just tumble a few feet and stop, but it kept going. It kept gliding and twisting. It's as if it was testing out different moves. I slowed down without even thinking and ended up trailing behind it. We were suddenly two strangers heading in the same direction.

Sometimes it hopped high enough to clear a crack in the pavement. Other times it skimmed so low it barely missed scraping the ground. The breeze would shift and it would suddenly turn left or right and wobble like it couldn’t decide where to land. A car passed and the gust lifted it up in a lazy spiral that made it look weirdly graceful.

I ended up following it for about half a block before it finally snagged on a tree branch and stayed there. The bag swayed a little like it was catching its breath. I stood there for a second watching it settle and then kept walking. Nothing about it was important, I know. For a few minutes though, it felt like I was tagging along on some tiny meaningless adventure. It's just me, the wind, and a piece of plastic doing its best impression of being alive.

chinese_item:
在操场上听到的对话

*穿着蜜桃色T恤的小孩：*我们不如看看艾米丽能不能玩？

*穿着野兽先生T恤的小孩：*艾米丽不能参加比赛。

*扎着法式辫子的女孩：*她也能玩，笨蛋。

*穿着野兽先生T恤的小孩：*不行。他们只招男生。

*穿着蜜桃色T恤的小孩：*哦。嗯。可能对。有时候我忘了艾米丽不是男生。

*扎着法式辫子的女孩：*我比男生强多了。我既有你们男生的速度，又有女生的智慧。

*穿着野兽先生T恤的小孩：*还有男生的讨厌劲儿。

*穿着蜜桃色T恤的小孩：*对啊。你真是两全其美。
english_item:
Conversation overheard at the playground
*Peach Vibe Celcius Kid:* Why don’t we just see if Emily can play? 


*Mr. Beast Shirt Kid:* Emily can’t play in a game. 


*French Braid Girl:* Can too, stupid. 


*Mr. Beast Shirt Kid:* Cannot. They’ll only let guys on the team. 


*Peach Vibe Celcius Kid:* Oh. Huh. That’s probably right. Sometimes I forget Emily’s not a guy. 


*French Braid Girl:* I’m better than a guy. I’ve got all the speed of you guys and the smarts of a girl. 



*Mr. Beast Shirt Kid:* And the obnoxiousness of a guy. 



*Peach Vibe Celcius Kid:* Yeah. You’re best of both worlds.

chinese_item:
我还清了车贷。

这其实很简单。五年半前，我买了一辆皮卡。对我来说，这象征着一种进步，因为之前为了支付奶奶的巨额医疗费，我不得不卖掉自己的车。（可惜的是，奶奶去世了，我当时的心情又是另一个故事了……）

这期间，我的生活起起伏伏，甚至一度差点把车还回去。但我坚持了下来，按时还款。开着它，我感到快乐和充满希望。

我拿到了一笔奖金，把所有积蓄都用来还清了债务，银行这周就会寄来最终的还款文件。

我现在很感激。我终于和哥哥和好了，我们上次见面还是在2016年。我们现在经常聊天。我们都成熟了，彼此之间也更加尊重对方。我终于放下了所有的愤怒，感觉如释重负。

一切都在好转。
english_item:
I paid of my car
Quite simple thing. 5 and a half years ago I bought a pickup truck. It was a symbol of progress for me, after having to sell my car to try and manage huge medical bills l had made on my granny's behalf. (Regrettably she passed away and how I dealt with that is another story...)
Things have gone up and down and I almost had to give it back at one stage. But I persevered and kept the payments up to date. Driving it gave me joy and hope.
Received an incentive bonus and put everything in to clear the debt, the bank will be sending the final paperwork this week.
I am thankful now. Have managed to reconcile with my brother who I had last seen in 2016. We are talking a lot. We have both matured and there is a new respect between us. I finally managed to bury all the anger and I feel freed.
Things did get better.

chinese_item:
有时候在Costco当个“老奶奶”也挺有意思的

前几天去了Costco。老公在结账，我排队买披萨。前面有个年轻妈妈带着她家的小宝宝（大概四岁吧？）。

小女孩转过头看着我，眼睛瞪得老大。“你是奶奶吗？”她问。

妈妈当时看起来既想笑又尴尬得要死。我只是笑了笑，说我选眼镜的时候就是想让自己看起来像个奶奶……

然后我告诉她，在我等披萨的这30秒里，我可以暂时当她奶奶。叫到我的号码，我拿着披萨离开的时候，母女俩都笑了，还拍手叫好。
english_item:
Sometimes Being a Boomer at Costco is a Good Thing
Went to Costco the other day. Husband was checking out and I was in line for a pizza. Ahead of me was a young mom with her toddler (maybe 4 years old?).

The little girl turned and looked at me and her eyes went huge. "Are you a grandma?" She asked.

Mom was looking like she wanted to laugh and die of embarrassment simultaneously. I just smiled and said that I was going for the grandma look when I picked out my glasses so...

Then I told her that I can be her grandma for the next 30 seconds while I wait for my pizza. Mom and daughter both laughed and clapped when my number was called and I walked away with my pizza.

chinese_item:
我坐在外面，夜色漆黑。

坐在后门廊的台阶上，光着脚踩在冰凉刺痒的草地上，感觉很舒服。直接对着罐子咕咚咕咚地喝着苹果汁——这是我的，谁也别想从我这儿传染上什么细菌。

我能听到远处汽车驶过的声音，近处则是风吹树叶的沙沙声。

人生只有一次，对吧？总的来说，我的人生还算不错。
english_item:
I’m sitting outside in the dark
On the back porch steps, bare feet in the cold prickly soothing grass. Swigging apple juice straight from the jug—it’s mine, nobody’s gonna catch my cooties from it. 

I can hear cars driving some ways away, and wind in the leaves closer by. 

We only get this one life, eh? All things considered, mine’s a pretty good one.

chinese_item:
小小的胜利

昨天我迎来了一个小小的胜利：我班上一个最难管教的学生被激怒了，但他没有还手。我平时大部分时间都花在管教他的行为上，所以今天能表扬他，真是太好了。对他来说是胜利，对我来说也是胜利。??
english_item:
Little win
I had a little win yesterday, when one of my most challenging students was provoked and chose not to retaliate. It was lovely to praise him for his great choices when so much of my time is usually devoted to managing his behaviour. A win for him and a win for me. ??

chinese_item:
灯塔

每个夜晚，我的客厅里都仿佛有一场风暴。

我会把耳朵贴在分隔我们世界的墙上，闭上双眼。

轻柔的声音。手指在琴键上翩翩起舞。每一段旋律都照亮了我。

如今，我的手指如同温柔的流水，轻柔地滑过象牙和缟玛瑙。

而我，总是希望你知道，你的光芒是如何指引我回家的。
english_item:
Lighthouse
Every evening there was a storm in my living room.

I would press my ear to the wall that separated our worlds and close my eyes. 

Soft voices. Fingers that danced over keys. Each melody cast light onto me. 

Now my fingers glide like gentle water over ivory and onyx stones. 

And I always wish you knew how you light guided me home.

chinese_item:
孩子们在街上乱跑

街角附近一栋出租房搬来了一户新住户，他们带着一群小孩。他们家还养了一条中等体型的黑狗。最近，我经常看到孩子们在街区里跑来跑去，胡闹尖叫，牵着狗到处乱跑，总之就是各种乱窜。我女朋友前几天还说：“这些孩子怎么到处乱跑啊？”

两天前，我遛狗的时候，看到两三个男孩在街的另一头牵着狗到处跑，就决定绕道走。昨天，其中一个男孩牵着狗从我家门口跑过，停在我邻居家的院子里，然后又跑了起来。要知道，这孩子个子很小，基本上是狗在遛他，还挺可爱的。

前几天我突然意识到，现在再也看不到像我们小时候那样在街上玩耍的孩子了。没人在街上跑来跑去，也没人骑自行车……跟我们八九十年代看到的完全不一样了。

他们或许不知道，但他们孩子间的喧闹却温暖了我的心。他们为我们这个阴郁世界的小角落带来了活力和欢笑。
english_item:
kids running the streets
a rental around the corner has a new family with a gaggle of small kids. the family also has a mid size black dog. recently, I have been seeing the kids run down the block, acting a fool, screaming, running with the leashed dog, just making general kid chaos. my gf recently said "why tf are these kids running all over the place". 

2 days ago I was walking my dogs and decided to skip their street because I could see 2 or 3 of the boys outside running around with the dog at the further end of their street. yesterday, one ran by my house with the leashed dog, stopped in my neighbors yard and ran along. mind you, this kid is small and the dog basically walks him, its kind of cute.

it occurred to me the other day you just don't see kids outside like we were. none running up and down the streets or on bikes....not the way we did in the 80s and 90s. 

they don't know it, but they warm my heart with their kid chaos. they've brought spirit and laughter to our little corner of this bleak bleak world.

chinese_item:
感觉很平静，但也有些空虚。

我之前坐在窗边，看着街对面建筑上的灯光变化。景色很美，一切都很宁静。没有喧嚣，也没有匆忙。房间里弥漫着一种柔和的静谧。感觉很平和，但又有些难以言喻的空虚。这并非悲伤，或许只是一种静静的提醒：你身处此地，一切都好，但仍然缺少一些说不清道不明的东西。
english_item:
It felt calm, but a little empty too
I was sitting by the window earlier just watching the light change on the buildings across the street. It looked nice and everything was really calm. There wasn’t any noise or rush around. But there's this soft stillness that fills the room. It felt peaceful but also a little empty in a way that’s hard to describe. It's not exactly sad but perhaps just a quiet reminder that you’re here and doing fine but still missing something you can’t name.

chinese_item:
在咖啡馆和陌生人聊了45分钟，让我重新体会到了随意交谈的感觉。

昨天我在一家咖啡馆用笔记本电脑工作。店里人很多，有人问我能不能拼桌。我爽快地答应了。

我们俩默默地工作了大概20分钟。然后我的笔记本电脑充电器没电了，我不禁叹了口气。对方抬起头，问道：“今天过得不太顺利吗？”

就这样，我们聊了45分钟。聊了工作、生活、旅行、读过的书，还有一些关于世界的随想。整个过程自然流畅，毫不拘束。

我甚至都不记得对方的名字了。我们没有交换电话号码或社交媒体账号。最后，对方离开时说了声“谢谢聊天”，我说了声“你也一样”，然后就结束了。

但从那以后，我一直在想这件事。我上一次和陌生人进行真正自然的、不经意的交谈是什么时候？不是闲聊天气，也不是那种例行公事的“请给我一杯咖啡”式的对话。一次真正的对话。我们都太沉迷于手机、耳机和自己的世界里了。我也是。但那次对话让我意识到，和陌生人交谈曾经是很平常的事。而且，这其实感觉很棒。
english_item:
Had a 45-minute conversation with a stranger at a coffee shop and remembered what it's like to just talk
I was at a coffee shop yesterday working on my laptop. The place was packed and someone asked if they could share my table. I said sure.

We both worked in silence for like 20 minutes. Then my laptop charger died and I sighed out loud. The person looked up and said "Rough day?"

And somehow that turned into a 45-minute conversation. About work, life, travel, books we've read, random observations about the world. It wasn't forced or awkward. It just flowed.

I don't even remember the person's name. We didn't exchange numbers or social media. They eventually left and said "Thanks for the chat" and I said "You too" and that was it.

But I've been thinking about it ever since. When was the last time I had a real, spontaneous conversation with a stranger? Not small talk about the weather. Not a transactional "can I get a coffee" exchange. A real conversation. We're all so in our phones and headphones and bubbles. I am too. But that conversation reminded me that talking to strangers used to be normal. And it's actually really nice?

chinese_item:
橡树山雀

我喂街对面大树上的蓝松鸦。松鼠们也发现了这个“自助餐”，现在也来了。它们笨拙地四处乱窜，互相叫喊，也对我叫，或许还在叫花生，叼走花生藏得到处都是。热闹又有趣，是我一天中最快乐的时光。

最近我注意到另一位“食客”，它会在树上等着，直到其他鸟儿都飞走了。这是一只小鸟，头顶有个小小的冠羽，会在一切安静下来后飞到篱笆边。它现在每天都来，如果我安静地坐在门廊上，也不会把它吓走。我查了一下，它叫“橡树山雀”。我的新朋友——橡树山雀！
english_item:
The Oak Titmouse
I feed the blue jays that live in the big tree across the street. The squirrels have learned about this buffet, and the come too now. They bumble around making noises at each other, and at me, and possibly at the peanuts, taking their peanuts and hiding them all around. It is noisy and fun and my most fun part of the day. 

I recently noticed another diner, who waits high up in the tree until everybody else has gone away. It is a little bird, with a little crest, and it comes down to the fence after everything is quiet. It comes every day now, and if I am sitting quietly on the porch, I don't scare it away. I looked it up, it's called an "oak titmouse". My tiny new friend the oak titmouse!

chinese_item:
我花了不少时间琢磨我的咖啡杯怎么会落灰。

我正准备泡杯咖啡，突然发现我最喜欢的杯子上积了一层薄薄的灰尘。不至于让人觉得恶心，但总觉得有点怪异。我又不是把它放在外面，而且几乎每天都用。

于是我站在那里，拿着杯子，琢磨了好几分钟，想弄明白这灰尘到底是从哪儿来的。最后，我还是把它擦掉了，泡了咖啡，继续忙自己的事。但我却一直想着这层灰尘，好像它就是故意冒出来跟我较劲似的。
english_item:
I spent a good amount of time trying to figure out how my coffee mug got dusty
I was about to make a cup of coffee when I noticed my favorite mug had this fine layer of dust on it. Not enough to make it gross, just enough to be weird. It’s not like I leave it out in the open, and I use it almost every day.

So I spent a few minutes standing there, holding the mug, trying to figure out where the dust could’ve come from. In the end, I just wiped it off, made my coffee, and went on with my day. But I couldn’t stop thinking about the dust, like it had somehow appeared just to challenge me

chinese_item:
我这辈子第一次要去“不给糖就捣蛋”。我42岁了。

我们小时候住的地方没有万圣节的习俗。今晚我要陪我的侄女们去，我兴奋极了。我准备了一身恐龙装（当然，我是不会去要糖的）。
english_item:
I’m going trick-or-treating first time in my life. I’m 42.
We grew up in a place where Halloween wasn’t a thing. I’m accompanying my nieces tonight and absolutely thrilled. I have a dinosaur costume (and obviously not picking any candies).

chinese_item:
一个我不认识的小孩认出了我

这周我开始参加社区剧院秋季演出的排练，我负责灯光。因为去年我也参演了，所以认识几个演员。

我刚坐下，就从包里拿出一个茶杯和一个保温壶（我没有足够大的保温瓶，怕被制片人折腾）。一个我不认识的小孩突然惊呼。

“你是送茶的阿姨！我妈妈上次也参演了，她说有个看起来很吓人但人很好的人，还给我送茶！你今天也留长指甲了吗？”

我有点懵，给她看了看我的手。

“哇，你是真人！我得告诉我妈！”

她跑去找她妈妈了，她妈妈去年好像也是演员，我可能帮她换过一次衣服？我们其实没怎么说话。我没想到我给她留下了那么深的印象，但显然，一年后她的孩子仅凭描述就能认出我。
english_item:
A child i didn't know recognized me
This week I started attending rehearsals for my community theater's fall production, where I will be running the lights. I'm acquaintances with a few cast members, since I was cast in last year's show. 

I had just settled in to my seat and pulled a teacup and insulated carafe (I do not own a large enough thermos to get me through the producer's meddling) from my bag. A child I don't recognize gasps.

"You're the tea lady! My mom was in the play last time and said there was a person who looked scary but was nice and brought tea! Do you have long nails today too?"

Somewhat baffled, I showed her my hands.

"Woah you're real! I gotta tell my mom!"

She ran off to her mother, who was apparently also a cast member last year I had maybe helped with a quick change once? We never really talked. I didn't realize I made that much of an impression but apparently it was enough for her kid to know me a year later from description alone.

chinese_item:
潜意识交流

我们结婚三十年了。能说的话已经不多了。但我们仍然“交流”。而且交流很多。

今天早上，我跌跌撞撞地走进厨房找咖啡，发出一些急促的低语。我丈夫正看着地板上的东西。他抬起头，发出“咕噜”一声。我问：“？”他指着地板上猫咪留下的“礼物”。我重复着他的“咕噜”声。

我们互相看着对方，同时重重地叹了口气。然后我去拿拖把，他去拿纸巾清理那团巨大的毛球。

即使没有语言，交流也不会停止。
english_item:
Sub Verbal Communication
We've been married for 30 years. There just aren't that many words left. But we still 'talk'. A lot.

This morning I stumble into the kitchen looking for coffee and making desperate little noises. My husband is looking at something on the floor. He looks up and makes a "rrr" noise. I go "?". He points at the spot on the floor where one of the cats left us a present. I repeat his "rrr".

We look at each other, sigh heavily in unison. Then I go for the mop while he gets the paper towels to clean up the massive hairball.

Just because there are no words doesn't mean communication stops

chinese_item:
为什么保健品公司非要把维生素软糖做得这么好吃？

他们明明知道最多只能吃一两颗，却还是做成蓝莓味的。

我快要崩溃了，想把一整盒蓝莓味南非醉茄软糖都吃光，因为它们太好吃了，我根本控制不住自己，健康什么的都见鬼去吧。
english_item:
Why do health companies have to make vitamin gummies so delicious
They know full well the maximum is one or two yet they make them taste like blue raspberry flavour 

Im about to crash out and eat a whole container of blue raspberry ashwaganda gummies because they're so delicious I just cant help it, health be damned.

chinese_item:
我总是忍不住用球打到我的狗，即使我努力不打到它。

我数过了。当我努力不打到它的时候，有一半的概率我会打到它。当我努力想打到它的时候，有四分之一的概率我会打到它。但之后我会感到内疚，因为我努力想打到它，结果还是打到了。所以我努力不打到它，结果反而打到它的概率更高了。

我的狗觉得这很有趣。
english_item:
I keep hitting my dog with his ball when I'm trying to not hit him with his ball
I counted. When I try to *not* hit my dog, I hit him half the time. When I try *to* hit the dog, I hit him 1/4 of the time. But then I feel guilty because I tried to hit him and then I did. So I try not to hit him. And hit him more.

My dog thinks it's great fun.

chinese_item:
昨天我淋了雨

昨天我（23岁，男）在房间里用音响听歌。我开始跟着音乐的节奏拍打身体，快速而轻柔地拍打着肚子、腿和臀部。我完全沉浸其中。我脱掉上衣，继续拍打着躯干，依然跟着歌曲重复的节奏。这就像在给自己做一次有趣的按摩。

接下来发生的事我完全无法理解。歌曲结束后，我感觉精力充沛，必须得动起来。我必须做点什么。于是我走到外面的小花园，开始像小狗玩耍一样绕着圈跑。

然后我想，不如离开这里吧。于是我光着膀子跑出家门，沿着家附近的街道一路狂奔。我感觉自己精力旺盛，需要好好释放。

最后，我来到家附近的一片森林，躺在一棵倒下的树干上。开始下雨了。我的天哪。我感觉自己仿佛置身于热带丛林之中。我没穿上衣，所以能感觉到每一滴水落在胸口的感觉。真是太有趣了，就像冰凉的痒痒痒一样。

我的裤子完全湿透了，但我并不在意。反正我就住在附近，所以这不成问题。

我觉得轻拍全身刺激到了我们日常生活中很少触及的皮肤部位，比如胸侧、腹部和手臂。我也不知道，我的理论是，这种刺激让我的身体感到兴奋之类的。

我很高兴自己有这段空闲时间，可以完全放松，让身体引领我。我感觉自己真正地活着。
english_item:
I was under the rain yesterday
Yesterday I (23M) was listening to a song in the loudspeakers of my room. I started to tap my body following the rhythm of the music, banging quickly but gently my stomach, my legs, my hips. I was being carried away. I took my shirt off to tap my trunk, still following the repetitive pattern of the tune. It was like a fun massage I was giving myself.

I cannot understand what happened next. When the song finished I felt so energetic I had to move. I had to do something. So I went outside to my small garden and started to literally run in circles, like a dog playing. 

Then I thought, let's get out of here. And I left my house shirtless and ran down the streets near my house. I had so much energy to spend.

I ended up in a forest near my house, where I lied down on the fallen trunk of a tree. It started to rain. Oh my god. I felt I was in a tropical jungle. I didn't have my shirt on, so I could feel every sprinkle of water against my chest. It was so funny. Like cold tickles. 

My trousers got completely soaked but I didn't care. It wasn't a problem as I lived up the road.

I believe that tapping my whole body stimulated my skin in places that are rerely sensed in our daily interactions, like the sides of the chest, or the stomach, or the arms. I don't know, my theory is that this stimulation made my body be excited or something like that.

I am so happy I had the free time to let myself be carried away, letting my body lead. I truly felt alive.

chinese_item:
出门忘了带手机

今天我做一道菜，需要的糖比家里的多，所以我决定出去买一些。因为刚才在看菜谱，我把手机忘在家里的台面上了。走到街对面才发现手机没连上车。我知道不会走太久，所以就继续往前走，但感觉……竟然有点自由？我知道这听起来很傻，毕竟只是个15分钟就能搞定的事，但那几分钟里，我意识到没人能联系到我。真的，没人能追踪我的位置（我给朋友和老公开了定位——这是我自己的选择），没人能给我打电话或发短信，我也没法听有声书，也没法让自己分心，只想好好享受当下。这让我感觉自己好像又回到了青少年时代。我真的很享受这种感觉。
english_item:
Left the house without my phone on accident
Today I was making a recipe that called for more sugar than I had - so I decided to run out and go grab some more. I had left my phone on the counter at home due to me reading off the recipe. I got about halfway down the street before realizing I had due to my phone not connecting to the car. I decided to continue on because I knew I wouldn’t be gone long, but it was…oddly freeing ? I know that sounds silly because this was a 15 min errand I was doing, but for those few minutes I realized that no one would be able to contact me. At all. No one would could track where I was at (I have locations on for my friend and husband-my choice), no one could call or text me, I had no ability to listen to my audiobook, or be distracted from just being in that moment. It gave me a strange sense of being a teenager again. I really enjoyed it.

chinese_item:
我的邻居知道我会为她做任何事。

我（23岁，女）和这位女士做了21年的邻居。她有两个儿子，分别比我大2-3岁和4-5岁，他们早就搬出去了。现在房子里只有她一个人，她也知道隔壁只有我一个人（我父亲去世后我继承了这套房子），所以我们一直互相照应。有一天，我发现大概一个星期没看到她的车了，就联系她确认她是不是回老家探亲了，是不是出了什么事。她确实在度假，她的小儿子记下了我的电话号码，给我发了条信息，告诉我有人在他不在的时候照顾她，她有多感动。

今天，我去街上买菜，她告诉我她把自己锁在门外了，联系不上锁匠。几分钟后我回到家，发现她正在外面和另一个邻居聊天，便走过去看看。她告诉我有人正在路上，我说：“好的。如果你开着窗户，我很乐意从那里钻进去，我甚至能从狗门钻过去。”

她看了看邻居，又看了看我，笑着说：“（我的名字）会为我做任何事的 :)”。我笑着告诉她，她说的完全正确。

她是我社区里很重要的一员，能得到她的肯定，知道我也是她社区里重要的人，我感到非常欣慰。不到一个小时，她就回来了。
english_item:
my neighbor knows i would do anything for her
i (23f) have lived next to the same woman for 21 years now. she has two sons that are older than me by 2-3 years and 4-5 years, and they’ve both long since moved out. it’s just her in the house now, and she knows that it is just me next door (inherited after my dad passed) and we make a conscious effort to look out for each other. one day i realized i hadn’t seen her car in about a week, and reached out to double check that she was visiting family in her home country and that nothing was wrong. she was indeed on vacation, and her younger son took down my number so he could send me a message letting me know how much it means to know someone looks out for her when he’s not there. 

today, i’m up the street grabbing groceries and she lets me know she has locked herself out and can’t get ahold of a locksmith. i get home a few minutes later to find her outside chatting with another neighbor and walk over to check in. she lets me know someone is on the way, and i say “okay. because if you had left a window open i’d be more than happy to shimmey through it, i can fit through my doggy door even”.
 in response, she looks at the neighbor and back at me and says “(my name) would do anything for me :)” with a big smile. i laugh and tell her she is absolutely right. 
she is an important part of my village, and it is so nice to be validated in the fact that i am that for her too. she got back in within the hour too.

chinese_item:
网络上还是有很多好人的。

我昨天心情有点低落——有时候脑子里会冒出一些不好的想法，通常我都会忽略它们。但昨天不知怎么的，我没能控制住这些想法，所以为了发泄一下，我就把它发到了另一个子版块，也没想太多。结果突然我的手机就被私信和回复刷屏了。大部分都是祝福和鼓励的信息。说实话，昨天这些真的让我心情好多了。看来网络上还是有很多好人的！
english_item:
There are good ppl in the net
I was a bit down yesterday - sometimes bad thoughts come into your head and usually I dismiss it. For some reason yesterday I couldn't fight it, so to let it off my chest, I just posted it in another subReddit, didn't think too much about it. Suddenly my phone was blowing off w dms and replies to my post. Mostly well wishers and motivating msgs. Ngl, made my day yesterday. There are some real good people in the internet after all!

chinese_item:
每次听到《扬帆远航》（Come Sail Away）这首歌，我都会想起我的高中男友。

他每年都会在我生日那天给我发邮件。他是个好人。
english_item:
I think of my high school boyfriend every time I hear Come Sail Away.
He emails me on my birthday every year. He's a good dude.

chinese_item:
我找到我的传家宝补针了——就在它们应该在的地方！

我有一些奶奶留给我的补针，她以前用它们织毛衣，现在我也用。我们俩一直不太合得来，但纤维艺术是我们之间真正能产生共鸣的共同点。

我把最喜欢的两根针放在一个小药管里，方便随身携带。后来我好像把它们弄丢了，真是太沮丧了。我把毛衣盒翻来覆去地找了好几遍，把家里翻了个底朝天，最后还是放弃了，心想这么个小东西总有一天会自己出现的。

结果，我惊喜地发现药管竟然就在我的毛衣盒里！我发誓它一定是瞬间移动过来的，因为它就在我之前找过好几遍的地方。我立刻把针放进一个更大、更显眼的盒子里——其实这个盒子也是奶奶留给我的——希望这次它们不会再丢了！
english_item:
Found my heirloom darning needles - right where they should've been!
I have a few darning needles from my late grandma, she used them in her knitting and now I do too. We always struggled to understand each other, but fibre arts were one thing we truly connected over. 

I'd put my two favourite needles in a little pill tube to carry them around conveniently, and was super upset when I seemed to have misplaced them. I turned my knitting kit upside down and inside out several times, hunted through the house and finally gave up on finding such a small thing. I decided it would turn up when it wanted to. 

Imagine my surprise when the tube turned up right in my knitting kit! I swear it must have teleported because it was exactly where I had looked multiple times already. I immediately rehoused the needles in a larger, more distinctive case - actually one that I also inherited from Grandma - so hopefully they will be harder to lose this time!

chinese_item:
人们对“你现在在干什么？”这类信息的回复

- 准备明天的午餐：*女性，30多岁*

- 用记号笔修补指甲上的缺口：*女性，30多岁*

- 嗯，没什么：*男性，20多岁*

- 有点担心。我不知道冷藏箱应该设置多少温度，也不知道怎么查看温度：*男性，40多岁*

- 清除了缓存。忘了记密码。今晚就到此为止了：*男性，30多岁*

- 和闺蜜们一起看电影：*女性，60多岁*

- 在上班。没什么特别的：*男性，30多岁*

- 睡觉：*男性，30多岁*

- 没什么。弹了会儿吉他。给几个公寓的线索打了电话。疲惫：*40多岁男性*

*过去几周，我在不同日期的晚上9点到午夜之间给不同的人发了这条信息。我问他们在做什么。当对方回答“没事”或“为什么？”等等时，我解释说我只是想知道他们收到信息时正在做什么。我对结果很满意！*
english_item:
People’s response to “What are you doing right now?” messages
-Packing lunch for tomorrow: *Female, 30s* 


-Trying to fix my chipped nail with a sharpie: *Female, 30s* 


-Eh, nothing: *Male, 20s*





-Worried. I don’t know what temperature a cooler is supposed to be and I don’t know how to check it’s temperature regardless: *Male, 40s* 


-Cleared my cache. Didn’t write down my passwords. That’s gonna be my whole night: *Male, 30s* 





-Movie night with the girls: *Female, 60s*



-At work. Not much: *Male, 30s* 



-Sleeping: *Male, 30s*



-Nothing. Played some guitar. Called some leads on apartments. Tired: *Male 40s*



*Sent to different people between 9:00pm and midnight on different days the last couple weeks. Asked people what they were doing. When told ‘nothing’ or ‘why?’ etc. I clarified I just wanted to know exactly what they were doing when they got my message. I am happy with the results!*

chinese_item:
绗缝婴儿毯

六年前，我在俄勒冈州照顾一位非常和蔼的女士，她做了个手术，具体是什么我已经记不清了。当时我明显怀孕了，我们聊起我打算把婴儿房布置成森林主题，她主动提出要给我做一条被子。我其实没指望她真的会做，但我还是给了她我的邮寄地址。几个月后，她真的寄来了一条手工绗缝被！被子很漂亮，上面有狐狸、兔子和松鼠的图案。即使我的孩子们还很小的时候，我也一直很爱惜它，现在它仍然铺在他们的床上。

事情就出在这里——装被子的盒子上有她的地址，我把它放在一边，想给她写封感谢信。我没有拍照，也没有做任何记录。几天后，我去拿盒子的时候，发现有人好心地把盒子清理干净了，盒子被扔掉了。

那是我唯一一次和她联系，我至今仍后悔没能当面感谢她为我和儿子做的那条毯子。所以，这位好心的陌生人，请知道我们都很感激你的善举，你的好意我们至今仍铭记于心。很抱歉我没能当面感谢你。
english_item:
Quilted Baby Blanket
6 years ago I was the nurse for a very nice lady in Oregon who had some surgery I can't recall anymore. I was visibly pregnant and we talked about my plan for a woodland themed nursery and she offered to make me a quilt. I didn't really expect her to do it, but I gave her my mailing address and a few months later she did actually send me a handmade quilt! It was beautiful and has foxes, rabbits, and squirrels on it. I kept it nice even when my kids were really little and it's still in rotation on their bed.
So here's where I messed up-- the box it came in had her address on it, and I set it aside so I could write her a thank you letter. I didn't take a picture of it or anything else, and a few days later I went to get the box to find that someone had helpfully cleaned up, and the box was thrown out.
That was my only contact for her, and I still regret not being able to thank her for making that blanket for me and my son. So, kind stranger, know you are appreciated and still thought of for your act of RandomActsofKindness. I'm sorry I never got to thank you for real.

chinese_item:
老公去超市帮我采购了。

有点不好意思说，我来例假的时候得了尿路感染，感觉糟透了。我老公现在正在超市，他一边给我发短信，一边确认他买的都是我需要的东西。我真幸运，嫁给了一个不会对帮我买卫生巾这件事感到尴尬的男人。我很感激他能在我不舒服的时候照顾我。
english_item:
Husband is at the store doing our running
So TMI, but I have a UTI while on my period and I'm feeling awful. My husband is at the store right now, and he's texting me to make sure he gets the exact right items that I need. I'm so lucky that I'm married to a man who doesn't have a weird hangup about picking up pads for his wife. I'm glad he puts in the effort to take care of me when I'm not feeling well.

chinese_item:
终于用我的“放纵日”吃了心心念念了一整周的巧克力牛角包！

我每天早上都会路过一家面包店，他们家的巧克力牛角包看起来总是那么诱人。我一直把它留到“放纵日”享用，在脑海里把它吹捧得天花乱坠，仿佛它会改变我的人生。所以今天我终于买了一个，回家后泡了杯咖啡，像对待一件大事一样坐下来。结果第一口就让我失望了。牛角包很干，巧克力味几乎可以忽略不计，而且吃起来好像放了很久。但我还是把它吃完了，因为我不想浪费我的“放纵日”，只是感觉自己被自己的期望骗了。
english_item:
Finally used my cheat day on that chocolate croissant I’ve been eyeing all week
There’s this bakery I walk past every morning, and their chocolate croissants always look unreal. I’ve been saving it for my cheat day, hyping it up in my head like it was going to be life-changing. So today I finally got one, went home, made some coffee, sat down like it was a big event. First bite and, disappointment. The pastry was dry, barely any chocolate, and it tasted kind of old. I still ate the whole thing because I wasn’t about to waste my cheat day, but it felt like I got scammed by my own expectations.

chinese_item:
我觉得我家厨房里的蜘蛛在密谋什么。

整个夏天它们都各自待在自己的角落里，但今晚这四只蜘蛛竟然都聚集在同一个角落里。这是怎么回事？
english_item:
I think the spiders in my kitchen are plotting something
All summer they kept to their own corners, but tonight all four of them have congregated in the same one. What's up with that?

chinese_item:
酒保还记得我

大约七个月前，我搬离了大学城里常去的酒吧。2023年的时候，我几乎每个周末都会去一两次。2024年，我暂时搬到了现在住的地方。我最喜欢的那位酒保，每周要接待成百上千的客人，却依然记得我。每次见到他，他都会免费给我一杯。
english_item:
Bartender remembered me
I moved away from my old college town bars about 7 months ago. I used to go every weekend 1-2x in 2023. In 2024 I temporarily moved to where I live currently. My favorite bartender, who sees hundreds of people a week, still remembers me. He always gives me a free shot when I see him.

chinese_item:
有人喝光了我的咖啡（其实是我自己喝的）

我每天都喝咖啡。有时候，喝完一杯后，我会垂头丧气地看着杯底，沮丧地自言自语道：“有人喝光了我的咖啡！”

然后，有时候我还会再去喝一杯。
english_item:
Someone Drank All My Coffee (it was me)
I'm a daily coffee drinker. And some days when I finish a cup I like to morosely declare aloud "Someone has drank-en all of my coffees" as I look forlornly at the now visible bottom of my mug. 
Then sometimes I go have a second round.

chinese_item: 美国总统特朗普近日接受福克斯新闻频道主持人劳拉·英格拉哈姆采访时，对公众担忧的经济问题不以为然，他表示：不管怎样，我总能从某些地方搞到钱，这没什么大不了的。"
english_item: ‘I always get the money from someplace regardless, it doesn’t matter,’ president tells Fox News host Laura Ingraham in latest interview as he dismisses public’s concerns about the economy

chinese_item: 医疗监管机构警告，假冒减肥注射剂不仅会让人们生病，在某些情况下还可能致命。
english_item: Fake weight-loss jabs are not only making people ill, but in some cases, they could be fatal, the medical watchdog warns

chinese_item: 埃里克·加西亚撰文指出，当民主党选民希望他们的领导人摩拳擦掌准备战斗时，这位纽约土生土长的少数党领袖却无力管控预期，也无法争取到共和党人的选票，这让他显得软弱无能、茫然无措。
english_item: New York-native minority leader’s inability to manage expectations or secure votes from Republicans has left him looking impotent and adrift at a time when Democratic voters want their leaders itching for a fight, Eric Garcia writes.

chinese_item: 尽管政府官员呼吁和平，但他们对美国的军事威胁公开表示不屑一顾。
english_item: Government officials publicly have been dismissive of a U.S. military threat, though they have called for peace

chinese_item: 作弊团队被抓到在智能手表上窃窃私语地传递问题——还在音乐环节使用Shazam软件识别曲目。
english_item: The cheating team were caught whispering questions into their smartwatches – and using Shazam for the music round

chinese_item: 卡米拉在奇彭纳姆车站受到学生们手持虞美人花的欢迎。
english_item: Camilla was greeted at Chippenham station by pupils with poppies

chinese_item: 这位播音员和儿童热线的创始人表示，SafeCall将为那些感到被忽视、无人倾听的孩子们提供一条生命线。她呼吁读者们支持《独立报》与失踪人口组织联合发起的这项活动。
english_item: The broadcaster and Childline founder says SafeCall will offer a lifeline to children who feel unseen and unheard, as she urges readers to back The Independent’s campaign with Missing People

chinese_item: 在一封公开信中，他们驳斥了关于自身是种族迫害乃至种族灭绝受害者的说法，指出这种论调不仅具有误导性，更是极其危险的。
english_item: In an open letter, they rejected the narrative that they were the victims of racial persecution and even genocide, saying that it was ‘not only misleading, but dangerous’

chinese_item: 诉讼称，登录和设置数字工作站可能花费员工长达30分钟的时间，如果遇到技术问题，时间可能更长，而且这些时间都没有报酬。
english_item: Logging in and setting up digital workstations could take employees up to 30 minutes — or longer in the event of technical issues — and it was all uncompensated, according to the lawsuit

chinese_item: 十多年来，网上开始出现数万张照片，显示巴沙尔·阿萨德总统统治下叙利亚境内残缺不全的尸体和酷刑场所。
english_item: It’s been more than a decade since tens of thousands of photos started appearing online showing the broken bodies and torture sites under President Bashar Assad’s rule in Syria

chinese_item: 我现在读八年级。六年级和七年级因为疫情一直在家上网课，今年才勉强回到学校上课。刚开始的时候，我意识到一切都变了。我不得不重新学习如何社交，结交新朋友。以前的朋友都走了。一开始我的情况很糟糕——我太害怕和任何人说话，甚至不敢举手发言或回答问题。只有被直接问到或被告知时，我才会开口。这太难了，因为这根本不是我！不过，有一位老师看穿了我的内心。我选修了她的课，她总是尽可能地把话题引到我身上，让我的同学们听到我的想法。每次这样，我都会变得更开朗一些。现在仍然很困难，但我正在努力变得更勇敢。然后，她做了一件我做梦都想不到的事：她提名我为年度学生！那段时间我情绪低落，她让我感受到她对我的信任。因为她，我每天都更加努力地为自己而奋斗。家里和学校的日子都很艰难，但我依然坚持下去。因为她，因为她相信我。
english_item: I'm currently in 8th grade. I had been in Remote Learning for 6th and 7th grade (because of COVID) and barely came back to in-person learning this year. When I first started, I realized how much things had changed. I had to relearn how to socialize and make new friends. All my old friends were gone. I was in a really bad place at the beginning - I was too scared to talk to anyone and to even raise my hand to share or answer a question. I only spoke when directly asked or told something. It was so hard because that wasn't who I was!However, there was one teacher who managed to see through it. I was in her elective class and she, whenever she could, directly pointed the conversation to me so my classmates could hear what I had to say. I became a little more open every time. It's still difficult now but I'm working on being braver. Then she did what I would've never expected: she nominated me for Student of the Year! That was a time when I was at a low moment and she showed me how much she believed in me. I'm fighting a little harder each day for myself because of her. It's been hard at home and school but I'm still going. Because of her, because she believes in me.

chinese_item: 我经历过的最伟大的善举，发生在我最悲痛的时刻——我九岁的女儿因先天性心脏病去世之后。当时我们刚在几个县外的山区买了一栋大房子。交房手续即将完成，我们正准备搬家，包括为女儿寻找新的医生和医院。她对搬进大房子充满了期待。然而，就在我们即将完成交房手续前不久，她在睡梦中去世了。我们当时经济拮据，所有的积蓄都投入到了房子里。一位善良慷慨的同事E.W.主动承担起这项责任，她开设了一个银行账户（这远在GoFundMe出现之前），并联系了她认识的所有人，包括个人、组织、教会团体，甚至商店，为我女儿的葬礼筹款，以便我们能够继续搬家。她筹集了超过4000美元。在1989年，这笔钱绰绰有余。我们得以搬进新家，并将女儿安葬在附近美丽的当地墓园。我并不认识大多数捐款的人，但他们和 E.W. 一样，永远活在我的心中。
english_item: The greatest act of RandomActsofKindness shown to me was after my greatest grief, the loss of my 9 year old daughter who was born with a congenital heart defect. We had just purchased a large house several counties away in a rural mountain area. Settlement was just around the corner and we were preparing to move; including getting a new doctor and hospital for our daughter. She was excited about moving to a big house. She died in her sleep shortly before we were to make settlement. We were not prepared financially, all our money was tied up with the house. A wonderful kind generous coworker E.W took it upon herself to open a bank fund (this was long before GoFundMe) and send everyone she ever knew, people, organizations, church groups.and even stores for money to cover my daughter's burial so we cold continue with our move. She raised over $4,000. In 1989 this was more than enough. We were able to move into the house and have our daughter buried in the beautiful local cemetery close to it. I didn't know most of the people who donated but they are all forever in my heart along with E.W.

chinese_item: 十几岁的时候我们家并不富裕，但我女朋友总是会给我买东西……嗯，是亲手做的，比如用口香糖包装纸做成的心形小玩意儿和纸条……虽然不多，但正是这些小事最能打动人心。
english_item: as a teenager we dont have a lot of money but my girlfriend always made sure to give me stuff... well make me stuff like gum wraper hearts and notes... it not much but its the tiny things that count

chinese_item: 多年前，我在一家公司上班，因为开了一个玩笑开得有点过火，那个人从此不再跟我说话。他在公司里出了名的喜欢跟人断绝关系。后来我在《指南针》（Guideposts）杂志上看到一篇文章，讲的是一个孩子送给另一个跟他闹矛盾的孩子一块自制软糖，结果两人关系就和好了。我决定也用这招对付我的同事。有人提醒我，这招可能有两种结果：一是他会欣然接受，一切顺利；二是他会把软糖摔在我的桌子上，大吵大闹。我还是决定试一试，结果出乎意料，这个人竟然第一次跟我说话了，我们之间的矛盾也迎刃而解，让整个办公室的人都感到惊讶。这些年来，我多次使用这招，效果都很好。
english_item: Years ago I was working in an office where I had taken a joke too far and that person was no longer speaking to me. He was known around the office as a person who would cut people off permanently. I read an article in Guideposts about a kid sending another kid with whom he had a disagreement with some home-baked fudge and the relationship was restored. I decided to do the same thing with my co-worker. I was warned it could go one of two ways 1) he could accept the gift and no problems or 2) he could slam the fudge down on my desk and make a scene. I took the chance and it turned out for the first time this person actually spoke to me and our differences were resolved to the amazement of the whole office. I have used this technique many times over the years to good results.

chinese_item: （顺便说一下，这不是我的故事，只是我当时在场的一件善事！）在我以前的学校，八年级的走廊在铃响的时候总是很拥挤。那时我读六年级。我当时最好的朋友因为扭伤了脚踝，拄着拐杖，我正在帮她。我们尽量（也应该）提前至少三分钟离开教室，这样她就可以拄着拐杖轻松地走完剩下的课。我们因为要先去储物柜拿东西而耽搁了一会儿，铃声就响了……而要到放学区，我们必须穿过八年级的走廊。有人差点踢到我朋友的拐杖。这时，一个八年级的男生看到了我们，决定帮忙。他帮我们开路，穿过了所有（当时在六年级的我看来，他们个头都很大）八年级的学生。他确保我们安全到达放学区后才去坐校车（我们是家长接孩子）。我永远不会忘记这位好心人，他帮助我和我的朋友穿过拥挤的走廊。
english_item: (Just an FYI, this is not my story, just an act of RandomActsofKindness that I was there for!) At my old school, the 8th grade hallways were always pretty congested when the bell rang. I was in 6th grade at the time. I was helping my best friend at the time who was on crutches due to a sprained ankle, and we tried to (and were supposed to) leave class at least 3 minutes early that way she could get through the classes easily with her crutches. We were a bit too late because we had to stop at our lockers, and the bell rang...and to get to the dismissal area, we had to go through the 8th grade hallway. Someone almost kicked my friend's crutch out from under her. An 8th grade boy then saw us struggling, and he decided to help. He was clearing a path for us through all the (at the time they seemed huge to 6th grade me) 8th graders. He made sure we made it to the dismissal area OK before going to the busses (we were parent pickups). I will always remember that kind person who helped my friend and I get through the crowded halls.

chinese_item: 走在街上，我注意到一位老妇人正费力地提着购物袋。我毫不犹豫地上前帮忙。当我帮她把袋子搬到家门口时，她眼中闪烁着感激的光芒。她连声道谢，告诉我丈夫去世后她一直过得很艰难。我的善举让她感动得热泪盈眶。第二天，我在家门口发现了一个她亲手做的馅饼和一张饱含深情的便条，上面写着：“你让我意识到，世间依然有善意。” 那一刻让我明白，即使是最小的善举也能对一个人的生活产生深远的影响。
english_item: As I walked through the city streets, I noticed an elderly woman struggling to carry her groceries. Without hesitation, I offered to help. Her eyes lit up with gratitude as I carried her bags to her doorstep. She thanked me profusely and shared that she'd been struggling since her husband's passing. My small act brought tears of joy to her eyes. The next day, I found a homemade pie and a heartfelt note on my doorstep from her, saying, "You reminded me that RandomActsofKindness still exists." That moment taught me that even the smallest gestures can profoundly impact someone's life.

chinese_item:
有一次期末考试的时候，我忘了带充电器，结果在做考试项目的时候笔记本电脑没电了。我当时压力很大，不知道该怎么办。班里有个女生注意到我的窘境，就走过来问我：“怎么了？” 我告诉她之后，她把自己的充电器给了我，说：“我用完了，你用它完成你的项目吧。”

她这简单的举动真的帮了我大忙——我最终完成了项目。从那以后，我也一直努力用同样的方式帮助别人，因为有时候一个小小的举动真的可以改变一个人的一整天。
english_item:
One time during finals, I forgot my charger and my laptop battery died while I was working on my exam project. I was really stressed and didn?ˉt know what to do. There was a girl in the same class who noticed how stressed I was. She came over and asked me, What?ˉs wrong?When I told her, she gave me her charger and said, ?°I?ˉm done using it, go ahead and use it to finish your project.?±
That simple act really saved me?aI was able to finish my project. Since then, I?ˉve been trying to help others the same way, because sometimes a small act can really change someones whole day.

chinese_item: 我是一个极度害羞、社交能力极差的青少年。很长一段时间里，我唯一的朋友就是书。最近，我因为参加一个会议，不得不和这两个女孩一起过夜。我担心她们会抱团，而我只能孤零零地抱着我那本翻烂了的《绿山墙的安妮》。但事实完全出乎我的意料。这两个女孩对我非常友善，帮助我走出了内向的性格，并积极地和我聊天。我非常喜欢圣诞节，当我提到这一点时，其中一个女孩借口去洗手间。结果她并没有去洗手间，而是从我们之前去过的那家附近的商店里拿回来一根圣诞树蜡烛。现在，每次我拿着这根蜡烛走进房间，都能闻到美妙的圣诞香气——同时也会想起这两位可爱的新朋友。
english_item: I am a hopelessly shy, socially inept teenager. For the longest time, my only friends were books. Recently, I had to stay overnight for a conference with these two girls. I worried that they would be cliquey and I would be stuck with my well-worn copy of "Anne of Green Gables". But this could not be farther from the truth. These girls were endlessly kind to me, helping me come out of my shell and engaging me in their conversations.
