我需要买东西的那条过道里只有两个人，我赶紧挪了挪，自助结账台前也只有两个人。

天哪，真是松了一口气！

如果不是长周末，我估计早就回家换衣服了。
english_item:
I’m so glad it’s the Labour Day weekend
I went out in shorts totally forgetting I haven’t shaved my legs since April.

I really wanted to go home and put on pants (like I always wear due to hairy legs), but I told myself I could do it.

All I needed to do was pick something up at the grocery store on my way to my friend’s place.

Pulling into the parking lot, I was so relieved that it was almost empty.

There were two people in the aisle where I needed something and I moved quickly and there were only two people at the self check out.

Holy, big Whew!!!!

If it wasn’t a long weekend I suspect I would have gone home and changed.

chinese_item:
午夜漫步，感觉仿佛置身于另一个城市。

最近事情很多，唉，人生就是如此。昨晚失眠难眠，于是我套上连帽衫，凌晨一点左右出门。街上几乎空无一人，没有车辆，也没有闲聊。只有路灯和自己的脚步声。我漫无目的地走着，没有计划。然后，我开始注意到许多白天从未留意的小细节，比如24小时面包店飘来的新鲜面包香气，人行道上心形的小裂缝，以及即使空无一人，交通灯依然不停地变换。

这听起来或许有些平淡，因为并没有发生什么惊天动地的大事。我只是漫无目的地走着，直到双腿发软，才回家。那一刻，感觉这座城市仿佛按下了暂停键，我看到了它午夜后才会显露的秘密一面。

依我看，这感觉出奇地平静，仿佛世界暂时都属于你了。
english_item:
Went for a midnight walk and it felt like a whole different city
Been dealing with a lot of stuff lately because, well, life happens. Last night was one of those nights when I couldn't sleep. So I threw on a hoodie and headed out around 1 a.m. The streets were almost completely empty. There's no traffic and no random conversations. Everything was just the streetlights and the sound of my own footsteps. I didn’t have a plan but to just keep walking. Then I ended up noticing all these little things I never pay attention to during the day, like the smell of fresh bread from a 24-hour bakery, a tiny crack in the sidewalk shaped like a heart, the way the traffic lights keep changing even when nobody’s there. 
 
It may sound anticlimactic because nothing huge really happened. I just wandered until my legs felt heavy and that's when I headed home. For a while, it felt like the city was on pause. It's as if I was seeing a secret version of it that only comes out after midnight.

It feels weirdly calming if you ask me, almost like the world belongs to you for a bit.

chinese_item:
给妈妈买了份礼物

终于用上大学打工攒的钱，给妈妈买了一套对我来说极其昂贵的面霜套装作为生日礼物（虽然她下个月初才过生日，但我太兴奋了，还是提前送给了她）。包装很精美，她收到后非常感动。她还说以前从来没有人送过她这样的礼物。我非常高兴买了这份礼物，尽管销售员明显看出我看着那些价格有点不自在，而且我还得尴尬地跟银行解释这不是欺诈交易，我的卡才能成功支付。:)))
english_item:
Bought my mother a gift
Finally used what I saved from working in college to buy my mother an extremely expensive (to me) facial cream set for her birthday (early next month but I got too excited and gave it to her anyways). The packaging was lovely and she looked very touched receiving it. She also mentioned that nobody had given her something like that before. I am incredibly glad that I got this gift even though the sales associate definitely flagged that I was uncomfortable I looking at those prices AND I had to awkwardly tell my bank that it was not a fraudulent transaction so my card could go through. :)))

chinese_item:
我的游戏角色设计居然有人夸奖了！

我玩的是《星际战甲》（Warframe），可能有些人不了解这款游戏，它提供一系列不同的“战斗服”，叫做“战甲”（Warframe）。你可以改变它们的颜色，还可以添加收集到的各种配件。我的主角色是拉沃斯Prime（Prime是普通战甲的升级版，外观更酷炫）。拉沃斯是个蛇形炼金术士，所以我当然要把他打扮得非常邪恶！我把他的主体颜色设定为绿色，一种亮眼的毒绿色，搭配白色和黑色点缀，最后用深紫色勾勒出反派的气质。

我玩了一局公开匹配，这种匹配通常节奏很快，进进出出，在随机匹配中很少和队友交流。所以，当我在聊天框里看到有人夸奖我的拉沃斯时，我真的非常开心！有人说喜欢他那酷炫的绿色造型，而且还有人表示赞同！我没有很多别人常用的时尚配饰，所以能靠配色就足够酷炫，真是太棒了 :)
english_item:
Got a compliment on my game character’s design
I play Warframe, and for those who don’t know the game, you get a selection of different “battle-suits” called Warframes. You can change their colors and add in accessories that you collect. My main is Lavos Prime (primes are upgraded versions of a standard Warframe, and they have a cooler appearance). Lavos is a snake-alchemist themed guy, so of course, I had to make him look positively toxic! So I made him mainly green, a bright toxic green with white and black accents, trimmed with a deep purple for that villainous look. 

So I played a public game and usually they’re pretty fast, you just go in and out and don’t really talk to the crew in these random match situations. So it was really awesome to see in the chat box a compliment on my Lavos that another person loved the awesome green look, and another person agreed! I don’t have many accessories that other people usually use for cool fashion, so it’s extra awesome that my color scheme alone is cool enough :)

chinese_item:
购物清单

我的伴侣在购物清单上写了“好咖啡”。我站在过道里足足五分钟，手里拿着我们常喝的那款咖啡。我把它放进购物车，然后又拿出来放回去。最后，我买了另一种稍微贵一点的咖啡，想看看周二喝上一杯会不会感觉像是一种特别的犒劳。结果确实如此。
english_item:
The grocery list
My partner wrote "good coffee" on the grocery list. I stood in the aisle for a full five minutes, just holding a bag of our usual brand. I put it in the cart. Then I took it out and put it back. I ended up buying a different, slightly more expensive one, just to see if it would feel like a special treat on a Tuesday. It did.

chinese_item:
今天我收到一个拥抱了。

我平时很少收到拥抱。我喜欢拥抱。但我是一名教师，肢体接触是不允许的，而且我是无浪漫倾向者，所以没有伴侣。我很期待每年见几次叔叔，因为我知道他会拥抱我。今天，我遇到了一位好久没碰面的同事，她拥抱了我。我个子很娇小，她个子很高。那一刻，我感觉自己像个小孩子。感觉真好。
english_item:
I got a hug today
I don't get hugs. I like hugs. But I'm a teacher, and touching is a nono and I'm aromantic, so no partner. I look forward to seeing my uncle several times a year, because I know I'll get hugged. Well today, I saw a coworker I haven't run into in a while and she hugged me. I am a tiny woman. She is a tall woman. I felt like a little kid for just a second. It was nice.

chinese_item:
我救助了一只疲惫不堪的蜥蜴

月初的时候，我在后院的一个盒子里发现了一只西部栅栏蜥蜴。它几乎一动不动，即使我把它抱起来也是如此。当时外面有点冷，这可能加剧了它的痛苦。我把它放在花园的花坛里，旁边放了一碗水，然后上网搜索该怎么做。有人告诉我应该把它放在黑暗温暖的地方，并试着往它的鼻子上滴水，帮它补充水分。我把它放在我们放在外面的一块地毯的折叠处入口处，那折叠处看起来像个隧道。然后，在它的鼻子上滴了两三滴水之后，它就钻进了折叠处。

我不知道它之后怎么样了，但我为自己做了这件事感到很欣慰。虽然这只是一件微不足道的小事，而且这些蜥蜴可能处于食物链的顶端，但我认为，哪怕只是帮助一下弱小的生命，也能让我的自信心得到一些提升。
english_item:
I helped out a stressed and tired lizard
At the beginning of this month, I found a Western Fence Lizard trapped in a box we have in our backyard. It was barely moving, even as I picked it up. It was a little chilly out, which probably didn't help. I put it on our garden bed next to a bowl of water while I searched up what to do. I was told to put it in a dark and warm place and try dripping water on its snout to hydrate it a bit. I put it at the entrance of a fold of a rug that we left outside that looked kind of like a tunnel. Then, after about 2-3 drips of water on it's snout, it scurried into the fold. 
I don't know what happened to it afterwards, but I feel pretty good about myself for doing that. Though it was a very small act and those lizards are probably on the top of the food chain, but I think just being the least bit helpful to vulnerable lives boosts my confidence a bit.

chinese_item:
有时候我会翻到手机相册的最前面，只是为了找回以前的自己。

不是因为伤感，只是……看到那些零零散散的截图、模糊的照片和傻乎乎的表情包，感觉就像是回到了过去的自己身边，而我其实有点想念那个自己。
english_item:
Sometimes I scroll back to the start of my camera roll just to remember who I used to be.
Not in a sad way, just… seeing all those random screenshots, blurry pics, and dumb memes feels like visiting an old version of myself I kinda miss.

chinese_item:
我把东西都放在路边了。

今天是我在老房子的最后一天，我厌倦了把东西送到二手店。所以我开始在两次送货之间把东西放在街上，大多是些小玩意儿和一些我觉得别人会喜欢的零零碎碎的小东西。我家住在主干道旁，所以每次我回家准备送下一车东西的时候，东西都已经被拿走了。

就在这时，我把一个不错的吉他音箱放了出来，坐在前廊上，看着街上的人停下来看。很快就会有人把它拿走，然后它就消失了。我喜欢这种感觉，也许我能让别人开心！而且我真的很高兴这些东西不再是我的责任了。感觉如释重负。
english_item:
I just put it all on the nature strip
It's my last day at my old house, and I'm tired of dropping things off at the op shop. So instead I started leaving things out on the street in between trips, mostly knick knacks and random but nice items I think people would enjoy. I live on a main road so things are always gone by the time I get home to do another load.

Just then I put out a nice guitar amp and I'm sitting on my front porch watching people on the street stop and have a look. Soon someone will grab it and it'll be gone. I like the idea that maybe I'm making someone's day! And I really like the fact that these things aren't my responsibility anymore. What a weight off.

chinese_item:
我奶奶的手，她跟我说话时拍我手的方式，就像摩斯密码一样。

有时候，你不会真的注意到祖父母的年纪，仿佛时间在慢镜头里流逝。

但最近我注意到，奶奶在沙发上跟我说话时，如果她对谈话的内容感到兴奋，她常常会握住我的手，轻轻拍打。就像摩斯密码一样，用手指敲击我的手背，传递着信息。我越来越注意到她手上的细纹和褶皱，她手上还有一些小黑点，她总是把纸巾塞在袖子里，用小指和无名指夹着，贴在手掌上。正是这些小细节提醒我她的年龄，她的头脑依然敏锐，作为一位老太太，她依然很活跃，但有时岁月的痕迹还是会显露出来。我开始时不时地拍下她握着我的手的照片。
english_item:
My abuelas hands, how she pats my hand when talking to me its like morse code
Sometimes you don't really notice how old your grandparents get it happens in slow motion almost. 
But lately I noticed how when my abuela is talking to me on the couch, she will often take my hand and pat it when she gets excited about what shes talking about. Like a kind of morse code, tapping the words into the top of my hand. I have noticed the soft wrinkles and the folds of skin a lot more, she has those little dark spots, she always has a tissue tucked into her sleeve that she holds with her pinky and ring finger against her palm. Its these little things that remind me of her age, her brain is sharp and shes lively for an old lady, but sometimes the age peeks through. I've started taking photos of her hand on my hand every now and then.

chinese_item:
每天早上，我和我的猫都有着同样的例行公事。

我起床，煮咖啡，然后坐在厨房的桌子旁。我的猫跳到我旁边的椅子上，望着窗外。我们之间没有互动。我们只是静静地待在那里，听着咖啡壶咕嘟咕嘟地冒泡，直到咖啡煮好。这是我一天中最安静的时刻。
english_item:
My cat and I have the same routine every morning.
I get up, put the coffee on, and sit at the kitchen table. My cat jumps onto the chair next to me and stares out the window. We don't interact. We just sit there together, listening to the coffee pot bubble, until it's ready. It's the quietest part of my day.

chinese_item:
我刚才像抱着睡着的小孩一样抱着我家老狗上楼，竟然没吵醒他！

我今晚决定熬夜追剧，我家14岁的老狗盖着毯子睡在我旁边。他现在听力不太好，睡得也很沉，所以我决定试着把他抱上楼，别吵醒他。我把手放在他身下，把他抱到胸前，他一路都在打呼噜，最后被我抱回他的窝里，一点儿也没吵醒他。感觉真是太温馨了:)
english_item:
I just carried my senior dog like an asleep toddler in a car, without waking him up
I decided to stay up late tonight and finish a series I was watching and my 14 year old dog was asleep next to me covered in a blanket. He's hard of hearing now and sleeps quite deeply, so I decided to try and carry him upstairs without disturbing his sleep. I put my hands underneath and lifted him close to my chest, he was snoring all the way up the stairs and was tucked in his bed without getting woken up. It just felt so precious:)

chinese_item:
以前的Carmex润唇膏盖……

它们颜色更红一些，形状也没那么圆润。轻轻一推盖子，再推回去，会发出清脆悦耳的“咔哒”声。得知他们稍微改了盖子，我真是心碎。“咔哒”声变得沉闷，没那么清晰了。所以每次买新的润唇膏，我都会把新盖子扔掉，然后把旧盖子换到新润唇膏上。前段时间搬家，我找到了好多以前丢的Carmex润唇膏，而且都是旧盖子！简直就像中了彩票一样！
english_item:
The previous Carmex chapstick caps...
They were a slightly redder red and less rounded. They made the most delightful click when you pushed the cap off a little bit and back on. I was heartbroken to find out they'd slightly changed the caps. The click is muted, less defined. So every time I get new chapstick, I throw away the new cap and swap my legacy cap over to the new stick. A bit ago, I moved and I found tons of past Carmex's that I had lost. With the old caps! It was like I'd won the lottery,

chinese_item:
我的植物长得比我想象的快多了

几个月前，我在窗台上种了几盆小植物。一开始我没抱太大希望，因为总是忘记浇水。但不知怎么的，它们最近长势喜人。每周我都能看到新叶子长出来，叶子更绿了些，也开始向着阳光伸展。它们就这样默默地生长，我几乎不用操心，真是太神奇了。这让我不禁想到，即使我不在意，生命也在悄然流逝。
english_item:
My plants are growing faster than I expected
I started a few small plants on my windowsill months ago. I did not expecting much from them since I kept forgetting to water them at first. But somehow they’ve been thriving lately. Every week I notice new leaves, a bit more green, and a little stretch toward the sunlight. It's fascinating, how they just keep doing their thing quietly without me needing to do much. Makes me think about how life keeps moving like that too even when I’m not paying attention.

chinese_item:
给可可盖被子

两年前的复活节，我收到了一只深棕灰色的毛绒兔子，我给它取名叫可可。没有它我就睡不着，它是我最温暖的伙伴。如果早上心情不好，我会把它放进包里，白天摸摸它的毛，这样就能记住晚上要回到哪里。

总之，每天早上醒来，我都会把床铺好，像酒店那样，摆得特别精致，把我的所有毛绒玩具都整齐地排列着。但是因为可可整晚都在“守护”着我，所以我会把它盖在我的被窝里，摆好我睡觉的姿势（侧卧，一条腿伸出去），让它“睡”到我叫醒它“值夜班”。

这是我表达对可可的感谢的小方式（而且这样我白天就不会再睡着了……我不想在可可休息的时候吵醒它！）
english_item:
tucking in cocoa
i got this dark brown-grey stuffed rabbit two easter's ago i named cocoa. i can't sleep w/o her in my arms, she's my most comforting companion. on days w bad mornings i slip her into my purse and rub at her fur during the day so i remember what i have to go back to.


anyways, every morning when i wake up i make my bed. hotel style, really fancy with all my stuffed animals lined up. but since cocoa spends all night "watching out for me" on the night shift— i tuck her in under my sheets and blankets— move her into the position i sleep in (on side with leg kicked out) and let her "sleep" till i wake her for the "night shift".


it's my private way of saying thank you to cocoa (and not fall back asleep in the middle of the day... don't wanna wake cocoa during her rest time!)

chinese_item:
我常去的那家咖啡店的咖啡师在我的拉花上画了一只小猫。

这拉花画得其实并不怎么样，就是一个歪歪扭扭的圆圈，耳朵是三角形的，眼睛是点点的。但不知为何，这个小小的惊喜让我整个上午都心情愉悦。咖啡师看起来对自己的作品非常满意，而我实在不忍心告诉她，这看起来更像个外星人而不是猫。
english_item:
The barista at my regular coffee shop drew a tiny cat in my foam
It wasn't even particularly good latte art, just a wobbly circle with triangle ears and dot eyes. But something about this small unexpected gesture has me smiling whole morning. The barista looked so proud of her creation and I didn't have the heart to tell her it looked more like an alien than a cat.

chinese_item:
有个孩子把玩具落在我工作的地方了。

有个孩子把玩具落在我工作的地方了。

我们打电话给孩子的父母，问他们是否愿意回来取，但他们直到回家才收到消息……而我们这里离家有两个小时的车程。

他们发短信问我们是否可以把玩具寄回去，因为小女孩找不到玩具后非常难过。

我把玩具放在一个大信封里，里面还有一些涂色页和一张纸条，上面写着：

“亲爱的[孩子的名字]，

我今天玩得好开心！但是我想你了，所以我回家了。”

我迫不及待地想看到他们打开信封的样子。
english_item:
A kid left her toy at my job.
A kid left her toy at my job.

We called the parents to see if they wanted to come back for it, but they didn't get the message until they got home... two hours away.

They texted our office asking if we could possibly mail it back to them, as the girl was so upset when she couldn't find it.

I put it in a big envelope with some coloring pages and a note that said,

"Dear [kid's name],

I had such an adventure! But I missed you, so I came home."

I can't wait for them to open it.

chinese_item:
我的同事们太棒了！

今天我在工作中犯了个财务错误。感觉就像天塌下来一样，我差点哭了。

我告诉了我的老板，他很和蔼。

我终于鼓起勇气告诉了财务主管。他笑着说：“你总是害怕这种事发生，但它从来没发生过。现在发生了，你就会明白一切都很好，根本不是什么问题。”

他很有父爱，非常体贴地对我说：“我没有生你的气。”

每个人都把我当成一个犯了很简单、很容易原谅的错误的人来对待。这种感觉我以前从未有过！
english_item:
My co workers are amazing
I made a financial mistake at work today. It felt like the biggest deal in the entire world and I almost cried. 

I told my boss, who was lovely. 

I finally braved telling the head of finance. He laughed at me. "You are constantly afraid this will happen and it never does. Now that it has, you will learn that everything is absolutely fine and not a problem."

He has big dad energy so very compassionatly told me "I am not mad at you". 

Everyone is treating me like a human being who made a very simple and forgivable mistake. A concept that is not familiar to me!

chinese_item:
一个粉红色的小忍者

去药店拿药，顺便买了个甜甜圈犒劳自己。我坐在阳光下的长椅上，一边吃一边看着来来往往的人，听到身后一位老太太笑了起来，于是我偷偷瞥了一眼，发现她女儿/孙女穿着一身亮粉色的衣服，看起来不超过五岁，像个游戏角色一样蹲着，试图悄悄地从我身边溜过去。

我有点想念做这种傻事的日子了 XD
english_item:
A tiny pink ninja
Went down to pick up some meds and decided to grab a donut as a little treat. Was sitting on a bench in the sun watching people while I ate and heard the older woman passing behind me laugh, so sneaked a glance and saw her daughter/grand-daughter in a bright pink outfit, not more than five, crouched like a video game character trying to stealth past my bench. 

I kinda miss doing goofy stuff like that XD

chinese_item:
我现在负责给我男朋友梳头。

我以前留着超长的头发，但打理起来太麻烦了，所以今年年初就剪短了。差不多同一时间，我男朋友也决定，如果他要留长发，那就趁现在赶紧留，所以他的头发现在比我的还长。

昨天，他正努力把两边的卷发弄服帖一些，我就主动提出帮他编辫子，这样头发就能更贴近头皮，他欣然接受了。我给他编了四条比较松散的法式麻花辫，然后把辫尾扎成马尾。说实话，他看起来很棒，即使戴着我那亮粉色的发圈也一样好看。

今天他又问我能不能再给他编一次。

这很快就成了我一天中最期待的十分钟。
english_item:
I do my boyfriends hair now
I used to have hip long hair until the maintenance became too tedious and I had it cut short early this year. Around the same time my boyfriend decided if he's ever gonna grow his hair long, it will be now or never and so his hair is now longer than mine. 


Yesterday, as he was trying to get his curly sides to lay flatish, I offered to braid his hair so it stays closer to his head and he took me up on it. I did 4 semi-loose french rope twist braids and tied the ends into a pony tail. Honestly he looks great, even with my bright pink hair ties. 
Today he asked if I could do it again. 


It's quickly becoming my favourite 10 minutes of the day.

chinese_item:
疙瘩黄瓜

前几天我在阿斯达超市取钱的时候，一个大概八岁左右的小男孩拿着两根小小的、疙疙瘩瘩的黄瓜走过来。他说：“打扰一下，女士，您想买一根我自家花园里种的黄瓜吗？” 我身上只有十英镑的钞票，就跟他说他没零钱，所以我买不了。我正要走开的时候，突然想起车里还有几英镑，于是赶紧跑去拿，然后又回去跟他说，我很佩服他的这份努力和热情，所以我愿意花两英镑买一根。我说，我希望他能一直保持这份自信，这对他以后的人生会有很大的帮助。我走开的时候，听到他叫他的朋友过来。“看，我居然卖出去一根了，耶！” 听到他这么兴奋，我心里暖暖的。
english_item:
Knobbly cucumbers
I was getting some cash out at Asda the other day when a boy prob about 8, approached me holding 2 small knobbly cucumbers. He said " excuse me lady but would you like to buy one of the cucumbers i grew in my garden?". I only had ??10 notes on me and explained he wouldnt have change so i couldnt buy them. As i walked away i remembered i had a couple of pounds in my car, so rushed off and got them and went back. I said i was so impressed with his effort and spirit that i would buy one for ??2. I said i hoped he carried on having that confidence and it would be a good skill in life. As i walked away i heard him call his friend over. "Look i actually sold one ,yes!!" It made my heart feel warm to hear how excited he was.

chinese_item:
与穿罗拉克斯服装的人互动

昨晚万圣节我在发糖，遇到一个穿罗拉克斯服装的人。我问她：“你收到来自树林的讯息了吗？”她愣了几秒钟，目光空洞地盯着前方，然后说：“没有。”我递给她一些糖，她就走了。

现在回想起来，我琢磨着她是不是在倾听树林的低语，但没收到。那沉默的时间比我想象的要长。

我还看到有人打扮成那种会扭动的充气人偶，就像活动或商家门口的那种。在我看来，这绝对是最佳装扮。
english_item:
interaction with person in Lorax costume
I was handing out candy for Halloween last night, and there was a person in a Lorax costume. I asked her "do you have a message from the trees?" and she went completely blank for a few seconds, stared into space, and, then said "no." I handed her some candy and she went on her way.

Looking back on it, I wonder if she was listening for the whispers of the trees but didn't receive them. The pause felt longer than I expected.

I also saw someone dressed as one of those inflatable tube people that wiggle, like the ones in front of special events or businesses. Peak costume choice in my opinion

chinese_item:
我给朋友买了一条希腊之眼手链。

我想跟人说说这件事，但又不想告诉我的朋友，因为感觉自己好像高人一等，其实只是我的注意力缺陷多动症（AUHD）让我忍不住想分享。

我的朋友最近几周过得很艰难，运气很差，甚至还受伤了。所以我想尽我所能地帮助她，因为她是我的朋友，我很关心她。

希腊之眼只有作为礼物才能发挥作用，所以我给她买了一条简单的手链，真心实意地想帮她。

我明天会把手链送给她，嗯，就这些。我只是想找个人说说，免得不小心跟他们说漏嘴。

谢谢阅读！
english_item:
Bought a Greek eye bracelet for my friend
I wanted to tell someone but I didn't wanna tell any of my friends because it feels like I'm being holier than thou when really it's just my audhd brain being unable to exist without oversharing.

My friend has been having a really rough few weeks, really bad luck to the point of being injured, so I wanted to help in whatever way I could, cos they're my friend and I care about them.

Greek eyes only work if they're gifted, so I bought them a simple little bracelet, genuinely just because I wanna help. 

I'm gonna be giving it to them tomorrow, and yeah, that's all. I just wanted to tell someone so I don't accidentally spill the beans to them.

Thanks for reading!!!

chinese_item:
邻居真好 :)

上周我忙得焦头烂额，回到家就把钥匙锁在车里了（我经常这样，而且最近还把备用钥匙弄丢了）。为了避免花钱请锁匠，我和女朋友决定自己试试能不能打开车门。我们在新公寓的停车场里费劲地用一根细窗帘杆撬车门的时候，一个男人喊道：“你们被锁在外面了吗？我有工具，给我一分钟！” 没过多久，我的邻居就拿着工具走了过来。他费了九牛二虎之力，终于把我的车门打开了！我从未如此感激过别人的善意和及时出现 :) 我以前的邻居肯定会让我一直折腾下去，但我的新邻居毫不犹豫地伸出了援手。
english_item:
Neighbor being neighborly :)
Last week I was having a busy and stressful day, and managed to lock my keys in my car right when I got home (I do this frequently and recently lost my spare car key). Before having to pay a locksmith to come let me in my car, my girlfriend and I decided to try and get it open ourselves. While struggling in the parking lot of our new apartment complex, trying to wedge a thin curtain rod through my car door, a man called out "are yall locked out? I've got the tools for that, give me a minute!". A few moments later my neighbor walked back over with his tools in hand. After a difficult 20 mins of struggling with it, he managed to get my door open! I've never been so grateful for the RandomActsofKindness or the timing of another human being :) my old neighbors would have let me struggle indefinitely, but my new neighbor didn't hesitate to lend a hand

chinese_item:
我们没能去露营。

我和我八岁的儿子原本计划这周末去露营，但因为一些琐碎的生活琐事不得不取消。我问他最喜欢露营的什么，他说是篝火。

所以昨晚——在我们处理完所有导致我们周末无法出行的事情之后——我们去了离家五分钟路程的河边，那里有一处鹅卵石滩，我们生了一小堆篝火。我们在冰冷的河水中玩了一会儿，然后烤棉花糖，在篝火旁暖脚，还看到了银河。

现在他每个星期天都想再去一次。
english_item:
We couldn't go camping.
My 8 year old and I were going to go camping this weekend but had to cancel for various boring life reasons. I asked what he loved most about camping and it was the campfire. 

So last night - after we'd done all the things we needed to do that had stopped us from being able to go away this weekend - we went out to a pebble beach near a river 5 minutes from our house and made a little campfire. We had a paddle in the freezing cold river and then ate marshmallows and warmed our feet up on the fire and saw the Milky Way. 

He wants to go back every Sunday now.

chinese_item:
每天早上，我丈夫洗澡的时候都会放80年代的音乐。

我躺在床上听着（等着我这老弱病残的身体决定要不要起床），回忆着我们19岁时一起度过的美好时光。我很感激我们一起走过的近40年。
english_item:
Every morning, my husband blasts music from the 80s while he showers
I’m lying in bed listening (and waiting for my old, chronically ill body to decide it’s ready to get up) and thinking of our early days together at the tender age of 19. I’m grateful for the nearly 40 years we’ve had together.

chinese_item:
我收到了一枚书签……

小时候，我妈总是因为我想要书签而冲我吼，说那是浪费钱。“你完全可以用原本要花的那块钱，”或者用收据、餐巾纸，甚至有时候用一小块卫生纸都行。

昨天，我老公给我买了一枚价值13美元的德古拉激光雕刻金属书签……我坐在车里，手里拿着新书，盯着它看了好久。他问我怎么样，我脑子里想的却是，长大后我们真的可以自己做决定……有时候，我们可以买一些小时候想要的东西……也许像书签这样的小东西，有时也能带来治愈的力量。
english_item:
I got a bookmark...
When I was little my mom used to yell at me for wanting bookmarks because they were a waste of money. "You can just use the dollar you would've spent on it" or a receipt, or a napkin, or sometimes a square of toilet paper. 
Yesterday my husband bought me a $13 Dracula laser engraved metal bookmark...I just sat there staring at it in the car, holding my new book. He asked if I was okay, and all I could think about is how as adults we really do get make our own choices.. and sometimes it's buying the things we wanted as a child... and maybe something as small as a bookmark feels like healing sometimes.

chinese_item:
我终于变成了那种会向没向我挥手的人挥手回应的人。

今天我走出咖啡馆时，看到街对面有个男的热情地朝我挥手。我愣了一下，回头看了看是不是有人在我身后，结果什么也没有。于是我勉强挥手回应。

然后我才意识到，他真正挥手的对象一直就站在我的视线盲区外。我看过很多别人被这样恶作剧的视频，总是觉得“这种事绝对不会发生在我身上”。结果，它真的发生了。我尴尬极了，只好假装看手机，装作什么都没发生一样走开了。
english_item:
I finally became the person who waves back at someone who wasn’t waving at them
I was walking out of a cafe today when I saw a guy across the street waving enthusiastically in my direction. I got that half-second of confusion, turned around to check if maybe someone was behind me and no one was there. So I reluctantly waved back.

Then I realized the person he was actually waving at was standing just outside my blind spot the whole time. I’ve seen so many videos of people getting pranked like this and always thought, “No way that could never happen to me.” But here we are. I was so embarrassed I just pretended to check my phone and walked off like nothing happened.

chinese_item:
好心没好报

于是我去了家附近的Dollar Tree，买了几样东西，总共花了17.19美元。我离开商店，走到停车的地方，检查了一下我的包。

我的打火机、眼镜、垃圾袋和收据……嗯……我的借记卡呢？……我的借记卡呢？……还好商店离我只有大约四分之三英里，所以我开车过去问了问。结果没人上交我的借记卡。

我倒也没太在意，反正账户里也没多少钱，于是我跳回车里，拨通了银行的电话。你知道，银行最搞笑的地方就是，虽然有很多电话号码可以打，但如果是周末，你就会接到自动语音系统，然后就得一直等啊等啊等……

我正等着，突然往右边看了一眼。好像有人捡到了我的卡，我猜他们以为是我的，就把它塞进了右侧副驾驶座的车窗里。

我不知道他们为什么这么做，除非他们以为车里会有乘客。如果他们往里看，就会发现我车里没有乘客（座位上堆满了东西）。

不过，总之，卡完好无损地放在那里，显然没人用过。他们似乎并不在意是否得到感谢或祝福，只是做了件好事，然后就走了……
english_item:
No reward for good deed
So I went to my local Dollar tree and picked up several items which came to a grand total of $17.19. so I left the store and I got to the place I park and I checked my bag.

My lighters, my glasses, my garbage bags and receipt.. um where's my debit card?.. WHERE IS MY DEBIT CARD?... Luckily the store was only about 3/4 of a mile away so I hauled out there and checked with them. Nope nobody had turned in a debit card.

I wasn't freaking out I didn't have that much in my account anyway, so I jumped back into my vehicle and dialed up my bank. You know that's the funny thing the bank is all these phone numbers to call, but if it's on the weekend you get the automated system where you have to wait and wait and wait....

As I'm waiting I look over to my right. Seems someone had found my card and I guess they assumed it belonged to me and stuck it in the right passenger side window. 

I don't know why they did that, unless they thought that I would have a passenger. If they looked inside they would realize that I don't have passengers (seat is full of stuff) 

But anyway there was the card, in good shape no damage to it and nobody had used it. They didn't care to receive a reward thanks or a blessing. They just did a good deed and went on their way...

chinese_item:
我把卡片反着放进信封里。

这样，当人们打开信封时，就必须把卡片翻过来才能看到正面，从而一眼就能看到完整的内容。

不到最后一刻，谜底始终未解。

我之前把生日贺卡放进信封的时候就想到了这一点。
english_item:
I put cards in the envelopes backwards.
That way when people open the envelope, they have to turn the card over to see the front, and get the full picture at once.

It remains a mystery until the last second. 

I was thinking of this earlier when putting a birthday card in an envelope.

chinese_item:
我更喜欢自己做的菜，而不是我妈做的。

这尤其让人匪夷所思，因为那些菜谱都是她教我的。所以她明明知道怎么做我喜欢吃的，只是她不想做而已。从我十五岁左右开始，如果她打算做我擅长的菜，我就会主动要求做。

现在想想，这或许是她为了让我多做饭而设的圈套。
english_item:
I prefer the food I cook to my mom’s
This is particularly wild because she taught me those recipes. So she knows how to make the food I like, she just chooses not to. I was at a point where from when I was like 15, I would volunteer to cook the food if she planned on making something I knew how to make.

Thinking back, this may have been her plot to make me cook more

chinese_item:
灯光

初雪降临。圣诞树上的白色彩灯。我应该用彩色的吗？凌晨5点半喝热红酒是不是太早了？我48岁，孩子都长大了，空巢老人。谁还需要什么规矩呢？
english_item:
Lights
The first snowfall. Christmas tree white lights. Should I have used multi colored? Too early for mulled wine at 530 a.m. ? Im 48 and an empty nester. Who needs rules.

chinese_item:
财务安全

我四岁的女儿给我们搭了个堡垒躲在里面。她说这样可以“保护我们免受债主的骚扰”。

她当然指的是“掠食者”。不过你知道吗？我更想要财务安全！
english_item:
Financial security
My 4 year old daughter made a fort for us to hide in. It's supposed to "keep us safe from creditors".

She of course meant "predators". But you know what? I would prefer the financial security!

chinese_item: 一名29岁的女子驾驶一辆被盗小型货车，在加州四个县引发了一场长达两小时、170英里的警方追捕。
english_item: A 29-year-old woman led authorities on a two-hour, 170-mile police pursuit across four Californian counties in a stolen minivan.

chinese_item: 已故的乐队乐队吉他手在这本令人振奋的回忆录中忧思：或许是我带入马蒂生活的摇滚生活方式导致了他的困境。帕特里克·史密斯透过这部作品，得以窥见他们长达数十载的深厚情谊。
english_item: ‘Maybe the rock’n’roll lifestyle I had brought into Marty’s life was to blame for his plight,’ worries the late guitarist of The Band in this bracing account. Patrick Smith enjoys a window into their decades-long friendship

chinese_item: 在成为福克斯新闻台常客和特朗普最喜爱的民主党人之前，约翰·费特曼在2022年竞选期间中风后，曾遭到该电视台的持续攻击和嘲讽。
english_item: Before he became a regular fixture on Fox News airwaves and ‘Trump’s favorite Democrat,’ John Fetterman was relantlessly attacked and ridiculed by the network following his stroke during the 2022 election campaign.

chinese_item: 投诉称，五角大楼在赫格塞斯领导下非法剥夺了被迫离开空军的跨性别军人的福利。
english_item: Pentagon illegally stripped benefits from trans troops forced out of the Air Force under Hegseth, complaint says

chinese_item: 在过去一年中，从门阶上被盗物品的价值增加了2.9亿英镑。
english_item: The value of items stolen from doorsteps rose by ￡290m over the past year

chinese_item: 财政大臣表示，必须警惕放任儿童贫困问题恶化给经济造成的代价。
english_item: The chancellor said it was important not to let the ‘costs to our economy in allowing child poverty to go unchecked’

chinese_item: 一对从事基督教传教工作的父女在前往牙买加运送飓风救援物资途中因飞机失事不幸遇难。
english_item: A Christian missionary father and daughter were killed after their plane crashed whilst en route to Jamaica to deliver hurricane relief supplies.

chinese_item: 有一次我独自去参加合唱团。我得坐97路公交车。一切都很顺利，直到我意识到我忘了带口罩。我去了街角的小店，买了一个一次性口罩和一包泡泡糖。走到车站时，我眼睁睁地看着97路公交车开走了！我拼命地朝它跑去，却无济于事，我哭着把脸埋进了外套里。一个陌生人拍了拍我的肩膀，问我为什么哭。我屏住呼吸，努力不让自己在他面前哭出来，把事情告诉了他。他点点头，给我看他手机上找到的一个网站。网站上说97路公交车还有9分钟就到，时间刚刚好，即使稍微有点延迟。最后，他上了车，我也上了车。我非常感谢他，也希望很快能把这种善举传递给其他人。
english_item: Once I went to choir by myself. I had to take the number 97 bus. I was feeling good until I realized I'd forgot my mask. I went to a corner shop and bought a disposable mask and a pack of bubblegum. As I walked up to the stop, I saw the 97 bus leaving the stop! I ran towards it in vain and I cried into my coat. A stranger tapped my shoulder and asked why I was crying. I told him, trying to hold my breath in front of him, trying not to burst into more tears whilst telling him. He nodded and showed me a website he found on his phone. It told me the 97 bus would arrive in 9 minutes, the perfect amount of time, even if it stretched a little bit. Eventually, he got on his bus, and I got on mine. I'm very grateful for him and I hope to pass on the act soon.

chinese_item: 我记得不久前，我和妈妈一起去蒂华纳看望爷爷。每次去看他，我都特别开心，因为他总是给我钱，而我也很期待花掉这些钱。有一次，我们在边境排队等着过境的时候，看到三个孩子在玩一个汽水瓶，他们踢来踢去，玩得不亦乐乎。看到他们仅仅因为一个瓶子就能这么开心，我真的觉得他们比我更需要爷爷给我的钱。所以我就把钱给了他们，看到他们脸上灿烂的笑容，我也感到非常开心。
english_item: I remember not too long ago I was with my mom and we went to Tijuana to visit my grandpa. Every time we go visit him I get happy because he always gives me money and i get excited to spend it. One time when we were at the border waiting in line to cross back I saw a group of 3 kids playing with a soda bottle kicking it back and forth having fun. It really made me see how happy they can be with just a bottle and I felt like they needed the money that my grandpa gave me more than me. So i gave it to them and to see them with a big smile made me really happy.

chinese_item: 我的母亲去世了。这对我和我的家人来说都是一场巨大的打击。我在公共卫生和心理健康部门工作。我知道如何应对压力和创伤，但当时我一个人真的难以承受。我需要一个朋友，或者一个可以依靠的人。我从未告诉过任何人，但其实我根本不需要。我的同事、朋友，甚至一些认识我母亲却素未谋面的陌生人都给了我莫大的支持。她离开这个世界时没有购买人寿保险，没有遗嘱，什么都没有。我是她的独生子。除了我，她没有其他亲人。我负责安排葬礼事宜，并承担所有费用。如果没有社区、朋友等等的帮助，我真的不知道自己该如何独自完成这一切。我的同事们非常善良，他们抽出自己的私人时间来帮我，让我可以请假几天来哀悼，处理这件事。社区里的陌生人纷纷捐款资助她的葬礼，在他们的帮助下，我得以全额支付葬礼费用。还有人组织送餐活动，为我和我的家人送饭，这样我就不用在忙碌之余还要操心做饭了。我永远不会忘记在自己社区感受到的这份善意。小镇有好有坏，一切都取决于他人的善意。我的社区在我需要帮助的时候伸出了援手，而我也一定会将这份善意传递下去，在我需要帮助的时候，尽我所能地去帮助别人。
english_item: My mother had passed away. It was a very traumatic event for my family and I. I work for a Public and Mental Health Department. I knew how to handle my stress and trauma, but at this point it was unbearable for only little ole me. I needed a friend or someone to lean on. I never told anyone that, but I didn't have to. I had tremendous outpour of support from my coworkers, friends and even some strangers that knew my mother, but I had never met. She left this world with no life insurance, no will, nothing. I was her only child. She didn't have anyone else, but me. I was in charge of figuring out funeral arrangements and the cost of it all. If it weren't for the RandomActsofKindness of my community, friends, etc. I truly do not know how I would have been able to pull it off alone. My coworkers were so kind, they donated some of their own personal time to me so I could have paid days off from work to grief and handle the situation. Strangers in the community were donating to her funeral fund, and I was able to completely fully cover the cost with their help. I had people start meal trains to deliver my family and I dinners so that cooking was not something I had to worry about on top of everything going on. I will never forget the RandomActsofKindness I experienced within my own community. Small Towns can be good or bad, it all depends on the RandomActsofKindness of others. My community chose to be kind to someone in their time of need and I will most certainly pay it forward when it comes my turn to lend RandomActsofKindness and a helping hand.

chinese_item:
我生日那天去上班了，那是我大学毕业后的第一个生日。我身处一个陌生的城市，人脉很广，在附近也没有亲朋好友。我跟几个同事提过我的生日，心想他们肯定忘了。我才上班几个月，跟谁都不算熟，估计他们都没注意到我。

那天早上我感觉很孤独，于是决定犒劳自己吃顿午饭，让自己开心一下。回到办公桌前，我发现一个信封，上面写着我的名字，旁边放着一份包装精美的礼物。我简直不敢相信。原来是一位同事特意给我买了生日贺卡和一双印着腊肠犬图案的趣味袜子（因为我总是穿有趣的袜子上班）。我这才意识到自己有多么孤独，多么需要有人注意到我，关心我。我感动得热泪盈眶。

那是我收到过的最意想不到、最贴心的礼物之一。当时我情绪低落，这份礼物让我瞬间振作起来，意义非凡。我希望我也能让别人感受到我在那一刻被理解和被重视的温暖。
english_item:
I went to work on my birthday, and it was my first birthday after leaving college. I was in a new city without much of a network or any family or friends in the area. I had mentioned when my birthday was to a few of my co-workers, fully expecting them to forget. I had only been at the job for a couple months and I didn?ˉt feel close enough to anybody for them to even notice me.
I was feeling lonely that morning, and decided to treat myself for lunch to try and pick up my spirits. When I got back to my desk, there was an envelope with my name on it and a very neatly wrapped gift sitting next to the envelope. I couldn?ˉt believe it. One of my co-workers went out of her way, and bought me a birthday card and pair of funky socks with wiener dogs on them (since I always wore fun socks to work). I didn?ˉt realize how alone I was feeling, and how much I needed somebody to notice me and show that they cared. I was brought to tears.
That was one of the most unexpected and thoughtful gifts I had ever received. I was feeling low and it was so impactful that it picked me right back up. I hope I can make somebody else feel seen the same way I did in that exact moment.

chinese_item: 我当时正在经历离婚，心情非常糟糕。我极度自卑，总是责怪自己。我最好的朋友会陪着我，让我尽情哭泣，然后告诉我我有多棒。她会在我上班的时候给我带星巴克，帮我熬过一天。她让我不再那么自卑，在我人生低谷的时候，每天都用爱温暖着我。她的善良和同情对我来说意义非凡，让我明白人们永远无法真正了解别人正在经历什么。我没有跟任何同事提起我的离婚。我永远不会忘记她的善意，我也努力把这份爱和同情传递到我所到之处。
english_item: I was going through my divorce and I wasn?ˉt in a good place. I had low self esteem, and was blaming myself a lot. My best friend would sit with me, she would let me cry, and tell me how great I am. She would bring me Starbucks while I was at work to help me get through the day. She helped me to stop thinking so poorly of myself, and just filled me with love everyday when I was at a low point in my life. Her RandomActsofKindness and compassion meant everything to me, it helped me realize people never know what someone is going through. I didn?ˉt tell anyone I worked with about my divorce. I have never forgotten her RandomActsofKindness, and I try to spread the same love and compassion wherever I go.

chinese_item:
我在加州一家超级火爆的快餐店工作。每天要接待几百位顾客，说实话，就算把他们都放在一个房间里，让我指认谁是谁，我连三分之一的人的脸都记不住。他们进来，冲我一笑，拿了小票就走，然后砰！他们的脸就被我忘得一干二净了。

但是，总有一个无家可归的男人站在店门口，想在晚上高峰期到来之前抓紧时间睡一会儿。人们从他身边走过，甚至连看都不看他一眼。有一天，我看到他靠墙坐着，我再也忍不住了。我赶紧跑去找经理，问他能不能让我休息十分钟，得到他的同意后，我跑回收银台，点了一份汉堡、薯条和一杯饮料。等食物做好后，我走到那个无家可归的男人面前，把食物递给他。他笑得合不拢嘴，我感觉自己像在云端漫步一样回到了店里。

十分钟休息后，经理告诉我，我要加薪升职了。

不要指望得到奖励。但要永远为别人着想，因为你永远不知道他们正在经历什么。
english_item:
I work at a super popular fast-food restaurant in California. I serve hundreds of people a day, and I honestly couldn't even remember a third of their faces if you put them all in a room and told me to point out who's who. They come by, flash a smile, take their order receipt, walk away, and boom! Their faces are forgotten.
But there's always this homeless man standing in front of the store, trying to catch sleep before the nightly rush. People walk by without even glancing at him. One day, I saw him sitting there against the wall and I couldn't take it anymore. I quickly ran to my manager, asked him if I could take my 10-min break right then, and with his approval I ran back to the register and put in an order for a burger, fries and a drink. Then, once the food was ready, I walked out to the homeless man and handed him the food. He smiled, beyond words, and I walked on clouds back inside.
After my ten-minute break, my manager told me I was getting a raise and a promotion.
Don't expect a reward. But always do things for other people because you NEVER know what they're going through.

chinese_item: 大学时，我在德克萨斯州阿比林市中心做绘图员。一天，我去街对面喝咖啡休息，看到一个男人刚买了一杯咖啡，当时这杯咖啡25美分，但还差2美分要交税。他说：“哦，我没零钱。” 我从口袋里掏出2美分给了他，他非常感激。“年轻人，你真是太好了，我想我应该谢谢你。” 他拿出一张纸条，写了一张便条，上面写着：我欠你一件衬衫。署名：菲舍尔先生，菲舍尔服装店。我就在附近。我绕过街角，在他的店里见到了他。他看了??看我的纸条，问我是否想从这个架子上挑一件衬衫。我选了一件我大学时最喜欢的衬衫。据说菲舍尔先生曾以类似的方式慷慨地帮助过许多大学生。他的善举远不止两分钱！
english_item: When I was in college I was working as a draftsman downtown Abilene Texas.I went across the street for a coffee break and there was a man there who had just received his cup of coffee that was 25 cents at the time., but there was two cents due for the taxes. He said 'oh I don't have the change with me.'I offered the two cents from my pocket and he was very appreciative. 'That was a very kind gesture young man, I think I owe you a Thank you.'.He took a slip of paper and wrote a note that said I owe you one shirt.Signed Mr Fishel, Fishels clothing store. I'm just around the corner.I went around the corner and met him in his store and he looked at my note and he said how would you like to select a shirt from this rack.I was able to select one of my favorite shirts that I had while I was in college.The rumor was that Mr Fishel had been generous to many college students in similar ways.His RandomActsofKindness was worth much more than two cents.!

chinese_item: 我的故事始于2021年8月。我和姐姐度过了一个美好的假期。当时新冠疫情非常严重，人们都戴着口罩和手套，孩子们都在上网课。我在食品行业工作了大约35年，我很喜欢这份工作。然而，这次新冠疫情却演变成了一场史无前例的全球大流行！人们陷入恐慌，甚至连卫生纸都买不到。这场全球大流行让整个世界停摆。我感染了新冠，但我以为自己不会有事，因为我的丈夫和儿子也感染了。长话短说，我的病情急剧恶化，在重症监护室住了好几个星期。之后我被转到佛罗里达州的一家综合医院。医生说我需要进行双肺移植，而正是我的姐姐救了我的命。姐姐从密歇根州飞到佛罗里达州，陪伴我度过了整个过程。我记不太清当时的情况了。医生说，如果没有人陪伴我至少三个月，我就无法进入移植等待名单。姐姐放下一切，赶来照顾我。我接受了移植手术，姐姐全程陪在我身边。移植手术后，因为我必须住在离医院15分钟车程的地方，姐姐又陪了我三个月。没有她，我根本活不到现在！姐姐帮我们找了三个月的住处。她每天都带我去做理疗。我几乎每天都要去看医生。我和姐姐一直都很亲密，但这次经历让我们的感情更加深厚。我非常感激有姐姐在身边。我爱她，我永远无法回报她为我付出的一切，但我一定会尽力而为。
english_item: My story began in August of 2021.i had a wonderful vacation with my sister.Covid was really on run. People wearing mask and gloves. Kids schooling via internet.I have worked in the grocery business for about 35 year's and I love it.however this Covid has become a pandemic , first ever!!People were in a panic, you couldn?ˉt even find toilet paper. It was a pandemic that shut the whole word down.Well I contracted Covid but I thought I would be ok as my husband and our son also had it.Long story short . I got so bad , I was in icu for weeks . I was transferred to a medical hospital in Florida .The doctors said I needed a double lung transplant and this I were my sister saved my life . My sister flew from Michigan to Florida to stay with me through the whole event.I don?ˉt remember to much. The doctors said I could not be on the transplant list without a person to be with me for 3 months or longer maybe, my sister dropped everything to come stay and help me. I did receive a transplant and sister stayed in the hospital the whole time with me . Sister also stayed with me for three months after transplant as I had to be 15 min. Away from the hospital .I would not be alive without her!! Sister got a place for us for 3 months .Sister took me to physical therapy every day. I had doctor appointments almost every day. my sister and I were always pretty close but after this event or bond it is so strong I?ˉm so grateful to have my sister. I love her so much I can never give back what she gave to me, but I can certainly try.

chinese_item: 我还没有自己的收入，还和爷爷奶奶住在一起。为了给爷爷奶奶买袋冰，我第一次独自一人去了超市，身上只有五美元。结账的时候，我看到一块看起来很诱人的糖果，就把它和冰块一起放在了传送带上。结账的时候，我对收银员报的价格有点怀疑，就问她有没有看到冰块旁边的糖果。她说没有，所以算下来，我的五美元少了不少。我正要把糖果放回原处的时候，另一个一直在旁边看着的店员主动提出要帮我买。我愿意相信，这位店员的出现和她的善良，是冥冥之中某种力量在奖励我，因为我没有撒谎。
english_item: I don't have my own income yet, and still live with my grandparents. I went into the grocery store alone for the first time ever with five dollars, in order to get my grandparents a bag of ice. At the checkout line, I saw a candy that looked really good, so I put it on the conveyor belt with the ice. I got a little suspicious of the cost that was given to me, and asked if the cashier had seen the candy next to the massive bag of ice. She had not, and so when it was taken into account, my five dollars was a fair few cents under. I was sadly putting the candy back when another employee who was watching offered to buy it for me. I like to think that employee's presence and inclination towards RandomActsofKindness was a reward from something out there for not lying about the candy.

chinese_item: 去年我买了一套公寓，需要大修，为了省钱我一直自己动手。不幸的是，这也意味着我没多少时间去超市买菜，所以没法好好吃顿饭。装修几周后，我在酒吧遇到一个朋友，跟他聊起装修的近况，他主动提出开车送我去超市，这样我随时都能买些东西。除此之外，他还推荐了一些价格实惠的工匠。因为我以前都是自己做这些事，听到他这么说我简直要哭了，因为我没想到会有人这么关心我，更别说主动帮忙了。所以我立刻答应了，从那以后他就一直在帮我。现在装修快完成了，真不知道如果没有他的帮助我会怎么办。他完全可以不用帮忙，但他却主动伸出援手，只希望我以后如果有人需要帮助，也请他帮忙。我永远不会忘记这份恩情。
english_item: I bought a flat last year that needed a lot of renovating so in order to cut costs I?ˉve been doing it up myself, unfortunately this also meant I haven't had much time to go food shopping so I can have a decent meal. Met a friend at the bar a few weeks into renovations and mentioned how things were and he offered to give me a lift to a supermarket so I can get some shopping in whenever I needed it. In addition to this he could recommend some trades people who could help out for cheap. As I was used to doing things like this by myself I nearly burst into tears as I didn't think anyone would even be bothered to even be interested let alone offer to help like this, so I immediately agreed and he's been helping out ever since. Renovations Are nearly done now and don't know what I would've done without his help. Didn't have to help out at all but he went out of his way to do so and only asked that I do the same if anyone was in need. Will never forget that

chinese_item: 超市收银员清点我买的东西时，发现我身上的钱比实际多出了12美元。我开始从袋子里拿出东西，这时另一位顾客递给我一张20美元的钞票。“别客气，”我对他说。“我给你讲个故事，”他说，“我母亲得了癌症，正在医院里。我每天都去看她，给她带花。今天早上我去看她，她因为我又把钱花在花上而生气了。她要求我把钱用在别的地方。所以，给你，收下这些花吧。这是我母亲的花。”
english_item: When the supermarket clerk tallied up my groceries, I was $12 over what I had on me. I began to remove items from the bags, when another shopper handed me a $20 bill. ?°Please don?ˉt put yourself out,?± I told him. ?°Let me tell you a story,?± he said. ?°My mother is in the hospital with cancer. I visit her every day and bring her flowers. I went this morning, and she got mad at me for spending my money on more flowers. She demanded that I do something else with that money. So, here, please accept this. It is my mother?ˉs flowers.?±

chinese_item: 我当时正提着装有项目剩余物品的塑料袋，从学校走下台阶。走到一半的时候，我不小心绊了一下，东西散落一地。我赶紧躲开，抓起东西塞进破了的袋子里。一开始没人帮我，后来有人走过来，嘲笑我说：“你是不是掉了什么东西？” 我继续捡东西，这时一个陌生人停在我旁边，接过我的东西，放回塑料袋里。我朝她笑了笑，说了声谢谢，但我当时并不知道她是谁。直到今天，我仍然不知道。但她的这个小举动让我的心情好了很多。
english_item: I was walking down the steps from school, holding plastic bags with items left over from a project. I got about halfway down and tripped, spilling my contents. I tried to get out of the way, grabbing at my things and putting them in the ripped bags. No one helped me at first, someone walked by and mocked "I think you dropped something." I continued to get her my things when a stranger stopped beside me, taking my things and placing them back in my plastic bag. I gave her a smile and told her thank you, but I never knew who she was. I still don't know to this day. But that little act made my day better.

chinese_item: 去年，我带着五个孩子中的四个去超市购物，收银员刚扫完我购物车里的东西（大约120美元），我才意识到我把信用卡忘在家里了，身上也没有现金。我顿时慌了神，努力保持镇定，免得孩子们因为买不到他们自己挑选的万圣节南瓜而难过。我告诉收银员我得把购物车放在旁边，问她能不能等我拿到卡再回来。我的小儿子开始哭了起来。我身后的一位女士注意到了我的窘境，说她可以用多余的钱买一张礼品卡。她说她也有四个孩子，所以很理解我的处境。我试图劝阻，但她非常坚持，一副事不关己的样子。我当时眼泪都快掉下来了。孩子们却高兴极了。这件事是我经历过的最慷慨的善举，但其实在超市里，我也经常遇到别人对我的善意之举。有人帮我把购物车推出来，有人帮我把东西放到收银台，因为我当时抱着一个哭闹的宝宝，等等。这个世界上仍然有很多美好的东西。
english_item: Last year I had 4 of my 5 young children out grocery shopping and had the checkout person had just finished scanning my full cart (around $120 worth) when I realized I'd left my credit card at home and had no cash. I went into silent panic mode and was trying to keep my composure so my kids wouldn't get upset that we weren't going to be able to buy the pumpkins they had picked out themselves for Halloween.. I told the cashier I would have to leave the cart on the side and asked if I could come backl after I had my card. My toddler started crying. A lady behind me noticed my dilemma and said something about using her extra funds on a gift card she had. She said she had 4 kids and understood. I tried to protest but she was so persisent and acted like it was no big deal. I had tears in my eyes.My kids were so happy.This event was the most generous act of kindess I've experienced, but there have been many times people have shown great RandomActsofKindness to me at the grocery store. Bringing out my cart, helping me put items on the checkout because I was holding a fussy baby, among others.So much good is still in this world.

chinese_item:
今天我去超市给快87岁的妈妈买东西。

几年前，我们每周都会一起去超市（她不能开车了），这样我可以帮她提东西，也能照看她。

超市里所有工作人员都认识她。

今天她没跟我一起去，收银员就问起她的情况。我告诉她妈妈身体不太好，腿上还有个开放性伤口。

“哦，天哪，”她说，“我会免费送她一束花，并代我向她问好。”

我感动得热泪盈眶。妈妈觉得很温暖??

这真是一份善意的举动！

来自荷兰的问候
english_item:
Today i went to the supermarket to buy groceries for my almost 87 year old mum.
Normally every week since a couple of years (she can't drive anymore) we went together so i can help carrying and keep an eye on her.
All the people who work there know her.
Today she was not with me so the lady behind the checkout asked about her. I told her she was not doing great and that she has an open wound on her leg.
Oh dear, she said, i will give a bunch of flowers free for her and give her my regards.
It brought tears to my eyes. My mum felt very special ??
This was an act of RandomActsofKindness!
Greetings from the Netherlands

chinese_item: 我们一家人不小心把一个女服务员弄哭了，我们让她很慌乱，她还弄错了另一个人的订单，我们当时的举动也让她很困惑。所以我们给了她100美元的小费，她出来敲了敲卡车的车窗，问我们是不是认真的，然后就开始哭了。能让她这么开心，感觉真是太棒了。
english_item: My family accidentally made a waitress cry, so we had made her flustered and she messed up another person's order, and we were super confusing. So we gave her a 100$ tip and she came out and knocked on the truck window and asking if we were serious and started to cry. It felt amazing to make her so happy.

chinese_item: 有没有人感觉自己比往年更能漠视他人，或者更能“不往心里去”？  大家好，有没有人有类似的经历，作为高敏感人群，在面对他人的恶意言行时，感觉自己变得更加漠视，或者更能适应“不往心里去”的态度？这有可能吗？我仍然觉得很难，因为我的价值观与周围环境不太一样。所以我只能把自己关在家里，学习情绪调节
english_item: Have anyone feel more indifference or able to don't take it personally more than past years? Hi, anyone with real life experience about you've felt become more indifference to or adapted to don't take it personally attitude as hsp to other ppl's mean behavior and words? Is it possible? I still find it hard, as i have different value with my surroundings. So i deal by being hermit at home and learning emotional regulation.

chinese_item: 有人哭不出来，却梦见自己哭吗？大家好。抱歉，如果有什么错误或者表达得不够清晰，英语不是我的母语。最近一段时间我感觉很不好受。我哭过之后从来不会觉得轻松。哭就像是情绪失控，尤其是在我无法理性地处理事情的时候，或者被强烈的情绪淹没的时候，那种感觉真的让人精疲力竭。最近，我连续几个晚上都梦见自己哭，哭得泣不成声，这种感觉我已经很久没有过了。醒来之后，我的眼眶会有点湿润，但那种感觉很快就消失了，我根本无法摆脱。感觉就像打喷嚏却没打出来一样……这种感觉很压抑，很沉闷，就像被困住了一样。我不想让任何人卷入我的这种情绪。我之前一直接受心理治疗，几年前停止了，但现在感觉重新开始治疗比处理目前的事情更糟糕。我没有服用任何药物，也从未服用过。
english_item: DAE is not able to cry, but dream of crying? Hi everyone. Sorry for any errors and my probably chaotic conveyance, English is not my first language.  I am having sort of a hard time, since some time. I never been one to feel relieved after crying. It just feels like spilling over when it seems like I have lost the battle to act on things in a helpful / rational way, or in case of overwhelming emotion, which is just exhausting and draining anyway.  Lately, for nights in a row, I have been dreaming of crying and sobbing in a way I haven't in a very long time, but when I am awake, my eyes can well up a bit but the feeling just washes over very quickly and I just can't get this over with. Feels sorta like losing a sneeze....  It just feels dull and oppressive, stuck. I don't want to bring anyone in my life into this. Been to talk therapy for long and stopped few years ago, but the feeling of starting that again feels worse than to just deal with what's on my plate atm. I am not and never been on any meds.

chinese_item: 发自内心的疑问  最近，我一直在思考一个令人痛苦的事实：我们身边最亲近的人，往往不会在我们真正重视的事情上给予支持——尤其是在我们开始成长、改变，或者迈向全新自我的时候。  多年来，我分享过瑜伽、茶道、女性聚会和灵性指导等活动……但我发现，我认识的大多数人似乎对此漠不关心。有时，我感觉自己像个隐形人，或者说，他们看待我的方式仍然停留在过去，而不是现在的我。  我想知道……是否有人也有过类似的感受？  你是否也曾与世界分享过一些意义非凡的事情，却遭到那些你以为会支持你的人的沉默？  如果你愿意分享，我很乐意倾听你的经历。我只是想了解这段旅程，或许还能从中找到一些共鸣。  （已转载
english_item: Gentle question from my heart Lately I’ve been sitting with a painful truth: how often the people closest to us don’t always show up for the things we care deeply about — especially when we start to grow, change, or step into a new version of ourselves.  For years, I’ve shared offerings like yoga, tea ceremony, women’s circles, and spiritual guidance… and I’ve noticed that most of the people I already know haven’t engaged with any of it. Sometimes it feels like I’m invisible, or like I’m still seen only through the lens of who I used to be, not who I’m becoming.  I’m wondering… has anyone else felt this?  Have you ever shared something meaningful with the world only to be met with silence from the people you thought might support you?  I’d love to hear your experience, if you feel like sharing. Just trying to understand this part of the journey, and maybe find some kinship in it.  (Cross posted)

chinese_item: 我该如何控制自己对别人的过度好感？  感觉每个对我特别好的男生……我都会不由自主地喜欢上他们。我该怎么解决这个问题
english_item: ？,How do I fix liking people so much? So I feel like every boy who is super nice to me… I just start liking them. How do I fix that?

chinese_item: 当对方停顿片刻，让我以为该轮到我说话了，结果我们俩却同时开口。  我讨厌这种情况，因为我们根本控制不了，它就这么发生了。  人们总是说打断别人说话很没礼貌，所以我通常都避免打断。但是，每次发生这种情况（有时甚至连续好几次），我都不得不停下来听他们继续唠叨，而人们总是劝我学会等待。然后当我解释发生了什么时，他们又不理解我的意思，因为看起来好像他们还没说完。  我知道这不是任何人的错，但是，该死，为什么“轮流说话”的定义这么模糊不清
english_item: ？,"When the other person pauses just enough for me to think it's my turn to talk, only for both of us to start talking at the same time I hate this because this isn't something we can control. It just happens.   People keep saying that it's rude to interrupt, so I usually don't. But then, every time this happens (sometimes even several times in a row), I have to stop and listen to more yapping, and people keep telling me to learn to wait anyway. And then when I explain what's happening, they don't understand my point because it always looks like their turn hasn't finished yet.    I get it's nobody's fault, but DAMN, why is it so ambiguous as to what turn taking actually is?

chinese_item: 天哪，这简直太矛盾了！！！  见面的时候？他简直棒极了。我们聊了好几个小时，眼神交流、肢体接触、欢笑不断，一刻也没有冷场。谈话内容丰富，既有深度，又有暗示，还很搞笑，各种元素应有尽有。他邀请我去看他的演唱会。他的父母也很喜欢我。我甚至还是个乳糜泻患者，但他还是带了无麸质香蕉面包来上课（我们都在上大学），这样我就可以参加期末考试后的小型聚会了。一切都很美好。  但是通过短信和社交媒体联系呢？简直糟糕透了。我一天能收到一条回复就不错了。而且大多数时候只有一句话。我到底是不是完蛋了
english_item: Holy mixed signals!!!!! In person? He’s excellent. We talk for hours with tons of eye contact, physical contact, laughing, and never a second of silence. Conversations get deep, suggestive, funny, all of the above. He invites me to his concerts. His parents like me. I’m even celiac, and he brought gluten free banana bread to class (we’re in college) so I could participate in the little post-finals party. Things are great.  Over text and social media? Terrible. I’m lucky if I get one response a day. Most of the time they’re one sentence. Am I cooked, or am I cooked?

chinese_item: 我可以认为这是一次约会吗？  我（27岁）被我的大学同学（22岁，女）邀请去她家购物并一起吃午饭。这是我人生中第一次和女生约会，我当时唯一的想法就是不能让她觉得我搞砸了。我们约在同一个地方见面，然后一起去了商店。她问了我一些关于裙子的喜好，然后买了一条。她试穿了裙子，并给我看了我穿的效果。之后我们一起吃了午饭。午饭后我送她回家，然后就离开了。她说非常感谢我抽出时间陪她。她说和她在一起的时候她感觉很开心。但我从她的朋友那里听说她暂时没有恋爱计划。  所以，这算是一次约会吗？还是只是普通的一天
english_item: ？,Can I assume it as a date I myself(27) asked by my Uni classmate(22F) to come for her shopping and also for Lunch. This was my first ever outing with a girl in my life and only thing I had in mind that I should not make her feel that I should screw this one up. We met a common place and traveled to the shop. She purchased the dress by asking my preferences. She trialed the dress and she showed me how I look. And then we went to have a lunch. After that I dropped her off at her home and left. She said thank you so much for spending time with me. I felt really happy when you're with me. But I heard from her friends that she's not going to be in relationship for some time.  So is this possibly a date or a normal day?

chinese_item: 有没有人跟我一样，做“简单”的事情时，如果被人看着就会感到紧张？比如，我知道怎么插USB，也知道怎么烤面包。但如果有人看着我，我就会突然觉得自己好像从来没接触过任何东西似的。我的大脑会忘记步骤，东西也会掉，整个过程就像一场表演。这是社交焦虑症的表现，还是……人之常情？
english_item: Does anybody else get stressed when someone watches you do a "simple" task? Like, yes I know how to plug in a USB. Yes, I know how to make toast. But if someone's watching me, I suddenly feel like I've never interacted with an object in my life. My brain forgets steps. I drop stuff. It becomes a full performance. Is this a social anxiety thing or just… human?

chinese_item: 克服表演焦虑：我与评判恐惧和被观察时的生理反应作斗争  您好，我是一名22岁的男性，一直深受被观察时表演焦虑的困扰。每当我在被注视的情况下尝试做某件事时，我都会感到颤抖、心跳加速，并且会过度思考。我担心即使是犯一个小小的错误也会导致批评、质疑或责骂，这会加剧我的焦虑，以至于我后悔尝试这件事。  例如，在实验室里，如果有人看着，我用移液器将试剂滴入样品时，我的手就会发抖。同样，当我开车时，如果妈妈坐在副驾驶座上，我会担心撞到坑洼，害怕她会因为我的任何失误而做出反应。这种对评判的恐惧和对审视的生理反应每天都在影响着我的自信心
english_item: Confronting Performance Anxiety: My Struggle with Fear of Judgment and Physical Symptoms Under Observation Hi, I am a 22 year old male struggling with a persistent fear when performing tasks under observation. Whenever I attempt something while being watched, I experience trembling, a racing heartbeat, and intense overthinking. I worry that making even a small mistake will lead to criticism, questions about my actions, or scolding, which amplifies my anxiety to the point where I regret attempting the task altogether.   For example, in lab settings, my hands shake when pipetting reagents into samples if someone is watching. Similarly, while driving with my mum in the passenger seat, I grow anxious about hitting a pothole, fearing her reaction to any error. This fear of judgment and physical response to scrutiny affects my confidence daily.

chinese_item: 想继续前进，但还没准备好  你好  我（23岁，男）四月初结束了一段感情，这让我很痛苦。她带我去戒指店，几天后就离开了。我已经接受了分手的事实，现在正在疗伤。我想利用五月好好思考一下我的人生方向。  我也搬到了一个新的地方，结交了一些新朋友。我还认识了一个女孩（23岁，女），她就像我的翻版。我们很合得来，有很多共同的兴趣爱好，而且她也对恋爱持开放态度（从她的描述来看，我并没有直接问她）。我们现在成了很好的朋友。  问题是，我决心利用五月好好思考，但我无法否认，在其他任何情况下我都会主动追求她。我发现自己给她发短信，让她随时可以提议其他的聚会，这样她就不会觉得被冷落了。这让我意识到，我内心深处藏着一些包袱。我们都是年轻人，生活也都过得不错。  我正在考虑要不要告诉她我对她有意思，但我需要一些时间，又不想拖着她。但我也不想一直沉默，最后彻底失去她。  不知道该怎么办
english_item: Want to move forward but not ready Hello   I(23m)got out of a relationship at the beginning of April, it was hard on me. She took me to a ring store then left a few days later. I’ve accepted that it’s over and now I’m healing. I want to spend May discerning where my life is headed.   I have also moved to a new area and have been making new friends. I also met this girl (23F) who seems to be a mirror image of me. We click very well, have the same interests as me, and is open to a relationship(from what she has said, I haven’t directly asked). We have become good friends.   The problem is I’m committed to taking May as discernment time, but I cant sit here and say I wouldn’t shoot my shot under literally any other circumstance. I caught myself texting her to feel free to suggest a different hangout so she doesn’t feel unheard, which made me realize I have baggage. We’re both young adults and in good places in life.   I’m contemplating telling her I’m interested but I need some time but I don’t want to drag her along. But I don’t want to stay silent and lose her all together.   Confused where to go

I know you don’t wanna be working all those crazy hours”  I said I’m happy, stop trying to play psychologist, you can’t tell me how I’m actually feeling.

chinese_item: 我觉得我的课堂演讲搞砸了  今天我要做一个关于一本书的演讲，还要制作一张假的电影海报，海报上要有扮演主角的演员，以及三句引语来说服观众。演讲开始时要概括故事情节和背景，然后解释选择这些引语的理由，之后再解释为什么选择这些演员。最后还要给这本书打分。我已经练习了好几天，本来以为胜券在握，直到说到第二句引语的时候，我突然忘了自己要说什么，整个人都懵了，一个字也说不出来。我不知道自己愣了多久，感觉像过了一个小时，周围的目光都集中在我身上。老师让我继续说第三句引语，但我还在努力回忆，结果说到第三句的时候又卡壳了。最后我总算说完了，但几乎没说出任何我想说的话。演讲的其他部分都还不错，但讲完之后我感觉自己快要崩溃了。那是我做过的最糟糕的演讲，感觉自己丢尽了脸。现在我担心自己是班上演讲最差的，别人会一直嘲笑我。希望我不是唯一一个搞砸学校演讲的人，不过我知道的是，接下来的三个月我都不想去上课了
english_item: I Think I Bombed My Class Speech Today I had to do a speech about a book, where you make a fake movie poster about the book that includes the actors starring as the main characters, and 3 quotes to persuade the audience. You start by summarizing the plot and setting, then describing the reasoning behind the quotes to persuade people, and then after that you explain why you chose the actors. You finally end it off with a rating of the book. So I’ve been practicing this for days and thought I had it in the bag, and I did until the second quote in which I randomly forgot what I was going to say, froze and couldn’t say a word. I’m not sure how long I froze but it felt like an hour with stares pelting me. My teacher just said to move on to the 3rd quote but I was still trying to recollect my memories and froze on that quote too. I was able to finish it but I barely said anything I wanted to. The rest of the speech was fine but after I just wanted to die. It was my worst speech yet and I felt humiliated in every way possible. Now I’m worried that I got the worst speech in the class and that people won’t let me live it down. Hopefully I’m not the only one who’s screwed up a school presentation, though all I know is that I don’t want to show up to class for the next 3 months. ??

chinese_item: 我到底是喜欢他，还是喜欢他？  我经常想起他，几乎一整天都在想他。但我跟他在一起的时候，并没有那种心动的感觉。感觉像是好朋友，但他可能只把我当朋友/同学。我们都练摔跤，他真的很有趣也很可爱。但我无法想象我们在一起的样子。跟他在一起我不会紧张，但他关注我的时候，我就会感到非常开心。  也可能是我在他身上看到了我爸爸的优点。他的幽默、低沉的嗓音，以及沉稳但又支持朋友的性格。（他跟我爸爸还是很不一样。）  我在一所小学校，他算是学校里的风云人物。每个人都曾经暗恋过他，我还撒谎说我以前喜欢过他。  但是！我几乎可以肯定他喜欢的是别人，我的朋友E。他们关系特别好，就像那种经常视频聊天的闺蜜一样。我另一个（可能是假的？）朋友也喜欢他，而且表现得很明显。（她有个交往七个月的男朋友……）  总之，我对他没有那种心动的感觉，也没有暧昧的举动，但我很欣赏他，也许只是想和他成为好朋友
english_item: ？,"Do I Like Him as a Friend or Crush? I think about him a lot. All day. But I don't feel all giggly and flirty around him. Kinda like close friends but he probably only sees me as a friend/classmate. We both do wrestling and he's really funny and cute. But I can't see us together. I don't get nervous around him but when he pays attention to me I just feel so happy.  I could also be me seeing the best parts of my dad in him. His humor, deep voice, and calmness but supportive of his friends. (He is still very different from my dad)  I'm in a small school and hes kinda the 'popular' guy. Everyone's had a crush on him at some point and I lied and told him I USED to like him.  HOWEVER! I'm pretty sure he likes someone else, my friend E. They're like TIGHT. Like best friends face timing casually close. And my other (maybe fake?) friend likes him too and makes it really obvious to me. (She has a bf of 7 months..).  TL;DR, I don't have like butterflies and flirtatious feelings towards him but I look up to him and maybe just want to be his close friend???

chinese_item: 为什么有些人这么粗鲁无礼？  今天我在墨尔本等过马路，几个美国人从后面走过来，开始大声地、粗鲁地批评我。他们说我没按过马路按钮，因为他们觉得我习惯了事事都有人帮忙。然后他们嚷嚷着最好赶紧按，说我显然没常识。但他们没意识到的是，我其实在他们过来之前就已经按了按钮，而且红色的指示灯也亮着，表明按钮已经被按过了。我真的很后悔当时没有为自己辩解，但作为一个独自旅行的年轻女性，当时感觉很脆弱，我觉得还是假装没听见比较好。  我平时很少发帖说这些事，但不知怎么的，这次的经历真的让我很恼火。我想发这个帖子就是想呼吁大家友善待人，也想看看有没有什么办法可以不被粗鲁的人影响。谢谢
english_item: Why are people so openly rude? I was in Melbourne today waiting to cross the road before some Americans came up behind me and started to criticise me out loud rudely. They claimed that I hadn't pressed the button to cross the road as they assumed that I was so used to having everything in life done for me. They then exclaimed they they had better press the button as II clearly had no common sense. What they didn't realise, however, was that I had indeed pressed the button prior to their arrival, and the red man was clearly lit up, indicating that the button had been pressed. I really regret not speaking up for myself, but as a young solo female traveller who was feeling rather vulnerable, I thought it was better to continue to pretend that I couldn't hear them.  I don't normally post about these things, but for some reason, this experience really bugged me. I guess this is just a post asking people to be kind and to see if people have any tips for not letting rude people get to you. Thanks xx

chinese_item: 我的狗被她未经我允许就关在我的房间里。我的室友通常比我待在家里的时间长，而我的狗会对很大的噪音（比如吸尘器、搅拌机、有人进出等等）吠叫。我知道这一点，也尝试过一些方法来安抚它（比如给它玩具、零食、骨头）。我知道训练我的狗不是她的责任。但是，我无法控制它在我不在家时对她行为的反应。然而，我认为当它焦虑不安、对着门吠叫时，她却一直关着我的门，这很不合适。我知道她这样做是瞒着我的，因为我经常回家时发现门半开着，而我离开时并没有关门。我该怎么办？
english_item: My dog is being kept in my room without my notice My roommate is usually home more than I am and my dog barks at loud noises (vacuums, blenders, when people leave/enter, etc). I know this and I've gone over ways to help calm him down (kongs, treats, bone). I know it's not her responsibility to train my dog. But I don't have any control over how he reacts to what she does when I'm not home, however, I don't think it's right to keep my door closed while he is anxious and stressed out, barking at the door. I know that she does this without telling me because I often come home with my door half open which is not how I left it. AIO? How do I address this?

chinese_item: 当有人说“放松点”的时候……好像我能控制似的。  啊，经典的“放松点”建议。要是真像按个开关那么简单就好了！如果我能关掉我过度兴奋的神经系统，我早就像猫一样在阳光下打盹儿了。但事实并非如此，我现在方圆五英里内的每个声音、光线和想法都异常敏感。拜托，能不能稍微理解一下

chinese_item: 过度兴奋的时候，你是怎么控制食欲的？哪怕只是一件小事，都可能让我心烦意乱，感觉一整天都毁了，胃都快要麻木好几个小时。  比如，我一直很想吃某种食物，但因为要去面包店，而面包店总是人满为患，所以一直拖着没吃。  最后，我在面包店找到了所需食材的一部分，这让我更加焦躁不安。  为了买到第二部分，我不得不去超市。  人们真的让我很恼火，他们不停地撞我，或者径直朝我走过来，把我堵在过道里唯一的瓶颈处，挡住了我需要拿东西的地方……  我急着离开，结果却忘了我老公上周忘了把黄油放回冰箱。但我吃这种食物的时候一定要抹黄油。  我不是故意找茬，真的不是。  但当时我被超市里的各种事情刺激得不行，再加上没买到所有需要的食材，实在太失望了，以至于我一点都不饿了。  两个小时过去了，我还是没吃东西，只是因为胃里一直处于兴奋状态，吃不下。  感觉好像因为一件事出了差错，我就什么都不想吃了。  这让我更加烦躁，因为我知道我应该吃东西，我也想吃东西，但我就是吃不下
english_item: How do you deal with eating while being overstimulated?Going through just a small event can upset me or make me feel like the entire day is ruined. Effectively closing off my stomach for hours. For example, had been craving a certain food for so long, but been putting it off because it required me to go to a bakery, which is always crowded.  Eventually I found 1 part of the required stuff at the bakery which already had me on edge.  Had to go to the grocery store to get part 2. People were really getting on my nerves because they kept bumping into me or coming straight for me, blocking me in the only bottlenecks of that aisle, blocking the parts I needed to take something,...  I needed to get out ASAP, and with that, forgot that my husband had forgotten to put back the butter in the fridge last week. But I only eat this food with butter. I'm not trying to be difficult, I'm really not.  But at that point I was so overstimulated by the store, disappointed by not having all the necessary ingredients anyway, that I wasn't even hungry anymore.  It's 2 hours later and I still haven't eaten just because my stomach doesn't allow it while feeling overstimulated. It feels like because one thing got ruined, I'd rather not have any food at all.  Which is only making me more cranky because I know I SHOULD be eating, I WANT to be eating, but I just physically can't...

chinese_item: 这个月我最难忘的高敏感人群日常：  - 我在玩马里奥赛车Wii，想在所有大奖赛里都拿到三星，结果玩得太投入了，只好停下来休息一下??  - 性爱后多巴胺骤降，我不得不休息一天来恢复。感觉就像宿醉一样哈哈哈哈  - 我在约会软件上匹配到一个人，结果被对方“放鸽子”了，我为此难过了四天。我们甚至都没见过面??  -我的猫咪们太可爱了，我都哭了??  -玩马里奥赛车玩到崩溃，对着电视大喊大叫，但我觉得电视听不见:(  -想到在一个宁静的周日早晨，一边喝着茉莉花茶，一边望着窗外，听着我最爱的音乐，就能让我感到如此幸福，我不禁热泪盈眶。这世间真正的美好就在那里，等着我去拥抱，而且永远都会在那里等着我去拥抱:)  人生就像一场精彩的旅程，虽然作为高敏感人群，有些时刻格外艰难，但我绝不会用任何东西来交换。好的和坏的，都让我觉得自己活得淋漓尽致。我们或许需要改变生活方式，学习一些技巧来应对我们敏感的天性，但天哪，那些非高敏感人群错过了我们高敏感人群所拥有的那些最美好的体验。这真是一种恩赐
english_item: My top hsp things of the month -i was trying to get 3 stars on all grand prix races on mario kart wii and it got really intens so i had to take a break ??  -I had to take a day to recover from having sex because of the dopamine crash. I felt like i was hungover whahaha  -I got ghosted by someone i matched with on a datingapp and was depressed for like 4 days. We didnt even meet ??   -I cried because my cats are just so darn cute ??  -got frustrated at mario kart so i screamed at my tv but i dont think the tv can hear :(   -got teary eyed because of the realization that a calm sunday morning with a cup of jasmine tea while staring out the windows, playing my fav music can make me feel as deeply happy as it does. This worlds true delights is just there for me to grab and will always be there for me to grab :)   Life is one hell of a ride and even tho some moments as an hsp are especially difficult, i wouldnt change it for the world. The good and the bad all contribute to making me feel like im living my life to the fullest. We might need to alter our lifes and learn skills to cope with our sensitive nature but gosh are non hsp missing out on some of the most beautiful experiences we hsp have. It truly is a blessing <33

chinese_item: 她纵容我那些糟糕透顶的冷笑话  我（20多岁，男）经常会讲一些很烂的冷笑话，连我自己都觉得不好笑，但我暗恋的女孩（20多岁，女）却偏偏纵容我。我们聊天的时候聊到了骷髅，我讲了个很烂的骨头梗，立刻就收回来了，说了句“嘿，我喜欢烂骨头梗”，然后就没再继续讲下去了。  不过，后来有一天，我们又聊到了井，我就开始讲井的梗，希望她能注意到，结果她终于忍不住笑出了声：“我要杀了你！” 我又一次妥协了，直到她澄清自己是在开玩笑，然后又一次纵容了我。不过这次，我坚持了下来，每句话都讲井的梗，讲了大概五分钟，直到我精疲力竭，不得不放弃。我真是彻底被她迷住了
english_item: 。,"She enables my terrible no good very bad puns So, I (20sM) can often slip into bad puns, jokes even I find to be not that good, but my crush (20sF) decided to enable me instead.
