chinese_item: 有人在说了/做了刻薄的事之后会立刻感到眼前发黑/头晕吗？我尝试搜索过相关信息，但没有找到任何类似的例子。先简单介绍一下我的性格，以防万一。我从来都不是一个喜欢与人发生冲突的人。我会在精神上和行动上都尽量避免冲突。我也很难说“不”，甚至会毫不犹豫地帮助别人，哪怕他们可能并不值得。从我记事起，我的头和眼睛就会出现这种情况：当我维护自己的权益，脱口而出表达自己的真实感受（就像口不择言一样）时，我的眼前就会立刻开始发黑，并且会感到头晕。这种情况只会持续一秒钟。有人有过类似的经历吗？我想知道这是一种生理反应，还是大脑中突然释放的化学物质？
english_item: DAE experience vision swimming/ dizziness immediately after saying/ doing something mean? I've tried looking this up but haven't been able to find anything similar.  Just for background on my character, in case this matters, I've never been a confrontational person. I will go out of my way mentally and physically to avoid conflict. I also have a very hard time saying no, and will give the shirt off my back to even people who may not deserve it.  Ever since I can remember, my head and eyes do this thing where, when I do stand up for myself and blurt out how I really feel (like word vomit), my vision will immediately start to swim and I get dizzy. It only lasts for a second.  DAE experience this? I'm wondering if it is a physical reaction or sudden chemical release in my brain?

chinese_item: 当你从迎面走来的人身边经过时，你会看向哪里？  我总是会过度思考，即使只是从别人身边走过，我的目光应该看向哪里。到底应该看向哪里呢？  我觉得只有在友好地打招呼时（但我从来不打招呼），眼神交流才是允许的。否则，我会觉得自己盯着对方看很奇怪。但我又是个好奇心很强的人，喜欢观察别人，所以很难控制自己。  我也不喜欢直视前方，因为那样感觉很奇怪，像个机器人一样。而看着地板又感觉像是在告诉别人我有社交焦虑症。  我觉得任何形式的眼神游移都会显得我缺乏自信，让人觉得我容易被抢劫。所以我感觉必须和对方进行眼神交流，才能让他们觉得我不容易被抢。但这样一来，他们又可能会觉得我在瞪他们，然后抢劫我/和我发生冲突（我知道这种情况发生的概率极低，但总会忍不住去想“万一呢？”）。  所以，你觉得在社交场合下，眼神应该看向哪里比较得体？或者，如果你也有这种困扰，你会怎么处理呢
english_item: Where do you look while walking past someone walking towards you? I always overthink where I'm looking when I'm just walking past someone. where are you supposed to look?  I feel like eye contact is only allowed if you are doing a friendly greeting (which I never do) otherwise I feel like a weirdo looking at the person but I'm such a curious person and I like looking at people so it's hard to stop myself.  I also don't like looking straight ahead either because that just feels weird to do like I'm a robot or something and looking at the floor feels like I'm just announcing I have social anxiety.  And just any form of looking away from someone I feel shows I have no confidence and makes people think I'm easy to rob so I feel like I have to make eye contact to show them I'm not easy to rob but then Ive made eye contact and they might think I'm giving them the stink eye and rob me/confront me (I know the chance of any of it happening is so incredibly low but it's just that 'what if?' situation)  So where do you think is most social acceptable place to look? Or how do you deal with this issue if you have it?

chinese_item: 有没有人和我一样，几乎每天上班都在刻意避免和人交谈？  比如，故意讲好几次冷笑话，或者说“圣诞快乐”，或者祝别人好运，这样就不用面对“该说什么”的焦虑，也不用让周围的人听到你的声音？有没有人和我一样，每天都在扼杀与人建立联系的机会
english_item: Does anyone else avoid having conversations almost all day everyday at work? By making intentionally lame jokes multiple times throughout the day or saying Merry Christmas, or telling someone good luck so you don't have to suffer the What to say anxiety and others around you hearing your voice? Does anyone else actually ruin the potential of getting to know anyone every day?

chinese_item: 人们总是想当然地认为所有的分手都是因为仇恨或不再相爱。
english_item: People automatically assuming all breakups come from hate or falling out of love.

chinese_item: 噩梦般的室友大家好，我非常需要大家的建议，因为我的伴侣遇到了这样的问题。我的伴侣和另外两个室友合住。他们最初是朋友，在学校认识的。其中一个室友，我姑且称她为“N”，是个控制欲极强、善于操纵的人。“N”一边贬低我的伴侣，一边又装作最好的朋友。我的伴侣终于忍无可忍，开始疏远她。现在，“N”又和第三个室友称兄道弟，她们成了好朋友，而且感觉她们好像在联手对付我的伴侣。她们经常占用厨房和公共空间，导致我的伴侣因为这种令人不舒服的环境而被困在自己的房间里。我的伴侣目前正在努力搬出去，但这需要时间。请问大家有什么建议可以应对这种噩梦般的室友吗？她们现在简直就是一群恶霸。
english_item: Nightmare roommates Hello everyone, I would really appreciate any advice in this situation on behalf of my partner. My partner lives with two other roommates. They all started out as friends and met in school. Their one roommate I'll call "N" is an extremely controlling and manipulative person. "N" was constantly belittling my partner while at the same time was acting like their best friend. My partner finally decided they had enough and started distancing themselves. N has now latched herself on to the third roommate and they're now best friends and it also feels like they're teaming up on my partner. They're constantly taking up space in the kitchen and common space so my partner is forced to be trapped into their room due to the uncomfortable setting. My partner is currently trying to move out but it's a process. Does anyone have advice dealing with nightmare roommates who are basically just bullies at this point?

chinese_item: 两年前我的个人卫生很差，这个问题一直影响着我。  现在我的个人卫生状况好多了，可以说两年前的所有问题我都解决了。但是，以前糟糕的体味就像幽灵一样挥之不去。我最大的问题是，有时候我甚至不敢离开教室去洗手间，或者干脆等到所有人都走了才去。因为我总担心别人会闻到我身上的味道，即使我已经洗过澡，也注意了个人卫生
english_item: I used to have bad hygiene 2 years ago and it still affects me till now. My hygiene got way better now I would say everything that I had problems with 2 years ago I fixed them . But still!! The aftermath of having bad BO before is hovering over me like a ghost that just cannot leave me alone. My biggest problem is that sometimes I avoid leaving class to go to the bathroom or even just wait till everyone left so I could get up. Because I have this paranoia that someone might get awhiff of me smelling even when I literally showered and took care of my hygiene?!

chinese_item: 批评  我的亲戚刚刚告诉我，我的几个小表弟表妹在她面前说了我的坏话，比如我为一些事情没办成而道歉，或者主动提出如果他们心情不好可以陪他们聊聊天。我不明白这怎么就成了批评，我只是做我自己而已。我不知道这些话有多少是他们说的，又有多少是她挑拨离间的结果。我感到非常难过，因为他们之前明明都表现得很喜欢和我在一起，甚至为了能和我单独相处而争吵。我现在完全不知道该怎么和他们相处了
english_item: Criticism My relative just told me mean things my young cousins have said about me to her, things like saying sorry about something not working out or offering myself as an additional person to chat to if they’re ever upset. I don’t understand why this was criticism when it was just me being me, I don’t how much to believe came from them or from her poisoning them against me. I feel devastated as they gave me every indication they loved spending time with me and fought over getting alone time with me. I have no idea how to be around them now.

chinese_item: 疯狂室友：把微波炉“劫持”了我的室友回老家一个月，临走前，她把微波炉包得严严实实，好像是什么宝贝似的。我根本不用它，但她包得也太夸张了吧……简直匪夷所思。不仅如此，她还把餐具、小苏打，还有其他一些乱七八糟的东西都藏了起来——总之就是确保我和另一个室友都用不了。我完全理解不想和别人分享自己的私人物品，这很正常。但如果微波炉是禁区，为什么不把它放在自己的房间里，非要占用公共厨房空间，放一个我们碰都碰不到的东西呢？感觉有点阴阳怪气，而且说实话，这让公共空间感觉很别扭。
english_item: Crazy flatmate: took her microwave hostage So my flatmate went to her hometown for a month, and before she left, she wrapped her microwave like it's a precious item. I don't even use it, but the way it's been wrapped off is… next level. On top of that, she hid all her cutlery, baking soda, and who knows what else—just to make sure neither I nor our other roommate can use any of it. I totally get not wanting to share your personal items. That's fair. But if the microwave is off-limits, why not just keep it in her room instead of occupying shared kitchen space with something we can't touch? Feels a bit passive-aggressive and honestly just makes the shared space feel weird.

chinese_item: 我该如何结束这段关系？  我和暗恋对象是很好的朋友，已经一年了。但我对她的感情难以承受，我觉得我只能和她断绝关系，因为这样下去不行。我该怎么做？直接拉黑她吗？还是告诉她我的感受，然后我们俩都拉黑对方？（我知道她不喜欢我，而且就算拉黑了我们可能也合不来）。我不知道该怎么办，因为我想让她彻底离开我的生活，这对我们俩都好，但我不知道该怎么做，但我今天就想这么做
english_item: How do I end this? Im good friends with my crush, for a year. But my feelings for her are unbearable and I feel I can only cut her off, because this is not working. How do I do it? Do I simply block her without a word? Tell her about my feelings and how we gotta both block eachother? ( I know she doesn't have feelings for me and we probably wouldn't work together anyway). I just don't know cause I want her out of my life, for the better of both of us, but idk how to go forth with it, but I want to do it today.

chinese_item: 当人们不给你机会就说你不擅长社交时很多人不想主导谈话，但当我们初次或第二次见面时，他们却会把主导谈话的责任推卸给我。我很难和不太熟的人开启对话，所以谈话往往无疾而终。然后对方会说我不擅长社交，却不承担任何责任。社交是每个人的责任，而不是一个人的责任。
english_item: When people say that you are bad at socialising without giving you a chance Lots of people don't want to lead the conversation, but then they through the responsibility of leading it when we are meeting for the first or second time. I have trouble leading a conversation with somebody that it is not that close, so then the conversation ends nowhere. Then the other person will say that I'm bad at socialising without taking responsibility, socialising is a responsibility of everyone involved, not of just one person.

chinese_item: 为什么从来没人邀请我参加课外活动？回首我的人生和养育我的人，有时真的会让我感到沮丧。我不想把现在的任何问题都归咎于童年，但确实有些选择*非常*不妥。我的时间在父母两边轮流度过。这里几年，那里几年。但没人真正关心我在做什么。只要我不被逮捕，或者不给他们添麻烦就行。我并不想要孩子，但我确实想过，如果我有孩子，我一定会努力让他们对*一些*课外活动感兴趣。足球、篮球、乐队、天文俱乐部。真的什么都行。但我小时候从来没有人邀请或要求我参加这些活动。小时候，我觉得这是件好事。有更多的时间可以和朋友们一起玩。但现在回想起来，如果当时有这些活动，真的能帮助我更好地为大学生活做好准备。学校对我的支持实在太差，所以我干脆辍学，这样就能早点上社区大学了。我常常会想，如果我有个孩子，你会希望他们拥有什么。比如医疗保险、必要时的牙齿矫正、大学储蓄账户、一些能让大学申请加分的课外活动、一辆二手车等等。这些……我觉得都是很正常的事情。我发这篇帖子并不是想自怨自艾，如果听起来像那样，我很抱歉。我只是好奇有没有人和我一样。如果你当年没有参加过这些课外活动，现在感觉如何？
english_item: WAE not ever asked to do extracurricular activities? I look back at my life and those who raised me, and it really depresses me some times. I don't want to blame any of my issues on my childhood, but there were some *really* questionable choices. I had my time split between my mother's side and father's side. A couple years here, a few there. But nobody really cared about what I was doing. As long as I wasn't getting arrested or anything, or causing them any personal annoyances. I don't think I want kids, but one thing I've thought about is that I'd definitely try to get them interested in *some* extracurricular activity. Football, basketball, band, an astronomy club. Literally anything. But I was never asked or told to do them. As a kid, I saw it as a blessing. More free time to hang out. But with the hindsight of adulthood, it could have really helped me prepare for college. My school support was so bad that I straight up dropped out so that I could start community college sooner.  I just think about if I had a kid. There's just things you want for them. Health insurance, braces if they need them, a savings account for college, a few extracurriculars to look good on a college application, a used beater car. Just... things I feel that are mostly normal. This isn't supposed to be a woe is me post, so sorry if it comes off that way. I'm just curious if this sounds familiar to anyone. If you didn't do any of those extracurriculars, how do you feel about it now?

chinese_item: 至于发明小组面试的那个人，我真希望你倒霉透顶。  首先，我本来就讨厌面试。光是认识陌生人就够让人头疼了，而且我知道自己已经有竞争对手了，更别提那些有关系的人基本上已经稳拿这份工作了。面试本身就已经够让人紧张的了。但是小组面试？？？我连去都不想去。我知道肯定还有其他更优秀的人，这已经够糟了，还要围坐在一起听他们吹嘘自己比我强多少，简直让人崩溃。我脱颖而出的机会更少了。而且感觉没人懂得等待轮到自己。大家都在抢话，抢着发言。我这个人比较有礼貌，即使轮到我发言，我也会主动闭嘴。结果到了面试结束，我却一句话都没说
english_item: And to the one who created group interviews, I wish nothing but the worst for you. First and foremost, I hate interviews to begin with. Like, the whole meeting new people thing, knowing that I have competition already, knowing that if somebody else has connections they basically already have the job. It's already a stressful circumstance. But GROUP INTERVIEWS??? I'm not even gonna come at that point. It's bad enough that I know that there are probably other better people, but having to sit in a circle and listen to them say how much better they are than you sucks. I have less of a chance to stand out. And it's like nobody understands waiting your turn. People are all talking over each other, speaking out of turn. And me being the polite person I am, I will gladly stop talking even though it's clearly my turn to talk. But then by the end of the interview, I've said nothing.

chinese_item: “共情者”让你心情糟糕  你本来在做自己的事，像往常一样过着一天，突然有人问你“你还好吗??”，你回答“还好”，然后他们就坚持说你“看起来很沮丧”。这尤其让我恼火，因为大多数时候问这个问题的人都是完全陌生的人，他们根本不了解我，也不知道我的行为举止是否有什么不同。就算有人真的心情不好，我也怀疑他们是否愿意在公共场合向同事倾诉
english_item: 。,"“Empaths” forcing a bad day on you You’ll be minding your business and going about your day like normal when suddenly they ask you “are you ok??”, you’ll say yes and then they’ll insist that you “seem upset”. It especially irks me because most of the time it’ll come from an absolute stranger that doesn’t know me well enough to know whether I’m acting differently or not . Even when someone isn’t ok I doubt they want to open up about it to their coworker in a public setting.

chinese_item: 我们必须每天面对恐惧。  我讨厌这种恐惧症，因为我每天都必须面对我所恐惧的事物。  人们患有各种各样的恐惧症，他们可能会尝试暴露疗法来克服它们。但他们并非必须如此。如果你害怕蜘蛛、飞机或其他类似的东西，你可以像平常一样生活，很少会再次感受到与触发因素相关的恐惧。如果有一天你鼓起勇气去面对它们，那很好。  而你呢？你必须每天与人交往。你无法躲进一个可以逃避恐惧的安全空间，因为那样的地方根本不存在。有些日子你可能没那么勇敢，但你仍然必须去面对恐惧。如果你无法克服恐惧，你就找不到工作，也无法支付账单。如果你无法克服恐惧，你可能会发现自己孤独终老，后悔自己本可以拥有的人生。  失败的代价高得离谱，这太不公平了
english_item: We have to face our fears everyday I hate this disorder because what I'm fearing is there to be tackled every single day.   People have various phobias, and they may try exposure therapy to overcome them. They don't have to, though. If you're scared of spiders or planes or something like that, you can just live your life normally, rarely ever experiencing fear connected to your triggers. If one day you're feeling brave enough to face them, good for you.  This? You have to interact with people every day. There is no falling back into a safe space, where you don't have to face your fear, because that place simply does not exist. Some days you might not be feeling as brave but you have to go and face this fear anyway. If you don't overcome your fear, you don't get a job and can't pay the bills. If you don't overcome your fear, you may find yourself alone and regretting the life you could have lived.   The cost of failing is ridiculously high, and it's just so unfair.

chinese_item: 当人们（通常是老年人）坚持用他们并不熟悉的方式做事，然后因为自身能力不足而感到沮丧，并期望别人来解决问题时，就会发生这种情况。我妈妈总是这样，但我注意到很多其他成年人也这样，这让我很抓狂。举个例子。我和我妈妈去得来速买外卖。我们完全可以直接开车去点餐，没有任何理由不能这样做。但她非要用手机应用。这倒没什么。我大多数时候点外卖也喜欢用手机应用。但是，她不会用手机应用，而且身体也不允许她正确操作。结果，她花了十分钟尝试使用手机应用，却因为操作不熟练而感到沮丧，然后她把手机递给我，命令我来操作。我不到一分钟就学会了，所以这部分没什么大不了的。我真是受不了听她发脾气，因为她非要去做她明知自己不会的事情。顺便一提，这已经是第三次在这家店发生这种事了。每次我提出用其他餐厅的app点餐，或者说用app点餐能更划算，她都说app太难用。可每次她自己点餐的时候，如果知道这家店有app，她都会尝试用，每次都一样，真是让我抓狂。
english_item: When people (usually older people) insist on doing things in a way they don't know how to properly do it and then get frustrated by their own lack of ability and expect someone else to fix it. My mom does this all the time but I've noticed a lot of other adults do it too and it drives me crazy.  For example. My mom and I went to get take out from a drive through. There is absolutely no reason we couldn't just go through the drive through and order. Instead she insisted on using the app. That's fine. I prefer to use the app most times when doing take out. However, she doesn't know how to use the app and is physically incapable of doing it properly so what actually happened is she spent 10 minutes trying to use the app and getting frustrated because she couldn't and then she handed me her phone and demanded I do it. Took me less than a minute to figure out so that part isn't a big deal.  I just hate having to listen to her anger because she insists on doing things she knows she doesn't know how to do.  For reference this is the 3rd time this has happened with this particular place ans anytime I offer to order from an app for other places or say that we can get a better deal from the app she says they are too hard to use. When she's ordering she will try to use the app (if she knows they have one) every time though and it makes me want to scream because it's always the same thing.

chinese_item: 缺乏安全感，感觉男朋友不喜欢我  你好！  我（25岁，女）在伦敦一家不错的公司上班，但我社交能力很差，简直让我难受哈哈。  我该如何克服这种不安全感，变得更外向一些呢？  我在伦敦有几个朋友，和他们相处得不错，但我注意到在某些群体场合，我就像个无人问津的“隐形人”，你知道的。  我有社交焦虑症和注意力缺陷多动障碍（ADHD）。我不擅长闲聊，希望能变得更喜欢自己。  我的男朋友是个内向的人。他和我的一个朋友相处得很好，他很活泼开朗，爱笑，很外向。我觉得我们在一起的时候，他经常不是这样。我逗不笑他，但他却说我们很般配，他爱我。我也爱他。我觉得我的很多不安全感都源于此。有时候，他和我说话也不太多。他说是因为他跟我在一起感觉很自在，所以才不会一直对我这么热情，因为那样会让他很累。  我该如何停止和别人比较，摆脱不安全感？我该如何变得更外向？我和熟悉的人在一起时很活泼，但我很讨厌自己大部分时间都显得很拘谨。  我想报名参加游泳课，觉得这或许会有帮助，因为我想学游泳，而且每周还能和一群固定的人一起训练
english_item: Insecure and feeling like my bf doesn't like me Hello!   I (25f) work in a nice London office and I'm so socially awkward it hurts haha.  How do I stop being insecure and more outgoing?   I have a few friends in the city and get on well with them but I've noticed in certain group settings I'm the person that noone would care is gone, you know.   I have social anxiety and ADHD. I'm bad a small talk and want to get better at just liking myself.   My bf is an introvert. He gets on so well with a friend of mine and is bubbly and laughs and is outgoing. I feel like when we spend time together he's often not like this. I can't make him laugh but he says that we're a good match and he loves me. I love him too. A lot of my insecurity stems from this i think. He's not too talkative with me either at times. He says it's cause he's super comfortable around me and is not gonna be so outgoing with me all the time because that'd be exhausting.   How do I stop comparing myself, and feeling insecure? And how do I become more outgoing. I'm a lively person with people I'm comfortable with but I hate tjat I'm mostly awkward around people.   I want to sign up to swimming lessons and thought maybe that would be helpful cause I want to know how to swim but also I'd be able to spend time with a set group of people each week.

chinese_item: 我亲眼目睹了一只小鸭子被车撞死，这件事毁了我一整天的心情。  今天早些时候，我和三个朋友一起开车。突然，我注意到好几辆车都在急刹车，于是我拼命踩刹车，并猛打方向盘避开了前面的车。然后我和朋友们才意识到，原来是一辆车撞到了一群鸭子??。幸运的是，一些在人行道上行走的人帮忙把受伤的小鸭子抱到了人行道上，还有一位路人停车帮忙，并开车把受伤的小鸭子送到了动物医院。不幸的是，其中一只小鸭子没能活下来，一想到它躺在路上的尸体和内脏，我就难过得无法自拔，一整天都无法释怀。我和朋友们也停车帮忙，把鸭子一家转移到安全的地方，我知道我们都尽力了，但我还是难过得哭个不停。我的朋友们显然也很难过，但还没到哭的地步。现在我哭得精疲力竭，一想到有多少可怜的动物因为我们的车而遭受不必要的死亡，我就觉得很难过。我希望有更多办法来防止动物被车撞死。我知道在欧洲（？），他们会设置小栅栏来阻止动物过马路。有没有人和我一样，对看到动物被车撞死感到非常痛心
english_item: I witnessed someone run over a baby duck and it ruined my entire day. Earlier today, I was driving while three of my other friends were in the car with me. Then I noticed that a bunch of cars were suddenly braking so I braked as hard as I could and swerved out the way of the car in front of me. And then my friends and I realized that the cars all stopped because one of the cars had struck a family of ducks?? Luckily, some people who were walking on the sidewalk helped carry the injured ducklings to the sidewalk, and another person pulled over to help and also drove to the animal hospital with the injured ducklings. Unfortunately, one of the baby ducklings did not make it and just remembering what its dead body and guts on the road looked like made me so incredibly upset that I could not stop thinking about it all day. My friends and I also had pulled over to help the duck family reach safety, and I know we all did what we could but I was so upset I was crying the whole time. My friends were obviously upset too, but not to the point that they were crying over it. Now I just feel so emotionally drained from crying so much and I feel so terrible thinking about it and just how many poor animals face such unnecessary deaths because of our death vehicles. I wish there were more ways to prevent roadkill, I know in Europe(?) they have little fences to prevent animals from crossing the road. Does anyone else absolutely hate seeing roadkill.

chinese_item: 糟糕的心理医生经历  大家好！  我只是想分享一下昨天和心理医生糟糕的经历，想听听大家的意见。我不知道自己是不是夸大了这种糟糕的感觉，还是只是焦虑的大脑在胡思乱想。  我最近换了心理医生，这是我第二次去看他。我们讨论了是否要调整治疗方案，以及在我已经服用多年的拉莫三嗪（Lamictal）之外加一些其他药物。我问他治疗社交焦虑症的最佳方法是什么。他一边说着一边指了指自己的头，然后告诉我社交焦虑症主要是心理作用，哈哈。我心想：废话，这还用说吗??。然后他又说，我描述的那种社交焦虑症（感觉每个人都在盯着你看等等）很像自我中心，感觉自己是世界的中心。我觉得他的意思是，因为我长得太普通，所以没人会注意到我……我知道这又是我自己的解读，但离开诊室的时候我感觉很糟糕。  有没有人和我一样，在面对心理医生/精神科医生时，遇到过类似的糟糕经历？我发现，讽刺的是，往往是医生让我感觉更糟
english_item: Bad experience with psychiatrist Hello everyone!  I just wanted to share a pretty bad experience with my psychiatrist yesterday and get your opinions about it. I can’t tell if I’m exaggerating for feeling bad and it’s just my anxious brain talking.  I recently changed psychiatrist and it was my 2nd appointment with him. We talked about maybe changing treatment and adding something to the lamictal I’ve been taking for years for depression. I asked him what treatment was best for social anxiety. He told me social anxiety is primarily in your head by making a gesture towards his head lol. I thought no shit Sherlock ??. Then he told me social anxiety the way I explained it ( the feeling everyone is watching you etc) feels a lot like egocentrism and feeling like you’re the center of the world. And I felt like he was implying that nobody would look at me in the street because I’m too plain or something… I know it’s again interpretations I’m making but I felt pretty bad walking out of this.   Did anyone get a similar bad experience with your psychologist / psychiatrist in their reactions towards this disorder ? I noticed it’s often doctors who make me feel bad ironically.

chinese_item: 我需要一些建议。  大家好，请原谅我的英语不好，这不是我的母语，我正在努力学习。我看到这个社区的人们都很理解我，这让我感到很安心，可以向大家寻求建议。事情是这样的，我目前在一家大公司实习，老师把我安排到这里是因为她认为我是一个受过良好教育且勤奋的女孩——至少我是这么认为的。从我入学那天起，我就一直感到焦虑。我不敢和同事交流，很多时候都说不出话来，感觉像是肌肉紧张导致的失声，或许是焦虑引起的。这让我很沮丧，也加剧了我的焦虑。现在公司想聘用我，这是一家很棒的公司，也是一个很好的机会，但我真的感觉压力很大，甚至不知道该怎么和老师说话。更糟糕的是，老板非常阴阳怪气。前几天我没跟他打招呼，并非故意，只是当时走神了，他就开始说“实习生很没礼貌”，这让我难过很久。我总是看到他训斥别人，不知道自己是否做好了准备。再加上和同学说话时那种尴尬的沉默，我不知道该怎么办，这种感觉让我难以承受，也让我很难过。我知道如果我拒绝这份工作，大家肯定会觉得我傻，怎么会拒绝这样的机会，然后都会想方设法“安慰”我
english_item: I need some advice Hello people, excuse my English, it is not my first language and I am trying, I have seen that this community is so understanding and I have felt calm to ask for advice, what happens is that I am an internship student in a big company, the teacher put me there because she thinks I am an educated and hardworking girl or so I think. Since the day I entered I have always felt anxious, I did not feel comfortable talking to my colleagues, I could not speak many times, it seemed like a dysphonia by muscle tension or so by anxiety maybe and that frustrated me and gave me more anxiety, now the company wants to hire me, it is a great company and a good opportunity, but I am really saturated and I do not even know how to talk to my teacher, on top of that the boss is very passive-aggressive, the other day I did not greet him, it was not intentional, I was just distracted and he started to say the interns are very rude, which obviously has made me feel bad for a long time and I always see how he yells at others and I do not know if I am ready . Plus the awkward silence when I try to talk to my classmates, I don't know, it's overwhelming and I feel bad. I know that surely if I turn down the offer everyone will think, what a fool, who would turn down this offer and everyone tries to cheer me up.

chinese_item: 邀请男友的朋友来个惊喜晚餐  我第一次见到男友的朋友时，就邀请他们来吃顿惊喜晚餐。  我爱我的男友，我们认识一年了。最近我认识了他的好朋友和朋友的男朋友。气氛有点尴尬，我觉得我和他的朋友们没什么共同语言——虽然这没什么大不了的，但还是有点不自在。  我一直不太擅长和陌生人交流，高中之前，哪怕只是在超市和收银员简单聊几句，我都会紧张得不行。但我还是努力让自己多接触别人。  因为这是一段新的恋情，我想给他一个惊喜，所以邀请了他的朋友们来吃晚餐。当我开口邀请他们的时候（当时他正在阳台上抽烟），我感觉气氛超级尴尬。他离开房间后，他们开始自顾自地聊天，我不得不打断他们邀请他们过来。  顺便说一下，我努力想融入大家，问了很多问题，但好像问错了问题。我问了一些关于教育、食物、工作、音乐之类的简单问题……都是些和新朋友聊天时常聊的话题。  他们尴尬地答应了。之后我把信息发给他们，他们只回复了一句“嗯，挺好的”。连个表情符号都没有。  但我相信这会让我男朋友开心。虽然我现在有点紧张，因为我觉得我可能不该安排和陌生人一起吃顿惊喜晚餐。  我是不是太焦虑了？还是说这真的是个馊主意
english_item: Inviting boyfriends friends to surprise dinner I invited my boyfriends friends to a surprise dinner, the first time I met them.  I love my boyfriend. I have known him for a year. I have recently met his best friend and the friends boyfriend. It was a little bit awkward, I dont think I have that much in common with his friends - which is fiiine but still.   I have always had an issue talking with new people and before high school I would have a panic attacks just talking briefly with a cashier in a supermarket. But I try to get myself out there.  Since this is a new relationship, I wanted to surprise him by inviting his friends to dinner. And the moment I asked them (while he was off in the balcony to smoke ) i just got this super awkward vibe. They started talking to each other when he left the room and I had to interrupt them to invite them.   And by the way I tried to be social, I asked a bunch of questions but it seemed like I was asking the wrong ones. I asked simple ones about education, food, job, music… regular new friends topics.  They agreed awkwardly. I texted them the info later and they just answered ”yeah thats cool”. No smiley.  But I believe this will make my bf happy. Even though I have a hard time breathing right know because I feel like maybe I shouldnt have planned a suprise dinner with strangers.  Am I anxious or was this a bad Idea?

chinese_item: 我最好的朋友在知道我喜欢他之后也开始喜欢他了。  我已经跟她说过两百遍了，求求你别跟我提他，尊重我的界限。但她根本不听。她总是想方设法找他，他一出现她就咯咯笑，还让我把他的一切都告诉她。她是我最好的朋友，她知道我喜欢他大概一年了，现在她说她也喜欢他了，因为自从我开始谈论他之后她也开始注意到他了。她就是不停地谈论他。我不想对她太苛刻，我知道她控制不了自己喜欢谁，但真的很难过。我大概一周前才开始跟他说话，现在她又想尽办法跟他搭讪。我真的受不了了，我们可是三年的好朋友了
english_item: my bestfriend started liking my crush after she knew i like him i told her 200 times to please dont talk about him with me and respect my boundaries. she wont. she keeps trying to look for him and giggling when he's around and telling me to tell her everything about him. she is my bestfriend, and she knows i like him since like a year, she says she likes him too now cause she started noticing him after i started talking about him. she just wont stop talking about him. i dont want to be mean to her cause i know she cant control who she likes but it honestly hurts. i started talking to him like a week ago and now shes trying to do everything to talk to him too. i just cant yall we have been best friends for like 3 years now.

chinese_item: 有些人明明看到了“谢绝推销”的告示，却还是把垃圾放在你家门口/门把手上。  今天早上我不在家的时候，Ring 的监控录像拍到两个背着背包的女人挨家挨户推销。回家后发现门口放着一张“我想帮你卖房子”的传单和一个装在透明塑料袋里的迷你魔术擦，应该是某个房产经纪人送的。照片上看起来很年轻，估计是刚拿到执照，想招揽生意。  我家门口就挂着一张非常醒目、写得很清楚的“谢绝推销”告示，上面写得清清楚楚——谢绝传单、谢绝推销、谢绝宗教电话等等——因为有些人显然不知道“谢绝推销”是什么意思。结果那个背着背包的女人还是把传单留下了，而且她也不是第一个这么做的推销员。  我把传单扔进了垃圾桶，但这些人无视告示的行为真的让我很生气，尤其是告示写得这么清楚。万一有人一整天都不在家，那张传单就一直放在那里怎么办？这简直就是告诉潜在窃贼家里没人。??  而且这房子恰好是我家的老房子，我以前一直和爸爸住在这里，直到他两年前去世。感觉每次有人去世，这些房产中介就都冒出来了，感觉特别像在骚扰，特别冒犯人。  好了，今天我这个脾气暴躁的中年女人的抱怨就到此为止
english_item: 。??,"People who disregard detailed No Soliciting signs and leave crap on your doorstep/door handle anyway. Ring cam showed two women with backpacks going door to door this morning while I was gone. Came home to an I Want To Sell Your House flyer and mini magic eraser sponge in a clear plastic bag on the doorstep from some realtor. Looks young in the photo, so probably just got her license and is trying to drum up business.   I have a prominently displayed and detailed No Soliciting sign right by the front door that spells it all out - No flyers, no sales, no religious calls, etc.- since some people apparently don't know what no soliciting mean. The one backpacker left it anyway and is not the first solicitor to do so.   It went in the trash, but it really irritates me that these folks disregard the sign, particularly when it's spelled out. What if someone's gone all day and that flier just sat there? Way to tell potential burglars no one is home.??  And it just so happens that this is my longtime family home where I lived with my dad until he passed away about two years ago. Seems like these realtors come out of the woodwork when someone passes away and it just feels really predatory and intrusive.   There. Cranky, middle-aged girl rant for the day is done.??

chinese_item: 为什么网上聊天那么容易，现实生活中却很难好好交谈？  我在网上和人聊天时几乎没有社交焦虑，但一到现实生活中，我就大脑一片空白，说不出话来。这是为什么呢
english_item: What is it that makes it easy to text online but makes it so hard to have a conversation in real life ? I feel almost no social anxiety when talking to people online but when I do in person i get a blank mind and can’t talk properly. Why is that ??

chinese_item: 请朋友指出我的缺点以便我改正，这样会不会很奇怪？  我还没问过，但我正在考虑要不要问问我最好的朋友（她是个女生），看看她能不能指出一些我的言行举止中存在的“危险信号”，比如我焦虑、自卑，或者我话太多（因为我最近因为自卑和强迫症被朋友“发好人卡”了
english_item: Is it weird to ask a friend to point out flaws I have in order to try and fix em? I haven't asked them but I'm debating on asking my best friend who's a girl if she can point out red flags or things I say or do that show that I am anxious or low self esteem or if I talk to much as I often do because just got friendzoned for my low self esteem and OCD

chinese_item: 我得赶紧搬走了……大概三个月前，我在上看到一个男同性恋找女室友的广告。我觉得这主意不错，价格也低得难以置信。一开始一切都挺好的，但后来我开始注意到一些奇怪的事情。简单来说，前几天晚上我睡着的时候，他突然抓住了我，我当时吓坏了。我假装没醒，因为我不知道该怎么办。第二天晚上他比我先睡着，我翻看他的手机，发现里面有很多我裸睡的照片，还有他趁我睡着时抚摸我的照片。我不知道该怎么办，但我必须马上离开这里。我感觉糟透了。我不知道该不该去住酒店，因为我真的负担不起。
english_item: Facebook Marketplace ,Gotta move now… So I responded to an ad on facebook marketplace about 3 months ago for a gay man looking for a female roommate. I thought this seemed like a great idea and the price seemed too good to be true. Things seemed fine at first but then I started noticing some weird stuff. Long story short I woke up to him grabbing me while I was sleeping the other night and I was extremely freaked out. I pretended not to wake up because I didn't know what to do. Then the next night he fell asleep before me and I went through his phone and found tons of pictures of me sleeping naked and him touching me while I was asleep. Not sure what I'm going to do but I definitely need to get out of here like now. I feel pretty awful about it. Not sure if I should go to a hotel because I really can't afford it.

chinese_item: 当年纪大的人自称青少年时……  这话可能有点刺耳，但我不得不说，因为我经常看到这种情况！我今年29岁，女，我极其反感同身受或年纪更大的人自称小孩或青少年。比如，一个30多岁的女人发帖说自己怀孕了，然后说“我33岁了，真不敢相信自己居然怀孕了！” 布列塔尼，你都33岁了，别再把人当小孩了！这太恶心太奇怪了。  再举个例子……一个28岁的人和34岁的人谈恋爱。然后人们就大惊小怪，因为那个28岁的人“像个孩子”？哈？说真的，这种观念什么时候开始出现了？为什么我们要把成年人当成真正的孩子？这难道不冒犯人吗？我真的搞不懂。如果有人这么跟我说，我会很生气，因为我花了整个二十多岁努力提升自己，可不是为了被人叫做“孩子”。  更何况，你为什么要把自己跟青少年/孩子比较呢？你是说你像青少年一样做蠢事吗？你是说你不成熟吗？你是说你一团糟吗？你肯定很自豪吧！  抱歉，我忍不住要吐槽一下。顺便说一句，我主要是感同身受，因为我的青少年时期和二十多岁简直糟透了哈哈——看到女性自然衰老真的让我很兴奋，因为这是一种特权，所以我不明白为什么我们不能坦然接受这一点
english_item: 。,"When older people call themselves teenagers…. This might ruffle some feathers but it has to be said because I see this all the time! I’m 29F, and I absolutely DESPISE when someone who’s my age or older calls themselves a kid or a teenager. For example, a woman who’s in her 30s will post about her pregnancy and say “I’m 33 and I can’t believe I’m having a teen pregnancy!” No Brittany, you’re grown. ENOUGH with the infantilization! It’s creepy and weird .  Or another example… When a 28year old is dating a 34 year old. And people get their panties up in a bunch because that 28 year old “is a child”? Huh? Honestly, when did this happen? Why are we perceiving grown adults as literal children? And is it not offensive? I’m genuinely confused. If someone said that to me, I would feel some type of way because I did not spend my entire twenties working on myself only to be called “a child.”  Not to mention, WHY would you even want to compare yourself to a teenager/child? Are you saying you make dumb decisions like teenagers do? Are you saying you’re immature? Are you saying you don’t have your sh*t together? You must be so proud!  Sorry, I had to let that out. FYI, I’m mainly projecting because my teens and twenties were absolutely horrid lol - and seeing women age genuinely excites me because it’s such a privilege, so I’m not sure why we can’t just accept that.

chinese_item: 在一段新的恋情中，你是如何控制多巴胺激增的？  当我开始和新对象约会时，我感觉自己快要疯了。思绪飞转，疲惫不堪等等。有人也有这种感觉吗？你们是如何平衡的
english_item: How do you manage your dopamine spikes when in a new relationship? When I start seeing someone new, I feel like I start to go crazy. Thoughts racing a million miles a minute, crazy levels of exhaustion, etc.  does anyone else get this? How do you balance it?

chinese_item: 谁是糟糕的室友？我现在真的很生气，想知道我的生气是否合理……请实话实说。我的室友在异地恋。她的男朋友住在离我们十二个小时车程的地方。我们在这个公寓住了大约八个月，他来过五次（？），每次至少待一周。最长的一次待了两周，那是三周前的事了。那次之后我很生气，因为她之前说他只会在我们放假的那一周待着，结果我回来的时候他还在，然后又在我们家待了一整周。我给她提了个醒，因为我们只住一个月了，我没想到他还会来，就像第一张图里说的那样。我没想到还会再见到他，因为我们一起住的时间不多了，而且她上周末也去看了他。没想到，她竟然又问他能不能再住。这次要住九天，这还是挺让人恼火的，但我只能说他得付我们热水用的丁烷气费。今天，她回复了另一个室友的问题，说他会比她之前要求的多住四天。我觉得这一切都太烦人了，我们的公寓很小，而且我们马上就要搬走了。她今年夏天会和他一起住几个月，然后搬到离他近的小镇。我生气难道错了吗？
english_item: who's the bad roommate I am really angry right now and need to know if my anger is justified…. be so honest. My roommate is in a ldr. Her boyfriend lives twelve hours away via transit. We have lived in this apartment for about eight months and he has visited five(?) times, each for at least a week. The longest visit was two weeks. That visit was three weeks ago. After that visit i was frustrated because she told us he would be staying for just our one week break, but he was still there when i returned, and then remained at our place for a whole extra week. I told her, as general advice, as it's our last month living together and i didn't expect him to visit again, the message in image one. I didn't expect to see him again as, again, we have so little time left living together and also she visited him last weekend. To my surprise, she asked if he could stay again. This time for nine days, which is annoying still, but i just said he had to pay for the butane that heats our water. Now, today, she answered other roommate's question saying he'd be here four days longer than she originally asked. I find this all so annoying, we have a small apartment, and we are so close to moving out. She will be living with him this summer for several months then moving to a town close to him. Am I wrong to be pissed?

chinese_item: 有人跟我一样，做完梦醒来后会感到孤独吗？感觉有点奇怪，我以前好像从来没这种感觉。我睡了很久，做了好多互不相关的梦，比如梦到遇到瑞恩·高斯林之类的，还有一些其他的搞笑梦。但是完全醒来之后，我就开始觉得很孤独（虽然我和室友住在一起，身体上并不孤单）。那种孤独感涌上心头，让我很想哭。我以前也有过这种感觉，但是从来没在清晨醒来的时候有过。有人跟我一样吗？
english_item: DAE feel lonely after waking up from a dream? It's kind of odd, I don't think I've ever felt this way before, I was sleeping for a long time so I was having a bunch of dreams unrelated to each other like meeting Ryan Gosling and other funny stuff like that, but once I completely woke up, I began feeling alone(even though I'm physically not since I have a roommate) , I felt an urge to cry at the lonely feeling in my chest, I've felt this feeling before but not first thing in the morning, anyone else?

chinese_item: 我好像喜欢上了一个年纪比较大的男生。这样行不行？  背景：我16岁，女生，十月份就17岁了。最近我好像有点喜欢上了一个认识的男生……但他21岁了??  我之前经历了一段非常痛苦的暗恋期，反反复复6个月，毫无进展。那个男生一直跟我说他不知道（不知道什么？不知道自己的感受，不知道该怎么做，不知道该怎么做等等），我最后实在太累了，就放弃了。大概放弃两周后，我和几个平时不太说话的朋友一起玩，结果有个男生坐在我们旁边，让我感觉有点紧张。  为了保护隐私，我叫他杰拉德——杰拉德是我经常见到的人，我们聊过几次，他通常和我的朋友们混在一起。那天我们聊天，我讲了很多笑话，他每个都笑，我觉得特别可爱。他还问我在玩什么游戏，而且好像对我的话题都很感兴趣。  说实话，我也不知道怎么会这样，这完全是个意外。我本来没打算喜欢上任何人（更别说喜欢一个年纪比我大的男生了！），但是……我觉得他真的好可爱，而且很有品味……还有，别人打断我说话的时候，他还会问我接下来要说什么。  我讲的笑话他都笑。  他还会唱歌。  我完了吗？我还有机会吗？这样是不是很糟糕？各位帮帮我，我真的不知道该怎么办了，我从来没对任何人这么失望过
english_item: i think i like an older guy. am i cooked? for context: i am 16F, turning 17 in october. and recently i think i sorta formed a lil crush on a dude i know……. but he’s 21 ??  so i just got out of a really rough crushing stage, 6 months back and forth, no progress, dude kept telling me he didn’t know (about what? his feelings, what to do, how to act, etc.) and i just got tired and gave up. and then about 2 weeks after i gave up, i was just hanging with some friends who i don’t rly talk to, and then this one guy in particular who sat with us lowkey made me kinda tense  for privacy, i’m gonna call him gerald — so gerald is someone i always see around, talked a few times, he usually hangs around my cohorts. and so that day we were just talking, i made a lot of jokes, and he laughed at EVERY one of them which i thought was rly cute. and he even asked me what game i was playing, and be seemed interested in whatever i was saying.   honestly i have no idea how this happened it literally is an accident i had no plans on liking anybody (and definitely not an older dude!) but i mean ?? i think he’s rly cute and he has a good sense of style.. and he also asks me what i was gonna say when someone cuts me off  laughs at all the jokes i say he can sing too   am i cooked. do i even have a chance. is this bad. guys help me please i’m at a loss i have never felt this down bad about anyone ever

chinese_item: 人际关系  ……真是太复杂了。我不得不和自己真正喜欢的人保持距离，以免破坏彼此的关系，或者伤害到别人。最让我难过的是：错失良缘
english_item: Human relationships …are so complicated. I have to take distance from people whom I really like, to not jeopardise my relationship or risk hurting someone. This is something that devastates me: the missed connections.

chinese_item: 我想搬到乡下去。  我刚结束了一趟漫长的欧洲之旅，如果说我有什么发现的话，那就是城市生活让我精疲力竭，甚至出现了生理症状。我被遇到的形形色色的人搞得不堪重负，我能从他们的脸上、肢体语言中读懂他们所有的情绪；交通的噪音、汽油味，还有火车上那些衣衫褴褛、不修边幅的人群……这一切都让我难以承受，没有一丝一毫让我感到愉悦。  然而，我现在却回到了家乡，住在一个大城市的郊区，我的所有工作都需要我通勤到市中心。这不适合我。  但我不知道该去哪里，也不知道到了那里该做什么。我能在小镇上找到工作吗？我应该搬到哪个小镇呢？我主要是一名音乐人，找演出机会已经够难了。  或许我应该搬到离城市更远的地方，但仍然在通勤范围内，然后买辆车？但就目前的情况来看，这超出了我的预算。或许拥有一辆车，避免乘坐公共交通工具，会更方便些。  我不知道。但我知道这种城市环境不适合我，我想离开，但我不知道自己有哪些选择
english_item: I want to move to the countryside I just went on a long trip around Europe, and if there's one thing I've discovered, it's that cities exhaust me to the point of giving me physical symptoms. I become overwhelmed by all the people I meet, I can read all their feelings on their faces, their body language; the noise of the traffic, the smell of gas and of the unwashed masses on the trains... it is all too much, there is nothing about it that I find pleasant.  And yet here I am back home, and I live on the outskirts of a major city, and all of my business requires me to commute to the city center. This is not for me.  But I don't know where I'd go, and what I would do there. Would I be able to find work in a small town? Which town should I move to? I am primarily a musician, and finding gigs is already difficult enough.   Perhaps I should further out of the city but still within range, and buy a car? But as it stands, that's out of my budget. Perhaps just owning a car and avoiding public transit would make things easier.   I don't know. But I know this urban environment is not for me, and I want out, but I don't know what my options are.

chinese_item: 意识到我失去了朋友  这些年来，我的社交焦虑时好时坏，但我一直不擅长交朋友和维持友谊。我总是忍不住想太多，感觉主动和别人联系会打扰到他们——尤其现在我们都成年了（我已经26岁了），时间如此有限。直到今天——我的生日——我才意识到自己在这方面有多么孤独。我很幸运地收到了家人的祝福，对此我非常感激。然而，朋友们却没有送上任何祝福。即使是我仅剩的几个朋友，他们也不知道/不记得我的生日——我并不是说他们不知道不好，只是觉得我们之间缺乏亲密感和熟悉感（尤其是我现在很少见到他们）。我也不知道他们的生日。这让我几乎怀念起高中时代，那时我有一群经常见面、一起庆祝生日的好朋友。以前在学校每天都能见到彼此，感觉轻松多了，即使焦虑或不善交际，社交也变得容易得多。我怀念那种归属感，怀念那些关心我的家人以外的人。如今这一切似乎都遥不可及，也让我感到非常孤独
english_item: Realizing I lost my friends My social anxiety has gotten better and worse over the years, but I've been consistently bad at making and keeping friends. I can't help but overthink things and feel like I'm bothering people when I reach out- especially since we're all adults now (I'm 26) and time is so limited. I didn't realize how lonely I had become in that regard until today- my birthday. I am very fortunate to recieve lots of well wishes from family members which I'm thankful for. However, there are none from friends. Even the few friends I do have remaining don't know/remember that it's my birthday- not to say they're bad for not knowing, but I guess it just shows a lack of closeness or that familiarity (especially since I don't see them much). I don't really know their birthdays either. It almost makes me miss high school, when I had a friend group that I saw all the time and celebrated birthdays and things with. It felt so much easier, seeing each other every day at school and it being so much easier to be social even if you're anxious or awkward. I miss being a part of something, having people that care for me that aren't in my family. It seems impossible to achieve nowadays, and I just feel very alone because of it.

chinese_item: 走到哪儿都觉得别人挺可怜的。  比如我去餐厅或酒吧，看到清洁工们辛苦地工作，看起来很疲惫，尤其是年纪比较大的清洁工——我就觉得很愧疚，因为我坐在那里享受美食，而他们却要伺候我、收拾残局，他们可能只想赶紧回家??  有人也有这种感觉吗
english_item: Feel sorry for people everywhere I go For example when I go to a restaurant or a bar and I see the cleaners working hard, looking exhausted, especially if they’re a little older - I just feel so bad and guilty that I’m sitting there enjoying my meal or whatever and someone has to wait on me and clean up after people when they probably just want to go home ??  Does anyone else get this?

chinese_item: 我急需一些真诚的建议。  我以前有个同学，虽然现在已经毕业了，但我们之前因为一个朋友的误会，已经敌对了一年半。后来我们成了朋友，但我总觉得他有点不对劲，我能感觉到。那个误会源于一个谣言，说我和那个朋友谈恋爱。一年半后他才告诉我这个谣言，当时他说他最近才知道是假的，但我知道他大概一年前就知道了。他给了我很多暗示，但当我试图澄清的时候，他却变得很冷淡。我并没有说我喜欢他，或者喜欢他什么，但还是有这种感觉。我不知道该怎么办
english_item: 。,"I need a honest advise ASAP So there's a guy from school now our school is over thou, for like 1.5 year we have been enemies, because of a friend created misunderstanding. Than we became friends but I knew it that he and something. I can sesnse it. The misunderstanding was a rumour which says that I was in relationship with that friend, he told me about this rumour after 1.5 year that time he said he just recently knew that it is fake, but I knew he knew from around 1 year ago. So gave me so many hints when I tried to clear things he became emotionally distant, I didn't said that I like you or like that but still. I don't know what should I do

chinese_item: 对剧集产生强烈的情感依恋？  好吧，我一直在思考这个问题，这让我觉得自己和身边的人格格不入，所以我想问问有没有其他同样敏感的人能感同身受。  我发现自己基本上已经不再追剧了，因为我对剧集产生了非常强烈、难以抑制的依恋，甚至希望自己能生活在剧中那样的生活中。我完全是个爱做白日梦的人，以至于只要想到自己多么希望能够继续追剧，就会感到生理上的焦虑。  真正让我下定决心追剧的是《古战场传奇》（Outlander）。我很想看，但我知道那种“兴奋感”肯定不会像以前那样强烈，我也不会像以前那样投入到剧情中，以至于感动落泪、被剧情深深触动。我几乎会沉迷于剧中的一切，幻想自己能够回到过去，然后开始厌恶现在的生活，诸如此类。  难道只有我这样吗？它让我感觉自己既疯狂又执着，同时也让我感到无比温暖和幸福。哭泣也释放了我很多情感上的负担，这也很棒。  不知道，有没有人能感同身受
english_item: Deep emotional attachment to shows? Okay, so I was thinking about this and it’s one of those things that makes me feel SO abnormal from the people in my life so I wondered if any of my fellow sensitives could relate.   I noticed that I have basically stopped binge watching shows and it’s because I get these STRONG, INTENSE attachments to them and wish I could just live the life of the show. I’m a total daydreamer, so much so that it will make me physically anxious if I think about how I wish I could watch it.   The show that sealed this deal for me is: Outlander. I have the hankering to watch it and I know the “high” of watching it isn’t gonna be the same and I’m not going to be so invested in it that it makes me cry and hits me in the feels. I would obsessively think about it almost? Wishing I could live back in the day and started hating the way life was now, the works.   Am I the only one like this or?? It made me feel so crazy and obsessive and also made me feel all of the warm and fuzzies. It also released a lot of emotional weight with the crying so that was nice too.   Idk, please someone relate ??

chinese_item: 当有人在描述共同经历时，总是坚持用“我”和“我的”而不是“我们”和“我们”。  我不知道为什么这会让我如此恼火，我想是因为我以前有个非常自我中心的伴侣，他经常这样，所以我开始特别注意！我最近在看一部关于真实犯罪的纪录片，里面有一对父母，他们的儿子被谋杀了，花了十年时间才将凶手绳之以法并找到孩子的遗体。父亲的措辞非常包容，比如“我们永远不会放弃”、“这对我们来说是毁灭性的打击”、“他是我们的孩子”等等。但母亲却一直用“我”和“我的”。“我当时悲痛欲绝”、“他们把我的儿子找回来了”。我不知道，也许这个例子不太恰当，但我总觉得有些人好像意识不到别人也和他们处境相同……还有一种情况是，兄弟姐妹之间谈论父母时，他们会说“我爸爸”或“我妈妈”，而不是直接说“爸爸”或“妈妈”。我觉得这种用词方式能反映出这个人很多东西，也体现出他们是否具备（或缺乏）为他人着想的能力
english_item: ??,"When someone who is describing a shared experience insists on saying 'I' and 'me' instead of 'us' and 'we'. I don't know why this irritates me so much, I think it's because I used to have a very self involved partner who did this a lot and I really started to notice it! I was just watching a true crime doc and there was a mum and dad on there who's son had been murdered and it had taken 10 years to bring the killer to justice and find his body. The dad was very inclusive with his language, 'we were never going to give up', 'for us it was devastating', 'he was our boy' etc. But the mum was constantly saying 'I' and 'my'. 'I was devasted', 'they brought my son back to me'. I don't know, maybe that's not a great example, but it just strikes me that some people don't seem to register that someone else is also in the same situation with them... Another one is when a sibling is discussing a parent, with or alongside other siblings, and they'll say 'my dad' or 'my mum', instead of just dad or mum. I just feel like that kind of language says a lot about the person and their capacity (or lack of) to think beyond themselves ??

chinese_item: 去医院之前我需要一些精神上的鼓励。  明天早上我要带儿子去医院做个门诊检查，之后他要做手术。那是一家公立医院（免费），我住在一个贫困现象严重的国家，这意味着即使预约了，也得排队几个小时才能看上病，而且医院工作人员通常都很疲惫，心情也很差。我儿子做过很多次手术，去过这家医院很多次，每次都是糟糕的经历，尤其是我还有社交焦虑症。我被吼过，被贬低过，还被说我傻，因为带错了表格等等。我生孩子的时候也是在那里，有个护士跑到我的病房里让我闭嘴，因为我的呻吟声吵到了护士站的护士。上次我去医院的时候，另一个病人想跟我吵架，因为我前妻没跟我商量就把儿子的一个玩具给了她孩子。我看到玩具掉在地上就捡了起来，结果那个陌生女人冲我大吼大叫，我差点崩溃。总之，我在这家医院有过几次不愉快的社交经历，我真的很讨厌这样，但又不得不这样做。现在只有我和他两个人生活了一年左右，这是我第一次独自一人来医院。想到明天的事，我现在焦虑得要命，真的需要一些安慰，也希望有人能给我一些缓解这种焦虑的小技巧
english_item: I need a moral boost before a hospital visit So tomorrow morning I need to take my son for to the hospital for a clinic checkup before he has a surgery there. It's a public hospital (free) and I live in a country where poverty is rampant, meaning there will be hundreds of people waiting in line for hours and hours to be seen, even if you have an appointment, and the overworked hospital staff are usually in a bad mood. I've had to go to this hospital many times for my sons surgeries and its always a terrible experience, especially when you have social anxiety. I've been yelled at, belittled, told I'm stupid for bringing the wrong forms etc. I also have birth there, and a nurse came into my room to tell me to shut up because my cries of pain where irritating the ladies at the nursing station. Last time I was there another patient tried to start a fight with me because my ex gave one of my son's toys to her child without telling me, so I took it when I saw it lying on the floor and nearly had a meltdown when this random woman started yelling at me. All this to say, I've had a few bad social experiences at this hospital and I absolutely hate doing this but it's something I have to do. Its been just me and him for about a year now and this is the first time I'll be doing this alone. I am an anxious wreck atm thinking about tomorrow, and I really just need some validation and maybe some tips for curbing anxiety in situations like this.

chinese_item: 有没有人跟我一样不想社交？比如，一天结束的时候，或者更糟的是周末，我老婆总是想安排活动，见见朋友什么的。我完全相反，我只想待在家里，放松放松，遛遛狗，最好谁也不想见，谁也不想说话。这有时会引发我们夫妻间的争吵，但大多数时候我真的没精力去应付这些。不过我以前可不是这样的，尤其是在年轻的时候。这种情况正常吗？我应该找人聊聊吗？
english_item: DAE just not want to socialize? Like, at the end of the day, or worse on the weekends, my wife always wants to make plans, see her friends, etc. I am the total opposite, I want to stay home, veg, play with the dogs, and preferably not see or talk to anyone.  It sometimes causes disagreements in our marriage, but I really just don't have the mental capacity to do it most the time, but it's not always been like this for me, especially not in my younger days. Is this normal? Should I talk with someone?

chinese_item: 有没有人跟我一样，哪怕只是稍微迟到，也会翘课，就为了避免走进教室时被所有人盯着看？  我高中时就因为这种事没少挨骂 :( 感觉走进教室时，所有人都会注意到我，这真的太难受了
english_item: did anyone else skip any class they were even slightly late to, so they didnt have to be looked at by everyone as they walked in? i got in trouble so much as a high schooler for stuff like this :( it felt genuinely physically impossible to walk into a room knowing everyone would notice me

chinese_item: 一个糟糕的室友如何变成两个噩梦般的室友
english_item: How one bad roomie turned into two nightmare roomies

chinese_item: 还有其他人有这种感觉吗？总是幻想被紧紧拥抱、被热烈亲吻，这种白日梦挥之不去，我受不了。还有其他人有这种感觉吗？
english_item: Does anybody else have this situation like fantasizing about being held tight, kissed hard this imagination daydreams won't leave me alone does anybody else

chinese_item: 难以承受失去朋友的痛苦  我是一个非常敏感且富有同理心的人。我的朋友最近自杀了，我难以接受这个事实。我几乎无法忍受任何人的痛苦，更何况是痛苦到想要结束生命。我的朋友也曾是高中生，多年来一直饱受心理健康问题的困扰。我们彼此分享了很多，我觉得我们非常了解对方。我害怕我再也找不到像她一样的朋友了。自从当了妈妈之后，我就一直没能结交新朋友。我只是想在这里寻求一些鼓励，帮助我走出悲伤。我也是一名基督徒，但我担心她不是。这让我更加难过。如果有人能给我一些鼓励的话语来帮助我走出悲伤，我将不胜感激。我没有任何自杀倾向，我只是想在一个有类似经历的人的陪伴下寻求慰藉
english_item: Struggling to cope with the death of a friend I am highly sensitive and an empath. My friend recently took her own life and I am struggling to cope with the loss. I can't hardly handle the thought of anyone suffering, let alone so badly that they wanted to die. My friend was also HS and struggled for many years with her mental health. We both shared so much together and I felt we understood one another so well. I fear I will never find another friend like her. Since becoming a mom, I haven't been able to make new friends at all. I just am here to find encouragement while I'm working through my grief. I am also a Christian and I fear that she was not. So that is haunting me as well. If anyone has any words of encouragement to help me through my grief, I would appreciate that. I'm not at all suicidal or anything of that nature, I just am looking for solice in a place where others think like I do.

chinese_item: 有没有人和我一样，会把某些人看作“NPC”（非玩家角色）？我也不知道自己为什么会这样，但现在完全是下意识的。我似乎会根据人的面部特征给他们贴上标签。基本上，我会根据以前遇到的、长相相似的人来给他们贴标签。这和性别或种族无关，面部的结构和视觉特征似乎才是最重要的因素。不管怎样，最终的结果是，对于某些人，我甚至无法理解他们的眼神背后是一个有血有肉、有故事、有激情、有欲望的生命。他们对我来说就像是周围世界的“填充物”。我相信有些人也会下意识地对每个陌生人这样做，但我似乎会非常明确地根据外貌来判断谁是“NPC”，谁不是。有人也有这种困扰吗？
english_item: Does anybody else see certain people as NPCs? I don't really know why I do it, but it's entirely subconscious at this point. I've seemed to have created categories of people based on characteristics of thier faces. Basically I stereotype them based off of similar looking people that I've met and interacted with before. It has nothing to do with gender or ethnicity, rather entirely the structural and visual makeup of the face seems to be the most important factors. However it works, the end result is that for some people I can't even seem to comprehend that there is a living being with a history, passions and desires behind those eyes. They just seem to be filler for the world around me. Im sure some people subconsciously do this with every stranger, but my mind seems to be very specific as to who is and isnt based solely off of looks. Anyone else have this problem?

chinese_item: 我需要帮助！  我们交往八个月了。在交往之前，他就是我的挚友。我比他小四岁。嗯，问题是，有时候我觉得他比我成熟一些。我有时自己心情不好，他却每时每刻都想要我的安慰。（每次他需要安慰的时候，我都会安慰他……我不是一个轻易敞开心扉的人，我很难表达自己的情绪。甚至我的父母都不知道我到底怎么了。）但有时候我真的太累了。我现在正在学习。所以有时候我太累了（我每天学习超过八个小时），没力气去安慰他。我是不是个危险信号？我该怎么办？因为他太敏感了。如果我们吵架，或者我开玩笑，他都会哭。而且他把所有的争吵都归咎于我。他肯定是个好人，但他的敏感有时候真的让我有点生气。 （我脾气很暴躁
english_item: I need helppp So we've been dating for 8 months now. He was my bestfriend before we started dating. I'm 4 years younger than him. Uhm, the thing is sometimes I feel he's a little less mature than me. I sometimes am going through my own shit and he'd be wanting reassurances every second. (I've even reassured him everytime he needed it....I'm a person that doesn't open up very fast. I have trouble conveying my emotions. Even my parents don't know what's going on with me) But sometimes I feel too tired. Im studying right now. So sometimes I'm too tired (i study 8+ hours a day) to reassure him. Am i a red flag? And what should I do about this. Coz He's over sensitive. He cries if we fight or if I even tell him something as a joke. And he blames all the fights over me. Hes a green flag surely but this sensitive thing gets me a little angry sometime. (I'M HOT TEMPERED)

chinese_item: 室友居然因为我把食物放进公用冰柜就“举报”我了…………还是在我生日那天。今天早上我收到办公室的邮件，问我能不能“把食物放到我自己的小冰箱里”，他们说有个室友去投诉了。没错，我有个小冰箱，不是小冰柜。我的小冰箱里连冷冻室都没有。那些食物需要冷冻。我可不想把肉放在小冰箱里让它烂掉。我不知道，她是不是想让我饿死似的。她还一整天都在给我发信息，骂我，还说我幼稚的话。我基本上一整天都没理她。而且，没错，就是这个室友，之前把一整条鱼（连眼睛都露出来了）就那么放在冰柜里。没装袋子，就这么放着。我指出来之后，她的回复是：“那是食物啊。”拜托，我的食物也是食物啊！然后她试图告诉我她对食物过敏，因为这东西会让她心悸。我自己也有严重的食物过敏（顺便说一句，她根本不在乎），所以我非常重视这件事，用好几个袋子把食物包好，放在门最下面的架子上，确保它不会接触到其他任何东西。
english_item: Roommate "reported" me to the office for putting food in the shared freezer… …ON my birthday. I got an email this morning from the office asking if I could "kindly move my food into my own mini fridge" and they mentioned that a roommate went in and complained. Yes, I do have a mini fridge, NOT a mini freezer. There is not even a freezer section in my mini fridge. The food in question needs to be frozen. I'm not about to keep meat in my mini fridge to let it rot. I don't know it's like she wants me to starve or something. She also continued to message me about it all day, calling me names and such (childish, imo). I basically just ignored her messages all day. And YES this is the same roommate who left a whole fish (eyes and EVERYTHING) just chilling out in the freezer. No bag, just out. Her response to me pointing that out? "That's food, though". GIRL. SO IS MINE. Then she tried to tell me she was allergic because it gives her heart palpitations. As someone with severe food allergies myself (which she ignores, btw), I took that very seriously and wrapped it in multiple bags, put it on the lowest shelf of the door, and made sure it wasn't touching anything else.

chinese_item: “大度一点，别惹事”  我讨厌这种文化，好像总是那些敢于发声的人反而成了问题，而不是那些一开始就不尊重别人的人。为什么总是受害者要表现得宽容大度？  或许一开始我确实表现得宽容大度了。或许我确实尝试过和平、建设性地与对方沟通。或许我确实尝试过多次讲道理，直到最终在对方的压力下崩溃。  我想说的是，在你劝别人“放下”或者“大度一点”之前，或许应该先多了解一下情况，而不是总是妄下断言
english_item: 。,"""Just Be the Bigger Person to Not Cause Drama I hate this culture that the person who is the one that speaks up is the one who is considered problematic instead of the person doing the disrespect in the first place. Why is the pressure always on the wronged party to be the bigger person?   Or maybe it's that I was the bigger person at first. Maybe I did try and talk to the other person peacefully and constructively. Maybe I did try and reason with them several times until I eventually cracked under their pressure.   Just saying, before you tell someone to just let it go and/or be the bigger person, maybe ask more about the situation before always jumping to that.

chinese_item: 如何面对注定孤独终老的现实？  我发现自己拥有归属感或融入社群的可能性为零。我的背景、爱好、观点等等都与众不同。我不是信教的人，也不是乡下人，与我的同胞和家乡那些说着相同母语的人相比，我简直是个异类。  无论我加入哪个班级或社交团体，我始终都是个局外人。我与家人疏远，也没有朋友。  明知自己身边的人根本不存在，或者根本无法接近，这种感觉越来越难熬。虽然我很想追求个人成功和成就，但我不知道这一切是否值得
english_item: How to handle the fact that you're gonna be alone forever? I found that the chances that i gonna have a belonging feeling or a community are zero. I have a unique background in everything and unique hobbies, views, etc. I'm ain't no religious guy, ain't a country guy and I'm an extreme anomaly compare to the people of my country and my place whom i share the same first language.  Every class/Social group that I've ever been in i was always an outsider, i parted aways from my family and don't have friends.   It's getting hard keep going knowing your people are just not exist or just unapproachable, and the self success and self achievements are somethings that i interesting at, but don't know if it's worth it.

chinese_item: 为什么我总是不自觉地相信别人而不是自己，即使我知道自己是对的
english_item: Why do I automatically believe other people over myself, even when I know I’m right?

chinese_item: 紧急求助。我是一名22岁的大学生，住在公寓里，有自己的车，从19岁起就基本独立生活。我和男朋友交往一年半了，他之前一直和奶奶、父母以及其他亲戚住在一起。他以前没上大学，但我鼓励他去读，现在他也是一名学生了。他也22岁。总而言之，我觉得他因为一直和家人住在一起，在实际生活中不够成熟和负责。话虽如此，我想让他交房租，我自己也能负担得起，但我又觉得照顾一个成年男人不太合适。我为了维持生计和支付房租而焦头烂额，而他却在担心下次买车升级。他似乎已经习惯了我会按时交房租。我一边疯狂地帮他收拾烂摊子，一边还不停地提醒他我不是他妈也不是他奶奶，我不会替任何人收拾烂摊子。我该怎么办？！
english_item: EMERGENCY ADVICE. I'm a college student 22 yrs old, living in an apartment, have my own car, pretty much independent since I was 19 years old. I have been with my boyfriend for about a year and a half now and he was living with his grandma, parent, and so much family. He wasn't in college but I pushed him to go now he's a student as well. He's 22 as well. Long story short I feel like he isn't as mature or responsible when it comes to actual life since he's been living free all of his (with his family). With that being said am I wrong for wanting to charge him rent because I can do it alone but I don't feel right taking care of a grown man at all. I'm stressing over bills and rent to have a roof over our head while he's worried about his next purchase to upgrade his car. He has became comfortable with knowing that im going to pay my rent regardless. I also clean up after him like crazy while constantly reminding him that I'm not his mom or grandma and won't be cleaning up after anybody. What should I do?!

chinese_item: 因伤害所爱之人而感到内疚  我认为，我们这些高敏感人群（HSP）难以做到宽恕，同时又难以忽视自身需求的主要原因之一，是看到所爱之人痛苦不堪，而看到他们因我们而快乐，又让我们感到无比欣慰。  我正在结束一段长达五年的感情，但每次试图触及这个话题，看到对方的痛苦时，我都觉得这是不可能完成的任务。我会感到极度内疚和悲伤，因为她原本计划我们一起生活，或者一起去旅行等等……这种感觉让我动弹不得，最终只能选择放弃。  我厌倦了这种感觉，因为每次我选择不伤害她，都感觉像是在伤害自己，但我又无法承受伤害她的痛苦！  从旁观者的角度来看，或者理性地思考，这似乎是一个很常见也很简单的问题，但实际上，我就是做不到。  有人遇到过类似的情况吗
english_item: Guilty by causing pain on loved one One of the main reasons I believe is so hard for us hsps to be so forgiving and at the same time neglect our own needs is that it’s incredibly painful to see the ones we love in pain and on the other hand so rewarding to see them happy because of us.  I’m in the process of breaking up my 5-year long relationship but every time I try to touch on the subject and see how much in pain the other person is I feel it’s an impossible job. I start feeling extremely guilty and sad that she had plans for us to live in together, or to go on that next trip etc… that it just paralises me, and I end up ceasing.   I’m tired of that because every time I choose not to hurt her, it feels like I’m hurting myself, yet I can’t possibly cope with hurting her!  It’s such a common and simple situation when thinking as an outsider or rationally, but in reality, I just can’t get around to doing it.   Has anyone experienced something similar?

chinese_item: 社交焦虑让我无法结婚。父母不理解。  有没有人成功应对过父母对婚姻和孩子的期望？  我从小就有社交焦虑，从未想过结婚生子。  在我家乡，婚礼是一场盛大的庆祝活动，至少有100-200人参加（这是最低标准），我根本不可能参加。虽然我平时不太在意，但随着他们长大，我仍然很担心。  我想知道是否有人有过类似的经历，以及你们是如何应对父母的期望的
english_item: Social anxiety makes marriage impossible for me. Parents don't understand Has anyone been able to handle their parents expectations around marriage and kids? I've had social anxiety since childhood and never had any intentions of getting married or having kids. Where I come from, marriage is a huge celebration with at least 100–200 people (that's the minimum), and there's no way I could ever go through with something like that. Though I don't respond much, still j worry as they get older. Just wanted to know if anyone else has dealt with this, and how you handled your parents expectations?

chinese_item: 你们可以裹得严严实实，我们可不能把皮都剥下来……我和一对情侣合租。女朋友是那种永远怕冷的人，即使天气很暖和/很热也一样。如果她不让我们受罪，我还会同情她。她总是坚持开暖气，把整个房子都弄得热气腾腾，就因为她怕冷。她根本不在乎我们热得受不了。她男朋友已经直接跟她说过，如果她冷，就穿暖和点的衣服，裹上毯子。但是当我们让她别把暖气开那么大时，她根本不理会他/我们。外面都80度了，她还开着暖气，我觉得这太让人无法接受了。我的房间特别热，因为我所有的东西都挤在一个大概12平方英尺（约11平方米）的卧室里。所以，外面热，房间里热得像汤一样，我养了一只双层毛的猫，有时候我回家看到它都热得喘不过气来。如果我们关掉暖气或者打开空调，她就会大发雷霆，通常会演变成一场争吵。她怎么能这么自我中心呢？
english_item: You can bundle up, we can't peel our skin off... I live with a couple. The girlfriend is one of those people who is always cold, even when it's warm/hot. I'd feel for her if she didn't make us suffer. She always insists on having the heat on and making the entire house hot because she's cold. She doesn't care that we get overheated. Her bf has straight up told her that if she's cold she can wear warmer clothes and wrap up in a blanket. But she will literally ignore him/us when we tell her to stop cranking the heat up. It'll be 80 outside and the heat will be on which I feel is unacceptable. My room gets especially hot because everything I've ever owned is cramped into a like 12 square foot bedroom. So with the heat being on + it being hot outside, the air in my room is so hot it feels like soup, and I have a double-coated cat who I've come home to panting because of the heat sometimes. If we turn the heat off and/or turn the air on she gets PISSED and it usually turns into a fight. How can one be so egocentric?

chinese_item: 有没有人和我一样，会发出别人都能听到自己却听不见的声音？这是真的吗？感觉自己好像是全世界唯一一个。在社交场合，尤其是我紧张的时候，我好像会发出一种别人能听到的声音，但我自己却完全不知道。听起来像是哼哼声，或者像是哼哼唧唧的笑声。我没办法录下来，因为我根本不知道是怎么发出这种声音的，因为我完全没意识到自己在做什么。这些年来，很多人都提到过这种情况，所以我确信自己不是被谁在“煤气灯效应”里操控了。一个人竟然会对自己发出的声音听不见，这真是太奇怪了。我感觉自己有点疯，而且非常在意自己的感受。没人相信我不知道自己在发出这种声音，我也不知道该怎么停止这种自己都没意识到的事情。这种情况有名字吗？只有我这样吗？我其他听力都正常，已经做过检查了。
english_item: DAE make a noise that everyone else can hear but you can't? Is this a thing? I feel like I'm the only one in the world. When I'm in social situations, and especially when I'm nervous, I apparently make a noise that other people can hear, but I have no idea that I did it. It's like a hum, or a humming laugh, apparently. I can't record myself doing it, because I don't know how I make that noise because I'm not aware I'm doing it. A lot of different people over the years have mentioned it, so I'm confident that I'm not being gaslighted by anyone in particular about this. It's so weird that one can be deaf to a noise they've made themselves. I feel a bit crazy about it, and very self-conscious. No one believes that I don't know I'm making the noise, and I don't know how to stop doing something I'm not aware I'm even doing. Is there a name for this? Is it just me? My hearing otherwise is fine. It's been tested.

chinese_item: 为什么我对他有种莫名的亲近感……但他似乎并没有同样的感觉？  我有个同事，比我大十岁左右。他很内向，话不多，性格也很矜持。但不知为何……我对他有一种说不清道不明的亲近感。  我们只说过几次话，而且都是简短的对话。有时只是打个招呼，或者偶尔聊几句。但每次交流，都感觉我们之间有种说不清道不明的默契。他看我的眼神，我说话时他认真倾听的样子，甚至他走开的姿态，都让我觉得我们之间有种真实的联系。我知道这听起来可能很奇怪，毕竟我们并不算很熟，但我感觉自己好像很了解他。  最近，我稍微敞开心扉地跟他聊了聊，说了我对他的感觉。他很友善，静静地听着，然后说他现在只想和我做朋友。我尊重他的决定，但他说话的方式让我感到……困惑。他是不是那种“也许以后会有更进一步发展”的暗示？还是他只是委婉地划清界限？  问题是，我觉得他并没有像我一样被我吸引。他总是保持距离，情感上很缄默。也许他只是对我没兴趣。但为什么我感觉这种联系如此真实，而他却感觉不到？  你们有没有过这种单方面的、仿佛来自灵魂深处的联系？我只是在投射自己的想法吗？或者，真的有人会有这种感觉，只是太缄默而不敢表露？  请坦诚地告诉我——但也要友善。我只是想弄明白这一切到底意味着什么
english_item: Why do I feel this deep connection with him… but he doesn’t seem to feel the same? There’s this guy I work with who’s about 10 years older than me. He keeps to himself, doesn’t talk much, and is very reserved. But somehow… I feel this deep connection with him that I can’t explain.  We’ve only had a few conversations, short ones. Sometimes just hellos or little exchanges here and there. But every time we interact, it feels like there’s something unspoken between us. The way he looks at me, listens when I speak, or even the way he walks away, it just feels like there’s something real. I know it probably sounds crazy since we don’t even know each other that well, but I feel like I know him.  Recently, I opened up to him a little and told him about him that how I feel. He was kind, listened quietly, and said he wants to be friends, for now. I respect that, but something about the way he said it made me feel… confused. Did he mean it in a “maybe something more one day” kind of way? Or was that just his way of setting boundaries gently?  The thing is, I don’t think he feels the same pull I do. He stays distant, emotionally guarded. Maybe he’s just not interested. But why does this connection feel so real on my side and so nonexistent on his?  Have any of you ever experienced a one-sided connection that felt like it came from the soul? Am I just projecting? Or is it possible that someone can be feeling something but just too guarded to show it?  Please be honest—but kind. I’m just trying to figure out what this all means.

chinese_item: 空调 vs 转租好吧，我之前有个很糟糕的室友，年中搬进来的，是公寓安排的。他宣布要搬走的时候我简直高兴坏了。结果转租季来了：他转租给了这个人，这人第一件事就是把中央空调关了。我在这公寓住了三年，换过好几个室友，从来没见过有人这么做。好吧，我把空调重新打开了，但直到今晚回家还没碰到这个人（我真的不知道他叫什么）。现在我见到他了。他出来后立刻又把空调关了，还跟我争论电费，说他冷，如果空调一直开着他就不交电费。我试图找个折中的办法，但他却一直跟我争论，一再拒绝。补充一下，之前我们一直把空调温度调到70度，看到他关掉空调后，我就把温度调到了72度，这样空调就不会那么耗电了。对他来说温度还是太低了，我建议试试74/75度。他还是不肯。还有很多其他问题，但我已经厌倦了跟他争论，就摔门上床睡觉了。我不知道该怎么办——我以前从来没遇到过这么不肯妥协的人，而且我也不确定办公室能不能帮上忙。他跟他的转租对象之间还有其他问题。说实话，真是乱成一团。
english_item: Air Conditioning vs Sublet Ok, so I had a pretty bad roommate who moved in mid year, and was placed by the apartment complex. I was elated when he announced he was moving. Enter sublet season: He's sublet to this guy whose first action was to TURN OFF THE CENTRAL AC. I have lived in this apartment for 3 years and cycled through a few roommates and have never seen anyone do that. Well, I flipped it back on but had yet to run into this guy (I legit don't know his name) when i got home tonight. I've met him now. He came back out to turn it off immediately and argued with me about costs, said he was cold, and that if it stayed on he wouldn't pay the power bill. I tried to find a compromise. He said instead argued and kept saying no.  For context, we've kept it at 70 prior, and when I saw he turned it off, I bumped it to 72 so it wouldn't run as much. This is still cold for him and I suggested we try 74/75. Still no. There are a host of other issues but I got tired of bickering with him and went to bed slamming my door.  I don't know what to do- I have never had anyone not agree to a compromise on this, and i'm not sure if the office will be any help. He has other issues ongoing with the guy he's subletting from. Honestly it's a mess

chinese_item: 我的治疗师告诉我，我是个高敏感人群（HSP），我感动得哭了，感觉自己被理解了。  我一直都知道自己与众不同，这让我很痛苦。我曾被告知患有自闭症、社交焦虑症和双相情感障碍。但我从未完全认同其中任何一种。  我讨厌那种不被理解的感觉。我无法向任何人恰当地表达我的敏感，也因此感到非常孤独。我开始把自己封闭起来，试图逃避那些我知道自己必须压抑的复杂情绪。我讨厌自己被情绪淹没的感觉，也为自己如此情绪化而感到羞愧。我有朋友，但他们却给我贴上了“内向”的标签，这让我同样感到难堪。我讨厌给自己贴上“敏感”的标签，即使我知道自己确实很敏感。  我知道自己极度共情，而且情绪过于敏感。这些特质，再加上我对触觉和声音的敏感，让我一度认为自己患有自闭症——部分原因是小时候就被这样告知过。  直到29岁那年，我的新治疗师才告诉我，我是个高敏感人群，我当时就崩溃大哭。我终于感到被理解了，这种疗愈是我从小就渴望的
english_item: My therapist taught me I was an HSP, and I cried at how understood I felt. I always knew I was different, and it made me struggle a lot. I was told I had autism, social anxiety disorder, bipolar disorder. But I never fully identified with any of them.   I hated how misunderstood I felt. I couldn’t properly express how sensitive I was to anybody, and I felt very alone because of it. I started to isolate to try and avoid the huge range of emotions that I knew I was gonna have to suppress. I hated how overwhelmed I got, and felt embarrassed at how emotional I could get. I had friends, but I was labeled as introverted, and I felt embarrassed because of that too. I hated labeling myself as “sensitive”, even though I knew I was.  I knew I was hyper-empathetic and overly emotional. That, mixed with my sensitivity to touch and sounds, caused me to think I had autism — partially due to being told it as a kid.   It wasn’t until the age of 29 that my new therapist explained that I was an HSP, and I broke down crying. I finally felt understood, and it was a type of healing that I needed since I was a kid.

chinese_item: 我害怕自己会失去朋友。  （吐槽）我有两个很要好的朋友。我们从七年级开始就是最好的朋友，幼儿园就认识了。我们现在都在同一所大学，过去五年里，我们都有男朋友，而且我们的男朋友彼此也都是朋友。去年，A朋友和她男朋友分手了，她加入了另一个我们不认识的朋友圈。她仍然会和我们一起玩，我们相处得很好，但她和他们相处的时间比和我们多（当然这没什么问题）。后来，她开始和那个圈子里的一个男生约会。最近，B朋友也和她男朋友分手了，现在她加入了A朋友的另一个朋友圈，经常和他们一起玩。我觉得我们以后再也见不到面了。一切都会不一样了，而且我有社交焦虑症，所以我永远也和他们的朋友做不来，我为此感到非常沮丧。我知道这有点自私，所以我才没跟他们说。这并不是因为他们有了新朋友。我意识到一切都回不到从前了，带着这种焦虑我也无法结交新朋友
english_item: fearing i’ll have no friends. (rant) i have 2 close friends. we’ve been best friends since like 7th grade known each other since kindergarten. we all go to the same college now and for 5 years we all had boyfriends and our boyfriends were all friends with each other. last year friend A broke up w her bf and she found another friend group that we don’t know. she still hung out w us we were all good but she hung out w them all more than us (not that that’s a problem) then she started dating a guy in that group. well recently friend B broke up w her bf and now she’s in friends As other friend group and hanging w them a lot. i feel like we’ll never hangout again now. everything’s going to be different and i have social anxiety so ill never be friends w their friends and im getting all depressed over this. it’s kinda selfish which is why i haven’t said anything to them. and its not the fact they have new friends. im just realizing things will never be the same and i cant make new friends w this anxiety

chinese_item: 有没有人跟我一样特别恋物？比如，我会把一些很奇怪的东西留着，就因为我拥有它们很久了，或者我就是舍不得扔。几个月前，我捐了一件我从来没穿过的毛衣，是那种容易沾碎屑的抓绒毛，我一直都不喜欢，但捐出去之后我却很难过。不知道为什么，我竟然有点想念它。我也无法扔掉或更换其他东西，比如旧的3D打印作品，甚至是我的手机壁纸或手机壳。我以为我弄丢了戴了两年多的手镯，结果哭得都吐了。我一点也不喜欢改变。我家院子里一棵树的大树枝掉下来，我都会难过，因为它跟以前不一样了。我不知道，这正常吗？还是我太奇怪了？
english_item: DAE get super attached to things? Like I'll keep the weirdest stuff just because I've had it a long time or I just don't wanna let go of it. chinese_item: 我该怎么办？？嗨。我只是想寻求一些建议。我和一个室友合租了大约三年，自从合租以来，他就一直拒绝支付任何市政税。我独自支付了一年，之后就负担不起了。我们最终收到了全年的账单。我攒钱支付了一部分（总共1000多英镑）。我要求室友每月还我一些钱。他每次还我150英镑，然后就停止了。我多次要求他帮忙支付，但他拒绝了，而且最近只要一提到账单，他就会对我进行言语攻击。我无法搬家，因为我的信用评分不好，也没有人可以一起搬。我目前正在和债务催收公司洽谈，但很明显他们只是想要钱。我们现在公寓的债务已经超过3000英镑，而且还在不断增加。他之前租房时欠了市政税（我之前不知道），所以欠了一笔债。我还发现他拆开过一封信，是因为他拖欠税款超过十年而签发的逮捕令。我再次寻求一些建议。我现在感觉很无助，很没有安全感。谢谢。
english_item: What should I do?? Hey.  So just looking for some advice, ive been living with a housemate for 3 years or so, since living together he has refused to pay any council tax whatsoever  I kept up with payments for a year on my own, then I just couldn't afford it.  We finally got billed the entire years amount. Which I saved and payed of (it was just over 1k) I asked gor my housemate to replay me monthly. I got 150 back before he stopped.  I have asked him multiple times to help pay, but he refuses and recently has been getting very verbally abusive at any mention of bills.  I can't move as I don't have a good credit score or anyone tmti move with.  I'm currently in talks with debt people, but obviously they just want the money.  We have debt over 3k on our flat now and increasing. He has debt (i didn't know about) from a previous tenancy for non payment of council tax.  I also found a letter he had opened which was a warrant for his arrest due to non payment over 10 years. Again just looking for some advice. Feeling rather stuck and unsafe in my current predicament.  Thank you. x

chinese_item: 那些“不喜欢被指挥”的人  当一个不称职的人对你指手画脚时，你会感到恼火，这当然可以理解。  但作为社会的一份子，你总会在某种程度上被告知该做什么，无论是遵守法律、学校规章，甚至是超市的规章制度。  你不可能完全脱离指挥系统而生活。  令人难以置信的是，竟然有那么多人持有这种观点，更令人难以置信的是，有些人竟然把被要求做某事或被期望完成任务误解为被告知该做什么。  我有一个好朋友，我有时能理解他，但当他说他因为受不了被指挥而无法找到工作时，我还是会翻白眼……不，你正值25-35岁的年龄段，你必须服从指示，服从并不代表你受到了轻视。  免责声明：我并不是说永远不要质疑权威或盲目遵守所有规则。但你不能因为别人让你做某事就大发脾气
english_item: 。,"People who don't like being told what to do There's being annoyed when someone not qualified is telling you what to do. Which I understand is a reason to get pissed.  But you as a functioning member of society, are gonna get told what to do in some level, be that following the rules of the law, following the rules of school or even a super market.  You can't literally live your life with no chain of command.   And it's crazy how many people have this stance and crazier still people who misinterpret being asked to do something or being expected to do your required tasks as being told what to do.  I have a close friend where I can be understanding to a point but roll my eyes when he says he can't work a job because he can't stand being told what to do.....no you're in the 25-35 age bracket, you have to follow someone's instructions and it's not a slight to you for having to obey.   Disclaimer: I'm not saying never question authority or blindly follow all rules given to you. But you can't throw a tantrum because you've been told to do something

chinese_item: 我的生理医生告诉我，高敏感人群（HSP）是网上编造出来的，而我坚持认为自己有强迫症（OCD）。  她认为这不是典型的强迫症，只是我的强迫症比较强势，但并不僵化，也没有陷入循环。  她让我继续服用百忧解（Prozac）20毫克，但我很讨厌这种药，它让我变得麻木，总是想着吃糖来获得快感和其他情绪上的刺激。她还给我开了哌甲酯（methylphenidate）。  我做了测试，如果高敏感人群真的存在，那我百分之百确定自己是。她只是说我的创造力其实就是高智商。  我的症状总结起来就是：过度思考、高度警觉/敏感、脑子里总是充斥着各种各样的杂念、对不同的事情过度执着。  你们怎么看
english_item: So my physiologist told me hsp is some made up thing in internet and I stuck with I have OCD. She thinks it's not stereotypical ocd. Just one that is intrusive, not rigid and not in loop.  She told me to continue Ssri Prozac 20mg which I hated, it made me blunt reaching for sugar high and other emotional highs. Also got methylphenidate.   I did the test If hsp exist I am 100% it's me. She just says my creative skills are just high intelligence.   What I suffer from tldr overthinking, hyper vigilance/aware, flood of distracting thoughts, obsessing over different things.   What do u guys think?

chinese_item: 灵性、高敏感人群、自杀、以及适应不良的白日梦者  作为一名高敏感人群，我的灵性觉醒。我更加善良、富有同情心，总是为他人着想。我更加虔诚。但正是这种高敏感特质让我产生了自杀的念头。因为我善待了那些嘲笑我、欺凌我的人。我接受过心理治疗，也服用过抗抑郁药物。但我仍然无法完全融入生活。现实世界到处都是丑陋的人。所以我产生了自杀的念头，祈求上帝带走我纯洁的灵魂。我不属于这个世界。我不断地沉浸在适应不良的白日梦中，幻想自己被所有人喜欢，人们尊重我、欣赏我
english_item: Spirituality and hsp and suicide and maladaptive daydreamer As an hsp , I was spiritually awake. More RandomActsofKindness and compassionate. Who thinks about other. I am more religious. But this hsp nature made me suicidal. Bcz i showed my RandomActsofKindness to those who made fun of mine , bullied me . I went to therapy too, taking depression medicine. But still can't participate wholely in life . Real world is full of nasty people every where. So i became suicidal and asked God to take my pure soul back. I don't belong to this world. I m Constantly doing maladaptive daydreaming seeing myself liked by all . People respect me and appreciate me .

chinese_item: 大家好，在大学宿舍里无视别人的问候正常吗？  我经常在宿舍里见到一个人。差不多一年来，每次路过我都会跟他打招呼。我不是那种咄咄逼人的人——只是简单地打个招呼而已。但每次他要么完全无视我，要么就把我转过头去，好像我根本不存在一样。有时候他会露出一种严肃而疏离的表情，好像想跟我保持距离。  我来自韩国（现在住在南达科他州），还在学习和适应美国文化——所以说实话，我很困惑。我不知道这只是性格问题，还是文化习俗，或者我是不是遇到了某种隐性的种族歧视。  我也有社交焦虑，所以很容易把一些小事想太多。但我从来没有对他无礼过，也没有做过任何不恰当的事情。只是礼貌地打个招呼而已。  我并非想指责任何人——我只是真心想更好地理解这种行为。  有没有人遇到过经常见面的人对你表现出这种冷淡的态度？  这在美国大学宿舍文化中常见吗？  欢迎大家分享想法。我只是想弄明白这一切
english_item: hello guys Is it normal to ignore people’s greetings in college dorms? There’s someone I see regularly in my dorm. For almost a year, I’ve always said “hi” when we pass by. I’m not pushy—just a simple greeting, nothing more. But every single time, he either ignores me completely or turns his head away like I don’t exist. Sometimes he gives this serious, distant look like he wants to keep a wall up.  I’m originally from South Korea,(staying in south dakota now) and I’m still learning and adjusting to American culture—so honestly, I’m confused. I don’t know if this is just a personality thing, a cultural norm, or if I might be dealing with some subtle form of racism.  I also have social anxiety, so I tend to overthink small interactions. But I’ve never been rude to him or done anything inappropriate. Just a polite hello.  I’m not trying to accuse anyone of anything—I genuinely just want to understand this behavior better.   Has anyone experienced this kind of cold reaction from someone you see often?   Is this something common in American dorm culture?  Any thoughts would be appreciated. I’m just trying to make sense of it all.

chinese_item: 室友把脏内裤挂在我毛巾上，还对我态度恶劣。我真的需要帮助，主要是心理上的，因为我不知道该怎么办，而且有点害怕。我浑身发抖，可能是我反应过度了……我的一个室友以前有个习惯，喜欢把穿过的内裤挂在我毛巾的挂钩上。那条毛巾是我洗完澡后用来擦身的。因为好几个月都没再发生过，而且一开始我也没觉得恶心，所以我就没在意。今天，这事又发生了，而且——你看，当时明明还有个空着的挂钩！！！我礼貌地问他毛巾上的东西是不是他的，他说他会去看看。然后他就去睡觉了，把这事忘了。我想去洗澡，但他的东西还在那里。我给他发了短信，截图如下。几分钟后，他开始非常用力地敲浴室的门，显然很生气。他把东西拿走了。我问他为什么要敲得这么响。他把门关上了。这是我刚刚写的最后一条信息。我知道他被质问的时候通常会生气好几个星期……虽然我觉得这跟性无关，但为什么总是我的毛巾？为什么不用免费的钩子？为什么这么咄咄逼人？有人能帮帮我吗??
english_item: Roommate hangs his dirty underwear on my towels and behaves aggressively I really need help, more psychological because I do not know what to do and I am kinda scared. I am shaking, maybe overreacting… One of my roommates used to have a habit of hanging his used underwear on the hook my towel was already hanging on. The towel I clean myself with after showering. I ignored it since it stopped for many months and didn't gross me out in the beginning.  Today, this happened again and mind you - there still was a free hook there!!!  I politely asked him personally if this was his stuff on my towel and he said he would check. He webt to sleep and forgot about it. I wanted to shower but his stuff was still there. I texted him, screenshot below.  Couple mins after he starts knocking on the bathrooms door VERY LOUD, is clearly mad. Takes the stuff. I ask him why he had to knock THIS LOUD. He closed the door on me. The last message I just wrote right now. I know he tends to be mad for weeks when being confronted… I do not think this is something sexual tho, but why always my towel??? Why not the free hook? Why the aggression? Can someone please help me??
