chinese_item:
律师称护士因跨性别投诉受到处罚

达灵顿纪念医院的护士们已将NHS的上司告上就业法庭。据称，NHS的上司对投诉跨性别女性使用她们更衣室的女护士进行了“处罚”。该信托机构表示，这些护士“妖魔化”了罗斯，并且当时的政策符合指导方针和法律。

费托先生还说，法规中提到的“男性”和“女性”是基于生物学上的性别，这一点在今年早些时候得到了英国最高法院的确认。

奇瑟姆先生表示，护士们对待罗斯的方式“不友善且毫无根据”，她们对行为的指控被“夸大了”。

洛克女士说，自从提出投诉后，这些护士感到“完全被排斥”，并补充说信托机构的政策“看起来完全不合理”。
english_item:
Nurses penalised for trans complaint, says lawyer11 November 2025 Share Save Share SavePA Media Nurses from Darlington Memorial Hospital have taken their NHS bosses to an employment tribunalNHS bosses "penalised" female nurses complaining about a transgender woman using their changing room, an employment tribunal has been told.
The trust said the nurses had "demonised" Rose and the policy conformed with guidance and laws at the time.
Mr Fetto also said the words "men" and "women" referred to in regulations were based on biological sex, as confirmed by the UK Supreme Court earlier this year.
Mr Cheetham said the nurses' treatment of Rose had been "unkind and unjustified", with their allegations about conduct "exaggerated".
Ms Lockey said the nurses had felt "totally ostracised" since raising their complaint, adding the trust's policy had "just seemed completely unreasonable".

chinese_item:
低头玩手机正在毁掉你的关系吗？
这就是所谓的“低头族”行为——无意识地因为手机而冷落了对方——悄无声息地渗透到了日常生活的点滴之中。
伦敦大学学院的副教授凯特琳·雷格博士提出了一种简单的方法，可以帮助你在与他人相处时不再无意识地拿起手机。
每次你想拿起手机时，告诉对方你为什么要这么做，用完之后就放下手机，重新投入到对话中。
雷格博士说：“这样做可以让对方不会感到被忽视。”
english_item:
Is phubbing ruining your relationship?
That's how so called phubbing - unintentionally snubbing someone in favour of your phone - creeps into everyday moments.
Dr Kaitlyn Regehr, Associate Professor at University College London suggests one simple way to stop you picking up your phone mindlessly when you're with someone else.
Every time you reach for your phone, tell the other person why you're doing it, and when you're done, put it down and re-engage.
"It stops the other person feeling ignored," Dr Regehr says.

chinese_item: 日本九州大分市发生了一场大火，至少170栋建筑受损，居民被迫疏散。空中拍摄的画面显示，这场据说面积相当于七个足球场的大火，在一个渔港附近的几栋住宅楼中蔓延。这是自1976年以来日本最大的城市火灾，不包括由地震引发的火灾。当地媒体报道称有一人死亡，而起火原因仍在调查中。
english_item:
A huge fire broke out in the city of Oita, on the Japanese island of Kyushu, damaging at least 170 buildings and forcing residents to evacuate, authorities said.
Aerial footage showed the scale of the blaze, said to be the size of seven football fields, that burned through several residential buildings near a fishing port.
It is Japan's largest urban fire since 1976, excluding incidents caused by earthquakes.
Local media say one person was killed, while the cause of the fire was still under investigation.

chinese_item:
我们可能会感到比平时更加疲倦，缺乏精力或是难以集中注意力。
对于那些症状较轻的人来说，这里有三种方法可以帮助你应对这些阴郁的日子，甚至学会享受它们。
“他们把冬天看作是一年中充满机会的时节，”她说。
这可能包括像编织、修补衣物这样的活动，或者是帮助你为来年做好准备的一些做法。
“虽然这些活动不像旅行或露营那样活跃，但它们以一种更具恢复性的方式同样积极。”
english_item:
We can feel more tired than normal, lack energy or find it difficult to concentrate.
For those with milder symptoms, here are three ways to help you manage the darker days and even embrace them.
"They're looking at winter as a time of year that's full of opportunities," she says.
This could include activities such as knitting, repairing clothes or practices that help get you ready for the year ahead.
"And while that's not as active as, say, travelling or camping, it's just as active in a more restorative way.

chinese_item:
警告：读者可能会觉得某些细节令人不安。一位天主教神父因承认在新纳粹在线聊天室中讨论炸毁清真寺和枪杀黑人而被判处12个月的社区服务令。
57岁的马克·罗尔斯神父承认在2024年5月和6月期间，通过Telegram应用程序发送了三条威胁或冒犯性的信息。
BBC记者科莱特·休姆在卡迪夫治安法院外问他，为什么他想要杀害穆斯林并炸毁清真寺。
english_item:
Warning: Readers may find some details distressingA Catholic priest who admitted discussing bombing mosques and shooting black people in neo-Nazi online chatrooms has been sentenced to a 12-month community order.
Father Mark Rowles, 57, admitted three counts of sending menacing or offensive messages using the Telegram app in May and June 2024.
BBC reporter Colette Hume asked him outside Cardiff Magistrates' Court why he wanted to kill Muslims and bomb mosques.

chinese_item: “我们不能放弃对我们小宝贝的希望” 一位正在等待肝脏和肠道移植手术的幼儿的父母恳请其他家长讨论一下如果处在相同情况下他们会怎么做。
english_item: 'We can't give up hope for our wee boy'The parents of a toddler waiting for a liver and bowel transplant are pleading with parents to have the conversation about what they would do in the same situation.

chinese_item:
以色列对加沙的袭击造成22名巴勒斯坦人死亡，医疗和民防官员表示双方都指责对方违反了六周前达成的停火协议。
哈马斯的医疗和民防官员称，在以色列对加沙北部和中部的一系列空袭中，至少有22名巴勒斯坦人丧生。
根据加沙卫生部的数据，自停火协议生效以来，已有超过310名巴勒斯坦人在以色列的袭击中丧生。
该地区的民防部门表示，周六以色列的袭击目标是加沙城北部以及中部的代尔巴拉赫和努赛拉特难民营。
他们补充说，在努赛拉特，以色列袭击了两栋房屋，其中阿布·阿穆内一家的房子被击中后，导致三人死亡。
english_item:
Israeli strikes across Gaza kill 22 Palestinians, medical and civil defence officials sayBoth sides have accused each other of violating the ceasefire deal agreed on six weeks ago.
At least 22 Palestinians have been killed in a series of Israeli air strikes across northern and central Gaza, Hamas medical and civil defence officials have said.
More than 310 Palestinians have been killed in Israeli strikes since the ceasefire came into force, according to Gaza's health ministry.
The territory's civil defence said Saturday's strikes by Israel targeted Gaza City in the north, as well as Deir al-Balah and Nuseirat camp in central Gaza.
Two houses were targeted in Israeli strikes in Nuseirat, they added, with three killed after the home of the Abu Amouneh family was hit.

chinese_item:
早产可能会让人感到非常孤独，碧翠丝说，“有时候，当你得知自己的宝宝会提前到来时，那种感觉可能极其孤单。”她的话是为早产研究慈善机构Borne发起的一项活动的一部分。几个月前，她的女儿提前几周出生后，她成为了该机构的赞助人之一。

碧翠丝公主在世界早产儿日前夕的一次播客中谈到自己的经历时表示，早产可以是“极其孤独”的体验。公主还提到，知道Borne正在支持相关研究并提出重要问题，这让她有一种“生活改变般的解脱感”。

每年的世界早产儿日是由世界卫生组织组织的。
english_item:
Premature birth can be incredibly lonely, says Beatrice"And sometimes, when you are faced with that moment of learning that your baby's going to come a bit early, it can be incredibly lonely."
Her words are part of a campaign for premature birth research charity Borne, of which she became a patron months after her daughter was born several weeks early.
Princess Beatrice has said premature birth can be "incredibly lonely", as she reflected on her own experience for a podcast ahead of World Prematurity Day.
The princess said there was a "sense of life changing relief" knowing Borne was supporting research and asking important questions.
World Prematurity Day is organised by the World Health Organization every year.

chinese_item:
家庭将在新的一年面临意外的能源价格上涨。三天前，生活成本记者凯文·皮奇报道，本周英国各地气温下降，周四英格兰东北部出现了雪景。数百万家庭将在寒冬时节看到能源价格略有上涨，此前监管机构Ofgem公布了下一次的价格上限。

这次涨价出乎意料，因为预测者曾表示年初价格上限可能会下降。天然气和电费仍然相对较高，而气温突然下降使人们更加关注这些费用。

据说正在考虑的一个选项是免除能源账单中的增值税，这将使每年的账单减少约80英镑。能源部长马丁·麦克卢斯基表示，能源账单“仍然过高”，但政府正在采取行动，“通过扩大温暖家园折扣计划，数百万更多家庭将从账单中减免150英镑”。
english_item:
Households face unexpected rise in energy prices in new year3 days ago Share Save Kevin Peachey Cost of living correspondent Share SaveGetty Images There were snowy scenes in north-east England on Thursday as temperatures dropped across the UK this weekMillions of households will see a slight rise in energy prices in the depths of winter, after regulator Ofgem outlined its next price cap.
The rise was a surprise after forecasters suggested the cap may fall at the start of the year.
Gas and electricity bills remain relatively high, and the sudden drop in temperature has brought the costs to the forefront of people's minds.
One option said to be under consideration is removing VAT from energy bills, which would cut about ￡80 from annual bills.
Energy Minister Martin McCluskey said energy bills "remain too high", but that the government was taking action, with "millions more families receiving ￡150 off their bills through the expanded Warm Home Discount scheme".

chinese_item:
前Radio 1电台DJ蒂姆·韦斯特伍德因强奸指控获保释
这位曾任职于BBC Radio 1和Capital Xtra的DJ蒂姆·韦斯特伍德自上个月被控四项强奸罪名以来，首次出庭受审。
现年68岁的他还面临九项猥亵侵犯和两项性侵犯的指控。
据称，其中三项猥亵侵犯发生在1990年代的BBC演播室。
这些指控涉及七名女性，其中一些人当时年仅17岁，指控时间跨度从1983年至2016年。
韦斯特伍德先生在周一威斯敏斯特地方法院的听证会上获得了保释。
english_item:
Former Radio 1 DJ Tim Westwood bailed over rape chargesEx-BBC Radio 1 and former Capital Xtra DJ Tim Westwood has appeared in court for his first hearing since being charged with four counts of rape last month.
The 68-year-old has also been charged with nine counts of indecent assault and two counts of sexual assault.
Three of the accounts of indecent assault are alleged to have taken place in BBC studios in the 1990s.
The charges related to seven women, some as young as 17 years old, with the allegations spanning from 1983 to 2016.
Mr Westwood was granted bail at a hearing at Westminster Magistrates' Court on Monday.

chinese_item:
融化的冰川威胁着要摧毁欧洲的村庄——保护它们所需的高昂成本是否值得？
几天前，地质学家警告说山体越来越不稳定后，这个村庄的300名居民已经被疏散。
并非每个阿尔卑斯山村庄都处于危险之中，但这种明显的不可预测性引起了巨大的担忧。
关于搬迁的争论
像所有瑞士山区村庄一样，布拉滕进行了风险测绘和监测；这就是为什么它的300名居民被疏散了。
保护阿尔卑斯山村庄非常昂贵。
english_item:
Melting glaciers threaten to wipe out European villages - is the steep cost to protect them worth it?
The village's 300 residents had been evacuated days earlier, after geologists warned that the mountain was increasingly unstable.
Not every alpine village is at risk, but the apparent unpredictability is causing huge concern.
The debate around relocationBlatten, like all Swiss mountain villages, was risk mapped and monitored; that's why its 300 residents were evacuated.
Preserving the alpine villages is expensive.

chinese_item: 一位前英国电信工程师因石棉相关癌症去世，其家人表示他们“不希望任何人经历同样的痛苦”，并呼吁他的前同事们站出来提供信息。来自萨里郡沃斯特公园的克利夫·霍顿，享年91岁，在为这家电信公司担任电气工程师40年后于8月去世。现在，霍顿先生的家人说他们的丈夫和父亲被“夺走了”，并且已经通知英国电信集团他们将对公司提起法律诉讼。英国电信集团的一位发言人表示，对于霍顿先生的去世感到“非常遗憾”，并确认收到了其家人的法律索赔通知，但拒绝进一步评论。
english_item:
The family of a former BT engineer who died of an asbestos-related cancer say they "wouldn't like anyone to go through the same pain" as they call for former colleagues to come forward.
Clive Houghton, 91, from Worcester Park, Surrey, died in August after working as an electrical engineer for the telecoms company for 40 years.
Now, Mr Houghton's family say their husband and father was "robbed" from them and have notified BT of a legal claim against the company.
A BT Group spokesperson said it was "very sorry" to hear of Mr Houghton's passing and that it had received a legal claim from his family but declined to comment further.

chinese_item:
一家为萨里和苏塞克斯贫困地区提供帮助的食品慈善机构表示，今年冬天可能是其用户经历过的最艰难的一个冬天。
FareShare Sussex & Surrey 提供食品包裹、支持社区食品储藏室，并在团体和住宅环境中为需要的人烹饪热餐。
该慈善机构还表示，“异常晚的收成”导致冬季农产品减少，进一步加剧了剩余食品供应的压力。
运营沃金Shopwise的Foodwise Limited信托主席马丁·沃登说：“FareShare Sussex & Surrey的支持使我们的货架保持充足。”
FareShare Sussex & Surrey 表示，它从吉尔福德和布莱顿的仓库中拯救并重新分配来自企业、超市、制造商和农场的剩余食品，供给这两个郡的248个当地组织。
english_item:
It will be toughest winter yet, says food charity2 hours ago Share Save Zac Sherratt South East Share SaveFareShare Surrey & Sussex A total of 728 people spent 32,963 hours volunteering at FareShare Sussex & Surrey last yearA food charity helping people in deprived areas across Surrey and Sussex says this winter could be the toughest yet for its users.
FareShare Sussex & Surrey provides food parcels, supports community pantries, and cooks hot meals in group and residential settings for those in need.
The charity also says "unusually late harvests" have led to less winter produce, putting further strain on surplus food supplies.
Martin Vodden, chairman of Trustees Foodwise Limited, which runs Shopwise in Woking, said: "FareShare Sussex & Surrey's support is what keeps our shelves well-stocked.
FareShare Sussex & Surrey says it rescues and redistributes surplus food from businesses, supermarkets, manufacturers and farms from its depots in Guildford and Brighton to 248 local organisations across the two counties.

chinese_item: 北伦敦一所学校用教育意识课程取代了对家长的逃学罚款。卡姆登区尼特利小学的校长加雷斯·莫里斯决定尝试一种不同的方法来解决孩子们经常旷课的问题，并且他的方法得到了广泛赞誉。学校表示，迄今为止参加该课程的十个家庭中有有八个的家庭的孩子出勤情况“显著”改善。“家长们反馈非常积极，特别是关于强调孩子因缺课而感到的焦虑方面。”莫里斯先生说。尼特利小学自称为一个“学习、玩耍和成长的地方”。卡姆登区议会于11月10日发布的一份报告中特别提到了尼特利小学在领导力方面的表现，该报告概述了该行政区在学校出勤率方面的脆弱性。
english_item:
School replaces fines with awareness courses1 day ago Share Save Josef Steen Local Democracy Reporting Service Share SaveNetley Primary school Netley Primary School's head teacher Gareth Morris with its family and children's champion Simone Sarosh-CambridgeA north London school has swapped truancy fines for parents for education awareness courses.
The head teacher of Netley Primary School in Camden, Gareth Morris, decided to try a different method to tackle the number of children regularly missing lessons - and has been praised for the success of his approach.
The courses have "dramatically" improved attendance for eight out of 10 families who have taken part so far, the school said.
'Essential' work"The feedback from parents has been incredibly positive, especially around highlighting the anxiety their children feel from missing school," Mr Morris said.
Pollard Edward Thomas Netley Primary School desribes itself as a "place to learn, play and grow"Camden Council highlighted Netley's leadership in a report published on 10 November that outlined the borough's vulnerability on school attendance.

chinese_item: 关于有报道称在新冠疫情初期一些护士不得不穿着垃圾袋工作，汉考克表示，NHS需要“吸取教训”，并为未来建立“更好的库存”。这位前卫生大臣正在就新冠疫情对医疗系统的影响第三次出席听证会。马特·汉考克称，在新冠疫情期间，英格兰的NHS距离防护服和其他防护装备耗尽仅剩“六到七个小时”。不过他补充说，尽管英格兰的一些医院承受了“巨大的压力”，但整个NHS系统从未被压垮。随后，汉考克被问及2020年因新冠去世的苏西·沙利文的案例。
english_item:
NHS hours from PPE running out in Covid - HancockAsked about reports that some nurses had to wear binbags early in the Covid crisis, he said the NHS needed to “learn the lessons of what went wrong” and put in place “better stockpiles” for the future.
The former health secretary was giving evidence for the third time at the Covid inquiry , about the impact on healthcare systems.
The NHS in England came within “six or seven hours” of running out of gowns and other protective equipment during the Covid pandemic, Matt Hancock has said.
But while some hospitals in England came under “extraordinary pressure”, the wider NHS system was never overwhelmed, he added.
Mr Hancock was then asked about the case of Suzie Sullivan, who died of Covid in 2020.

chinese_item: 国家电力系统运营商（NESO）警告称，到2030年，总金额可能达到每年近80亿英镑。那么，政府真的准备好冒自35年前私有化以来对英国电力市场最激进的改革风险吗？一项又一项的民调显示，对于大多数人来说，生活成本问题远比这重要，人们经常特别提到对能源价格上涨的担忧。支持地区定价的包括NESO、公民咨询局以及能源监管机构Ofgem的负责人。然而，许多参与建设和运营可再生能源发电厂的企业反对这一举措。
english_item:
The total could reach almost ￡8bn a year by 2030, warns the National Electricity System Operator (NESO), the body in charge of the electricity network.
So is the government really ready to risk the most radical shake-up of the UK electricity market since privatisation 35 years ago?
Poll after poll says cost of living is a much more important for most people, and people often specifically cite concerns about rising energy prices.
The supporters of regional pricing include NESO, Citizens Advice and the head of the energy regulator, Ofgem.
Energy firms push backThere are, however, many businesses involved in building and running renewable energy plants that oppose the move.

chinese_item:
从汽油到食品杂货，特朗普是否兑现了应对价格上涨的承诺？
生活成本的急剧上升是选民最关心的问题，而特朗普将此归咎于总统乔·拜登。
一位白宫官员告诉BBC Verify，特朗普总统无法控制南美的天气模式，咖啡价格的上涨是一个全球现象。
同一位官员表示，总统正在通过暂时增加进口来解决牛肉价格上涨的问题。
自他成为总统以来，物价一直在上涨。
english_item:
From gas to groceries, has Trump kept his promise to tackle rising prices?
The steep rise in the cost of living was top of voters' minds and Trump blamed President Joe Biden.
Getty ImagesA White House official told BBC Verify President Trump did not control weather patterns in South America and coffee prices hikes were a global phenomenon.
The same official said the president was addressing rising beef prices by temporarily increasing imports.
Since he became president, prices have risen.

chinese_item:
这些机器人可以打扫卫生、帮助锻炼——还能在你年老时照顾你。
去年，慈善机构Skills for Care的一份报告发现，英格兰有131,000个成人护工职位空缺。
而根据Age UK的数据，英格兰大约有200万65岁及以上的老年人未得到所需的照顾。
Shadow Robot公司的沃克先生坚决认为，不应忽视机器人在护理中的应用。
从实验室到现实生活诺丁汉大学的普拉明达·卡莱布-索利教授决心让这些机器人在实际中发挥良好作用。
english_item:
These robots can clean, exercise - and care for you in old age.
To view this content choose Accept and continueThere were 131,000 vacancies for adult care workers in England, a report by charity, Skills for Care, found last year.
And in all, around two million people aged 65 and over in England are living with unmet care needs, according to Age UK.
Mr Walker of Shadow Robot is adamant that the use of robots in care should not be dismissed.
From labs to the real worldPraminda Caleb-Solly, a professor at the University of Nottingham is determined to make these robots work well in practice.

chinese_item:
首尔市中心安装了一位真人大小的全息警察，以期震慑犯罪。
这个全息图每两分钟在晚上7点到10点之间投射在夜生活繁忙、附近有许多酒吧的解洞公园。
警方表示，自从安装了全息警察后，该地区的犯罪率下降了约22%，促使他们考虑在其他地区推广。
BBC的具允娜访问了全息警察现场，了解人们对它的看法。
english_item:
A life-sized holographic police officer has been installed in central Seoul in a bid to deter crime.
The hologram is projected every two minutes from 19:00 to 22:00 in Jeo-dong Park, an area which is busy at night with nearby bars.
Police say crime rates in the area have dropped approximately 22 percent since the installation, prompting them to consider roll out in other areas.
The BBC's Yuna Ku visited the hologram to see what people think.

chinese_item: 自2003年以来，汤布里奇浸信会教堂为那些在12月25日可能会独自一人的人提供了一顿补贴餐。除了圣诞午餐外，布尔太太还会号召大家捐赠礼物，并在当天分发给来宾。当被问及是什么激励她在22年后仍然坚持这项活动时，布尔太太说：“每个人都喜欢来参加，每个人都乐于帮助。”教堂方面表示，志愿者应具备相关的餐饮资格证书，并且在圣诞节当天有自己的交通工具。相关报道：呼吁寻找厨师以挽救教堂的圣诞日午餐
english_item:
Tonbridge Baptist Church has provided a subsidised meal for anyone who would otherwise be alone on 25 December since 2003.
Alongside the Christmas lunch, Mrs Bull puts the call out for gifts which are then handed out on the day.
When asked what inspires her to continue with this initiative after 22 years, Mrs Bull said: "Everybody loves coming, everybody loves to help.
The church said volunteers should have relevant catering certification and have their own means of transport on Christmas Day.
Related stories Appeal for chef to save church Christmas Day lunch

chinese_item: 上议院成员对安乐死法案提出了超过900项修改建议，随着议员们继续讨论该立法。议员们现已开始逐条审议该法案，这一阶段预计至少需要四天时间。在周五的辩论中，多位议员表示担心目前形式的法案存在缺陷。各政党将该法案视为良心问题，这意味着他们不会指示其议员或议员如何投票。如果该法案最终通过成为法律，政府有四年的时间来建立安乐死服务，这意味着首次安乐死可能要到2029/30年才会发生。
english_item:
Peers suggest over 900 changes to assisted dying bill14 November 2025 Share Save Jennifer McKiernan , Political reporter and Harry Farley , Political correspondent Share SavePA MediaMembers of the House of Lords have put forward more than 900 proposed changes to the law to deliver assisted dying, as peers continue to debate the legislation.
Peers have now begun line-by-line examination of the bill, with this stage due to take place over at least four days.
During Friday's debate, a number of peers raised concerns that the bill in its current form was flawed.
The bill is being treated by parties as a matter of conscience, meaning they will not instruct their MPs or peers how to vote.
If it does pass into law, the government has four years in which to get an assisted dying service into place, meaning it could be 2029/30 before the first assisted death happens.

chinese_item: 暴雨引发的洪水导致西班牙东北部加泰罗尼亚地区多人被困车内，官方表示。社交媒体上出现了视频，显示塔拉戈纳省的拉帕伊塔和圣巴巴拉镇被浑浊的洪水席卷，沿途一切都被冲走。西班牙国家气象局AEMET已在该沿海省份发布了最高级别的红色警报。目前尚无人员伤亡报告。
english_item:
Floods trap people in cars in Spain's Catalonia regionLa Ràpita is among the towns ravaged by floodsFloods caused by torrential rain have left a number of people trapped in vehicles in Spain's north-eastern Catalonia region, officials say.
Videos on social media have emerged showing torrents of muddy water sweeping through the towns of La Ràpita and Santa Bàrbara in the Tarragona province, and moving everything in their path.
The highest red alert has been declared in the coastal province by Spain's national weather agency AEMET.
So far there have been no reports of any deaths or injuries.

chinese_item: 一名校长因在学校的三角恋纠纷中用扳手袭击副校长而被判入狱两年四个月。54岁的安东尼·费尔顿承认，在3月5日于阿伯文圣约瑟夫罗马天主教综合学校内，故意对51岁的理查德·派克实施了严重身体伤害未遂的行为。法庭上透露，费尔顿得知自己是该校一位女教师孩子的父亲后感到非常痛苦，并且最近发现派克也在与这位女教师交往。派克遭受了“多次打击”，随后因轻微伤势在医院接受了治疗。视频由艾琳·利斯特编辑。
english_item:
A head teacher has been jailed for two years and four months for attacking his deputy with a wrench after a love triangle row at their school.
Anthony Felton, 54, admitted attempted grievous bodily harm with intent after attacking 51-year-old Richard Pyke at St Joseph's Roman Catholic Comprehensive in Aberavon, Neath Port Talbot, on 5 March.
The court heard Felton was distressed after finding out he was the father of a fellow teacher's child and had recently discovered Mr Pyke was in a relationship with the same teacher.
After suffering a "number of blows", Mr Pyke was treated for minor injuries in hospital.
Video edited by Erin Lister

chinese_item:
听布兰温朗读这篇文章。尼克·格林在教书17年后辞职，其中一个最主要的原因就是再也不想经历一次Ofsted（英国教育标准局）的检查了。
他和其他一些老师、校长以及教育工会一样，都批评过Ofsted过去的检查方法——尤其是那种直白的评级系统：“优秀”、“良好”、“需要改进”或“不合格”。
后来，一位验尸官裁定，Ofsted的一次检查是导致她死亡的一个因素，并发布了一份报告呼吁进行改革。
一份Ofsted检查报告可能看起来是这样的。但尽管新制度已于本周早些时候生效，其具体如何运作尚不完全清楚。
“关于学校检查最重要的一点是：‘什么最符合孩子们的利益？’检查符合他们的利益，因为归根结底，这关乎‘质量控制’。”
english_item:
Listen to Branwen reading this article When Nick Green quit teaching after 17 years, one factor drove him above all others: to never go through another Ofsted inspection.
He joins a line of teachers, head teachers and education unions that have criticised Ofsted's past inspection methods - often on account of the blunt rating system: "outstanding", "good", "requires improvement" or "inadequate".
A coroner later ruled an Ofsted inspection had contributed to her death - and issued a report calling for changes.
How an Ofsted inspection report could lookBut it's not yet fully clear how the new system will work in practice - even though it came into effect earlier this week.
”The overriding matter of importance with regard to school inspection is: ’What is in the best interests of the children?’ “Inspection is in their best interests because above all else it is about 'quality control'.

chinese_item:
我帮她照看我以前照顾过的宝宝。

我从她五个月大的时候开始照顾她，一直到她刚满两岁。她现在刚满四岁。我差不多一年没见过她了，几乎可以肯定她已经不记得我了。想到自己现在对她来说基本上只是个陌生人，我感到一阵莫名的伤感。

但是，当我们在外面玩耍的时候，她转过身来，安静地看着我，过了好一会儿。然后，她完全没有开口，对我说：“你以前抱过我。”

我笑着对她说：“你说得对！你小时候我们一起做了很多好玩的事情。我们一起散步，一起去公园玩，一起游泳……”她兴奋地说：“你还记得我们一起去图书馆吗？”我说记得。她笑了笑，然后又继续玩了。

我的心都快要跳出来了??????
english_item:
I babysat the baby I used to nanny
I nannied her from when she was 5 months old to shortly after she turned 2. She just turned 4. I hadn’t seen her in almost a year and felt almost certain she wouldn’t really remember me. I was feeling melancholy sitting with the assumption that I’m basically just a stranger to her now.

But when we were playing outside, she turned and looked at me quietly for a long moment. Then, completely unprompted, she said to me, “You used to hold me when I was a baby.” 

I smiled at her and said, “You’re right! We did all kinds of fun stuff together when you were a baby. We went for walks, we played at the park, we went swimming…” She got excited and said, “Do you remember when we would go to the library?” I said yes. She smiled and then went back to playing. 

My heart felt like it was bursting ??????

chinese_item:
一个小孩向我挥手

我当时正在散步，想走够步数。我有点衣衫不整，而且过去几周一直情绪低落，所以看起来不太精神。我注意到有两个小孩拿着塑料棒球棍在车道上跑来跑去，所以我尽量避免被打到。

当我经过他们家时，其中一个小男孩一边挥手一边喊“嗨！！！”。我当时很惊讶，但也挥手回应了一声“嗨！”，另一个小孩和他们的爸爸也都向我挥手了。
english_item:
a kid waved to me
i was on a walk just trying to get my steps in. i'm kinda disheveled and i've been depressed for the past few weeks so i didn't look the greatest. i noticed two kids running around in the driveway of a house w plastic baseball bats so i was trying not to get hit.

when i passed their house, one of the little boys shouted out a "hi!!!" with a wave. i was so shocked but i waved back and gave back a little "hi!" and the other kid waved as well as their dad.

chinese_item:
我今天早上看到的那只狗

我的祖父母上周让他们的狗安乐死了。她陪伴我度过了大部分童年时光，全家人都非常爱她——她真是个小淘气，我一直非常想念她。今天早上在公交车上，我看到一只狗长得和她一模一样：一样的脸，一样的毛色，一样的性格。这让我想起了她，也让我感到了一丝慰藉。
english_item:
The dog I saw this morning
My grandparents put their dog down last week. She'd been around for most of my childhood and the entire family adored her - she was was such a little menace and I've been missing her terribly. This morning on the bus I saw a dog that looked just like her: same face, same colouring, same attitude. It reminded me of her and gave me a little moment of joy.

chinese_item:
和兄弟们一起打扫

我们家之前请了两位女士每周来做三次深度清洁，但昨天清洁公司打电话来说她们生病了，来不了……我们没让妈妈再安排清洁人员，而是告诉她我们自己来。

她给了我们清洁用品，我们换上运动服，放上音乐，然后就让我们在屋里自由活动了。

妈妈很快就不耐烦了，因为我们一开始干得不错，每个房间都打扫得让她满意，但到了楼下，我们却把它变成了一个小型音乐会，还跳起了达布卡舞，甚至还有个观众/评委团（两岁的外甥、妈妈、爸爸和嫂子）。

爸爸很快就退出了评委团，因为我弟弟跳的达布卡舞不合他的心意，所以他自己领舞。

总而言之，妈妈一点也不满意，但这让我想起了我们小时候还住在一起的日子。虽然我已经回来几个星期了，几乎每天24小时都和他们在一起，但我还是强烈地意识到我有多么想念他们，想念我们一起胡闹的日子，我希望我能以某种方式找回那种感觉。
english_item:
Cleaning with my brothers
We have 2 ladies that come and deep clean 3 times a week but the agency called yesterday and said they're sick and won't be able to come... We didn't let mom request another duo and instead told her we'd clean ourselves.

She gave us the cleaning supplies, we put on workout clothes and music and then she let us loose in the house.

Mom quickly got exasperated because while we started out strong and got each of our rooms cleaned to her satisfaction, we got to the downstairs area and turned it into a concert completed with dabkeh and an audience/judging panel (2yo nephew, Mom, Dad & SIL) 

Dad quickly abandoned the panel because my brother was not leading the dabkeh to his satisfaction so he led it instead. 

All in all mom wasn't impressed in the least but it brought me back to when we were younger and still living together. Eveb though I've been back for a few weeks now and I've been seeing them practically 24/7, it hit me full force how much I actually missed them and missed our antics and I wish I could get that back in a way.

chinese_item:
加油站闲聊

我常去的加油站的服务员都很友善，现在连我点软椒盐卷饼的号码都没人喊了，因为他们都知道总是我点，哈哈。

那里有个和我年纪相仿的年轻女孩，几周前她跟我闲聊时提到她开始了一段新的恋情。她听起来很兴奋，听到她过得这么好，我也很开心。

几天前晚上，我和男朋友去加油站买东西，她正好在收银台后面。她一边给我们结账，一边兴奋地告诉我，他们现在关系很稳定，而且进展很顺利！我祝贺了她一会儿，正要离开，她突然说了一句话，让我停下了脚步：

“我只是觉得，如果有人会关心我，那肯定是你。”

我不知道为什么这句话让我有点感动，但我很喜欢这种人与人之间建立的小小联系，那种不求回报的真诚。我们不必交换电话号码，也不必在彼此的婚礼上做伴娘，但我们总会停下来聊聊天，让彼此的生活更美好。听到别人觉得我会关心（大多是）陌生人生活中的点点滴滴，我真的很高兴。我希望当我走进房间，她看到我的时候，能从她上夜班和应付各种人的压力中解脱出来一些，因为她知道她会有时间倾诉，卸下伪装。这正是我想要成为的人！
english_item:
gas station small talk
the attendants at my local gas station are all kind people, nobody even bothers calling out the number for my soft pretzel anymore as they know it’s always me lol. 

there’s a young lady that works there that is about my age and a few weeks ago she was telling me in passing about starting a new relationship. she sounded so giddy and it really made me smile to hear she was doing well. 

my boyfriend and i stopped in a few nights ago to grab a few things, and she was behind the register. as she’s ringing us up, she lights up and tells me that things are pretty official now, and going very well! i congratulated her for a minute and then went to head towards the door, when she said something that stopped me in my tracks:

“i just figured that if anybody would care, it would be you.” 

i don’t know why it almost made me emotional, but i love when people form little connections like that, those without the expectation of it going anywhere. we don’t have to exchange numbers and be maid of honor each other’s weddings, but but we always stop to chat and brighten each other’s days. it was just really nice to hear that I am perceived as someone that would care about the small details of a (mostly) strangers’s life. I hope when I walked in and she saw me, some of the stress from working the overnight shift and dealing with people fell off of her shoulders, because she knew she would have a minute to vent and drop the mask. that’s exactly who i wanna be!

chinese_item:
闻玫瑰

昨天我独自散步——在一个十字路口，有两个人正朝相反的方向走（所以我们本来会擦肩而过）。走到拐角处，其中一个人突然走开了，我看到她跑到拐角处去闻附近一朵玫瑰。然后她又回到朋友身边，我们俩在街上擦肩而过。

现在我有点后悔当时没也去闻闻那朵玫瑰了！
english_item:
smelling roses
Took myself on a walk yesterday - at one of the intersections there were two people walking the opposite direction of me (so we were going to pass each other). At the corner, one of them split off and I saw her run around the corner to smell a rose growing nearby. Then she went back to her friend and we passed each other on the street.

Now I kinda wish I’d gone to smell the rose too!

chinese_item:
好心的女同事

昨天是我上班的第一天。我坐的椅子有点儿坏了，一直发出嘎吱嘎吱的声音，坐着很不舒服。

这位女同事注意到了，主动把她的椅子让给我，说她没问题。她想让我在这家略显沉闷的新公司第一天就能舒服些。我永远都会感激她的这份善意。

但是第二天我到公司的时候，发现她已经把椅子换回去了。好吧，哈哈??

我跟她说：“你把椅子换回去了。”我们都笑了。她真是太好了。我平时是个很懂得感恩的人，本来想表达更多感激之情，但是因为那天我遇到了一些事，实在提不起劲儿。不过我还是尽力道了谢。
english_item:
The kind girl at work
Yesterday was my first day at work. The chair I was sitting on was kind of broken and kept making noise, and it was clearly uncomfortable for me.

This girl noticed and offered me her chair, saying it wouldn’t be a problem for her. She wanted me to be comfortable on my first day at this dull new place. I’ll always be grateful to her for that act of RandomActsofKindness.

But the very next day, when I arrived at work, I saw that she had already swapped the chairs back. Fair enough, ahah ??

I just said to her, “You changed the chairs back,” and we both laughed. It was very nice of her. I’m generally a very appreciative person and would have expressed more gratitude, but since I was going through some issues, I didn’t have the energy. But I tried my best and thanked her.

chinese_item:
四叶草

从我大概12岁开始，我就经常能找到四叶草，还有一株五叶草和两株六叶草。大多数时候，我甚至都没刻意去找。我只是走路的时候瞥了一眼三叶草丛，碰巧就看到了。现在就算看到了，我也不会摘，因为我已经不珍惜它们了。反正，每次我跟别人提起这件事，他们总是会说：“哎，真希望我也有你这么幸运，我以前也找过，但总是找不到。” 说实话，我觉得这纯属扯淡。你根本不可能刻意去找。我都没费什么力气就找到了一大堆，你居然还说你是在找？真的假的？我知道这很可能只是客套话，并非真心想找，但这话总是让我很恼火。
english_item:
4-leaf clovers
So, ever since I was about 12 years old, I have found a lot of 4-leaf clovers, one 5-leaf clover, and two 6-leaf clovers. Most of the time, it’s not even purposeful looking. I just glance over at a clover patch while walking and I just happen to see one. It’s gotten to the point where even if I see one, I won’t pick it because I don’t value them anymore. Anyway, anytime I mention this to someone, they always end up saying something along the lines of “man, I wish were that lucky, I’ve tried before but I can never seem to find any”. Frankly, I call bs. There is no way you are actively looking. I am overflowing with these things without even trying and you’re telling me you are actually searching? Really? Like, I understand that this is more than likely just a pleasantry and not actually serious, but it always manages to annoy me.

chinese_item:
为了爱狗

我和我丈夫养了五只狗。除了Jj之外，它们大多数都同样爱我们。Jj是一只小型犬，是马尔济斯、西施犬和约克夏的混血。它非常爱我丈夫，我叫它“我丈夫的屁股伙伴”。我丈夫在家里的任何地方都离不开Jj。是的，包括上厕所。如果门关不上，它就乖乖地在门口等着。

但是今天早上，我凌晨四点就醒了，准备开始锻炼和工作，Jj还睡在我旁边。它醒了之后，在我上厕所、去厨房给我拿咖啡、以及我躺在地板上锻炼的时候，都跟着我到处走，还缠着我摸摸它。

啊，这就是拥有像Jj这样爱的狗狗的感觉。

别误会，我也有我最爱的狗狗，但它不像Jj那样爱人。它更独立一些。

今天早上感觉格外特别。这将是美好的一天。我希望你们生活中都有特别的宠物。宠物给予的爱是人类无法比拟的。
english_item:
For the love of a dog
My husband and I have 5 dogs. Most of them love us equally except Jj. Jj is a small dog who is Maltese, Schitzu, and Yorkie. He loves my husband and I call him my husband's butt buddy. My husband cant go anywhere in our home without Jj by his side. Yes, including the bathroom. If he cant run in before the door shuts he waits patiently by the door.
But this morning when I woke up at 4 am to get ready for my workout and workday, Jj was sleeping next to me. He woke up and followed me around while I used the restroom, went out to kitchen and got my coffee and when I laid down on the floor to exercise he demanded pets. 
Ah??, this is what it feels like to have the love of a dog like Jj. 
Dont get me wrong I have my favorite girl but she doesn't love a human like Jj. She is more independent. 
This morning felt a little more special. It's going to be a great day. I hope you all have a special pet in your life. Pets love in a way no human can equal.

chinese_item:
带爸爸去Dairy Queen

爸爸90岁了，住在养老院。今天我和兄弟姐妹带他去看医生，然后去Dairy Queen吃了暴风雪冰淇淋。黄油手指口味仍然是他的最爱。
english_item:
Took Dad to Dairy Queen
Dad is 90 years old and in assisted living. Today me and my sib took him to a dr appt and then to dairy queen for a blizzard. Butterfinger is still his favorite.

chinese_item:
你知道自己有口音吗？

我是一名在澳大利亚布里斯班的国际学生。为了补贴学习期间的生活开销，我会在晚上和周末做保姆。

我照顾了一个非常可爱的八岁男孩，他来自离布里斯班大约六个小时车程的小镇，当时他的妈妈正在城里参加一个活动。

我猜他可能不太常接触外国人，因为大约三个小时后，当他和我相处得更自在些时，他问我：

- 你知道自己有口音吗？

- 哦，是的，我知道！每个人都有口音，这取决于你来自哪里以及你讲什么语言。是不是很酷？

- ……哦。那是不是说我也有口音？

- 是的！你也有口音！

- 哦！！！是什么样的口音？

- 澳大利亚口音！

- 哇哦！

我喜欢孩子！！他们对最微小的事物都充满好奇。这让我心里暖暖的????
english_item:
Do you know you have an accent?
I'm an international student in Brisbane, Australia. I babysit at night and on weekends to cover for at least some expenses while studying.
I looked after a very cute 8 year old boy from a small town about 6 hours away from Brisbane while his mum attended a function in the city.
I guess he isn't around foreigners very often, because about 3 hours in and when he felt more comfortable around me, he asked:
-Do you know you have an accent?
-Oh, yes, I do! Everyone has an accent, it differs depending on where you are from and what languages you speak. Isn't that cool?
-...Oh. does that mean I have an accent too?
-Yup! You have an accent too!
-Oh!!!!!! What is it like?
-Australian!
-Wooooaaaaaaahhh

I love children!! They are amazed by the smallest things. It warms my heart ????

chinese_item:
录音

我大学时，一位老师在第三学期用录音的方式讲授了部分课程内容。我当然会听，但当时只是为了了解内容。那学期我特别忙，压力很大，根本没时间去细究细节。上课的时候我总是缠着他，他偶尔会抛给我一些小问题，比如室内空气中氧气的百分比是多少（21%），我敢肯定他问是因为他知道我知道。不过有时候，他完全是在逗我。比如，他问我肘关节的名称，还没等我喊出“鹰嘴”，他就自己回答了，我当时就想“卧槽！”

总之，我现在偶尔还会听这些录音，因为我对它们有种特殊的感情，而且，我的天哪，他的声音，他的语气。我仿佛看到他录下这些课，只是想找个人聊聊天，而那个人就是我。我们业内人士称之为“参照物错觉”，但女孩做梦总是可以的。我觉得他录音里的声音已经告诉我关于他的一切，谁能不被他迷住呢？
english_item:
voice recordings
one of my teachers in college delivered part of the course content through voice recordings in our class third semester. i listened to them, obviously, but at the time really just for content. it was a busy semester for me, i was pretty stressed and didn't really have the time for nuance. in class i was always on him for attention and every now and then he'd throw me a bone like what percentage of oxygen is in room air (21%) which i'm sure he asked because he knew i knew. sometimes, though, it was a total tease. like, asking what the name of the elbow is and then, before i could even shout out 'olecranon' he'd answer it himself and i'd be like 'fuck!'

anyway, i still listen to them sometimes because i'm sentimental about it and oh. my. god. his voice. his tone. i'm imagining him recording these lessons when he just wanted someone to talk to and that someone was me. we in the business call that a 'delusion of reference' but a girl can dream. i feel like his voice in these recordings tells me absolutely everything i need to know about him and who wouldn't be smitten?

chinese_item:
我咖啡喝多了。

我现在很兴奋，但过段时间就会感到疲惫。我有太多话想说，却无人倾诉。来杯咖啡，和我一起沉浸在咖啡因的胡言乱语中吧！
english_item:
I've had too much coffee.
I'm on the high; the crash will follow in due course. I've got too much to say and no one to say it to. Have a coffee and join me in my caffeinated rambles!

chinese_item:
昨天生了今年第一堆火。

我记得小时候经常围坐在烧柴的壁炉旁，看着爸爸生起的火，享受着那份温暖舒适。后来我搬出去上大学，我的公寓里没有壁炉，我的父母搬进的房子也没有壁炉。

嗯，去年我搬进了一间有真正壁炉的房子，昨天是我第一次在里面生火！

我都忘了那味道了！也忘了那味道能持续多久。今天早上我走进客厅，还能闻到温暖的炉火香。我都忘了，当最后几根木柴没完全烧完的时候，壁炉里还会留下一些余烬，可以用来生下一堆火。我都忘了一堆火能烧掉多少木柴，也忘了照料炉火、让它保持旺盛燃烧，或者调小火苗让它燃烧更久，是多么有趣的一件事。对着余烬吹气，看着它们闪烁，真是太神奇了。

我以前没意识到自己从小时候看爸爸生火学到了那么多东西，但事实证明，所有这些知识都还保存在那里。:)
english_item:
Built my first fire of the season yesterday
I remember sitting around the wood burning fireplace when I was a kid, watching a fire that my dad had set up and enjoying how cozy it was. When I moved out for college, my apartment didn’t have a fireplace, and my parents moved into houses without fireplaces as well. 

Well, this past year I moved into a house with a real fireplace, and yesterday was the first time that I set up a fire in it!

I’d forgotten the smell! And I’d forgotten how long it stays. I came into the living room this morning, and it still smells like a cozy fire. I’d forgotten that when the last couple of logs don’t burn entirely, you’re left with the remains of a fire in there, ready to be used to start the next. I’d forgotten just how much wood a fire can eat, and I’d forgotten how much fun it is to tend to a fire and keep it bright, or bring it down to make it last longer. There’s something really amazing about blowing on the embers and watching them flare. 

I hadn’t realized how much I’d retained from watching my dad set up fires as a kid, but it turns out that all of that knowledge is still stored away back there. :)

chinese_item:
听棒球比赛。

几天前，我参加了我儿子球队的橄榄球颁奖晚宴。如果你没参加过，大概就是教练简短致辞，然后给一些你都不认识的孩子们颁奖。

结果，这场晚宴正好赶上美联冠军赛第七场，蓝鸟队对阵水手队（懂的都懂）。于是，我做了每个真正的棒球/水手队球迷都会做的事：戴上耳机听比赛。

我旁边坐着一位水手队球迷（我能看到他手机上的比分），所以我把另一只耳机递给了他。他犹豫了一下，直到他妻子碰了碰他，说：“就用吧！”

于是我和吉姆一起听了第七场比赛，胡里奥和卡尔打出本垒打时，我们默默地击掌庆祝。环顾四周，我看到其他人的屏幕上也在播放比赛，但和别人一起听真的很有趣。

幸好宴会在比赛开始前就结束了，不然我们肯定会抱在一起哭。不过，这是一次很棒的经历。我和吉姆现在是一辈子的好朋友了。
english_item:
Listening to a baseball game.
A couple days ago go i attended a football banquet for my sons team. If you've never been to one, its a quick speech from the coach and then handing out awards to kids you dont know. 

Well this banquet landed right in the middle of game 7 of the ALCS, Jay's vs Mariners (IYKYK). So i did what every good baseball/Mariners fan does and popped an earbud into one ear to listen to the game. 

A fellow Mariners fan sat next to me (I could see the score on his phone he was watching) so I offered him my other earbud. He hesitated until his wife nudged him and said, just do it!

So me and Jim sat and listened to game 7, giving quiet high fives when Julio and Cal homered. Looking around i could see other screens with the game on but listening with someone else was really fun. 

The banquet ended before the game luckily, otherwise we'd have been crying in each other's arms. But it was a great experience. Me and Jim are besties for life now.

chinese_item:
早餐吃澳新军团饼干？

我做了炒鸡蛋和蔬菜卷饼（早餐卷饼？我不知道美国人怎么称呼它，但我觉得应该是这样），我儿子决定把剩下的澳新军团饼干当甜点吃。（这是在他吃完卷饼和半个杏子之后。）

饼干是我们昨晚做的。这是个用所有“剩料”做成的超大饼干（看得出来是他负责的）。

我之所以提到这个有点傻的早餐小点心，是因为在这个艰难的时期，这种日常的甜食真的很少得到人们的重视……
english_item:
Anzac biscuit for breakfast?
I made scrambled eggs and veg in a wrap (breakfast wrap? Idk what Americans call it but I think it’s that) and my kid decided to have a ‘spare’ Anzac biscuit as desert. (This was after his wrap and half an apricot).

We made the biscuits last night. This was a huge one made from all the ‘leftovers’ (you can tell he was in charge of the bowl)

I mention this silly morning treat as it’s this daily sweet stuff that just doesn’t get enough love in this difficult time…

chinese_item:
公园里的浪漫

我最近做了左手无名指手术，不得不摘下结婚戒指。手指肿胀，戒指也需要放大。直接戴回去感觉不太合适，所以我和妻子回到了47年前我们结婚的公园。我们走出停车场时，正好遇到一位新娘。我们向她道贺。之后，我和妻子回到了举行婚礼的地方，她把戒指重新戴在了我的手指上。然后我们散步，遇到了一对正在拍订婚照的情侣。
english_item:
Romance in the park
I recently had surgery on my left ring finger and had to take off my wedding ring. The finger is swollen and I had to get the ring enlarged. It didn't seem right to just put it back on, so my wife and I went back to the park where we were married 47 years ago. As we walked out of the parking lot, a new bride was just leaving. We congratulated her. My wife and I went back to the spot where the ceremony was, and she put the ring back on my finger. Then we went for a walk, and met a couple having their engagement pictures taken.

chinese_item:
晒太阳

我正坐在停车场里，享受着阳光的炙烤。我可以回屋里去，但现在这样感觉更好，至少暂时是这样。
english_item:
Baking in the Sun
Im sitting in a parking lot paradise just baking in the sun. I could go back inside, but it feels better right now, at least temporarily.

chinese_item:
终于用完了疫情初期买的那袋20磅重的糖。

我老公说我们永远也用不完。当时买它只是因为货架上只有这一种选择。现在把剩下的糖都倒出来做巧克力香蕉面包了。一个时代结束了。
english_item:
Just finished the 20lb bag of sugar I bought at the beginning of COVID.
My husband said we would never finish it. I only bought it because it was the only option available on the shelf at that time. Just poured the last of it making chocolate chip banana bread. The end of an era.

chinese_item:
好朋友

今天朋友打电话问我今晚要不要去她家吃晚饭。有人给我做饭？当然好啊！

在她家的时候，我收到了昨晚约会对象的拒绝短信——我们玩得很开心，而且我对她比对她更感兴趣。有点难过，最近我的约会运气不太好，不过好在朋友给我准备了一碗热腾腾的家常菜、一杯酒、一个拥抱，还有一部电视剧让我转移注意力。

我的朋友们都这么温柔善良，即使没有伴侣，我也不会觉得缺少什么。
english_item:
Good friends
My friend called today and asked if I wanted to go over for dinner tonight. Someone else cooking for me? Absolutely.

While there, I got a rejection text from a date the night before - we'd both had fun, I was more interested than they were. Sad, I've had an unlucky go of it in dating recently, but there was my friend with a warm bowl of homemade food, a glass of wine, a hug and a TV show to distract me.

My community is so gentle and good that I don't feel like I'm missing something by not having a partner.

chinese_item: 英国大幅削减了其向全球抗击艾滋病、结核病和疟疾基金提供的资金支持，无异于雪上加霜。今年早些时候，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普宣布了削减援助的消息。
english_item: Britain has significantly slashed its contribution to the Global Fund to fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria – compounding aid cuts announced by Donald Trump earlier this year

chinese_item: 佛罗里达州沃卢西亚县警方公布一名11岁嫌疑人被捕视频，引发脸书用户批评。
english_item: Facebook users criticized Florida’s Volusia Sheriff's Office for posting the ‘perp walk’ of 11-year-old suspect

chinese_item: 政府收到警告称，残障人士薪酬差距较去年略有缩小，但仍需采取更多措施。
english_item: The disability pay gap has slightly decreased from last year, but more still needs to be done, the government has been warned

chinese_item: 2023年对我来说，毫不夸张地说，是艰难的一年。10月份，我的祖母因乳腺癌去世；我当时（现在也是）有很多身材方面的困扰；而且，我的朋友圈也出现了一些问题。但幸运的是，当时有很多人支持我，比如青年团契的领袖、其他朋友、我的挚友、家人和老师，我对此感激不尽，因为没有他们，我根本无法熬过那一年。我非常感激生命中有他们，我爱他们。如果你也正经历着艰难的时刻，请寻求帮助和支持。相信我，这绝对值得。
english_item: The year 2023 was, to say the least, rough for me. I lost my grandmother to breast cancer in October, I had (and still have) a lot of body issues, and I was going through a lot of friend drama at the time. But, luckily, I had people such as youth group leaders, other friends, my best friend, family members, and teachers that were supporting me at the time, which I am so thankful for because I would have not gotten through the year without them. I am so thankful that I have them in my life and I love them so much. If you are going through a hard time, just ask for help and support. Just trust me, it is worth it.

chinese_item: 新冠疫情爆发初期，我因为抗体紊乱而居家隔离。我希望能帮助那些正在经历疫情或其他困境的人们。于是，我开始制作玻璃心，我称之为“口袋心”。我是一位玻璃艺术家，擅长玻璃熔合——我将多层玻璃放入窑炉中，以1480华氏度（约880摄氏度）的高温烧制，使各层玻璃熔合在一起。最初，我使用的窑炉很小，一次只能制作九颗心。我把这些心送给医护人员、急救人员、牙医、邮递员，以及任何需要慰藉的人，因为他们正经历着艰难的时期。我也会把它们分享给Facebook上的朋友们。我把出售这些心的所得捐赠给了圣巴巴拉的Direct Relief慈善机构和我们当地的心理健康组织。截至上个月（七月），我已经制作并赠送了1000颗“口袋心”。这不仅让我找到了生活的意义，也让我充满了正能量。收到心的人们都给予了极大的鼓励和积极的反馈。我会继续制作下去。
english_item: When COVID began, I was stuck at home because of an antibody disorder. I wanted to help others who were having a hard time with COVID or other issues. I began making glass hearts that I call pocket hearts. I am a glass artist and I do glass fusion-I put layers of glass in a kiln and fire it to 1480 F. The layers fuse together. When I first started I was using a very small kiln and could only make nine hearts at a time. I gave these hearts to medical personnel, first responders, dentists, mailmen, and to anyone who needed one because they were going through hard times. I would send them to friends on Facebook. Donated the funds from selling them to Direct Relief in Santa Barbara and to our local mental health organization. As of last month (July), I have made and given away 1,000 pocket hearts. This also gave me focus and positive energy. I have had wonderful responses from the recipients. I will continue making them.

chinese_item: 一个星期天，我和家人开车去教堂。我带了20美元，打算之后去附近的商店买点东西。我们开到这个十字路口时，我看到路边有个流浪汉在乞讨。我决定把20美元给他。他很高兴，但我妈妈却不这么认为。她告诉我，流浪汉可能会把钱用在食物以外的地方。我知道她说得没错，但我也知道人们是否会善用这笔钱。我反驳道：“我们难道不应该帮助有需要的人吗？”我知道我做的是对的，即使我妈妈可能不这么认为。
english_item: One Sunday, my family and I were driving to church. I had brought $20 with me to buy something at a store we were going to afterward. We had come up to this intersection as I saw a homeless man on the side of the road begging for a few dollars. I decided to give the twenty dollars to him. He was happy but I can't say the same for my mom. She told me how they might use the money for things other than food. I knew this was true but I also know whether people will use it right. I came back with an argument basically saying: "Are we not called to give to the needy?" I knew that I did the right thing to do, even though my mom may disagree.

chinese_item: 我18岁的儿子这周出了车祸。他开车去上课的路上追尾了另一辆车。我儿子没事，但是车子完全报废了。对方司机没有受伤，车子也只有轻微的损坏。他当时正要去参加一个工作会议，但他看到我儿子受到了很大的惊吓，就一直陪着我儿子，直到我赶到事故现场。他对这个打乱了他一天行程的年轻人表现出了极大的善意。先生，非常感谢您展现出的耐心和同情心。我将永远感激不尽！
english_item: My 18 year old son was in a car accident this week. He rear ended another car while driving to his college classes. My son is ok, but totaled the car. The other driver was not injured and had minor car damage. The other driver was on his way to a work meeting, but he saw that my son was very shaken up. He stayed with my son until I was able to get to the accident scene. He showed tremendous RandomActsofKindness to the young man who disrupted his day. Thank you, sir, for demonstrating patience and compassion. Forever grateful!

chinese_item: 我在学校里有个非常好的朋友。但我发现他很伤心，而且一天比一天难过。后来，我趁他不注意偷偷溜到他家，看到三个同学开始欺负他，还打他。于是我叫来我的朋友们，我们一起制止了欺凌，让他开心起来。
english_item: In my school, I had a very good friend. But I saw that he was very sad and each day he was sadder. Then I followed him to his house, without him noticing and I saw that three kids from the school started bullying him, and hitting him. So I called my friends and me and my friends sort to stop the bullying. And made him happier.

chinese_item: 我小学时学校里有个叫纳撒尼尔的孩子，他是个非常善良可爱的孩子，但因为脸部畸形，总是被欺负。还有一个叫娜塔莎的女孩，她几乎和所有人都是朋友，而且她能强迫任何人做她想做的事，因为她根本不在乎惹上麻烦。由于她非常有名，尤其以酷炫著称，所以如果哪个孩子告诉老师她做了坏事，老师们总是半信半疑。娜塔莎是欺负纳撒尼尔的主要对象，她散布关于他的谣言和谎言，让大家都不喜欢他，导致他几乎被全校的人欺负。只有一个孩子佩顿例外，佩顿也是个很酷的男孩，他总是对纳撒尼尔很好，总是维护他，但没过多久，佩顿也开始被欺负，仅仅因为他选择和纳撒尼尔做朋友。我知道他并不想欺负纳撒尼尔，但他还是开始欺负他。那时候我性格比较内向，朋友也只有几个，所以我尽量不去管，因为我不想欺负他，也不想被欺负。老师们几乎对此毫不知情，所以我决定告诉校长，我们启动了一个项目——“纳撒尼尔项目”。这个项目一开始很有效，但后来换了校长，项目就被取消了。纳撒尼尔再次遭到欺凌，情况很快恶化到他自杀的地步。学校召开了一次关于校园欺凌的集会，之后校园欺凌现象就很少见了，老师们也开始严格管教学生与朋友交谈和小组作业。我真希望我当时能坚持下去。
english_item: There was this kid Nathaniel who went to my school in elementary and he was a really nice and sweet kid but he always got bullied because of his facial deformation. There was also a girl named Natasha who pretty much was friends with everyone and could force anyone into doing what she wanted because she didn't care about getting in trouble, and since she was so well-known especially for being a cool person, teachers always doubted them if a kid told them she did a bad thing. She was Nathaniel main bully, she spread rumors and lies about him that made people not like him, causing him to get bullied by pretty much the whole school. Except from this one kid Payton, Payton was also a really cool guy, he was always nice to Nathaniel and always stuck up for him, but soon enough Payton also started to get bullied, just because he chose to be friends with Nathaniel. I knew he didnt want to but he started to bully Nathaniel too, I was a quiter kid then and had only a couple friends so, i tried not to get involved as i didnt want to bully him, nor be bullied. The teachers were pretty much oblivious and so i decided to tell the principal and we started a project, Project Nathaniel it worked for some time but then we got a new principal and the project was cancelled. He sted to get bullied again and soon enough it got so bad to the point that he took his life. We had a school assembly about bulling and after that bullying was a rare sight, and teachers became more strict with talking with friends or group projects. I really wish I'd kept trying.

chinese_item: 超市收银员清点我买的东西时，发现比我身上带的钱多了12美元。我正要从袋子里拿出东西，这时另一个顾客递给我一张20美元的钞票。“别给自己找麻烦了，”我对他说。“我给你讲个故事，”他说。“我母亲得了癌症，正在医院里。我每天都去看她，给她带花。今天早上我又去了，她因为我又把钱花在花上而生气了。她要求我用这笔钱做点别的事。所以，给你，收下吧。”
english_item: When the supermarket clerk tallied up my groceries, it was $12 over what I had on me. I began to remove items from the bags when another shopper handed me a $20 bill. ?°Please don?ˉt put yourself out,?± I told him. ?°Let me tell you a story,?± he said. ?°My mother is in the hospital with cancer. I visit her every day and bring her flowers. I went this morning, and she got mad at me for spending my money on more flowers. She demanded that I do something else with that money. So, here, please accept this.

chinese_item: 在我十几岁快二十岁的时候，我经常去爷爷的墓地。我经常去，因为爷爷总是能给我带来慰藉。有一天，我特别难过，非常想念他。这时一位老先生路过，问我是来探望谁的。我告诉他后，他说：“他真是个幸运的人，有这么一位关心他的人。” 二十多年过去了，我常常想起这位老先生。他永远不会知道，他的话对我意义有多么重大。
english_item: When I was in my late teens, I was visiting my grandpa's grave site. I did this a lot, for my grandpa always gave me comfort. One particular day, I was feeling exceptionally sad and missing him greatly. An elderly gentleman walked by. He asked me who I was there visiting. I told him and he said to me, "He was one very lucky man to have someone who cared so much." Over 20 years later, I think of this man often. He will never know how much his words meant.

chinese_item: 为什么我现在对任何人都没有感觉？  我现在在上大学，这里有很多漂亮女生，客观来说她们都很吸引人，但我就是对她们没有感觉。  我曾经暗恋一个高中女生五年多，直到几个月前才彻底放下她。现在我对其他女生完全没有感觉，尽管我和不少女生聊过天，也很享受了解她们的过程。在放下她之前，我对一些女生还有点好感，但现在这种感觉完全消失了。  以前我害羞不敢和女生说话的时候，更容易对她们产生好感/喜欢上她们。但现在我意识到她们也只是普通人，而且自信心也增强了，所以和她们说话时，我反而比和男生说话时更害羞一些。  这只是暂时的麻木期吗？我其实对女生没什么欲望，所以需要很长时间才能喜欢上一个人，但现在那种喜欢上一个人的感觉却让我感到麻木，甚至有点空虚
english_item: Why do I have no attraction towards anyone right now? I’m in university right now and there’s so many pretty girls that I can say are objectively attractive but I just don’t feel anything for them.  I used to have a crush on a girl from highschool for over 5 years and only recently got fully over her a few months ago and I just don’t feel that I have any attraction towards any other girls despite talking to quite a few and enjoying getting to know them. Before being over her I still felt low levels of attraction to some girls but now it’s all gone.  It felt easier to get crushes/feel attraction to girls when I was shy of talking to girls but after realising they’re legit just people like me and increased confidence I’m only an inkling more shy talking to them compared to a guy.  Is this just a numb period? I don’t really lust at all after girls so it makes me take a while to start to like someone but right now that feeling of being able to like someone just feels numb and it makes me feel kinda empty?

chinese_item: 没祝福我嫂子  今天早些时候，我嫂子和哥哥告诉我们她怀孕了。我真心为他们高兴，没有任何理由不高兴。但不知为何，当时我却无法表达我的喜悦。我真希望当时至少能说声“恭喜”。不过说实话，我平时就是这样，对谁都比较内向。  他们最近刚结婚，但之前我和嫂子关系不太好，因为一些误会。所以，我不知道不祝福她会不会显得不妥。我有时觉得她很敏感，容易把事情往心里去，即使那些话不是针对她的（尤其是在结婚前）。我并不怪她，只是希望她能更理解我。并不是我不喜欢她，我只是性格比较内向，不爱说话。  总之，我现在不知道该怎么办了。我觉得我没办法当面说这些，那我应该给她发条短信解释一下我为什么看起来有点不对劲吗
english_item: Didn't wish my SIL Earlier today, my SIL and brother told us that she is pregnant. I'm genuinely happy for them; I have no reason not to be. But for some reason, I couldn’t express my happiness at the time. I wish I had at least said a simple congratulations. Honestly, though, that's just me most of the time with everyone—I tend to be quiet.  They recently got married, but things weren't great between my SIL and I before, due to some misunderstandings. So, I’m unsure how not wishing her will come across. I sometimes feel like she’s sensitive and takes things personally, even when it wasn’t directed at her (especially before marriage). I don’t blame her, but I just wish she understood me better. It's not that I don’t like her, I'm just a shy, quiet person.  Anyway, I’m not sure what to do now. I don’t think I’ll be able to say this in person, so should I send her a text explaining why I might’ve seemed off?

chinese_item: 明天要和朋友见面，我既害怕又紧张。我讨厌这种感觉。  好久没和朋友出去玩了，现在又要和他们见面，我一想到就紧张得要命。感觉自己好可怜。大多数人都喜欢出去和朋友见面、叙旧什么的。  而我呢，光是想想就浑身发抖，手指都快抠破了。  我怎么能这样生活下去？和喜欢我、也乐于见到我的朋友们去酒吧待几个小时，这种平常的事情，竟然让我感觉如此“糟糕”。  我觉得做什么都像是在做苦差事，而不是享受。感觉自己没什么可说的。但为了应付，我只能强颜欢笑，装模作样。  有没有人能感同身受，或者给我一些建议，帮我摆脱这种感觉
english_item: Dreading and so nervous about meet friends tomorrow. Hate this feeling. Going out with friends again for the first time in a while and I'm a ball of nerves thinking about it. Makes you feel so pathetic. Most people love going out meeting people, catching up etc  Here I'm literally shaking and picking my fingers to bits thinking about it.  How can I live like this? When something so mundane as going to the pub for a few hours with friends who like you and are happy to see you is such a dreadful experience.  I feel like everything is such a chore something ive got to do rather than something I enjoy. Feel like I have nothing to say to people. Got to fake it and put on a performance to get though tho  Anyone relate or offer me advice to deal with these feelings.

chinese_item: 作为一个最近才搬到美国的美国人，感觉无论走到哪里，都会被提醒正在发生的事情，这让我精疲力竭，甚至连简单的日常活动都难以完成……有什么建议吗？  作为一个高敏感人群，我看到美国现在发生的一切，真的为人们感到难过。我看新闻，几乎全是标题都在说每个人的生活都会变得更糟。我住在保守州，走到哪儿都能看到极右翼的广告攻击民众。感觉无论我做什么都无法摆脱这一切，真的太累了。想出去散散步，清醒一下头脑？出门到处都是巨大的特朗普旗帜。想想想生活中即将到来的美好事物？没办法——等到真正到来的时候，我可能也负担不起。想和朋友们聚会？几乎每次社交场合都会遇到朋友或熟人，他们因为当前的政治环境而遭遇不幸。想看场体育比赛放松一下？那些奇怪的政治广告，说什么要抓人然后把人遣返，真是够了。  我不知道该怎么办。我精疲力竭，情绪崩溃。有没有其他高敏感人群能给我一些建议
english_item: As an American recently, seems like wherever I go I get reminded of what's going on and it's draining me to the point where I am struggling to do simple tasks... Any advice? As an HSP, I see everything going on in America at the moment and I just feel so bad for people. I read the news and it's pretty much headlines saying that everyone's lives are going to get worse. I live in a red state and I can't go anywhere without seeing alt-right ads attacking people. I feel like no matter what I do I can't escape it and it is SO draining. Want to go for a walk to clear my head? Can't go outside without seeing huge Trump flags. Want to think about the good things coming up in my life? Can't - probably won't be able to afford it when it finally comes around. Want to hang out with my friends? Practically every social situation involves a friend or someone they know that something bad is happening to them because of the political climate. Want to watch a sporting event to chill? Weird political ads saying they're going to find and deport people come on.    I just don't know what to do. I'm exhausted and emotionally drained. Do any other HSPs have advice?

chinese_item: 被虚构故事影响  我八个月前读过一本小说，后来它被改编成了漫画。每次看到漫画的画面，我都会感到一阵强烈的情绪涌上心头，忍不住哭泣。这让我开始讨厌这个故事。没错，故事确实很糟糕，但我为什么无法释怀？我刻意回避所有与它有关的东西，但只要看到一张漫画的图片，那种强烈的情绪就会再次袭来。我感到很沮丧，为什么我无法摆脱它？为什么它对我的影响如此之大？我读到过，这可能是因为故事里包含了我在生活中缺失的东西。我可以嫉妒一个虚构的角色吗？这就是我如此讨厌它的原因吗？我无法释怀，这让我更加难过，甚至想哭
english_item: getting affected by a fictional story so there's a novel i've read 8 months ago it's turned into a comic, it aggected me so much everytime i see a panel of the comic i get an overwhelming sensation and start crying, it leads me to hating on the story sure the story is horrible but why i can't move on i stayed away from everything that concerns it but the moment i saw a pic from it all the overwhelming feeling came again, i'm so frustrated why i can't get over it, why does it affect me so much, i've read that it may be bcs the story have something i miss in mylife, can i be jealous of a fictional character? it this why i excessively hate on it, i'm so upset that i can't get over it, it makes me wanna cry even more

chinese_item: 为人父母真难  我完全没意识到为人父母意味着什么。  我一直想要孩子，这是我们从小到大的生活习惯，我甚至都没怎么考虑过这个问题。  儿子出生后，一些现实狠狠地打击了我。我的意思是，每个父母都知道这是一件多么改变世界的事情。但除此之外，我的高敏感人格（当时我并不知道自己有）也让我感到非常困扰，比如，我极度害怕他会出任何意外。一开始，所有的小细节都让我焦虑不已，然后是第一次生病、第一次高烧等等。我害怕在某些情况下自己会束手无策。  我们刚生了第二个孩子（5个月大），所以妻子大部分时间都在照顾小宝宝，而我则成了现在3岁儿子的“主要”照顾者。  虽然这种情况有所缓解，但这种焦虑仍然存在。随着时间的推移，又出现了一些我几乎无法应对的事情。比如，他现在在某些情况下完全失去了理智，会尖叫，甚至可能会打人。我知道这是正常的行为，我应该尽量不去理会，但这真的让我很难受。  他开始偏爱一位家长，他的母亲。所以一年来，我经常听到他说“我喜欢妈妈，我不喜欢你”。或者，有时候他想要什么东西却得不到，或者纯粹是因为沮丧而尖叫，他根本不想让我安抚他。我根本无法靠近他。他只会尖叫着喊妈妈。  有时候我真的快要崩溃了。我也不知道自己为什么要写这些，大概只是想倾诉一下吧
english_item: Parenting is hard I was absolutely blindsided what parenting means.  i always wanted kids, it was just the way we grew up, didn’t even think much about it.  After my son was born, some realities hit me hard. I mean, every parent knows what a world changing event this is. But besides that, my HSP side (i didn’t know i was back then) had real problems with f. e. the extreme fear anything could happen to him. At the beginning all these small details, then the first illnesses, the first high fever etc. The fear of being helpless in some situations.  We just had another baby (5mo), so my wife is mostly busy with the small one and i became the ?main“ person for the now 3yo.   and while this settled a little, it’s still there. But over time other things came up i hardly could handle. F. e. when he is now totally unrationel in some situations and screaming and possibly hitting. I know this is normal behavior and i should just mostly sit it out, but it hits me so hard.  He also started favoring a parent. His Mother. So for a year now i hear things like ?i like momy, i don’t like you“. or in some situations he wants something he cannot get or out of pure frustration when he screams he absolutely doesn’t want me to soothe him. No way to get near him. He will mostly scream momy.  I can hardly bear this sometimes. Idk why i’m writing this. I guess just to get it off.

chinese_item: 交朋友很难，我该怎么办？  上个月我和这个女孩出去玩过一次。说实话，那次真的很尴尬，我们俩都很紧张。  我已经七个月没和人一起出去玩了。自从毕业工作以来，就一直没怎么和人一起玩。  我们都还年轻，才19岁，但我们该怎么做才能避免尴尬，真正玩得开心，像朋友一样相处呢？我感觉很勉强，很不自在，我相信她也有同感。  她一直想再和我出去玩，但我一直推脱，因为我知道上次的经历
english_item: What should I do when it’s hard to make friends? I hung out with this girl once already last month. To be honest, it was really awkward and we were both nervous.   I haven’t hung out with someone like that in seven months. After graduation and working in the mean time.   We’re young, 19, but what can we do to not be awkward and actually have fun and be how friends are supposed to be? It felt really forced and uncomfortable on my end and I don’t doubt she felt that way too.    She’s been asking to hang out again but I’ve been pushing it off because I know how the last time went.

chinese_item: 天哪，我必须做出改变。  我买了一块智能手表，第一次拥有了让我感到压力巨大的数据。比如在酒吧或人群中，和朋友一起听音乐、听人说话，坐公交车，打电话等等。所有这些显然都会给我的身体带来很大的压力。我开始记录并查看数据。我还看到了我的“冻结反应”。但对我来说，很明显，我做了那么多让我压力山大的事情，却因为孤独而继续去做。我真的太不关心自己的健康了。我知道这些事情会给我带来压力，但不知道压力有多大。我一直以高敏感人群的身份生活，却表现得像个普通人。我真的不知道对于高度敏感的人来说，生活究竟是如何度过的。但我必须开始尝试改变
english_item: Holy shhh i have to change something I purchased a smart watch and for the frist time i have the data what is high stress for me. Being in a bar or outside in a crowd, being with friends with music and people speaking, being in a bus, a phone call ect. All of this is apparently very high stress for my body. I just started taking notes and looking at the data. I also saw freeze reactions. But for me is clear, how long i made things, that stress me the hell out but i did them anyways because lonliness. I really did not care enough for my wellbeing. I did know, that these things are stressful for me but not how much. I was living as HSP and acted like i was a normie. I really don't know how life is even possible for very sensitive persons. But i have to start trying.

chinese_item: 关于别人“欠”我钱的焦虑  当亲密的朋友或伴侣主动提出要帮我付钱（比如活动门票或餐费），而你因为他们说会还钱就买了，结果他们却“恰好”忘记了，你会怎么做？尤其令人沮丧的是，如果不是他们说服了你，你根本不会买。举个例子，最近我的伴侣提前一周说，他们会帮我买活动门票（在我买好票之后，因为我会先到现场），因为是他们想让我去的。我买了票，等着他们还钱，结果他们好像忘了？你会怎么跟他们沟通这件事？我真的不想直接说，因为那样会显得我很贪婪，我受不了这种感觉。想到他们会觉得我因为开口要钱而显得很麻烦、很贪婪，我就很焦虑。我也在想，我这样想是不是其实很贪婪——如果是这样的话，我希望有人能直言不讳地告诉我
english_item: Anxiety around people “owing” me money What do you do when a close friend or partner offers to pay for something (like an event ticket or meal) so you go ahead and buy it cause they say they’ll pay you back, but then they conveniently forget? It’s especially frustrating when you wouldn’t have bought it in the first place if they hadn’t convinced you. For example, recently my partner said a week in advance that they’d cover my ticket to an event (after I buy my ticket, since I’d reach the event first) because _they’re_ the one who wanted me to go to the event. I bought my ticket for myself waiting for them to pay me back, only for them to seemingly forget? How would you go about communicating that? I really don’t wanna say it directly because it’ll make me sound so greedy and I can’t stand that. It’s really anxiety inducing to think about them seeing me as a problematic and greedy person for asking. I’m also thinking that I actually am greedy for thinking this way — if so, I’d appreciate brutal honesty.

chinese_item: 有没有人跟我一样，跟人吵架或者关系紧张之后，会感到非常沮丧甚至想自杀？我现在真的很难过，就这些。我不太想谈这件事，但我希望我不是唯一一个在跟人发生冲突后会有这种强烈情绪的人。
english_item: DAE feel really depressed/suicidal after getting into a fight or some tension with someone? I'm really sad rn, that's all. I don't really want to talk about it but I hope I'm not the only one with such intense feelings after conflict with someone.

chinese_item: 我该给每天午餐都见到的男生留个纸条吗？  嘿，Reddit，我（17岁，女）突然喜欢上了一个男生，不知道自己是不是想太多了，需要大家的帮助。  有个男生我每天都看到他一个人吃午饭。他很安静，总是戴着AirPods，坐在同一个位置，看起来很内向。我平时也是一个人吃午饭，所以有一天我注意到他，突然觉得他长得挺帅，感觉他挺适合做男朋友的（虽然我对他的了解一无所知——年级、名字什么的都不知道）。  我们从来没说过话。我甚至不知道他知不知道我的存在。但是眼看学年就要结束了，我总觉得这会不会是个错失良机。我想过写个小纸条，比如“嘿，我午餐时间经常看到你，你看起来挺可爱的。我有点害羞，不过如果你想聊聊的话，这是我的[Instagram/Snapchat/等等]。” 不用太正式。但我很害怕。  万一被别人发现了怎么办？万一他看到我把纸条放下，觉得我很奇怪怎么办？万一他有女朋友，或者比我小很多怎么办？万一被老师看到了，我会不会因此惹上麻烦？我知道这只是一张纸条，但对我来说意义重大。而且我以前从来没做过这种事。  有人做过类似的事情吗？你们觉得这样的纸条是贴心还是奇怪？我现在很害羞，总是胡思乱想各种可能的结果。  谢谢
english_item: 。,"Should I leave a note for a guy I see at lunch every day? Hey Reddit, I (17F) have a random crush situation and I need help deciding if I’m doing too much or just overthinking.  So there’s this guy who I always see eating lunch by himself. He’s really quiet, always has his AirPods in, sits in the same spot, and seems super reserved. I sit by myself too, so I guess I just noticed him one day and started thinking he’s really cute and seems like boyfriend material (even though I don’t know anything about him—not his grade, name, nothing).  We’ve never spoken. I don’t even know if he knows I exist. But with the school year almost over, I keep thinking this might be a missed opportunity. I thought about writing a short note, like “Hey, I see you around at lunch and you seem really sweet. I’m kinda shy but if you ever want to talk, here’s my [Insta/snap/etc].” Nothing too serious. But I’m terrified.  What if someone else finds it? What if he sees me drop it off and thinks I’m weird? What if he has a girlfriend or is way younger than me? What if a teacher sees it and I get in trouble somehow? I know it’s just a note, but it feels huge to me. And I’ve never done anything like this before.  Has anyone ever done something similar? Would you find a note like that sweet or weird? I’m shy and overthinking every possible outcome right now.  Thanks.

chinese_item: 室友带毒贩回家我住在英国的一间合租房里，我们生活方式不太一样。我工作，平时低调生活，想着以后怎么才能摆脱合租。我的室友不工作，整天抽大麻，也不打扫卫生。他似乎对这种生活方式很满意。他懒到连出门买大麻都不用，直接让毒贩上门。我劝过他别这么做，因为这会让房子变得不安全。有很多理由不应该把毒贩带回家。万一交易失败了怎么办？万一他欠债了怎么办？他不仅让自己身处险境，也让其他人身处险境。结果他又让毒贩来家里买了价值超过100英镑的大麻。买这么多钱，而且毒贩还知道你住哪儿，就有可能被他雇人抢回来，然后装作什么都没发生过。这次我有点生气了，告诉他这样做很不尊重人。他没看出问题在哪，还骂我像个孩子。他似乎完全不明白抽大麻（我自己也偶尔抽）和把毒贩带回家有什么区别。
english_item: Housemate bringing drug dealer into house I live in a shared house (UK) where we have lifestyle differences. I work and just keep my head down whilst figuring out how to eventually not need to share. We have a housemate who doesn't work and just smokes weed all day whilst not cleaning. They seem perfectly content with this lifestyle. He's so lazy he doesn't even leave the house to buy weed, he gets the dealer to come to the house. I've asked him not to do this as it makes the house less safe. There are many reasons not to bring a dealer to your house What if a deal goes bad? What if he gets into debt? It's not just himself he's putting in danger. Well he got the dealer to come to the house again so he can buy over ￡100 worth of weed. When you buy that kind of amount and they know exactly where you live there is a risk the dealer will get someone to rob it back off him and pretend he had nothing to do with it. This time I kinda kicked off and told him it's not respectful. He didn't see the problem and called me a child. He doesn't seem to understand the difference between smoking weed (which I dabble in myself) and bringing the dealer to the fucking house.
